Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Reusable subsystem?block that generates a hidden buffer might result in incorrect code
Description: The code generator might generate an incorrect conversion operation when all ?of the following are true: The model contains a reusable subsystem that is connected to a Data Type Conversion block The Buffer for reusable subsystem ?model configuration parameter is selected? The Data Type Conversion block has the Input and output to have equal ?parameter set to Stored Integer (SI)  The input and output of the Data Type Conversion block have different types

Summary: Simulink Report Generator expected results in the DO Qualification Kit do not match generated reports
Description: Baseline qualification reports for Simulink Report Generator that ship with the DO Qualification Kit do not reflect changes made to the Simulink Report Generator.? Starting in R2019a, the Simulink Report Generator System Design Description report does not include sections for empty subsystems.? As such, you should manually review the differences between baseline reports and generated reports.?

Summary: Incorrect merged results when using three-way merge for Stateflow charts
Description: Three-way merge of Simulink models containing Stateflow charts creates incorrect merged charts for various chart objects. Incorrect merged result occurs when you perform these actions: Add, delete, or change properties of Stateflow data, event, or message objects.? Add or delete states, transitions, junctions under a box or a graphical function. Change junction positions. Add super-transitions crossing sub-chart boundaries.? Add or delete default and dangling transitions.?
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Vehicle Body 3DOF Longitudinal block has incorrect planar response for non-zero relative front and rear pitch angles
Description: The Vehicle Body 3DOF Longitudinal block incorrectlyapplies the suspension forces, resulting in incorrect planar response for non-zero relative pitch angles. Specifically, theblock uses the wrong sign for the internal longitudinal component of the front and rear axle forces. If you select?External forces , the issue does not apply.?
Workaround: Reroute these Info bus output port longitudinal suspension force signals:?BdyFrm.Forces.FrntAxl.FxBdyFrm.Forces.RearAxl.Fx?1. Subtract them.2. Multiply them by 2.?3. Input them to Fext input port.?See the attached example model (R2019a) and image.?
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 5, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: MATLAB crashes when executing Model Advisor check for?AUTOSAR model with message outport
Description: MATLAB crashes when you run the Model Advisor check "Check for bitwise operations on signed integers" on an AUTOSAR model where both of the following conditions are true: The model contains a Stateflow chart that sends messages. The Stateflow chart sends messages to a root outport that has QueuedExplicitSend ?data access mode.

Summary: Data Type Propagation block does not support symbolic dimensions
Description: A Simulink model with Data Type Propagation  block?containing symbolic dimensions returns this error message:Modelname/Data Type Propagation does not support symbolic dimensions propagation.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 5, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink might use an outdated enum definition when simulating or generating code for a referenced model
Description: Simulink might use the previous definition of a dynamic enumerated data type when all of the following conditions are true: The enumerated type is defined by using the?Simulink.defineIntEnumType ?function. The model hierarchy contains at least one referenced model. The Rebuild ?configuration parameter of the top model is set to If any changes in known dependencies detected . The enumerated type definition has changed since the model reference simulation or code generation target was last built.
Workaround: Set the Rebuild configuration parameter of the top model to If any changes detected.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier incorrectly reports decision objective for the last input index?of a Multiport switch block as Unsatisfiable.
Description: If a Simulink model contains a Multiport switch  block with following configuration:Number of data ports  set to 1 The data port is connected to the output of a?Mux ?block  then, Simulink Design Verifier always reports decision objective associated with the last input index?as Unsatisfiable  during the test generation analysis, even if it ?can potentially be covered by a simulation. Similarly, during dead logic analysis Simulink Design Verifier always reports this objective as Dead Logic .
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b

Summary: MATLAB might crash for models with an AUTOSAR mapping
Description: For models with an AUTOSAR mapping, MATLAB might crash during model load, while updating the diagram, or during code generation due to corrupt code mappings.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink model containing a Demux block connected to a Stateflow chart might generate incorrect code
Description: Simulink model containing a Demux block connected to a Stateflow chart might generate incorrect code as shown below: RootModel_P.ConstantValue[-127]  This issue is caused due to integer overflow in the matrix index.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 5, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Parameters are evaluated incorrectly when starting with double literal followed by more than approximately 4100 whitespace characters followed by additional operations
Description: When setting a block parameter to a value consisting of a double literal followed by more than approximately 4100 characters of whitespace (depending on the length of the double literal) followed by subsequent operations, the block ignores the subsequent operations.? This issue applies to any block numerical parameter.
Workaround: Ensure that the first 4096 characters of a block parameter include code in addition to the double literal and whitespace. ?For example, either of the following produces correct results:>> set_param(gcb, 'Value', ['0 + 1.5' repmat(' ', 1, 5000) '+ 7'])>> set_param(gcb, 'Value', ['1.5 + 0' repmat(' ', 1, 5000) '+ 7'])??
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Generating coverage report takes very long time
Description: It takes a very long time to generate a coverage report, especially when attempting to generate one report for multiple cvdata  objects, such as in this example: cvhtml('covReport', cvdata1, cvdata2, cvdata3, cvdata4);?
Workaround: To generate a report showing the total cumulative coverage result accumulated from multiple simulations, do not pass multiple cvdata as input arguments to cvhtml command. Instead, sum the cvdata objects from each individual simulation together and then, pass this cumulative result as an? input argument to cvhtml.?cvdataSum = cvdata1 + cvdata2 + cvdata3 + cvdata4;cvhtml('covReport_Cumulative', cvdataSum);?
Products: Simulink Check, Requirements Toolbox, Simulink Coverage
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier analysis on a subsystem fails if model parameter SolverPrmCheckMsg is disabled
Description: If SolverPrmCheckMsg  model parameter is disabled then Simulink Design Verifier analysis on any subsystem inside that model fails with the following error message: Changing property 'SolverPrmCheckMsg' is not allowed .
Workaround: This issue can be avoided by using either of the following two workarounds:Ensure that the?SolverPrmCheckMsg model parameter is enabled.Generate subsystem harness using Simulink Test and invoke Simulink Design Verifier analysis on that harness model.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model with local block outputs enabled and a Zero-Order Hold block with multiple rates inside a For Each subsystem
Description: Incorrect generated code is possible for a model that meets all of the following conditions: The? Enable local block outputs  model?configuration parameter is selected. A Zero-Order Hold block is inside a For Each subsystem  The Zero-Order Hold block's input and output ports have different sample times. The Treat each discrete rate as a separate task  model?configuration parameter is selected.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Copy block with the Exclude this block from Block Reduction optimization?parameter selected between the Zero-Order Hold block and the For Each subsystem output.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: PLC Coder could generate incorrectly typed matrix address expressions
Description: PLC Coder may generate code that has incorrect matrix address expressions when such addresses are not constants. This may lead to errors while compiling the code in the target IDE.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 5, R2021b

Summary: MATLAB might crash when building a model with the parameter?RollThreshold higher than the number of iterations of a For Each?Subsystem nested in a For Iterator?Subsystem
Description: Consider a model that has a For Each?Subsystem nested within a For Iterator?Subsystem. If the number of iterations of the nested For Each?Subsystem  is less than the RollThreshold  configuration setting of the model, then building such a model might result in a crash.? Note that not all models that meet the above conditions will result in a crash. The possibility of a crash is subject to the modeling semantics of the nested For Each?S ubsystem.
Workaround: Set the RollThreshold to a value less than or equal to the number of iterations of the nested For Each?Subsystem.For example, if the For Each?Subsystem is supposed to execute 4 times, then? set the RollThreshold to a value less than or equal to 4.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink might log wrong answer at root output port with automatic unit conversion
Description: Simulink automatic unit conversion might log the wrong answer at a root output port during simulation when all of the following are true: The logging SaveFormat  is set to Dataset .  The signal before the root output port is branched.  The root output port unit does not match the source signal unit and the units are convertible.  Simulink automatically converts units at the root output port.  At least one of the other branches has a different unit from this root output port.
Workaround: Manually insert a Unit Conversion block at the root output port instead of relying on automatic unit conversion.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model hierarchy in which one model has a single instance of a reusable library subsystem and another model has multiple instances of the same reusable library subsystem
Description: Incorrect generated code might occur for a model hierarchy containing two models that meet all of these conditions:?Model A references a reusable library subsystem once for a given specialization such as dimensions or data type. Model B references the same reusable library subsystem at least twice with the same specialization. The reusable library subsystem contains a block whose output is determined to be constant.  The generated code might have the wrong number of elements in the initialization code for the constant block outputs structure relating to the reusable library subsystems in Model B. The target compiler might not catch this incorrect code and produce an error. This code is an example of the constant block output structure for the reusable library subsystem:  typedef struct {? const int32_T Width;?????????? } ConstB_RLS_5QZ55YRG;  This code is an example of the constant block output structure for Model B:  /* Invariant block signals (default storage) */typedef struct {? ConstB_RLS_5QZ55YRG RLS1;????? ? ConstB_RLS_5QZ55YRG RLS2;????? } ConstB;   This code an example of the incorrect initialization of the model constant block output structure including the reusable library subsystem constant block output structure in Model B: const ConstB rtConstB = {? {??? 4,???????????????????????????? ??? 3????????????????????????????? ? }? ? {??? 4,????????????????????????????? ??? 3?????????????????????????????? ? }}; ?
Workaround: You can either make the reusable library subsystem a nonreusable library subsystem, or in all models that reference the reusable library subsystem, override the reusable library subsystem settings to break reuse with other models.?
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 2, R2021b

Summary: Error when attempting to import a DOORS 9 requirements module
Description: When you attempt to import a module from IBM DOORS 9, an error message that says?Could not recognize the date/ time format...  might appear. ?The date and time string included in the error message shows "HH:mm:ss" inserted in between the module creation or modification date and time. The error prevents the requirements module from importing to Simulink Requirements. This only happens on some installations of DOORS. One known reported case was with DOORS version 9.5.2.4, but other versions may be affected.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: MATLAB might crash when unit mismatch exists between?Simulink.Signal object and port
Description: When you update the diagram, MATLAB might crash while propagating data types and dimensions when:? A unit is specified on a Simulink.Signal  object assigned to a signal. The unit does not match the unit specified at the source or destination port of the signal.  The crash report might contain output similar to: [? 0] 0x00007ffc28512fe3 sl_units_diagnostics.dll+00143331 MLTerminate_sl_units_diagnostics+00122563[? 1] 0x00007ffc2850803d sl_units_diagnostics.dll+00098365 MLTerminate_sl_units_diagnostics+00077597[? 2] 0x00007ffc2851bf41 sl_units_diagnostics.dll+00180033 MLTerminate_sl_units_diagnostics+00159265[? 3] 0x00007ffc2853d9d1 sl_units_diagnostics.dll+00317905 slUnitTable::reportUnitMismatch+00000577
Workaround: Change the Unit?field of the Simulink.Signal object, or the source or destination port so that the units are the same.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 2, R2021b

Summary: Uninstalling a previous version of a toolbox that includes apps fails when multiple versions of the toolbox are installed
Description: If multiple versions of a toolbox that includes apps are installed, uninstalling a previous version of the toolbox fails silently.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 2, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation might occur for Simulink model when a data dictionary hierarchy is closed between simulations
Description: Simulation might result in a silent wrong answer when the following conditions are satisified: A model links to a data dictionary hierarchy. For example, a model links to adata dictionary, A.sldd , that links to data dictionary B.sldd .? The model uses a variable that is defined in the data dictionary hierarchy, but not in a dictionary that the model is directly linked to. For example, B.sldd .  ?Under these conditions, the silent wrong answer can occur when you take these actions:  Perform an update diagram or simulate the model. Close the data dictionaries by calling the function Simulink.data.dictionary.closeAll() . Modify entries defined in B.sldd  that are used by the model. Perform an update diagram or simulate the model again.
Workaround: To prevent incorrect simulation you can take either of these actions:Avoid using Simulink.data.dictionary.closeAll() between model compilations or simulations.Close model after calling Simulink.data.dictionary.closeAll().??
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 2, R2021b

Summary: Build failure might occur when using C Function or C Caller blocks in model references
Description: When you try to run or update a model, Simulink might throw a build error when all of the following conditions are true: The model contains a model reference The referenced model contains a link instance to a C Function or C Caller block in a Simulink library The Simulink library has simulation target custom code specified
Workaround: Load the Simulink library containing C Function or C Caller blocks explicitly before simulating the affected model.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: PLC code generated for Stateflow models that contain temporal conditions inside IF statements may be incorrect
Description: Consider following two possible transitions in a Stateflow chart with:Case 1:  [condition && after(5, sec)]  Case 2:  [after(5,sec) and condition]?  Following PLC code will be generated for? these cases because of the issue: Case 1:  IF condition <> 0.0 THEN? ? temporalCounter_i1(timerAction := 2, maxTime := 5000);? ? IF temporalCounter_i1.done THEN? ? ? ?result := TRUE;? ? END_IF;END_IF;  Case 2:  temporalCounter_i1(timerAction := 2, maxTime := 5000);IF temporalCounter_i1.done THEN?? ? IF condition <> 0.0 THEN? ? ? ? result := TRUE;? ? END_IF;END_IF;  The code generated in both these cases is different, however, the simulation behavior is same as case 2 code for both the Stateflow transitions.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 5, R2021b

Summary: Writing data to Azure Blob Storage fails with remote connection error
Description: Writing data to Azure Blob Storage fails and returns the error: Remote connection failed. Check the connection and/or try again later.  For example, using writetable:  >> writetable(T, 'wasbs://container@account/path_to_file/file.txt', 'WriteVariableNames', 0)Error using table/write (line 241)Unable to write to: 'wasbs://container@account/path_to_file/file.txt'Remote connection failed. Check the connection and/or try again later.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier Compatibility check fails with error changing property '<parameterName>' is not allowed
Description: When Compatibility check or SLDV analysis is run on a model having locked (read only) configuration parameter, the operation fails with error:Changing property '<parameterName>' is not allowed  For example if compatibility check is run on a model having configuration parameter BusObjectLabelMismatch ?set to read only, compatibility check fails with error: Changing property 'BusObjectLabelMismatch' is not allowed
Workaround: Before running Simulink Design Verifier analysis, ensure that the parameter (e.g. BusObjectLabelMismatch,?MultiTaskRateTransMsg) mentioned in the error message is not marked as read-only.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 7, R2021b

Summary: AUTOSAR map block: error message for invalid fixed point word length configuration of two input ports is not clear
Description: The following error message for cross port validation isunclear when generating code for an incorrectly configured Map block:  The word length of the first port must be greater than or equal to the word lengthof the second port. If both have the same word length, a port with an unsignedtype is considered greater than a port with a signed type. In this error message, the description of first and second ports refer to the signal constraints registration and not the physical port index of the block underneath.

Summary: Simulink might stop responding or give wrong answer from String Constant block when any block in model has?Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct as parameter value
Description: Simulink might stop responding or return an incorrect answer from the String Constant block when the model contains:  A block with a parameter that contains a MATLAB expression like Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct(aBusType)  A String Constant block?
Workaround: In a script or model callback function, to get the MATLAB?struct?as a variable, call the Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct(aBusType)?function before updating the diagram for the model.?Use the MATLAB?struct?variable as the parameter value for the block parameter.?
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check fails with an error Empty character vector corresponding to an enumerated value
Description: When compatibility check is run on an Export Function model having Model Arguments  of type Simulink.Parameter , the check fails with an error?Empty character vector corresponding to enumerated value .
Workaround: Remove Model Arguments from the Export Function model.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b

Summary: impzest?produces wrong answers when used with multichannel input
Description: The impzest  function swaps channels when used with a multichannel?recording. For example, the output channels in this example are swapped when the input is stereo. exc = sweeptone(2,100/44100);ir1 = impzest(exc,exc);ir2 = impzest(exc,0.5*exc);stereo = [exc 0.5*exc];ir = impzest(exc,stereo);subplot(2,1,1), stem([ir1 ir2])subplot(2,1,2), stem(ir)  This bug does not affect the Impulse Response Measurer  application because the app records only one channel at a time.
Workaround: Use impzest with single-channel inputs only. Process multichannel signals one channel at a time. (See example in Description of Problem.)
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: fwrite does not write UTF-8 character data correctly
Description: Using fwrite  with character or string data results in the wrong bytes written to a file with UTF-8 encoding. For example, this code returns 255 . The correct output is [207;168] . >> fid = fopen('file.txt','w','n','UTF-8');>> fwrite(fid,char(1000));>> fclose(fid);>> fid = fopen('file.txt','r','n','UTF-8');>> bytes = fread(fid,'uint8')>> fclose(fid);bytes =   255% But bytes should equal [207;168]
Workaround: Instead of fwrite(fid,textdata) use fprint(fid,'%s',textdata).For example:>> fid = fopen('file.txt','w','n','UTF-8');>> fprintf(fid,'%s',char(1000));>> fclose(fid);>> fid = fopen('file.txt','r','n','UTF-8');>> bytes = fread(fid,'uint8')>> fclose(fid);bytes =   207   168
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Simulink Test Manager degraded performance when test case has many baseline signals
Description: If you have a test case with more than a few hundred signal criteria objects, the performance of the Test Manager might be degraded.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 5, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Option Ensure outport is virtual?not honored in generated code for root Outport block when driven by a signal with storage class
Description: Incorrect code generated from a model when all of the following conditions are present: The model contains a root Outport block with option Ensure outport is virtual  selected. The signal driving the input port of the root Outport block resolves to a Simulink.Signal  object. The StorageClass ?property of the CoderInfo  property of the Simulink.Signal  object has a value other than Auto .   In this situation the generated code should combine the memory for the root Outport block and the memory specified by the Simulink.Signal  object. However,?the root Outport block incorrectly makes an extra copy of input signal.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Signal Routing distorted with load_system on a model containing Subsystem Reference block
Description: The input signals to the Subsystem Reference  block is distorted when the following conditions are TRUE:?Subsystem Reference ?block has few input and output signals. load_system ?used before open_system  command to open the model.  The input signal lines to the Subsystem Reference  block have incorrect layout, and when the spacebar is pressed to autofit the same, the zooming turns to 0%.?
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b

Summary: Computing model checksum during simulation and code generation takes a long time for some model configurations
Description: For some model configurations, simulation or code generation can take a long time and may appear to be stuck when the status in the lower left corner of the Simulink window displays Compiling:?Computing checksums: Started . For example, a model that contains several Model blocks might display this performance issue.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 2, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code may be generated in model with multiple n-D Lookup Table blocks with fixed-point Table data?data type and floating point Intermediate results data type
Description: Incorrect code may be generated if all of the following conditions are true: The model has multiple n-D Lookup Table blocks The individual n-D Lookup Table blocks have: Number of table dimensions  set to 1  or 2 . Same input and table data types. Table data  data type parameter is a fixed-point data type. Intermediate results  data type parameter is a floating point data type.  The multiple n-D Lookup Table blocks differ in their fixed-point slope values for the?Table data  data type.
Workaround: Replace each affected n-D Lookup Table block with Prelookup block(s) connected to an Interpolation Using Prelookup block.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: Error when updating or deleting tasks for common job scheduler (CJS) clusters
Description: After you use the vectorized?createTask() ?syntax to create multiple tasks at the same time on your local cluster or other common job scheduler (CJS) clusters, if you either update a task's?Function ?or?InputArguments , or delete one or more (but not all) of the tasks  then you can see a?'MATLAB:badsubscript' ?error or, in rare cases, can change a task's?InputArguments ?to incorrect values. In the latter case, the task might produce a silent wrong answer if the task runs successfully with the incorrect?InputArguments .
Workaround: As a workaround, you canAvoid updating the?Function or InputArguments?properties of tasks after you create them with the vectorized createTask() syntaxAvoid deleting some (but not all of) tasks created with the vectorized?createTask()?syntaxUse the non-vectorized createTask() syntax to create tasks in a loop
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: MATLAB may crash when using an S-function with tunable parameters that does not register run-time parameters
Description: MATLAB might occasionally crash when all of the following conditions are true: You are using an S-function with tunable parameters You do not register any run-time parameters through ssSetNumRunTimeParams , and  The parameters are n ot of type: double, single, integer, boolean, 1D char array or 2D char array?  For example, MATLAB might crash if the tunable parameters are fixed-point, struct, or char arrays of 3 or more dimensions, and no run-time parameter is registered.?
Workaround: Set the number of run-time parameters using ssSetNumRunTimeParams in your S-function with tunable parameters. Also, register the run-time parameters using?ssRegAllTunableParamsAsRunTimeParams, ssRegDlgParamAsRunTimeParam, or ssSetRunTimeParamInfo.?To detect if your Simulink model contains S-functions that could trigger this crash, set the?SfunCompatabilityCheckMsg?Configuration Parameter to?'warning'?or?'error'. Incompatible S-Functions will throw the error (or warning):"S-function "system" in "model" does not explicitly set the number of run-time parameters using?ssSetNumRunTimeParams. This compatibility diagnostic on the need for S-function upgrades can be set to?none,?warning, or?error?using the Configuration Parameters dialog".The stacktrace may contain lines similar to:[? 0] 0x00000000fe9ea270 libut.dll+00303728 ut_md5ChecksumRec::~ut_md5ChecksumRec+00000896[? 1] 0x00000000fe9eab7d libut.dll+00306045 ut_md5ChecksumRec::~ut_md5ChecksumRec+00003213[? 2] 0x00000001b825fdc7 sl_compile.dll+03210695 BusConnectionChecker::runChecks+00010407[? 3] 0x00000001b827626c sl_compile.dll+03301996 slcomp::slAddRTParamDataToChecksum+00000700[? 4] 0x00000001b8299a50 sl_compile.dll+03447376 slcomp::BdComputeChecksums+0002582??
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Greater than expressions that use the duration operator on the left hand side do not return true at the correct simulation step
Description: Stateflow incorrectly evaluates greater than expressions when all of the following conditions are true: The Stateflow chart uses a fixed sample time The expression includes a duration  operator on the left hand side The right side of the expression evaluates to a value that is not a whole multiple of the chart's sample time  When these conditions are met, Stateflow evaluates the expression as?true  one time step later than expected.
Workaround: For charts that include the duration operator on the left side of a greater than expression, ensure that the right side of the expression specifies time that matches sample time of the chart.Alternatively, you can change the sample time of the chart to accommodate all values of time that are used in the chart.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Discrepancy in value for?Override SIL or PIL Mode?between test case and expected report in Simulink Test
Description: In the IEC Certification Kit test suite for Simulink Test, the information section in test case?SLT_TIT_020? incorrectly specifies?Override SIL or PIL Mode  ?as?1 .? The correct value is?0 . As a result, the validation status fails due to a discrepancy between the?tTIR? baseline report and generated report.? Impacted files include:? <matlabroot>/toolbox/qualkits/iec/sltest/expected_results/tTIR.docx  <matlabroot>/toolbox/qualkits/iec/sltest/expected_results/tTIR.pdf  <matlabroot>/toolbox/qualkits/iec/sltest/expected_results/tTIR.zip

Summary: Wireless Waveform Generator app might transmit a distorted signal constellation from lab instruments and result in incorrect EVM
Description: The Wireless Waveform Generator app can transmit generated waveforms using lab test instruments supported by the?rfsiggen  function (Instrument Control Toolbox, see https://www.mathworks.com/support/bugreports/details/2413350 ). While using the app in the Transmitter mode, the signal constellation transmitted by an instrument might be distorted, leading to larger-than-expected EVM values. This occurs when provided IQ data values are greater than 1. The operation of the app in the Generator mode is not affected. This issue affects the output of RF instruments from all supported vendors, including: Rohde & Schwarz Keysight

Summary: Override SIL/PIL mode setting might be incorrect in the test result report
Description: If the model used in a test file is not on the path, the value of Override SIL/PIL mode ?set in the Test Manager might show an incorrect value in the test result report.

Summary: Discrepancy in value for Override SIL or PIL Mode between test case and expected report in Simulink Test
Description: In the DO Qualification Kit test suite for Simulink Test, the information section in test case SLT_TIT_020  incorrectly specifies Override SIL or PIL Mode   as 1 .? The correct value is 0 . As a result, the qualification status fails due to a discrepancy between the tTIR  baseline report and generated report.? Impacted files include:? <matlabroot>/toolbox/qualkits/do/sltest/expected_results/tTIR.docx  <matlabroot>/toolbox/qualkits/do/sltest/expected_results/tTIR.pdf  <matlabroot>/toolbox/qualkits/do/sltest/expected_results/tTIR.zip

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier Analysis finishes quickly without any progress
Description: When div-by-zero check is selected and user performs active logic analysis, in some cases, analysis may abruptly end reporting most objectives as undecided.

Summary: RF instruments might generate distorted signal constellation and incorrect EVM
Description: The signal constellation generated by an RF signal generator instrument might be distorted, leading to larger than expected EVM values. This occurs when provided IQ data values are greater than 1. This affects the following functions and apps: rfsiggen  (Instrument Control Toolbox) Wireless Waveform Generator app (Communications Toolbox, since R2019a), see https://www.mathworks.com/support/bugreports/details/2421208   This affects the output of RF instruments from all supported vendors, including: Rohde & Schwarz Keysight
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 5, R2021a

Summary: Unexpected error because of unresolved component names when simulating a?SimBiology.Scenarios?object using?SimFunction
Description: When simulating a?SimBiology.Scenarios ?object using?SimFunction , the simulation fails unexpectedly because component names are not resolved to model components in the SimBiology model. This happens when the names contain unnecessary square brackets. Examples of unnecessary brackets:  A species DrugTarget  is specified as [DrugTarget] . A parameter k ?is specified as?[k] . In a single compartment model, a species Drug 1 ?is specified as [Drug 1] .
Workaround: Remove the unnecessary brackets from quantity component names in the SimBiology.Scenarios object by using the?updateEntry?function to change their names.For example:Create a SimBiology.Scenarios object referencing species DrugTarget and Drug 1 with brackets:sObj = SimBiology.Scenarios("[DrugTarget]", [0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7]);?add(sObj, "cartesian", "[Drug 1]", [2.4, 2.5, 2.6, 2.7, 2.8, 2.9]);Change the names of the first and second entries in the SimBiology.Scenarios object:updateEntry(sObj, 1, "Name", "DrugTarget");??updateEntry(sObj, 2, "Name", "Drug 1");
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 5, R2021a

Summary: Exporting test-cases from Simulink Design Verifier to Simulink Test causes?Export operation aborted error
Description: Exporting test-cases from Simulink Design Verifier to Simulink Test could fail and display an?Export operation aborted  error dialog box under the following conditions:A Signal Builder harness is chosen for exporting the test-cases Simulink Design Verifier analysis is configured with parameter constraints
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 5, R2021a

Summary: Second calls to dlgradient do not return correct derivatives after using the lstm or gru functions with inputs containing a gpuArray.
Description: For models that use the lstm  or gru  functions for dlarray  inputs containing a gpuArray , calling dlgradient  more than once on values that depend on the outputs of the lstm ?or?gru  functions result in incorrect gradients in the second and subsequent calls. For example, in the following code snippet, the second, third and fourth calls to dlgradient  return incorrect results. dlY = lstm(dlX,H0,C0,weights,recurrentWeights,bias);  loss = mse(dlY,targets);  gradients1 = dlgradient(loss,dlX);  gradients2 = dlgradient(loss,H0);  gradients3 = dlgradient(loss,C0);  gradients4 = dlgradient(loss,weights);  ?

Summary: Incorrect storage class access function is used for custom TLC code
Description: Embedded Coder might use the value function of a storage class, even though the user authored TLC code requests the address.  For example, LibBlockInputSignalAddress ?returns the value function with a C address operator, if the custom block has a signal with a storage class that returns access function as its input.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 5, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for Mod block with AUTOSAR 4.0 code replacement library
Description: Incorrect code might occur for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains at least one Mod block whose two input types are of the same type. The input types are one of the following: int8  uint8  int16  uint16  int32  uint32   The AUTOSAR 4. 0 code replacement library is used to generate code. The? Mfx_Mod  function? is present in the generated code.  Because the block behavior is different than the AUTOSAR standard, numerical results from code generation will be incorrect when mod(x1, x2)  meets one of the following conditions:? ?x1 < 0  ?x2 = 0
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 4, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: Signal selection in 'BaselineCriteria' table of test case not applied correctly
Description: If you specify a bus signal in an external baseline file, the bus and its leaf signals are expanded in the BaselineCriteria table of the Test Manager. Bus leaf signals that you do not select in the table are incorrectly included in the baseline comparison test.

Summary: Incorrect markers for residuals plot in SimBiology Model Analyzer
Description: In the SimBiology Model Analyzer  app, if you try to plot residuals for fit results generated using a mixed effects fitting algorithm, the plot markers for IWRES  and CWRES  are swapped from those indicated in the legend.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 4, R2021a

Summary: Indexing a function as a variable causes MATLAB to crash
Description: Indexing a function as a variable is not allowed in MATLAB. For most cases, MATLAB throws the appropriate error, however, some cases might cause MATLAB to crash. The following example shows one such case: function thisWillCrash     function nest     end     nest.foo % Index nested function as a struct variable with field foo. This crashes MATLAB.end

Summary: Hierarchical signals are not plotted in the Simulation Data Inspector when you load a view
Description: When you load a view in the Simulation Data Inspector that includes plotted hierarchical signals, the hierarchical signals are not plotted by the view if the Block Path  property does not have a value. You can still plot the signals manually.

Summary: Unexpected error when using model arguments on a masked Model block
Description: Simulink will incorrectly error when you have a model hierarchy with three or more levels for which all of the following conditions are true: A referenced model has a model argument. Its parent model is referenced in normal mode. The Model block in this parent model is masked. The value of the model argument in this parent model is a model argument specified in the model workspace of this parent model.  The error will be similar to: The symbol 'X' resolved to a parameter object that was created by the MaskInitialization code of 'mid/ModelBlock'. Parameter objects should only be created in the base workspace, data dictionary, or model workspace.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a

Summary: Large CAD assemblies imported from cloud storage might affect simulation performance
Description: Models containing large assemblies that are stored on the cloud (such as, in OneDrive) might have degraded performance during simulations subsequent to the first simulation. The degraded performance is a slowdown in the simulation compared to runtimes when files are stored on a local or network directory. The slowdown occurs even if the files have not been changed since last being read. Normally, when models with CAD assemblies are loaded into a Simulink session for the first time, the operation takes some time. Subsequent simulations of the model during the same session run faster because they do not require reimporting the files unless they have changed since last being read.?
Workaround: Move the CAD files to a local or network directory.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 8, R2020b Update 4, R2021a

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility checking fails for model containing AUTOSAR Blockset lookup table blocks
Description: If you check Simulink Design Verifier compatibility for a model that contains AUTOSAR Blockset lookup table blocks, the function fails with the following error message: Dot indexing is not supported for variables of this type.Component:simulink | Category:Design Verifier compatibility error
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 4, R2021a

Summary: Final state not restored for referenced model when a noncontinuous derivative output changes at the final state
Description: Simulation results might be incorrect if all the following conditions are true:? The top model in a model hierarchy references a model that does not reference other models. The referenced model outputs a noncontinuous derivative signal. For example, the referenced model might contain a Pulse Generator block that connects to an Integrator block that connects to a root output port. The referenced model simulates in accelerator mode. The referenced model Type ?configuration parameter is set to?Variable-step . The referenced model?Final states ?and?Initial state ?configuration parameters are selected.  Simulation does not restore the final state when the referenced model output changes at the final state.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Changing a model argument value might not trigger rebuild of model reference target
Description: Simulation and code generation might use an out-of-date model reference target when a model argument value has changed and Simulink adds hidden Model blocks to the model. For example, Simulink adds hidden Model blocks when: The output of a Model block is a virtual bus. A Model block receives input from a?function-call input port.
Workaround: Delete the?slprj?folder and?.slxc?file from the previous build, then build the model again.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 5, R2021a

Summary: SLibIfVariantConditionForm16b error for S-Function blocks using?LibAddToCommonIncludes
Description: Simulink model containing S-Function results in the build error Attempt to call a non-function value: SLibIfVariantConditionForm16b ?when the below conditions are TRUE :The S-Function includes a header(example TestHeader. h) using the LibAddToCommonIncludes . This header is also included by another parameter/ dwork/ signal which is variant.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 4, R2021a

Summary: Extraneous AUTOSAR array data types exported when shared base data type has min or max values
Description: Array data types exported from an AUTOSAR model might differ from the corresponding imported array data types if both of the following conditions exist: Imported ARXML descriptions contain AUTOSAR array data types and a ServerCallPoint  argument that reference the same base data type. The shared base data type specifies min or max values.  When you generate code, the exported ARXML contains additional unnecessary array data types.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 4, R2021a

Summary: rtw.asap2SetAddress fails with the DWARF file when used to set the addresses in the ASAP2(a2l) file
Description: When you use the?rtw.asap2SetAddress? function? with the DWARF file to set the addresses in the ASAP2(a2l) file, a failure occurs with the following error message: ?"Index exceeds the number of array elements (0)".

Summary: Generated code does not compile when an Intel SSE (Windows) code replacement library is used with a MinGW64 compiler
Description: The generated code does not compile on a Windows platform when all of the following conditions are true:?The Code replacement libraries  parameter is set to?Intel SSE(Windows) . ?The Toolchain  parameter is set to?MinGW64 ?or  Automatically locate an installed toolchain  with MinGW64 being the default toolchain.  ?The generated code contains SIMD code replacements for the floor  or ceil  functions, so the generated code contains _mm_floor_pd  or _mm_ceil_pd function s.
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, set the Build configuration parameter to Specify.Click Toolchain details. In the table, if you are generating C code, for the?C Compiler?row, add -msse4 to the end of the statement in the Option column . If you are generating C++ code,?add -msse4 to the end of the statement in the?C++ Compiler?row.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 6, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Simulink crashes when same identifier is used for root-level Inport or Outport of a subsystem or referenced model that use Auto storage class
Description: Simulink crashes when these conditions are met:?Root-level Inport or Outport of a subsystem or referenced model use Auto  storage class. Same identifier is used for ports or signals inside the subsystem or referenced model.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020b Update 4, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: C Caller, C Function, MATLAB System, and Dataflow Subsystem blocks may generate wrong simulation results with bus type data
Description: The following blocks may yield wrong simulation results when using bus type data of any scope: C Caller C Function MATLAB System (when simulating using code generation) Dataflow Subsystem
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 3, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect ARXML data constraints exported for an AUTOSAR model containing multiple lookup tables
Description: Incorrect ARXML data constraints might be exported for an AUTOSAR model when all of the following conditions are true: The model contains multiple Simulink lookup tables. The lookup tables use the same field names for some of their table values or breakpoints. Table values or breakpoints that have the same field name specify different Simulink data constraints. For example, the table values or breakpoints use different min and max values or different data types.  In the exported ARXML for the model, the data constraints for table values and breakpoints that use the same field name are not correctly differentiated.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a

Summary: MAAB check for na_0005?displays an incorrect warning for ports that displays names
Description: Model Advisor MAAB check for?na_0005 :?Block port name visibility ?displays the following warning and recommended action for?ports?that displays name explicitly set by user when Hide Automatic Names  Model setting is turned ON  and Hide Automatic Name  for the port is Auto .Warning : Model does not display port names for the following blocks . Recommended Action : Display the port block name. Deselect Format > Auto Name > Hide Automatic Block Names.?  This warning message is incorrect as the Hide Automatic Names  setting does not affect blocks that are named explicitly.

Summary: ARXML exported for COM_AXIS lookup table in AUTOSAR model contains extra SwRecordLayoutGroup
Description: In the ARXML exported for a COM_AXIS lookup table in an AUTOSAR model, the SwRecordLayout  contains an extra SwRecordLayoutGroup .?The additional SwRecordLayoutGroup  is not required because the lookup table implementation data type is an array.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 4, R2021a

Summary: Analysis may terminate with an?Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier back end?error message.
Description: Analysis might terminate without producing the results due to floating-point computations in the model that produce very large values. When you run the analysis with a user interface, it displays the following error message: Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier back end.  When you run the analysis in the?Test Generation ?mode, the error message displayed at the MATLAB Command Window is: Test generation produced errors  In releases beginning in R2018a, the below stack trace is produced: src/data_graph/graph_rat_prop-new.c:475 err=data_graph_err_rat_val_too_big_for_double    src/data_graph/graph_rat_prop-new.c:110 err=data_graph_err_rat_val_too_big_for_double    src/tempo/tempo_safe_induction.c:1343 tempo_error=tempo_err_unknown  When this issue is hit, the stack trace will contain data_graph_err_rat_val_too_big_for_double
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 5, R2021a

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier ignores certain justification rules in filter files
Description: Simulink Design Verifier may ignore certain justification rules for individual objective outcomes while running Test Generation or Dead Logic Detection.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 2, R2021a

Summary: The WindXYZ inport of the Vehicle Body 3DOF Longitudinal?block can become wrongly connected internally to the external forces port when External suspension is selected
Description: When the External suspension  option of the block is selected, the WindXYZ inport can be incorrectly connected internally to the external forces port Fext, causing a units propagation error to occur.
Workaround: Check the External forces checkbox, then uncheck it. This will fix the miswiring issue.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: Requirements Traceability Report for MATLAB code file mentions obsolete file type
Description: When you generate a Requirements Traceability Report for a .m ?file, the section title includes an incorrect reference to an obsolete file type. The section title is? "Requirements from PATH_TO_FILE.req" ?but should be? "Requirements from PATH_TO_FILE.slmx."  Similarly, when you generate a report for a .m  file without traceability links, the displayed message says "PATH_TO_FILE.req does not exist or has no links"  instead of "PATH_TO_FILE.slmx does not exist or has no links."

Summary: Simulink Code Inspector might fail to verify the initial value of outports of an enabled subsystem in a referenced model
Description: Code inspection fails to verify the initial value of the outports of an enabled subsystem in a referenced model under all of these conditions: The outports are of Simulink.Bus  datatype. The initial value of the outports is set to a Simulink.Parameter ?that uses the?storage class Auto .

Summary: Custom template makefile on Windows does not fully honor?'MAKEFILE_FILESEP = /'?macro
Description: On Windows, the file separator is by default the  \ ? (backslash) character. If you use a custom template makefile that defines the file separator with this macro:  'MAKEFILE_FILESEP = /'??   then, the source-to-object rules in the generated makefile can contain paths with mixed file separators. For example:?  C:\MATLAB\R2019a\toolbox\coder\rtiostream\src\utils/rtiostream_utils.c   Only the last file separator is set to the? /  (forward slash) character, which results in compilation errors: . ../llvm-9.0.1.1/WIN64/bin/clang -c -D__VXWORKS__ ....... -O2 C:\MATLAB\R2019a\toolbox\coder\rtiostream\src\utils/rtiostream_utils.c  clang: error: no such file or directory: 'C:MATLABR2019atoolboxcoderrtiostreamsrcutils/rtiostream_utils.c'  clang: error: no input files  make.exe: *** [rtiostream_utils.o] Error 1
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 3, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB code with System objects stored in cell arrays generates HDL code with only one instance for all objects in the cell array
Description: When System objects are stored in cell arrays and step method is called by dynamically selecting one element from the cell array, the generated HDL code does not create instances for all System objects. In generated HDL code, one instance gets reused for all the elements in the cell array. For the following example MATLAB code only one RAM is created. function y = fcn(u, c)    persistent objs  if isempty(objs)  ? ? objs = cell(3,1);  ? ? for ii=1:3  ? ? ? ? objs{ii} = hdl.RAM;  ? ? end  end    y = step(objs{c}, u, uint8(1), true);
Workaround: Store System objects in individual variables and not in cell arrays. Do not assign to System object variables conditionally.function y = fcn_cell_workaround(u, c)persistent obj1 obj2 obj3if isempty(obj1)? ? obj1 = hdl.RAM;? ? obj2 = hdl.RAM;? ? obj3 = hdl.RAM;endy = coder.nullcopy(uint8(0));if c == 1? ? y = step(obj1, u, uint8(1), true);endif c == 2? ? y = step(obj2, u, uint8(1), true);endif c == 3? ? y = step(obj3, u, uint8(1), true);end
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 8, R2020b Update 4, R2021a

Summary: Closed data dictionary might cause unnecessary rebuild of model reference simulation or code generation target
Description: Simulating, generating code, or updating the diagram of a top model in a model hierarchy might cause an unnecessary rebuild of a model reference simulation or code generation target when all of the following conditions are true: The referenced model is configured to simulate in accelerator mode. The referenced model is?not loaded. The referenced model uses a data dictionary that is not loaded. The referenced model contains a MATLAB Function block or a Stateflow block that uses a data type defined in the data dictionary. The first build of the model hierarchy successfully completed.  The rebuild message looks like: ### Model reference simulation target (aModelRef_msf.mexa64) for model aModelRef is out of date because a MATLAB file used by a Stateflow block or a MATLAB Function block has changed.
Workaround: Load the referenced model or the linked data dictionary.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 2, R2021a

Summary: Simulation 3D Probabilistic Radar does not use?Detection range parameter to detect objects
Description: The Simulation 3D Probabilistic Radar does not use the?Detection range ?parameter value to detect objects.??Instead, if you specify a Detection Range  parameter value, the block incorrectly returns detections within the default 1 m to 150 m range.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: The command window password prompt for Matlab Job Scheduler does not correctly wait for input
Description: When using a Matlab Job Scheduler configured with security level greater than zero, certain operations require the user to interact with a password prompt to confirm their identity. If the MATLAB client is running in nodisplay mode, this prompt switches from an interactive window to a running in the command window. For some operations, this command window prompt does not correctly wait for the user input. This means the prompt cannot be correctly completed, and instead the user input is executed as a command in the MATLAB console.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect documentation for configuration parameter?Use algorithms optimized for row-major array layout?(UseRowMajorAlgorithm)
Description: The documentation for configuration parameter?Use algorithms optimized for row-major array layout ?(UseRowMajorAlgorithm ) contains this incorrect statement: "You can enable the Use algorithms optimized for row-major array layout  configuration parameter to enable efficient algorithms, which might result in numeric differences between simulation and generated code."
Workaround: Refer the updated documentation link -?https://www.mathworks.com/help/simulink/gui/use-algorithms-optimized-for-row-major-array-layout.html

The assertion looks like the following:In cast: Assertion failed: isa(aValue)   [? 0] ../matlab/bin/glnxa64/builtins/sl_main/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcg_ir.so+13427972 _ZN2CG11DoubleValue4castEPKNS_5ValueE+00000086 [? 1] ../matlab/bin/glnxa64/simulink_plugins/sl_pir_cpp/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcgir_hdl.so+25912710  [? 2] ../matlab/bin/glnxa64/simulink_plugins/sl_pir_cpp/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcgir_hdl.so+25919655  [? 3] ../matlab/bin/glnxa64/simulink_plugins/sl_pir_cpp/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcgir_hdl.so+25921796  [? 4] ../matlab/bin/glnxa64/simulink_plugins/sl_pir_cpp/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcgir_hdl.so+20326342  [? 5] ../matlab/bin/glnxa64/simulink_plugins/sl_pir_cpp/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcgir_hdl.so+20590166?

Summary: Crash might occur when a function in MATLAB Compiler SDK Python package is called repeatedly
Description: If a function in a MATLAB Compiler SDK Python package is called repeatedly in a tight loop for thousands of iterations, a crash might eventually occur.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect simulation results when referencing a function-call model in accelerator mode
Description: Simulation results might be incorrect when all of these conditions apply: The model hierarchy contains at least three levels of models. The top model of the model hierarchy simulates in normal mode. A referenced model contains a Model block that has a function-call or trigger port and a triggered sample time type. That Model block's?Simulation mode  is Accelerator . The number of sample times varies among the models in that Model block's path. For example, the top model might have three discrete sample times, and the model that references that Model block might have two discrete sample times.

Summary: dateshift returns the wrong answer for the nearest end of a time unit
Description: The calculation of the nearest end of a time unit in dateshift  is incorrect, adding an extra increment to the result. For example, this call to dateshift  returns a datetime  value for the end of September 2019 (30-Sep-2019 )?when it should return the end of August 2019 (31-Aug-2019 ). >> dateshift(datetime(2019, 8, 17),'end','month','nearest')ans =   datetime   30-Sep-2019 Furthermore, for units of 'week' , 'month' , 'quarter' , and 'year' , the nearest value is determined based on the beginning of the week, month, quarter, or year rather than on the end of the specified time unit. This issue has an impact on results within a day of the midpoint of the specified time unit.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: The Capacity vector and Head vector?parameters?in the Centrifugal Pump (TL) block cannot be set to Run-time
Description: In the Centrifugal Pump (TL) block, the?Capacity vector ?and?Head vector? parameters?do not currently support run-time capabilities. However, the block dialog incorrectly provides the option to choose between the?Compile-time ?or?Run-time ?settings. Selecting?Run-time ?and simulating the model results in a compilation error: Function 'fluids_functions. polynomial_fit' must be an unprotected MATLAB function. ?In fluids. thermal_liquid. pumps_motors. centrifugal_pump
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: The Number of pole pairs parameter in PMSM (Single-Phase) block does not affect the frequency of the back EMF
Description: If, in a PMSM (Single-Phase) block, you modify the value of the Number of pole pairs  parameter, the frequency of the back EMF is unchanged.

Summary: Compilation error when Dynamic compressibility is set to Off?for heat exchanger sample variants in the?thermal liquid domain
Description: This issue occurs if Dynamic compressibility ?is set to Off  in: Heat Exchanger (TL), Simple Variant Heat Exchanger (TL-TL), Simple Variant Heat Exchanger (TL-G), Simple Variant? Simple Heat Exchanger Interface (TL)  In such situations, simulating the model generates this error message: Number of equations exceeds number of variables .?

Summary: Aggregated coverage data description missing from coverage report
Description: When a description is applied to aggregated coverage data, the aggregated coverage report does not display this description.  Descriptions applied to coverage data for individual test runs included in the aggregated results will be shown in the report, in the Aggregated Tests table next to the corresponding test.? The description for the overall aggregated coverage result is not shown in the report.
