Summary: Assertion thrown during HDL code generation from a Simulink model containing Gain blocks that are operating in "u*K" mode
Description: When performing HDL code generation from a Simulink model containing a Gain block, we throw the following assertion if the gain mode is "u*K", the input vector is unordered and the gain factor is a square matrix: Error: Evaluation of elaborate function on class hdldefaults. Gain failed with the error message: pir_core:pirudd:assertionFailed: Assertion failed: dom_pir_udd/ paramset. cpp:65:orientation. size() == 1 ?

Summary: MATLAB might crash when querying the input elements of a Bus Selector block with?get_param
Description: Querying the input elements of a Bus Selector block with get_param  might cause MATLAB to crash when all of these conditions apply: One of the elements of the input bus for the Bus Selector block originates from a Function Caller block. Any blocks between the Function Caller block and Bus Selector block are virtual blocks.  The query might look like: get_param('model/Bus Selector','InputSignals')
Workaround: If the output of the Function Caller block is not a bus, connect a Gain block with Gain set to 1?between the Function Caller block and the Bus Selector block.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 8, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: Error when creating link with IBM DOORS Next (DNG)
Description: When you create a link from an item in MATLAB or Simulink to an item in IBM DOORS Next (formerly known as IBM DOORS Next Generation, and often abbreviated as DNG), a dialog box might appear with an error that says "Failed to parse ProjectID ". This error might occur if the IBM DOORS Next service is hosted on Jazz server 6.0.6.1 or later version. As a result of this error, you cannot create links between items in MATLAB or Simulink and items in IBM DOORS Next.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 5, R2020b

Summary: If you set the Vehicle Body 3DOF Dual Track block Axle force?parameter to?External forces, the block incorrectly implements the lateral force signal input to the front right tire
Description: If you set the Vehicle Body 3DOF Dual Track block?Axle force? parameter to?External forces , the block incorrectly implements the signal array connected to the input port?FwF . Specifically, the block does not correctly use the array element for lateral force on the right front tire. The issue does not affect the Vehicle Body 3DOF Single Track block.?
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Error?Unable to launch the MATLABWindow application on?Red Hat based distributions
Description: This issue is applicable to the following Linux distributions:  Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.2 and later CentOS 8.2 and later Fedora 32 and later  The following error can be observed while installing MATLAB or while running certain features in MATLAB (such as Simulink Start Page, Help Browser, Live Editor, UIFigure, App Designer, Simulink Data Inspector, etc.): terminate called after throwing an instance of 'std::runtime_error'??  ????? what():? Unable to launch the MATLABWindow application ??? Aborted    You might also see: MATLABWindow application failed to launch. Unable to launch the MATLABWindow application.  Or if you enter this command in MATLAB: system(fullfile(matlabroot, '/bin/glnxa64/MATLABWindow'))   MATLAB displays: ./MATLABWindow: symbol lookup error: /lib64/libk5crypto.so.3: undefined symbol: EVP_KDF_ctrl, version OPENSSL_1_1_1b
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Delays while editing Simulink model with Simscape blocks
Description: Simulink models that contain Simscape blocks might experience a delay when moving and connecting blocks that have multiple ports.
Workaround: Connect at least one of the ports on the block before moving the block.

Summary: Block replacement fails for models containing Saturation Dynamic block
Description: Simulink Design Verifier block replacement fails when the block replacement rules are set to FactoryDefaultRules ?for a model containing Saturation Dynamic  block. The following error is reported in the Diagnostic Viewer: Sldv Switch Replacement block (mask) does not have a parameter named 'DoSatur'.

Summary: Creating a subsystem from a selection that contains an area that is connected to an annotation might crash MATLAB
Description: Creating a subsystem from a selection might crash MATLAB when the selection contains an area that is connected to an annotation with a line. For example, dragging a selection box around the area, right-clicking a selected element, and selecting Create Subsystem from Selection ?might crash MATLAB.
Workaround: Create a subsystem from only the area. For example, right-click the area, then select Create Subsystem from Area. Alternatively, delete the annotation connector before creating a subsystem from the selection. Then, re-create the connector.

Summary: Initial simulation does not use parameters derived from updated block parameters
Description: If you change a block parameter value that the block uses to derive additional parameters, the model does not use the derived parameters during the initial simulation. During the second simulation, however, the model does use the derived parameters. Before the initial simulation, if you invoke an update diagram, the model will use parameters derived from updated block parameters.
Workaround: If you change a block parameter value, invoke a diagram update. To update the diagram, from the Modeling tab, click Update Model or press Ctrl+D.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier might fail to complete normally when performing Out of bound array access detection on models containing matrix-typed data with an InitialValue
Description: Simulink Design Verifier might fail to complete normally when all of the following conditions are true: Design Error Detection is performed with?Out of bound array access   enabled A data object of matrix type has a non-empty InitialValue   specification (such as through the Model Workspace).  In some cases, analysis might fail with an "Error detection produced errors" message. In all cases, the return status of sldvrun will be 0 when this issue is applicable.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b

Summary: Connections inside a Subsystem Reference block appears broken when a model is loaded
Description: In certain situations, when you load a model that contains a Subsystem Reference block, the blocks inside the Subsystem Reference block might not get rendered correctly. Also, the connections inside the Subsystem Reference block might be broken. This issue might happen if you try loading a model with a Subsystem Reference block immediately after performing?any  of these actions: Copy and paste to the Subsystem Reference block in which the paste operation fails and throws an error? Converting a Subsystem block to a Subsystem Reference block in which the conversion fails and throws an error
Fixed In: R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: MATLAB warns about being disconnected from the license server after connection has been restored
Description: If you are using MATLAB with a network license and you have enabled idle timeout, you might encounter this issue. When the MATLAB connection to the license server times out and is idle a long time, MATLAB continues to issue warnings about not being connected even after the connection is restored. This commonly happens when a VPN connection is stopped and MATLAB is left idle overnight. Then, in the morning after the VPN connection is restored, MATLAB issues these warnings.
Workaround: To clear the messages, restart MATLAB.

Summary: Error when linking MATLAB Function block code to selection in IBM DOORS Next
Description: When you link a requirement from IBM DOORS Next to code in a MATLAB Function block by right-clicking the code selection and selecting Link to Selected Item(s) in DOORS Next , this error occurs:  Selection linking failed: index exceeds number of array elements (0) .

Summary: Requirements report fails to generate if links in the report have a custom link type
Description: Simulink Requirements link objects have a Type  property. The Type  property can be one of the built-in link types or a custom link type. When you generate a requirements report, you can choose to include the links to or from the requirements in the report. If any of these links have a custom link type, then the report fails to generate.

Summary: Simulink Requirements link is not copied from library block to referenced instance of library block
Description: When you add a masked library block or a?Subsystem?block from a Library to a Simulink model, a referenced instance of the library block is created. If the library block contains a link to a requirement in Simulink Requirements, the referenced instance of the library block also contains the requirements link. You can edit the referenced instance of the library block by disabling the link to the library block. Then, you can re-establish the library link by pushing the changes back to the library block. However, the requirements link might not be copied into the referenced instance of the library block in some cases. This can happen when you are working with a referenced instance of a library block that is nested inside of another referenced instance of a library block. In this case, if you click Disable this link ?from the warning message on the Simulink canvas, then the requirements link will not be copied into the referenced instance of the library block. Instead, if you click Disable Links ?in the Subsystem ?tab, or right-click the subsystem and select Library Links  > Disable Link , then the requirements link is copied into the referenced instance of the library block as expected.
Workaround: If you click?Disable Links?in the?Subsystem?tab, or right-click the subsystem and select?Library Links >?Disable Link, then the requirements link is copied as expected.
Products: Simulink Check, Simulink Coverage, Requirements Toolbox

Summary: Project does not automatically rename usages of data dictionaries inside internal test harnesses
Description: Project automatic renaming does not update data dictionary names  inside internal test harnesses saved in a model. When renaming a data dictionary, in the Rename  dialog box, Project offers to automatically update all links to the data dictionary. However, the automatic renaming does not complete the expected actions :?  When the user expands the impacted model and double-clicks the Data Dictionary  line in the Rename  dialog box, Project highlights the data dictionary of the containing model instead. When the user confirms renaming the data dictionary in the Rename  dialog box, Project does not update the link in the internal test harness.
Workaround: Manually update the data dictionary name in the internal test harness.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Requirements Editor might not display images in descriptionfor requirements imported with ReqIF
Description: When you import aReqIF file into Simulink Requirements, if the imported requirements containimages, the images might not display in the Requirements Editor.

Summary: Synchronous Machine block displays incorrect unsaturated characteristic
Description: For the Synchronous Machine block,?if you select the Electrical > Plot Open-Circuit Saturation (pu)  option on the block context menu, the displayed unsaturated characteristic is incorrect.?

Summary: Unable to import an SMBL file containing reaction modifiers
Description: When importing an SBML file with sbmlimport , SimBiology incorrectly handles reaction modifiers if the model contains multiple compartments or the name of the reaction modifier is not a valid MATLAB variable name. In some cases, the file will import without error, but the model incorrectly import species and reactions. In other cases, the import will fail with an error message such as the following: Invalid Reaction value. Spaces are required before and after species names and stoichiometric values, for example, 2 A + B -> 2 C. Stoichiometric values must be greater than zero. Type 'help SimBiology.Model.addreaction' for more information.Error in privatesbmlio
Workaround: Replace each modifier in the SBML file with a corresponding reactant and product with the same stoichiometric coefficient. For example, consider an SBML file containing the following definition for reactants, products, and modifiers in a reaction:<listOfReactants>  <speciesReference species="reactant" stoichiometry="2" constant="true"/></listOfReactants><listOfProducts>  <speciesReference species="product" stoichiometry="1" constant="true"/></listOfProducts><listOfModifiers>  <modifierSpeciesReference species="modifier"/></listOfModifiers>Replace the above SBML with the following:<listOfReactants>  <speciesReference species="reactant" stoichiometry="2" constant="true"/>  <speciesReference species="modifier" stoichiometry="1" constant="true"/></listOfReactants><listOfProducts>  <speciesReference species="product" stoichiometry="1" constant="true"/>  <speciesReference species="modifier" stoichiometry="1" constant="true"/></listOfProducts>

Summary: On Windows 10 May 2020 Update, a computer restart can occur when model simulation ends
Description: On Windows 10 May 2020 Update   and later operating systems, a computer restart can occur when Simulink Desktop Real-Time model simulation in external mode ends. The computer restart occurs more often when the model uses a large value for the ExtModeTrigDuration  parameter. This parameter specifies the size of external mode signal buffers.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for the cast?function might return a real matrix instead of a complex matrix with zero imaginary part
Description: If you use the 'like' ?argument of the?cast ?function in the generated code , the result might have incorrect complexity if all ?these conditions are true: The first input A  (the input array that is to be cast) is sparse, real, and numeric (not logical) The last input p  (the prototype) is complex
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Autorename fails for a Simulink bus object containing multiple bus elements.
Description: The autorename fails for a Simulink bus object that meets the following conditions: The bus object contains two or more bus elements. One of the bus elements is based on the type that is being renamed.  MATLAB stack trace: Operands to the || and && operators must be convertible to logical scalar values.  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.BusObjectHandler  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.BusObjectHandler  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.BusObjectHandler  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.BusObjectHandler  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.BusObjectHandler  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.BaseWorkspaceFileHandler/modifyObject  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.BusObjectHandler/refactor  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.VariableRefactoringHandler/refactor  Error in dependencies.internal.action.refactor  Error in dependencies.internal.action.refactor  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Dependency.refactor?
Workaround: Manually modify the remaining instances of the bus usages.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Polyspace on Windows crashes in the C to intermediate language translation phase
Description: When running Polyspace Code Prover, you might see an error message in the?C to intermediate language translation   phase. The message refers to a?ps_c_me.exe ?executable. For instance: System error Invalid argument (inval: Invalid argument)?Error: unable to execute C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2018b\bin\win64\ps_c_me.exe (errno=22).? The issue occurs because the internal?ps_c_me.exe  executable crashes on Windows if the full command is too long (32k characters limit). This might happen if you use a very large number of options when running Polyspace Code Prover.
Products: Polyspace Code Prover, Polyspace Code Prover Server

Summary: Failure to login to IBM DOORS Next when performing oslc.configure() procedure
Description: When logging in to IBM DOORS Next (previously known as IBM DOORS Next Generation or DNG), after the correct server name, user name, and password are provided by user, MATLAB prompts for the password again, and again, and then prompts to confirm server name and/or user name, followed by more password prompts, but login never succeeds after entering all correct information.

Summary: Simulink might crash when multiple models simulate simultaneously, but the models finishing last contain string signals
Description: Simulink allows multiple unrelated models to simulate at the same time. Simulink might crash if one model finishes simulation and the remaining models contain string signals.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: The JMAAB checks for jc_0025 and jc_0026 flag bus elements of?Boolean data type for missing mix/max values
Description: The Model Advisor checks Design min/max specification of input interfaces  (ID:mathworks.jmaab.jc_0025) and Design min/max specification of output interfaces  (ID:mathworks.jmaab.jc_0026) might flag bus elements of Boolean data type for missing min/max values.

Summary: SimBiology Model Analyzer can hang when loading saved projects with unfinished sampling runs
Description: If you add a generate samples step to a simulation program and then stop the program in the middle of execution, plots might be left in a broken state. ?If you save the project and tries to reopen it, the app might hang.

Summary: The spectrum plot is displayed in linear units.
Description: The spectrum plot of radarWaveformAnalyzer?is displayed in linear units but should be display in dB to agree with the y-axis label.?
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: The function factormay return wrong results for polynomial expressions when called with the option'FactorMode', 'Full'
Description: The function factor might return a wrong factorization of polynomial expressions when called withthe option 'FactorMode', 'Full' . The product of all factors and the expressionmay differ by a constant factor.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: sum(x,dim) might return incorrect answers for?single?and?half?inputs?in generated code that uses row-major layout
Description: Code generated for the function  sum(x,dim) , where?dim ?is the dimension to operate along,?might return incorrect answers if all  the following conditions are true: class(x)  is 'single ' or 'half ' The generated code stores  x ?in row-major order x  is not a vector x  is not empty The dim  argument is supplied size(x,dim)  is greater than 1024  size(x,k)~=1 ?for some?k < dim   This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: fmincon?code generated with?MATLAB Coder can return an incorrect result
Description: Because of an error in the code generation version of the fmincon  'sqp'  algorithm, the internal line search can sometimes cause the algorithm to converge to an infeasible point or a point that is not a local minimum. You can observe when the internal line search operates in the Step Length column of iterative display (Display  option = 'iter' ). When any reported step length is less than 1, the line search operates, and the code generation version of fmincon  can sometimes give an incorrect result. For example, consider the following nonlinear programming problem. objective = @(x)? x(1)*x(2) + x(2)*x(3);  nonlcon = @(x) deal( [x(1)^2 - x(2)^2 + x(3)^2 - 2; x(1)^2 + x(2)^2 + x(3)^2 - 10], []);  opts = optimoptions('fmincon','Algorithm','sqp', 'Display', 'iter');?  [x,fval,exitflag,output] = fmincon(@objective,ones(3,1),[],[],[],[],[],[],@nonlcon,opts);   When run in MATLAB, fmincon  returns?exitflag = 1? and the display shows that the Step Length at iteration 3 is less than 1. ?Iter? Summary: Incorrect analysis results on certain models containing Sqrt blocks with fixed-point input signal
Description: Simulink Design Verifier might report incorrect derived ranges and analysis results under the following conditions: The model contains a?Sqrt? block with function?parameter set to sqrt  or  rSqrt  The input signal type of the Sqrt  block is?fixed-point with more than 32 bits wordlength and/or non-zero fractionlength

Summary: removedose?causes MATLAB to crash
Description: When you call?removedose ?on a dose object that is not on a model, MATLAB crashes. To check whether the dose you are trying to remove is on a model, use getdose .

Summary: Generating a coverage report in the Test Manager for subsystem harnesses at different levels in a library causes an assertion failure
Description: Generating a coverage report in the Test Manager for subsystem harnesses at different levels in a library causes an assertion failure in cvi.TopModelCov.moveHarnessTest: failed assertion in b:\matlab\?toolbox\?slcoverage\src\?slvnv\?utils\?object.?cpp at line 2472: id>0 && id<pool_capacity && object[id]!=NULL[??0] 0x00007ffe7a7a775f Q:\?matlab\?bin\?win64\?slvnv.dll+00423775 modelcov_root_relatives_and_consistency+00000239[??1] 0x00007ffe7a7a6ec1 Q:\?matlab\?bin\win64\?slvnv.?dll+00421569 modelcov_merge_data+00000385..?.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 7, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Branched lines might be disconnected while automatically arranging system
Description: Automatically arranging a system block diagram might disconnect a line that is branched on one end and not connected on the other end.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when opening AUTOSAR model in which blocks are linked to a library
Description: MATLAB might crash when opening an AUTOSAR model if both of these conditions exist in the model: Simulink states, signals, or data stores are mapped to AUTOSAR variables. The mapped states, signals, or data stores are associated with blocks that are linked to a library.

Summary: Overwritten Embedded Coder Dictionary in Simulink data dictionary
Description: When you open the Embedded Coder app or the Embedded Coder Dictionary dialog for a model, if the model uses a configuration set that you store in a Simulink data dictionary, the Embedded Coder Dictionary in the Simulink data dictionary might be overwritten.

Summary: Unconnected Stateflow message input port without an internal receiving queue may crash MATLAB when you generate code
Description: MATLAB might crash when you generate code for a model that meets all of the following conditions: The model contains a Stateflow chart with a message input For message input Use internal queue ?check box is not selected The message input port is unconnected

Summary: Crash or assertion when using certain mappings in atomic subcharts
Description: Generating code from an atomic subchart with certain mappings and nested function calls might result in an error message in the Diagnostic Viewer:? ?CGIR assertion 'In flags: This code should be unreachable!'  The same issue might cause MATLAB to crash. Both situations produce a stack similar to:? [ 0] 0x000000028ef9e52c bin\win64\stateflow.dll+00910636 mwboost::serialization::singleton_module::unlock+00050156   [ 1] 0x000000028ef9e0cb bin\win64\stateflow.dll+00909515 mwboost::serialization::singleton_module::unlock+00049035?  [ 2] 0x000000028f6c8b36 bin\win64\stateflow.dll+08424246 sl_hmi::InstrumentedSignals::getCount+00474662?
Workaround: Only use mappings of matching scope:Map subchart inputs to main chart inputs.Map subchart outputs to main chart outputs.Map subchart data store memory to main chart data store memory or local data.Map subchart parameters to main chart parameters

Summary: MATLAB might crash with mask custom table parameter
Description: MATLAB might crash for a Simulink model when all of the following conditions are true :   Block dialog having mask custom table parameter. The custom table parameter contains associated dialog callback.   Example stack trace: [0] 0x000000000eed9bac SLCustomTableParamHandle::IsPrmOnOpenDialog [1] 0x0000000283ac7e72 setTableCellParamMI::invoke  [2] 0x0000000027b03c51 OpaqueMethodCallMetaData::callCMethod

Summary: Empty file and segmentation fault message when generating RTF documents on Red Hat Enterprise Linux 7 or CentOS 7.8
Description: If you generate an RTF document on a Red Hat Enterprise Linux 7 or CentOS 7.8 platform, the size of the resulting RTF document is 0 bytes and a segmentation fault occurs.

Summary: Compilation error when simulating model containing certain Simscape blocks
Description: When the underlying equations?for a Simscape block use the prod  function applied to a single-element vector or matrix, simulating a model containing such block may generate a compilation error similar to the following: Error compiling Simscape network for model m1.  Caused by:  ? ? Passing empty expression container where non-empty container is expected.

Summary: Saving a symfun object and loading it in a different MATLAB version can result in the loaded object having class sym
Description: Saving a symfun  object in R2019b or earlier and loading the mat file in R2020a can result in the loaded object having class sym  instead of symfun . No warning is printed.
Workaround: Convert the loaded object into a symfun:load data.matf(x) = f;?orload data.matf = symfun(f,x);

Summary: An error can occur when Model Advisor generates a report in PDF format
Description: When using the Model Advisor to generate a report in PDF format, an error can occur with a message similar to the following: Error using mlreportgen. dom. HTMLHTML error: attribute name expected

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB functions that recursively call themselves might produce incorrect code
Description: C/C++ code generation from MATLAB might produce incorrect code if all  of these conditions are true: The source MATLAB code contains a function that (directly or indirectly) calls itself? The first call to the recursive function (or the function that starts the recursion) passes a constant input to that function During recursion, the subsequent calls to that function pass different values for that input parameter?  For example, define a MATLAB function simple2 ?that calls the recursive function? recursive_fcn ? with a constant input?5 : function out = simple2 %#codegenout = recursive_fcn(5);end The function recursive_fcn ? recursively calls itself with a different input value for each call:function out = recursive_fcn(n)if n > 0    m = n - 1;    out = 0;    out = recursive_fcn(m);    out = out + do_something(n);else    out = 0;endend function out = do_something(n)out = cumsum(1,n);end The generated C code contains an infinite recursion and does not terminate. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.?
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Drive Cycle Source block produces an incompatible file error
Description: The Drive Cycle Source block produces an incompatible file error dialogue message when importing files with non-uniformly sampled time data.
Workaround: Click?OK?to accept the error. You will then see the correct error message regarding the issue with non-uniformly sampled data.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generation might ignore AUTOSAR per-instance properties of mapped signal, state, or data store
Description: AUTOSAR code generation might ignore the per-instance properties of a mapped signal, state, or Data Store Memory block after the corresponding block is resolved to a library. For example, in the generated C code, the ShortName ?value specified for a signal mapped to AUTOSAR ArTypedPerInstanceMemory  might be absent.
Workaround: After a block corresponding to a mapped signal, state, or data store resolves to a library, and before generating code, re-specify the per-instance properties in the AUTOSAR mapping.?Repeat each time the model is re-opened.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Check trigger signal names?flags Simulink functions nested in Stateflow charts
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check trigger signal names ?(Check ID:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0281 ) incorrectly flags the Simulink functions nested in the Stateflow charts.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simscape HDL Workflow Advisor run might run incorrectly when filter input setting used for Simulink to physical signal converter blocks
Description: If the Simscape model contains Simulink-PS Converter blocks, in the Block Parameters dialog box for the blocks, on the Input Handling  tab, the Filtering and derivatives ?parameter must be set to Zero derivatives (piecewise constant)  or Provide Signals  and the Provided signals  parameter must be set to Input only. Using a different setting can cause the converter blocks in the HDL implementation model generated by running the Simscape to HDL Workflow Advisor to not preserve the filtering behavior. This can result in a numeric mismatch between the Simscape model and the implementation model. In R2018b and R2019a these filtering options are not caught by the Simscape to HDL Workflow advisor and the generated model does not have the filtering behavior that is set on the Simulink-PS Converter. In? R2019b_update6, the error in the Simscape to HDL advisor will be: Unrecognized property 'SPSFilterBlocks' for class  ????????????'ssccodegenworkflow. SwitchedLinearWorkflow  In R2020a_update4 onward the error will read:  Only the ''Provide signals'' and ''Input only'' settings on S-SP conveters are supported with the Simscape HDL Workflow Advisor.
Workaround: Add a Discrete Filter block before the Simulink-PS Converter block to capture the filter behavior and change the Block Parameters of the Simulink-PS Converter block to use Provide Signals and Input only.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Multiple CAN FD  channel connections cannot run on the same MathWorks virtual channel
Description: You cannot simultaneously run more than one CAN FD channel connected to the same MathWorks virtual device channel.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 5, R2020b

Summary: Code generation assertion for AUTOSAR model with corrupt signal, state, or data store mapping
Description: AUTOSAR code generation might fail with an assertion if the model has a corrupt signal, state, or data store mapping. While this corruption can happen for multiple reasons, it usually happens when an AUTOSAR model contains a Stateflow chart with atomic subcharts. The build displays an error message containing this text: In getIndividualDSMGroupPropertyFromMapping: Assertion failed: aBlk != nullptr

Summary: Pressure-Compensated Flow Control Valve might incorrectly calculate the Pressure Compensator valve and Variable Orifice areas
Description: The Pressure-Compensated Flow Control Valve incorrectly calculates the Variable Orifice opening fornonzero Initial opening  values and might use an incorrect value for the Pressure Compensator maximum passage area.
Workaround: If the block has a nonzero value for Initial Opening, you can remedy this with the following steps:Set the?InitialOpening?parameter to 0 m.Add a PS Add block and connect its output port to the Pressure-Compensated Flow Control Valveblock port?C.?Connect a PS Constant block to one of the PS Add block input ports. Set the Constant parameter equal to the desired?Initial Opening?value.?Connect the control signal originally connected to port?C?to the other PS Add block input port.Additionally, if the Valve parameterization setting is?By area vs. opening table, set theOrifice maximum area?equal to the lastelement of Tabulated orifice area. Whilethe?Orifice maximum area?is not used in the?By area vs. opening table setting, the parameter is used to set the maximum passage area of the Pressure Compensator valve.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 6, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Discrepancy between results for normal vs. SIL/PIL simulation for export-function Model block with Initialize port
Description: Software-in-the-loop (SIL) or Processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation results differ from normal simulation results for a model when all of the following conditions are met:  The top model or test harness contains a Model block with?Show model initialize port ?selected.  In the same Model block, Simulation mode  is set as Software-in-the-loop (SIL)  or Processor-in-the-loop (PIL).  In the referenced model, the configuration parameter?Code Generation ?>  Optimization?> ?Remove internal data zero initialization ?is selected. The initial value for any internal data in the referenced model is zero.  The difference occurs when the Initialize port is called a second time during simulation. The normal mode simulation reinitializes the internal data of the referenced model to zero, while the SIL/PIL simulation does not.

Description: Reading MDF data from certain byte array channels might crash MATLAB. The issue can occur when the byte arrays have variable length. The generated stack trace contains lines similar to: [  0] 0x000000001bf77894 bin\win64\libmwfoundation_matlabdata_matlab.dll+00293012 foundation::matlabdata::matlab::CustomWorkspace::setVariables+00242212[  1] 0x000000001187cb67 bin\win64\libmwfoundation_matlabdata.dll+00510823 foundation::matlabdata::Array::~Array+00000055[  2] 0x0000000015210c8c bin\win64\libmwfoundation_matlabdata_standalone.dll+00068748 foundation::matlabdata::standalone::detail::ArrayFactoryHelpers::initializeArray+00000124[  3] 0x000000001bfdd29d bin\win64\libmwfoundation_matlabdata_matlab.dll+00709277 foundation::matlabdata::matlab::ServerArrayFactory::releaseMxArray+00153373[  4] 0x0000000021f658e6 bin\win64\libMatlabDataArray.dll+00022758 create_array_with_dims_and_data+00000134[  5] 0x000000000450b1fb toolbox\shared\asam\mdf\mexMDF.mexw64+00242171[  6] 0x0000000004506761 toolbox\shared\asam\mdf\mexMDF.mexw64+00223073[  7] 0x00000000044f41c9 toolbox\shared\asam\mdf\mexMDF.mexw64+00147913[  8] 0x000000000452238e toolbox\shared\asam\mdf\mexMDF.mexw64+00336782 mexFunctionAdapter+00000270

Summary: readtable errors if the SpreadsheetImportOptions property VariableNamesRange is specified as a positive scalar integer
Description: readtable ?errors if the?SpreadsheetImportOptions ?property?VariableNamesRange ?is specified as a positive scalar integer, which is a valid VariableNamesRange  value. >> opts = spreadsheetImportOptions("VariableNamesRange", 1);?>> readtable('patients.xls', opts)Error using matlab.io.ImportOptions/readtable (line 499)Comparison between double and string is not supported.
Workaround: To prevent readtable from throwing an error, specify the VariableNamesRange?property as a character vector that represents the starting cell for the data.>> opts = spreadsheetImportOptions("VariableNamesRange", "A1");?>> readtable('patients.xls', opts);

Summary: MATLAB crashes after deleting a dose from a SimBiology model
Description: SimBiology dose objects maintain references to targeted species as well as parameters in parameterized dose properties. Those references become invalid if you delete the dose object using the delete  function, or if you move a target species from one compartment to another in the SimBiology app. MATLAB crashes if you: Rename a species or a parameter referenced by the deleted dose object. Save a project containing invalid references in the SimBiology app.
Workaround: In the MATLAB command lineUse the method removedose?to safely remove doses from the model. For more information on how to use this method, type:help SimBiology.Model.removedose The dose object returned by removedose can be deleted with the delete function.For example, to delete a dose object?dObj with the name 'doseName' from a SimBiology model?mObj, enter:delete(removedose(mObj,'doseName'));? % instead of: delete(getdose(mObj,'doseName'));?ordelete(removedose(mObj,dObj));? % instead of: delete(dObj);??In the SimBiology appClear the TargetName property of the dose object before deleting a dose, or before moving a species that is targeted by a dose from one compartment to another.To clear the TargetName?property, right-click the dose and select Properties. Delete the TargetName property in the Block Property Editor.
