Summary: Training RNNs with trainNetwork?and datastore input silently ignores "Shuffle" option
Description: When training a recurrent neural network (RNN), such as long short-term memory (LSTM) or gated recurrent unit (GRU) networks, using?trainNetwork ? with an input datastore, the data is not shuffled.?? Under the following conditions, trainNetwork   does not shuffle the input data during training: Network layers contain sequenceInputLayer , lstmLayer , bilstmLayer , or gruLayer  Input training data is a datastore  This behavior occurs even when using training options  that specify the "Shuffle"  option as one of the following values:? "once"  "every-epoch"   The "Shuffle"  option is silently ignored by trainNetwork . The training result is equivalent to setting "Shuffle","never" .?
Workaround: To obtain the "Shuffle","once" behavior, call shuffle on the input datastore before calling trainNetwork. For example:layers = [ ...    sequenceInputLayer(numFeatures)    lstmLayer(numHiddenUnits1,'OutputMode','last')    fullyConnectedLayer(numClasses)    softmaxLayer     classificationLayer];myDatastore = sequenceDatastore(filepath);opts = trainingOptions('sgdm');?myDatastore = shuffle(myDatastore);?net = trainNetwork(myDatastore,?layers, opts);?There is no workaround to obtain the "Shuffle","every-epoch" behavior.?

Summary: Entity Find block deletes impending events associated with the found entity during entity modification without extraction
Description: If the Entity Fin d block finds an entity in the model, and the following two criteria are met: The found entity is being modified in place without extraction The found entity has any impending event on the Event Calendar  at the same simulation time   The impending event is inadvertently deleted from the Event Calendar  and this causes incorrect simulation results.

Summary: The allanvar function returns zeroes for matrix input of large dimensions
Description: When matrices of large dimensions are passed into the allanvar  function, some output columns might contain all zeros instead of the expected data.
Workaround: You can work around this issue by individually passing each column of the input matrix into the allanvar function.

Summary: MATLAB stops responding after updating previously imported requirements in Requirements Editor
Description: When you have apreviously imported requirement set loaded in Requirements Editor,?and ?you use the Update  button?under Requirement Interchange  pane pull-in changes from the original external document,?MATLAB may become unresponsive for a long time after the update is finished. Depending on the number of changes received in the update, the unresponsiveness might continue for over an hour. If MATLAB eventually starts responding, any action that requires a refresh of the Import node property pane might lead to another prolong period of unresponsiveness. Alternatively, if the update is performed using the command-line API, MATLAB stops responding when opening the updated?.slreqx  file in the Requirements Editor, for the same reason.?

Summary: Persistent lateral drift for vehicle models with solid axle suspensions and non-zero initial roll angles
Description: If your vehicle model has a non-zero initial roll angle and a solid axle suspension, the vehicle can have a persistent lateral drift during simulation.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 5, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Memory allocated for a dynamic array in the generated code might be?freed before last use of the array
Description: In the generated code, memory allocated for a dynamic array might be?freed before the last use of the array if all  these conditions are true: Your MATLAB code contains an array for which the code generator allocates memory dynamically on the heap. (Such arrays must be variable-size and unbounded, or variable-size with bound that is greater than a threshold.) In your MATLAB code, the last reference of the dynamic array is used inside a function call. This function call appears in the condition of an if  or while ?statement.  For example, consider this MATLAB function: function y = example(n1,n2) assert(isa(n1, 'double'));assert(n1 <= 100);assert(isa(n2, 'double')); % Create a 1x100 upper bounded struct array that contains an inner unbounded arrays1 = repmat(struct('x', zeros(1,n2)), 1, n1);% Create an unbounded struct array that contains an inner unbounded arrays2 = repmat(struct('x', zeros(1,n2)), 1, n2);for i = 1:numel(s1)    s1(i).x(10) = n1;end a2 = subfcn(s2);if subfcn(s1) == 42    y = 123 + s2(10).x(2);else    y = 456 + a2;end function y = subfcn(s)coder.inline('never');y = 0;for i = 1:numel(s)    y = y + s(i).x(1);end Generate MEX code for this function by running:? codegen example?  The condition "if  subfcn(s1) == 42 " ?contains the last reference to s1 . This last reference ?occurs inside a call to the function subfcn . In the generated code,?s1  contains dynamic matrices that are heap allocated and need to be freed after the last use of s1 . (The reason for the prior call subfcn(s2)  is to make sure that the code generated for the function subfcn ? can handle arbitrary array sizes. The second call subfcn(s1) ?reuses this generated code.)  The generated code looks like: b_s1.data = &s1.data[0];b_s1.size = &s1.size[0];b_s1.allocatedSize = 100;b_s1.numDimensions = 2;b_s1.canFreeData = false;emxFree_struct_T_1x100(&s1); /* The free happens too early because s1 is indirectly used below via b_s1 */st.site = &d_emlrtRSI;if (subfcn(&st, &b_s1) == 42.0) { Calling the generated MEX function example_mex  might result in a MATLAB crash and/or an incorrect answer. For example, example_mex(10,10)?  This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Workaround: Create a new temporary variable that holds the last reference to the dynamic array instead of putting that reference directly in the condition of the if or while statement. For example, in the previous MATLAB function example, change:?if subfcn(s1) == 42?to:t = subfcn(s1);if t == 42
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: When opening Embedded Coder Dictionary from later release, MATLAB might crash
Description: When you open an Embedded Coder Dictionary from a Simulink data dictionary that was saved in a later release, MATLAB might crash.?
Workaround: Before you open the Embedded Coder Dictionary, export the Simulink data dictionary from the later release to R2018b or R2019a.1. Launch MATLAB in the later release.2. Open the data dictionary file.?dictionaryObj = Simulink.data.dictionary.open('dictionaryFile.sldd')3. Export the data dictionary to R2018b or R2019a.?exportToVersion(dictionaryObj, 'myR2018bFolder', 'R2018b')exportToVersion(dictionaryObj, 'myR2019aFolder', 'R2019a')

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Lookup Table block with linear interpolation and output word length less than or equal to table word length causes HDL mismatch
Description: When calculating the interval of two consecutive points in a Lookup Table block for linear interpolation, overflow might occur if the result cannot be represented by the output type set on the block. This can cause a numeric mismatch in the generated HDL code.
Workaround: Increase the output data type to be 1 bit larger in word length than the data type of the table data.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: ReqIF ID values might change between revisions when exporting to ReqIF
Description: When you make changes to a requirement set in Simulink Requirements that has already been exported to a ReqIF file and then export it again, some existing ReqIF ID values might change. For example, in the first export, the ReqIF?replicationID  might have some value "a", and, when changes are made to the requirement set and exported a second time, the  replicationID ?might change to some value "b".  This might happen for requirement sets that are created in or imported to Simulink Requirements.

Summary: Embedded Coder Model Advisor Check errors out for bus signals that are switched by a Switch block
Description: When code is generated for a model that contains a Switch block that switches bus signals defined by a workspace bus object, and the?Embedded Coder Model Advisor Checks are enabled, ?the Check ID:  mathworks. codegen. BlockSpecificQuestionableFxptOperations  (Identify blocks that generate expensive fixed-point and saturation code) errors out with the message "Unrecognized data type name ".
Workaround: Use a Bus Creator block instead of a workspace bus object, or use a Multiport Switch block instead of a Switch block.

Summary: Vector CAN FD channels and NI CAN or CAN FD channels might experience large memory consumption
Description: The following vendor channels might cause larger than expected memory consumption in MATLAB if messages are left unreceived in the channel, resulting in a possible slowdown in system performance: Vector CAN FD NI CAN FD NI CAN

Summary: Blockset Designer project takes a long time to load
Description: Blockset Designer project takes a long time to load during blockset project loading when there is a large number of blocks present in the blockset. Load time can be several minutes with 50 blocks.

Summary: SimBiology Model Analyzer might not show legend information in the Property Editor when plotting simulation and external data together
Description: If you perform a scan using doses derived from an imported data set with a single group and plot this simulation data together with an imported data set, the Property Editor does not display the correct information, and you can no longer modify the plot.

Summary: Induction machine blocks display incorrect unsaturated characteristic
Description: Forthe Induction Machine Wound Rotor and Induction Machine Squirrel Cageblocks,?if you set the Parameterization unit  parameter to?SI and you select the?Electrical > Plot Open-Circuit Saturation ?option on the blocks context menu, the displayed unsaturated characteristic is incorrect.

Summary: An error occurs in the Simulink Test Manager while aggregating coverage data for a Subsystem Harness if the subsystem contains a call to an external MATLAB file
Description: The following error occurs during subsystem coverage aggregation if the subsystem contains a call to an external MATLAB file:?  >> Error using stm.internal.Coverage.getMetrics  Input argument #1 is an invalid cvdata object. CVDATA objects  become invalid when their associated models are closed or  modified  Error in stm.internal.Coverage.addAndReturnFile>aggregateSubsystem  Error in stm.internal.Coverage.addAndReturnFile

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect initial value for block output inside reusable subsystem
Description: The initial value for block output in a reusable subsystem may be incorrectly computed under all these conditions:?The model configuration parameter?Buffer for reusable subsystems ?is selected. The reusable subsystem's outport is multi-rate (for example, a Mux block combines a Inf  sample time signal and a discrete sample time signal feeds into the subsystem's outport).

Summary: Passing ReadRowNames to detectImportOptions for spreadsheet files might cause an error
Description: When detecting import options for spreadsheet files, detectImportOptions  errors if both of the following conditions exist: The name-value pair ReadRowNames  is passed in as true . The spreadsheet file has not more than 2 non-empty columns.  For example: >> opts = detectImportOptions(filename,"ReadRowNames",true,"Range","A:B");Error using detectImportOptions (line 140)Invalid 'VariableNamesRange'. The column size must match the number of variables.
Workaround: Instead of passing ReadRowNames to detectImportOptions, you can workaround this issue by setting the RowNamesRange and SelectedVariableNames properties on the SpreadsheetImportOptions object returned by detectImportOptions:>> opts = detectImportOptions(filename, "Range", "A:B");>> opts.SelectedVariableNames = opts.SelectedVariableNames(2:end)>> opts.RowNamesRange = "A2";

Summary: Using Start simulation from steady state increases compilation time?for some models
Description: When the?Start simulation from steady state  check box in the Solver Configuration block is selected, models that include nested intermediate expressions compile slowly. These nested?intermediate expressions?can be caused by?the use of intermediates  sections or nested let  statements in the block source, or by variable elimination using linear equations.

Summary: Initial simulation does not use parameters derived from updated block parameters
Description: If you change a block parameter value that the block uses to derive additional parameters, the model does not use the derived parameters during the initial simulation. During the second simulation, however, the model does use the derived parameters. Before the initial simulation, if you invoke an update diagram, the model will use parameters derived from updated block parameters.
Workaround: If you change a block parameter value, invoke a diagram update. To update the diagram, from the Modeling tab, click Update Model or press Ctrl+D.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code might be generated for a model containing a MATLAB Function block with similar expressions over struct type variables
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains a MATLAB Function block The MATLAB Function block uses the same expression more than once with different operands. One or more of the operands to the expression has a?struct  data type.  In the attached example m2244678.slx, the MATLAB Function block contains two uses of the sqrt  expression with different input variables of struct  type: function var = bar(var)  ? coder.inline('never');  ? ?P11 = var;  ? ?var(1).f1(1,1) = (var(1).f2(1:1:1,1:1:1)) - (P11(1).f1(1,1));  ? ?var(1).f2(1,1) = floor(mod(sqrt(abs(var(1).f1(1:1:1,1:1:1))),sqrt(abs(P11(1).f1))));  end?   The code generator generates incorrect code containing invalid reuse of the return value of sqrt? in the function?m2244678_bar  in?m2244678.c : static void m2244678_bar(IA1_busObject *var)  {  ? ?real32_T tmp;  ? ?var->f1 = var->f2 - var->f1;  ? ?tmp = (real32_T)sqrt((real32_T)fabs(var->f1));  ? ?var->f2 = (real32_T)floor(m2244678_mod(tmp, tmp));  }   The expected correct code without the invalid reuse is: static void m2244678_bar(IA1_busObject *var)  {  ? real32_T P11_f1;  ? P11_f1 = var->f1;  ? var->f1 = var->f2 - var->f1;  ? var->f2 = (real32_T)floor(m2244678_mod((real32_T)sqrt((real32_T)fabs(var->f1)),(real32_T)sqrt((real32_T)fabs(P11_f1))));  } ?
Fixed In: R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 3, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Discrete-Time Integrator block logs incorrect minimum and maximum values for fixed-point data types with slope and bias scaling
Description: The Discrete-Time Integrator block logs incorrect minimum and maximum values during simulation when its output data type is fixed-point with slope and bias scaling.  To observe this issue, perform?Range Analysis ?with the Fixed-Point Designer.  Alternatively, search for "Range Analysis using Fixed Point Tool" in the documentation to find instructions on how to do this.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: diric?returns incorrect output for some input values equal or close to multiples of 2*pi.?
Description: diric  returns incorrect output for some input values equal or close to multiples of 2*pi . The inaccuracy occurs for a wider range of input values than in previous versions due to a change of the tolerance value that prevents division by zero.?

Summary: Analysis may terminate with an?Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier back end error message
Description: Analysis might terminate without producing the results. When you run the analysis with a user interface, it displays the following error message: Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier back end . When you run the analysis in the?Test Generation ?mode, the error message displayed at the MATLAB Command Window is: Test generation produced errors?  Stack trace observed from R2018a: src/tempo/instance.c:807 tempo_error=tempo_err_usr    src/tempo/tempo_saturate.c:260 tempo_error=tempo_err_usr    src/data_graph/rat_prop_bt.c:702 err=data_graph_err_internal    src/data_graph/graph_rat_prop-new.c:110 err=data_graph_err_internal   When this issue is hit, the stack trace will contain rat_prop_bt.c  with a number between 690 and 720

Summary: sbiofit with scattersearch uses incorrect bounds for the response-specific combined error model
Description: sbiofit  uses incorrect bounds for the error model parameters if you specify 'scattersearch' ? as the estimation function and?'combined'  as response-specific error models. This can result in an error exception about invalid upper bounds for the estimation.?

Summary: Error when reading text files with 'Range' specified beyond 'XFD1048576'
Description: When using the readtable , readmatrix , readcell , readtimetable  or readvars  functions to read a text file by specifying a value for 'Range'  beyond 'XFD1048576' , the following error is encountered: >> m = readmatrix('filename.txt',"Range", "1:1048577");Error using readmatrix (line 149)Unable to determine range. Range must be a named range in the sheet or a single cell within 'XFD1048576'.>> m = readtable('filename.txt',"Range", "A:XFE");Error using readtable (line 198)Unable to determine range. Range must be a named range in the sheet or a single cell within 'XFD1048576'.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Shared utility code folder error for modified Vehicle Dynamics Blockset and Powertrain Blockset example
Description: Simulating or updating the diagram of a Powertrain Blockset or Vehicle Dynamics Blockset example model might produce an unexpected error if you have edited a model in the model hierarchy. The error might look like: ? Error while bringing model references of model SiCiPtReferenceApplication up to date: Error????building model 'SiEngineController'.  The current model configuration differs from the model????configuration used to generate the shared utility code folder, ????'<myPath>\ slprj\ sim\ _sharedutils'.
Workaround: Delete the slprj folder and .slxc files, then try again.?
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 5, R2020b

Summary: Generated model after HDL code generation might fail to compile for Constant blocks with complex values
Description: If the Simulink model contains Constant blocks that have complex values, then the generated model after HDL code generation might fail to compile.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Missing logged signals from Stateflow charts when Simulink.fileGenControl sets the CacheFolder to a directory other than pwd
Description: Signals inside Stateflow charts that are inside a model block (set to Accelerator mode simulation) can be logged using the Data Import/Export  pane. When Simulink.fileGenControl  is set to generate code and cache files in a directory other than pwd  using the following command:? Simulink.fileGenControl('set','CacheFolder','C:\otherDirectory')?  Such signals may not appear in the final logged data.?
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Constant sample time output signal in referenced model might lead to incorrect code
Description: Incorrect code might be generated if all these conditions are true:Signals, states and internal data is default mapped to a built-in storage class. Model contains Model reference block. Referenced model has both constant and non-constant sample time output signals.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Precision error in double-typed initial values in RAM blocks inferred during HDL code generation from persistent variables in MATLAB function block
Description: When the initial values are in double precision, more specifically, for values such as pi, 1/3, 0.1, only ?15 digits are preserved by default during HDL code generation. The initial value is therefore not bit-accurate and has lower precision than the original input value, which results in a numeric mismatch.

Summary: Cannot load app with grid that has row height or column width of zero
Description: If you create an app with a grid layout and set any row or column to have a size of zero, then you will not be able to load the app.

Summary: Image Labeler session returns an error after importing? a modified  groundTruth object.
Description: The Image Labeler  app returns an error after importing? a groundTruth  object which was previously modified. After using the Image Labeler  app to remove a labeled image, the app exports the  groundTruth  with LabelData  as structs rather than double arrays.? Importing this groundTruth  object into an?Image Labeler  in a later session returns the error: Error using vision.internal.imageLabeler.tool.Session/addLabelData (line 308)  Unrecognized table variable name 'Hierarchy'.  Warning: Error occurred while evaluating listener callback.?

Summary: Single scan operations using multiple counter input channels return data only for the final channel
Description: Single scan operations containing multiple counter input channels return incorrect data (0 ) for all counters except for the channel with the highest index. For example, the following code with two EdgeCount  measurement channels, returns data from only only the higher: s = daq.createSession("ni");ch0 = addAnalogInputChannel(s, "Dev1", "ai0", "Voltage");ch2 = addCounterInputChannel(s, "Dev1", "ctr2", "EdgeCount");ch3 = addCounterInputChannel(s, "Dev1", "ctr3", "EdgeCount");inputSingleScan(s)ans =   -0.1495         0  125.0000 In this case, the EdgeCount  value for the channel ctr2  returns a false value of 0 .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Unexpected result or error when using a tabulated data parameterization of the Pressure Relief Valve
Description: In the Pressure Relief Valve block, when the Opening area parameterization  is set to Tabulated data - Area vs. pressure , and the pressure drop between port A  and port B  exceeds the last element of the?Pressure differential vector , one of the following may occur: The orifice area may incorrectly change to an unexpected value. The block may erroneously generate the following message: "Nonlinear solver: failed to converge, ?residual norm too large."
Workaround: To avoid these errors, either:Replace the Pressure Relief Valve with a Pressure Compensator block.Set the?Opening area parameterization?to?Tabulated data - Area vs. pressure.Connect the pressure sensor port X to port B. Connect the sensor port Y to port A.Reverse the order of the elements in the?Opening area vector. For example, if the existing vector is [0.25, 0.8, 1.3], adjust it to [1.3, 0.8, 0.25]. This can be achieved by setting it to?fliplr([0.25, 0.8, 1.3]).Reverse?the order of the elements in the Pressure differential vector and flip the element signs. For example, if the existing vector is [-10, 5, 13], adjust it to [-13, -5, 10]. This can be achieved by setting it to?-fliplr([-10, 5, 13]).All other parameters and port connections should match the original Pressure Relief Valve block settings.?In the Pressure Relief Valve block, use a linear parameterization by setting the?Opening area parameterization?to Linear area-pressure relationship.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 3, R2020b

Summary: Simscape model compilation fails when using legacy random number generator
Description: When you are using a legacy random number generator (for which RandStream.getGlobalStream.Type  returns 'legacy' ), running or compiling a Simscape model results in the following error:   Conversion to double from cell is not possible.
Workaround: Upgrade your code to use a nonlegacy random number generator, as described in https://www.mathworks.com/help/matlab/math/updating-your-random-number-generator-syntax.html, then recompile the Simscape model.

Summary: Setting gain on graphicEQ object after closing parameterTuner returns an error
Description: The following steps return an error:? Create a graphicEQ  object: h = graphicEQ ; Open parameterTuner  on the object:? parameterTuner(h)  Close the parameterTuner  UI. Set graphicEQ  gain property programmatically: h.Gains(1) = 1 ;  The error message looks similar to:     Invalid or deleted object.    Error in graphicEQ/createParameterTunerWidgets (line 763)                        f.Position(3) = 80 * 11;    Error in graphicEQ/updatePlot (line 717)                        createParameterTunerWidgets(obj);    Error in graphicEQ/set.pGains (line 361)                updatePlot(obj);    Error in graphicEQ/set.Gains (line 353)                obj.pGains = gainVec;
Workaround: To avoid this issue, set graphicEQ gains either programmatically or with the parameterTuner user interface. Avoid using both with the same object.To continue working after encountering the issue, reopen the parameterTuner UI by calling parameterTuner on your graphicEQ object and keep it open. While the parameterTuner UI is open, you can set gain values programmatically or using the UI.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Index outputs of union, setdiff, and setxor might refer to the last instances of repeated elements instead of the first instances
Description: The generated code for setxor  and union  might return the indices corresponding to the last occurrence of repeated elements in its second and third outputs rather than the first occurrences. For example, with?[y,ia,ib] = x_union([2,2],1) ,  we should have?ia = 1 , but code generation returns?ia = 2 .?  Similarly, the generated code for setdiff ?might return the last occurrence of repeated elements in its second output rather than the first occurrence. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 3, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Streaming and sharing of resources that are clock-rate pipelined can cause HDL mismatch
Description: If streaming and sharing are both requested when Clock Rate Pipelining  is enabled, the generated HDL code might have a numeric mismatch when compared to the original model. The mismatch occurs because the scheduling logic inside the clock-rate pipelined region does not correctly handle resources participating in multiple optimizations that require scheduling such as streaming and sharing. This can happen if streaming is requested on a subsystem which contains another subsystem requesting sharing.
Workaround: Remove the sharing request from the lower subsystem, or avoid using Clock Rate Pipelining when requesting the streaming and sharing optimizations on the same resources.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b

Summary: Model Advisor check Model object names error on clicking "What's this?"
Description: The Model Advisor check Model object names  (ID: mathworks.hism.hisl_0032 ) errors out on clicking "What's this?" with the following error: Error using helpUtils.csh.helpviewMLFactory/checkForMapFile (line 38)Specified topic id (mathworks.do178b.hisl_0032) does not exist in map file Error in helpview (line 213)??? help_path = factory.checkForMapFile(mapfilename, topic_id);Error in ModelAdvisor.Node.opencsh

Summary: The Pressure Compensated 3-Way Flow Control Valve may default to an incorrect value for the maximum orifice area.
Description: In the Pressure-Compensated 3-Way Flow Control Valve block, when?Orifice parameterization ?is set to By orifice area vs. orifice opening table , and the sum of the signal at port C  and the Orifice initial opening  ?exceeds a certain threshold, the orifice area may equal a value that is unrelated to the values in?Tabulated orifice area .   In R2018b and earlier, this threshold is the value of the?Orifice maximum opening , which is visible when the?Orifice parameterization  is By maximum opening and area .?In R2019a and later, this threshold is 0.005 m, the default value of?Orifice maximum opening .
Workaround: To avoid these errors, either:Replace the Pressure-Compensated 3-Way Flow Control Valve with a Variable Orifice block and Pressure Relief Valve block.Connect the Variable Orifice S port to the node that was previously connected to the original block C port.Connect the Variable Orifice port A and Pressure Relief Valve A to the node that was previously connected to the original block A port.Connect the Variable Orifice port B to the node that was previously connected to the original block B port.Connected the Pressure Relief Valve B to the node that was previously connected to the original block R port.Set the Variable Orifice?Model parameterization?to?By area vs. opening table.Leave the Variable Orifice Orifice orientation?as?Opens in positive direction.Set all other Variable Orifice parameters to match the parameters on the Pressure-Compensated 3-Way Flow Control Valve?block Variable orifice tab.Leave the Pressure Relief Valve Opening area parameterization??as Linear area-pressure relationship.?Set all other?Pressure Relief Valve parameters to match the parameters on the Pressure-Compensated 3-Way Flow Control Valve?block Pressure compensator tab.?In the Pressure-Compensated 3-Way Flow Control Valve block, use a linear parameterization by setting the?Orifice parameterization?to?By maximum opening and area.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 6, R2020b

Summary: Simulating models with variant blocks might crash or generate different (but equivalent) preprocessor conditions
Description: When variant blocks are used in a Simulink model, one of the following cases might occur: Model might crash during simulation or code generation on certain platforms such as Mac. Code generation of the model might generate different (but equivalent) preprocessor conditions across multiple runs and on different platforms.  For example, code generation might generate different (but equivalent) conditions on Linux and Windows platforms as shown below: Linux:  '(NN == 1 && O == 1) || (NN == 1 && P == 1) || (NN == 2 && O == 1) || (NN == 2 && PP == 1)'     Windows:  '(NN == 1 && O == 1 && P ~= 1) || (NN == 1 && P == 1) || (NN == 2 && O == 1 && PP ~= 1) || (NN == 2 && PP == 1)'

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch in value of parameter Scalarize vector ports?between protected model and model in which it is used generates incorrect HDL code
Description: If the value of the Configuration parameter Scalarize vector ports  does not match between the model underneath a protected subsystem and the model in which the protected subsystem is being used, then HDL Coder can generate incorrect code. If the design contains a protected subsystem for HDL code generation, then all Configuration Parameters must match between the top model and the protected model as mentioned in this HDL Coder documentation page:?Protected Model Restrictions for HDL Code Generation .
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation for PC Worx 6.0?target when using models with?Shift Arithmetic?blocks
Description: Behaviour of?Shift Arithmetic ?instructions on target?PC Worx 6.0? is different from that in Simulink. This could lead to incorrect code generation for Out-Of-Range bit shift operations.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Unable to complete an HTTP request from MATLAB in an environment with Kerberos proxy
Description: The HTTP Interface will cause an authentication failure when authenticating with a Kerberos proxy.? You will not be able to successfully complete the HTTP request.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, disable the Kerberos proxy.

Summary: Code generation fails when indexing into a table or a timetable using logical row indices that have a variable size
Description: Calling codegen  on a function that indexes into a table or timetable and that specifies the table rows using variable-size logical arrays might return this error: ??? Loop unrolling failed because the number of iterations is unknown. The error occurs if the table or timetable contains at least one variable that is a cell array.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation for a function-call, triggered, or enabled and triggered subsystem that is configured for reusable function packaging
Description: When a model contains a function-call, triggered, or enabled and triggered subsystem with the parameter?Function Packaging? set to?Reusable function, ?generated code might be incorrectly placed in the? <model>_step0 ?function instead of another?<model>_step ?function. This incorrect code generation occurs when the trigger port for the subsystem connects to a block that has a sample time different from the fundamental sample time of the model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: HDL coder might generate incorrect code for 1-bit HDL Counter with step value of -1
Description: The generated code from the HDL Counter might be incorrect when the?step value  is -1  and the difference between the?Word length  and Fraction length  is 1 .
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 7, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Calling discretize with repeated bin edges and the 'IncludedEdge' option set to 'right' might produce incorrect answer
Description: In the generated code, calling  discretize  might produce incorrect answer if all  these conditions are true:?  The bin edges provided to discretize   include repeated values, implying that at least one bin has right and left edges that are the same.  The 'IncludedEdge'  option is set to 'right'  and the bin with repeated edges is not the first (i.e. left-most) bin. This indicates that the bin with repeated edges must be empty because the conditions for an element to be placed in it contradict each other.   MATLAB correctly leaves such a bin empty, but the generated code might place elements in it.? Here is an example that illustrates this issue: discretize([0 1 2],[0 0 1 1 2],'IncludedEdge','right')?  MATLAB execution produces this result:?   [1? ? ?2? ? ?4]?  Generated code produces this result:? [1? ? ?3? ? ?4]?  Note that if the call to discretize ?does not use the ?'IncludedEdge' ?option or if the?'IncludedEdge' ?option is set to?'left' , the generated code ?is not affected by this issue.? This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Constant global variable passed to external C/C++ function by using coder.ceval might not be initialized correctly in the generated code
Description: This issue affects code generation if all of these conditions are true: Your MATLAB code passes a global variable to an external C/C++ function by using coder.ceval  The global variable is passed by reference by using?coder.rref  During code generation, you initialize the global variable to a constant value by using coder.Constant?    In such cases, the global variable might not be initialized correctly in the generated code.?
Workaround: In your MATLAB code, assign the global variable to a temporary local variable. Pass the temporary variable to the external C/C++ function by using coder.ceval.For example, suppose that your MATLAB code looks like this:function usingConstantGlobalglobal G;coder.ceval("DoSomething",coder.rref(G));end To work around this issue, change the code to: function usingConstantGlobalglobal G;tmp = G;coder.ceval("DoSomething",coder.rref(tmp));end
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 2, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Potentially incorrect VHDL test bench code generated for multirate design with a vector port named phase?at DUT interface and ScalarizePorts parameter set to on
Description: The generated VHDL test bench code from HDL coder might fail to compile and generates an error message similar to: # ** Error: s_tb.vhd(160): No feasible entries for infix operator "=".? # ** Error: s_tb.vhd(160): Type error resolving infix expression "=" as type std.STANDARD.BOOLEAN.?  # ** Error: s_tb.vhd(474): VHDL Compiler exiting?  This error occurs when the following conditions are met:  HDL test bench is generated with VHDL  as the TargetLanguage  The design has multiple rates with a vector port named phase  at DUT interface ScalarizePorts  property is set to on.
Workaround: Either set ScalarizePorts to off or use a different name other than phase for the vector port.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 3, R2020b

Summary: Dragging signal around unconnected enable port might produce an error
Description: When working with enabled subsystems, dragging a signal around an unconnected enable port might cause this error: Unhandled exception thrown during 'commitTransactionsAndHandlePostDo' of command 'Create Segment'.

Summary: interpolateHRTF might compute wrong output when using 'VBAP' algorithm
Description: Certain combinations of HRTF databases and desired source positions might cause a numerical warning that is indicative of a wrong output. For example, using the ARI HRTF database: ARIDataset = load('ReferenceARI.mat');hrtfData = permute(ARIDataset.hM,[2,3,1]);sourcePosition = ARIDataset.meta.pos(:,[1,2]);interpolateHRTF(hrtfData,sourcePosition, [99.5 -2],'Algorithm','VBAP');Warning: Matrix is close to singular or badly scaled. Results may be inaccurate. RCOND =  4.151500e-17. > In audio.internal/HRTFInterpolation/performVBAPInterpolation (line 368)  In audio.internal/HRTFInterpolation/performInterpolation (line 79)  In interpolateHRTF (line 84)
Workaround: Use the default 'bilinear' method, or revert to the?'bilinear'?method when the?'VBAP'?method produces a numerical warning (ID 'MATLAB:nearlySingularMatrix').

Summary: The instfreq function returns a silent wrong answer when called with complex-valued input
Description: Between R2018a and R2020a, the instfreq  function returned a silent wrong answer when called to apply the 'tfmoment'  method to complex-valued input. As of R2020a, the function supports complex-valued inputs correctly. instfreq  versions fixed with release updates prior to R2020a throw an error if the input is complex-valued and the 'tfmoment'  method is used.

Summary: Modifications to HDL Block Properties of blocks inside Subsystem Reference are not saved on to the model
Description: Changes made to any of the HDL Block Properties of blocks inside a Subsystem Reference do not dirty the model. Hence, saving these changes by clicking Apply  in the HDL Block Properties dialog box do not apply the properties onto the model.
Workaround: After changing HDL block properties make some trivial changes to the model structure and force dirty flag on the model. For example moving the position of the block in question should trigger Dirty flag. Click save model to force HDL block property changes registered to the disk.

Summary: Using System Composer model in File Exchange submission causes MATLAB to crash
Description: Opening and working with the 'Pilot_Inputs.slx' model from File Exchange for the?DO178_case_study ?causes MATLAB to crash.

Summary: blfread does not recognize CAN FD Rx messages
Description: When reading CAN FD messages from a BLF-file using blfread , messages denoted as "Rx" direction are not imported as expected. This issue also occurs when parsing metadata with blfinfo . Messages with "Tx" direction are properly imported. Messages with "TxRq" are ignored by design.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 2, R2020b

Summary: Requirements report displays unrelated text with requirement description
Description: When you generate a Requirements Report for a Simulink model with the "Include details from linked documents"?option enabled?in Report Settings,?the generated page will?display HTML style noise like this: "p, li { white-space: pre-wrap; }" for every reported link destination in a Simulink Requirements Set (links that point to entries?in?.slreqx files).
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 1, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Signal object InitialValue ignored on root inputs of referenced models when storage class is 'Model default'
Description: If a signal object with InitialValue  is resolved for a root input and the model does not have external input data specified, the signal object's InitialValue  will be used for the root input value throughout simulation. However, if the signal object is resolved for a root input of a referenced model and its storage class is 'Model default ', the signal object is not propagated from the referenced model up to the calling model and the InitialValue  is ignored.
Workaround: Use any one of these workarounds:Specify external input data for the top model so that root input values are explicitly specified for all simulation time steps.Specify a storage class other than 'Model default'.

Summary: Incorrect carrier damping loss calculation in Limited Slip Differential and Open Differential blocks
Description: The carrier damping loss calculation, set using the parameter Carrier damping, bd , is incorrect by a factor of one quarter of the carrier to driveshaft ratio, Ndiff/4. The affected blocks are Limited Slip Differential and Open Differential.  For typically small values of Carrier damping, bd ?or in applications with a?Carrier to drive shaft ratio, Ndiff? of approximately 4, there might not be any noticeable effect.
Workaround: Apply a quarter factor of the Carrier to drive shaft ratio, Ndiff to the nominal value used for the parameter Carrier damping, bd.For example, if Carrier damping, bd is nominally 1e-3 and the Carrier to drive shaft ratio, Ndiff is 3.7, then the corrected entry for the parameter Carrier damping, bd is 1e-3*3.7/4.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 1, R2020b

Summary: Simulation mode set to Normal mode, once the analysis is finished
Description: If simulation mode of a model is set to a value other than Normal   mode and Simulink Design Verifier analysis is performed on that model, then simulation mode changes to Normal   once the analysis finishes.

Summary: Fast Restart mode gets disabled after Simulink Design Verifier analysis
Description: If Fast Restart ?is enabled for a model and Simulink Design Verifier analysis is performed on that model, then Fast Restart  gets disabled once the analysis has finished.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB Function block using MATLAB Datapath architecture might result in mismatch with nested user function calls
Description: For repeated function calls to the same function that are nested, such as?subfunc(a, ?b, ?subfunc(c, ?d, ?e)),? the validation model might show a mismatch or code generation might error out unexpectedly. This issue can occur when floating-point signals are present at the MATLAB Function block interface, or when fixed-point signals are present at the block interface with MATLAB Datapath  specified as the HDL architecture.
Workaround: For the nested function call, store the result in an intermediate variable and use that as an input to the outer function call instead.

Summary: Performance regression during code generation for deep learning object detectors with a large number of classes
Description: Code generation for object detectors such as yolov2ObjectDetector  or ssdObjectDetector  with a large number of classes (100 or more) could take several minutes or hours.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when simulating models with non-complementary motion actuation and at least one implicit 6-DOF tree joint
Description: If a Simscape Multibody model has non-complementary motion actuation and at least one implicit 6-DOF tree joint, MATLAB might crash during a normal simulation. The crash occurs when an insufficient computed torque error occurs, and the joint with the largest magnitude error is an implicit 6-DOF joint.  When generating code for such a model (rapid accelerator, etc), MATLAB will always crash.
Workaround: Simscape Statistics Viewer displays the number of implicit 6-DOF joints in a model; try to eliminate all implicit 6-DOF tree joints by adding explicit joint blocks, such as the 6-DOF Joint block, from the Simscape Multibody library.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 2, R2020b

Summary: Running steps in Test Sequence are not highlighted in the animation during simulation
Description: Some steps deep in the step hierarchy of a Test Sequence block are not highlighted correctly in the animation during simulation, even though the steps are actually running.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The Cartridge Valve Insert block may implement an incorrect value for the poppet stroke.
Description: In the Cartridge Valve Insert block, when Orifice specification  is set to either?Area vs. opening table  or Pressure-flow characteristic , the poppet stroke remains at its default value of 0.005 m, irrespective of the?Tabulated orifice opening   parameter value.
Workaround: To avoid this error, replace the Cartridge Valve Insert block with a Hydraulic Cartridge Valve Actuator block and Variable Orifice block as shown on the block?Documentation page:Connect the Hydraulic Cartridge Valve Actuator port A to the Variable Orifice port A.Connect the Hydraulic Cartridge Valve Actuator port B to the Variable Orifice port B.In the Cartridge Valve Actuator block,?Set the?Poppet stroke equal to the last element of the Tabulated orifice opening, minus the first element of the?Tabulated orifice opening, from the Cartridge Valve Insert block.Set the?Poppet-seat Initial gap?equal to the Cartridge Valve Insert Initial opening setting.Ensure that the Actuator Orientation?remains at its default setting of Acts in positive direction.All other parameters should match the parameter values in the Cartridge Valve Insert block.In the Variable Orifice block,?Ensure that the Orifice?Orientation?remains at its default setting of Opens?in positive direction.Ensure that the Initial opening?parameter remains at its default value of 0 m.All other parameters should match the parameter values in the Cartridge Valve Insert block. The Cartridge Valve Insert block parameters correspond to the following Variable Orifice block parameters:Orifice specification to?Model parameterizationTabulated orifice opening to?Orifice opening vectorTabulated orifice area to Orifice area vectorTabulated pressure differential to?Pressure differential vectorTabulated flow rate to?Volumetric flow rate table
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 6, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Improper sample time propagation to nonvirtual blocks outside of Initialize, Reset and Terminate subsystems
Description: Sample time used in Initialize, Reset and Terminate subsystems may propagate to nonvirtual blocks outside the subsystem. This may cause incorrect generated code or a crash during code generation.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Copy block in front of the nonvirtual block to stop the sample time propagation.

Summary: Simulating a model hierarchy with model arguments that span multiple levels might produce incorrect results
Description: Simulation results for a model hierarchy will be incorrect when all of these conditions apply: A model argument is promoted up through multiple levels of the model hierarchy. The referenced model that defines the model argument in the model workspace is a function-call model or has a virtual bus that crosses the model boundary. A Model block that receives the model argument from a lower level specifies a value for it.  Simulation will use a model argument value that is specified at a lower level of the model hierarchy instead of the value specified at the highest level Model block that receives the model argument.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 3, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink might not rebuild rapid accelerator target when?accelerator target is up to date
Description: Simulink might not rebuild the rapid accelerator target for the top model of a model hierarchy when all of the following conditions apply: You have a model hierarchy with at least one referenced model.  You make a change to a model in the hierarchy that requires a rebuild of the model reference simulation target during simulation in accelerator mode. You simulate the top model of the hierarchy in accelerator mode. You simulate the top model of the hierarchy in rapid accelerator mode.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 2, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Unit Conversion block returns incorrect generated code for conversions with offsets and fixed-point data types with bias
Description: The Unit Conversion block might return incorrect answers for accelerator mode, rapid accelerator mode simulation, or code generation when the following conditions are true:The Output data type  parameter is set to Inherit: Inherit via back propagation , The input or output data type is a fixed-point data type with a bias, The unit conversion has an offset, for example, ˇăC  to ˇăF  or ˇăC  to K .
Workaround: Set the Output data type parameter of the Unit Conversion block to Inherit: Inherit via internal rule and insert a Data Type Conversion block after the Unit Conversion block.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 1, R2020b

Summary: trajectoryOptimalFrenet throws error if no feasible trajectory is found
Description: If you use the trajectoryOptimalFrenet ?function for local trajectory generation and no feasible trajectory is found, the trajectory generator?throws an error.

Summary: Check for root Inports with missing properties incorrectly flags inherited sample time
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check for root Inports with missing properties  (Check ID:mathworks.hism.hisl_0024) incorrectly flags?inherited sample time? when? the only entity that makes the? model an export-function model is Simulink Function block.

Summary: Ignored values for model diagnostic parameters?NonCurrentReleaseOperatingPointMsg and?OperatingPointInterfaceChecksumMismatchMsg
Description: Setting the values for the model diagnostic parameters NonCurrentReleaseOperatingPointMsg  and?OperatingPointInterfaceChecksumMismatchMsg ?has no effect on the behavior controlled by these parameters.
Workaround: Use the alternative command-line names for these parameters to change the model settings:For NonCurrentReleaseOperatingPointMsg?use SimStateOlderReleaseMsg.For OperatingPointInterfaceChecksumMismatchMsg use SimStateInterfaceChecksumMismatchMsg.

Summary: Simulink Code Inspector fails to inspect compatible shared utilities
Description: Compatible shared utilities pass compatibility checks, but Code Inspectorfails to inspect those successfully.

Summary: OptimizationExpressions with multiple power or mpower operators can return incorrect results
Description: When creating an OptimizationExpression  in the Problem-Based workflow, using multiple successive power  or mpower  operators can return an incorrect result. For example: x = optimvar('x');  p = x^2;  expr = p + p^2   yields: expr =?  ? Nonlinear OptimizationExpression  ? ? (x.^4 + x.^4)   The correct expression is (x.^2 + x.^4) .
Workaround: Avoid writing successive power or mpower operations on the same expression.For the example above, instead write:expr = p + x^4;orp = x^2;q = x^4;expr = p + q;

Summary: NumericEditField and Spinner?objects do not update their display properly when Value is set programmatically in the ValueChangedFcn
Description: When the?Value  property of the Spinner  or NumericEditField ?object is set programmatically within the ValueChangedFcn  of the component, the component view might not update its display to reflect this new value. When you click into the field, you see the correct value. This behavior is most likely to happen only the first time the value is changed.
Workaround: Consider using other component properties such as Limits and ValueDisplayFormat to avoid programmatically changing the Value in response to an end user typing a number into the NumericEditField or Spinner object.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Customized step function prototype?with custom storage class on Root-level Outport might generate incorrect code
Description: Incorrect code might be generated when all the following conditions are met:Model has customized step function prototype. Step function is configured to have return argument. Root-level Outport corresponding to this return argument has custom storage class.  The generated code might have uninitialized return value in the step function.

Summary: Windows user names with non-English characters can cause issues when opening some MATLAB features
Description: MATLAB installed on a Windows system with a user name containing non-English characters might run into issues and be unable to open the Add-Ons Explorer, Add-Ons Manager, App Designer, and others.MATLAB stores preferences in the user's home folder on Windows, for example, C:\Users\username \AppData\Roaming\MathWorks\MATLAB\R2020a . When the username  is composed of non-English characters, MATLAB runs into issues while creating and modifying files that are needed during startup from the folder where preferences are stored.
Workaround: To avoid these issues:When installing MATLAB, do not use a username that contains non-English characters.?Set the environment variable MATLAB_PREFDIR to a preferences folder that does not have any non-English characters in the path.

Summary: Subsystem Reference block's port properties are lost on reloading the model or if the contents of the Subysystem Reference block are refreshed
Description: Port properties (such as tag, description, signal info etc.) specified for a Subsystem Reference block are lost if you either reload the model or refresh the Subsystem Reference block's contents. A refresh happens if you modify and save the contents of the block. These properties are also lost for a Subsystem block when it is converted to a Subsystem Reference block.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Tunable parameters in non-inlined S-function might lead to incorrect code
Description: Incorrect code might be generated if all these conditions are true:?Model uses a non-inlined S-function with parameters. Model configuration parameter Default parameter behavior  is set to Tunable . Model parameters are default mapped to a structured storage class.

Summary: Subsystem code reuse regression in R2019a update 5 and R2019b for subsystems containing different units
Description: If two subsystems have these characteristics: They are identical with respect to their interface and contents. The only difference is the 'Unit' property of the signals feeding the subsystems. The subsystems do not contain any Unit Conversion blocks along the signal path from the input signal in (2).  The functions for the two subsystems might not be not reused on the generated code.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 1, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect initial value for block output inside reusable subsystem
Description: The initial value for block output in a reusable subsystem may be incorrectly computed under all these conditions:?The model configuration parameter?Buffer for reusable subsystems  is selected. The reusable subsystem's inport is multi-rate (For example, a Mux block combines a Inf sample time signal and a discrete sample time signal feeds into the subsystem's inport).

Summary: Incorrect results when using sbiofit with nlinfit and proportional?or combined error model
Description: sbiofit  returns incorrect results when?using nlinfit ?as the estimation function and proportional  or combined  as the error model.?nlinfit ?returns residuals that are weighted, but?sbiofit ?does not correctly account for this weighting.

Summary: Model update hangs at "Configuring subsystem output ports"
Description: If a model has a large virtual bus with an uninterrupted feedback loop, updating the model might hang at "Configuring subsystem output ports" and the memory usage might increase when the model is compiling.
Workaround: Interrupt the feedback loop with a Unit Delay block or convert the large virtual bus into several smaller buses.

Summary: sldvisactive may incorrectly return false when translating a model with model blocks
Description: sldvisactive  may incorrectly return false  when Simulink Design Verifier is run on a model that contains one or more model  blocks.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b

Summary: Xilinx System Generator and HDL Coder integration broken due to Vivado incompatible upgrades
Description: The workflow described in?this document ?is broken in R2019a release and generates this error: Error: Evaluation of elaborate function on class hdldefaults.XilinxSystemGeneratorSubsystem failed with the error message: Xilinx:Environment: Unknown path type identifier.?
Workaround: An updated version of this example is available in R2020a. To see the example, refer to?this document.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Extra warning on unresolved configuration reference while loading model
Description: An extra warning regarding unresolved configuration reference might occur while a model is being loaded. The warning indicates an error handling an SLX part. This might happen when all of the following conditions are true: Model is using configuration reference. Model is using a data dictionary. In the Model Properties dialog box,?Enable model access to base workspace  is cleared. In the Model Explorer, for the data dictionary, Enable dictionary access to base workspace ?is cleared.

Summary: Automated Driving Toolbox examples for monoCamera and birdsEyeView classes do not display expected results when installed with Vehicle Dynamics Blockset.
Description: The results returned by these examples are incorrect when the Vehicle Dynamics Blockset is installed.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 8, R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 1, R2020b

Summary: MATLAB might error out during code generation when the same identifier is used for a top model and a system within it
Description: When the same identifier is to be used for a variable in the code generated from a top model and another system within it such as a Stateflow Chart or reusable subsystem, MATLAB might error out during code generation with this message: ?CGIR assertion 'globalTailBinder->prevTop == 0'

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation results for some simulation modes for models containing model reference hierarchy?with particular LUT object struct order settings using Simulink.LookupTableObject as model argument
Description: Incorrect simulation results for a model with these settings: Top-level model contains: Model configuration parameter LUT object struct order for explicit value specification  set to 'Size,Table,Breakpoints'  A? Model reference block with these characteristics: Refers to a model containing an n-D Lookup Table block with these configurations LUT object struct order for explicit value specification ?set to? 'Size,Table,Breakpoints'  n-D Lookup Table block contains a Simulink.LookupTableObject with these characteristics Data Specification  parameter set to Lookup table object  Lookup table object BreakpointsSpecification? parameter set to  Explict values  Lookup table object configured to be passed as an argument to the Model reference block      Incorrect simulation results might also occur when one of these is true for the model: The simulation mode  of the model is set to Normal ?and the simulation mode?of the Model reference block is set to Accelerator .    The simulation mode?of the model is set to Accelerator   and the simulation mode  of the Model reference block is set to Software-in-the-loop (SIL)
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 3, R2020b

Summary: Using smexportonshape function to export an Onshape assembly to Simscape Multibody returns a blank MATLAB web browser
Description: If you are using smexportonshape  function to export Onshape assembly to Simscape Multibody, it will return a blank MATLAB web browser.

Summary: Generating a model coverage report using an exported object can cause an error
Description: If a model has a custom library block and you export the coverage data object from a Simulink Test run, regenerating the report causes an error: Error using cvi.? TopModelCov.? updateModelHandles (line 23)?  Attempt to modify 'MyLibrary' which is a locked (read-only) library
Fixed In: R2019a Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: MATLAB sporadically crashes when editing a transition in a Stateflow chart
Description: MATLAB might crash when you edit a transition in a Stateflow chart. The crash report refers to buildTransition. On Windows, the call stack is similar to: [ 16] 0x... CxxThrowException ? (in VCRUNTIME140.dll)[ 17] 0x... std::_Xout_of_range ? (in MSVCP140.dll)[ 18] 0x... std::_String_val::_Xran ? (in stateflow.dll)[ 19] 0x... SF::ir::TransitionAdapter::getConditionString ? (in stateflow.dll)[ 20] 0x... SF::ir::IrBuilder::buildTransition ? (in stateflow.dll)

Summary: Missing header files for enumerations used on root inports and outports for AUTOSAR adaptive models
Description: When models configured for the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform use enumeration data types on root inports and outports the header files for the enumerated types are not generated. The generated code cannot be compiled and linked into an executable application.
Workaround: Manually add header files for enumeration data types after inspecting the code and compilation errors. Information for the enumerated types can be taken from the generated ARXML.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Data mismatch between Simulink and HDL simulation for partly serial architecture of Discrete FIR Filter HDL Optimized block
Description: A data mismatch between Simulink and HDL simulation can happen for partly serial architecture of Discrete FIR Filter HDL Optimized block and for the dsp.HDLFIRFilter  System object in either of these cases: When casting the coefficients to the specified data type produces different values depending on the Saturate on integer overflow  parameter. ?When the coefficients are only partly symmetric/anti-symmetric.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 1, R2020b, R2021a
