Summary: Broken hyperlinks to linked documents in optional Requirements panel of model Web view
Description: When using Export to Web  feature for a Simulink model, and enabling the optional Requirements view,  the generated content lists linked artifacts with apparent hyperlinks, but clicking on a label does nothing.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Relay and Backlash blocks might produce incorrect results when block is in frame-based mode and model uses row-major array layout
Description: The Relay and Backlash blocks might produce incorrect results in generated code when all of the following conditions are true:? The?Input Processing ?parameter of the Relay or Backlash block is set to?Columns as channels (frame based)  The model configuration parameter?Code Generation? >?Interface? >?Array Layout?  is set to? Row-Major   The input signal is a 2-D matrix
Fixed In: R2018b Update 7, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 4, R2020a

Summary: Simulink Web View unable to export to file path that contains non-ASCII characters
Description: If you export a Simulink model to a Web View and provide a file path that contains non-ASCII characters, files in the support  folder can be corrupt and have a file size of zero.
Workaround: Specify a file path that contains only ASCII characters and then copy the files to the final destination.

Summary: When accelerating a model or SimFunction, SimBiology incorrectly discards accelerated code and reports that results did not match
Description: The accelerated code for some SimBiology models results in small but negligibly different results, when compared to the results of unaccelerated code. Due to these small differences, SimBiology incorrectly discards the accelerated code and issues a warning such as the following: Warning: Reported from ODE Compilation:Following error occurred while testing a MEX file created for SimBiology acceleration: Accelerated results for initialAssignment rules did not match the unaccelerated results. Following error occurred while testing a MEX file created for SimBiology acceleration: Accelerated results for initialAssignment rules did not match the unaccelerated results. Check that initialAssignment rule expressions and any user-defined functions are valid and supported for code generation as described in the Language, Function, and Object Support in MATLAB documentation. If this problem persists, contact Technical Support.?Click here ?for more information.?  When SimBiology discards the accelerated code, simulations may run more slowly than expected.?

Summary: datetime time zones do not reflect recent change to Brazil's time zone
Description: In April 2019, the government of Brazil announced that the country no longer observes Daylight Saving Time. The time zone data in MATLAB does not reflect this recent change. In MATLAB, this change manifests itself as datetime  arrays in what used to be the DST period (for example, 17 November 2019 to 16 February 2020) with Brazilian time zones (for example, 'America/Sao_Paulo' ) being treated as UTC-2 rather than UTC-3. The Brazilian time zones impacted by this change are listed here. 'America/Sao_Paulo'  'America/Campo_Grande'  'America/Cuiaba'
Workaround: If working only with recent datetime values (since 17 February 2019), use the fixed time zone offset. For each time zone listed below, change the time zone to the corresponding fixed time zone offset.?'America/Sao_Paulo' -> 'Etc/GMT+3''America/Campo_Grande' -> 'Etc/GMT+4''America/Cuiaba' -> 'Etc/GMT+4'For example, for datetime values associated with S?o Paulo, change the TimeZone property to 'Etc/GMT+3'.if all(d > datetime(2019,2,17))    d.TimeZone = 'Etc/GMT+3'end For datetime values associated with Campo Grande or Cuiab¨˘, use 'Etc/GMT+4'.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 7, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 4, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: MATLAB might crash when performing Test Generation with?DVParameters and??DVExtendExistingTests enabled
Description: MATLAB might crash when performing the Test Generation when the following conditions are met: The parameter?Enable parameter configuration? is enabled?in the?Design Verifier > Parameters ?pane The configuration parameter?Extend existing test cases?? is enabled in the Design Verifier > Test Generation  pane There is an instance of a matrix parameter that is defined in a base workspace or data dictionary, and is used only as a mask parameter?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Subsequent tasks in Simscape HDL Workflow Advisor do not reset when previous task is run
Description: When you run the Simscape HDL Workflow Advisor, if you rerun a task in the Advisor that was previously run, the subsequent tasks are not reset automatically. This can cause an issue in this example scenario: You run the Generate implementation model  task to generate the HDL implementation model for the original model Now you update the original Simscape model and rerun the Check switched linear  task.  In this case, the?Generate implementation model ?task does not reset. If you do not rerun this task, there can be a simulation mismatch between the implementation model and the original model because the model does not have the updated state-space parameters.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generator might produce incorrect C/C++ code for subscripted assignment of cell arrays containing upper bounded arrays
Description: Suppose that you generate C/C++ code for MATLAB code that contains subscripted assignments to a cell array that contains upper bounded arrays. When all  the following conditions are true, the generated C/C++ code might read uninitialized memory: You create a cell array by using the cell  function You perform a series of subscripted assignments to this cell array The right hand sides of these subscripted assignments are also cell arrays The right hand sides of these subscripted assignments contain arrays with different and explicitly specified upper bounds  For such MATLAB code patterns, the generated code assigns the same upper bound to all the elements of the left-hand side array, taking the upper bound from first assignment.? For example, suppose that you define this MATLAB function: function out = main(in1, in2)out = cell(1,2);out{1} = in1;out{2} = in2;end During code generation, define the types of in1  and in2 ?as: in1_element = coder.typeof(1,[1 5], [false true]);in1 = repmat({in1_element}, 1, 2);in2_element = coder.typeof(1,[1 6], [false true]);in2 = repmat({in2_element}, 1, 2); codegen -config:lib main -args {in1,int2}? Both in1  and in2  are cell arrays that contain variable-sized, upper bounded arrays?in1_element  and in2_element ?with different upper bounds. So, this issue might affect the generated code.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect overflow in Simulink.LookupTable and Simulink.Breakpoint objects when?number of breakpoints exceed the limit for derived Tunable Size data type
Description: Incorrect simulation results are generated for models that contain an n-D Lookup Table or Prelookup block with these settings: Model contains an n-D Lookup Table block: In the n-D lookup table block, Data Specification  is set to Lookup table object  Name  field is set to the name of an existing or newly added  Simulink.LookupTable object   In the Simulink.LookupTable object,   The Support tunable size  property is set Number of breakpoints exceeds the limit for the derived tunable size data type   Model contains a Prelookup block In the Prelookup block, Breakpoints data Specification  parameter is set to Breakpoint object  Name  field is set to the name of an existing or newly added  Simulink.Breakpoint object   In the Simulink.Breakpoint object ,  The Support tunable size  property is set Number of breakpoints exceeds the limit for the derived tunable size data type
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 2, R2020b

Summary: Simulink might crash when creating a subsystem that contains signal lines with port value labels
Description: Simulink will crash when all of the following conditions apply: You create a subsystem from selected blocks. At least one of these blocks has a signal that will cross the new subsystem boundary. The signal line has a label that shows its value. This label corresponds to the Show Output Value of selected signal ?button on the Debug ?tab.
Workaround: Remove port value labels from the signal lines connected to blocks that will be in the new subsystem.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Calling Simulink functions with large bus inputs or outputs from event actions of SimEvents blocks might produce incorrect results
Description: Simulink might produce incorrect results when you simulate a model that meet that following conditions: The model has SimEvents blocks that call Simulink functions in their event actions. The Simulink functions have large bus inputs or outputs with an approximate size of 200,000 bytes. For example, a bus with a single bus element of 26,000 double values (26,000 x 8 bytes = 208,000 bytes) will trigger this bug.

Summary: The goertzel function throws an error when running in a MATLAB Compiler compiled application
Description: The?goertzel ?function throws an error when running in a MATLAB Compiler compiled application. The error will be similar to Unable to resolve the name coder.internal.constNonSingletonDim  but might refer to a different coder.internal  function.

Summary: Repeatedly saving models that open in multiple Simulink Editor windows causes slower model opening
Description: When you save a Simulink model with two or more Simulink Editor windows open, information is stored in the model file to recreate those windows when the model is later reopened. However, in some circumstances, that information might increase in size with each save cycle. Eventually, this extra information can slow down the process of reopening and resaving the model.
Workaround: You can avoid this problem with either of the following:Close all but one Simulink Editor window before saving the model. This mitigates additional file size increases due to extra window information, but it may not reduce the number of copies already created.?Export the model to R2019a. The duplicated information is excised from the R2019a version of the model. This workaround is not applicable if your model requires R2019b features.

Summary: Internal error or crash when reusable subsystem is in a hierarchy that has constant and other sample times
Description: When there are reusable subsystems in a non-virtual hierarchy that has constant and other sample times, updating the block diagram might lead to an internal error or even crash MATLAB.

Summary: Code generator might throw an error if a data replacement type for 'char' type is specified
Description: The code generator might trigger an error when all the following conditions are  met: Model configuration parameter?Replace data type names in the generated code  is selected. A name is specified for model configuration parameter Replacement Name: char . A name is specified for model configuration parameter Replacement Name: uint64  OR Replacement Name: int64 .  The error message might be similar to this message:? 'CHAR' is an invalid duplicate replacement type for 'char'.?

Summary: MATLAB might crash while converting a subsystem to Subsystem Reference
Description: MATLAB might crash when you convert the outer subsystem to Subsystem Reference while an inner subsystem is open in a separate window.? Workaround: Close the inner subsystem before converting the outer subsystem to Subsystem Reference

Summary: Evaluation of assessments using enumeration constants with same underlying value might product wrong answer
Description: In Simulink Test Manager, if several symbols in an assessment are mapped to different enumerated values with the same underlying value, the evaluation of the assessment might produce a wrong answer. You might see an error like this, even if c1  and c2  are mapped to the same enumerated type: Error during assessment evaluation.  Caused by:  ? ? Error during evaluation of expression '...'  ? ? Type error for expression '...': type of 'c1' is 'enum1' and type of 'c2' is 'enum2'.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Conversion of logical values to fi objects with non-zero bias might be incorrect
Description: Conversion of logical values (true, false) to a fi object with non-zero bias might be incorrect. For example:? >> fi(true,0,8,0.1,-2)  ans =?  ? ? 0.8000  ? ? ? ? ? DataTypeMode: Fixed-point: slope and bias scaling  ? ? ? ? ? ? Signedness: Unsigned  ? ? ? ? ? ? WordLength: 8  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?Slope: 0.1  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? Bias: -2?
Workaround: Convert logical values to uint8 before converting to fi.For example:?>>? fi(uint8(true),0,8,0.1,-2)ans =?? ? ?1? ? ? ? ? DataTypeMode: Fixed-point: slope and bias scaling? ? ? ? ? ? Signedness: Unsigned? ? ? ? ? ? WordLength: 8? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?Slope: 0.1? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? Bias: -2
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 4, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: Setting Modulation to PAM4 does not generate PAM4 thresholds in Init code if no DFECDR or CDR block is present in Rx subsystem
Description: If the SerDes system does not contain any DFECDR or CDR block in the Rx subsystem and the Modulation  is set to PAM4 , the PAM4 thresholds do not get generated from the Init code. This has limited effect on the Simulink??model simulation, but could result in incorrect thresholds being evaluated by the channel simulator?when an exported IBIS-AMI model is used.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulation and code generation results might vary when Outport signal with non-Auto storage class is connected to Stateflow Graphical Function
Description: Simulation and code generation results might vary under the following conditions: Model has an Outport driven by a signal with non-Auto  storage class. Outport is connected to a Stateflow Graphical Function.  In such a case, the Outport value might be written before the graphical function is executed. If the Outport is virtualized in the generated code due to the non-Auto  storage class, there is no assignment code for the Outport in the model step function.
Workaround: Use any one of these workarounds:Avoid using non-Auto storage class for the signal driving the Outport that is is directly connected to Stateflow Graphical Function.?Select the model configuration parameter MAT-file logging for the model so that all Outports are non-virtual in the generated code.

Summary: Starting MATLAB Job Scheduler on Windows can fail if earlier versions of MATLAB exist on the system path
Description: When you try to start the MATLAB Job Scheduler on Windows, the following commands can fail with different errors when you run them: ?mjs start  An error has occurred. The procedure entry point? Initialize @ i18n @ fl @@ YAX_N @ Z was not found from the dynamic link library (MATLAB installation directory) \ ?toolbox \ ?parallel \ ?bin \ ?win64 \ ?setMATLABExecutable.exe.   An error has occurred, ?but mjs has been started.     startjobmanager     Exception in thread "main" java.lang.UnsatisfiedLinkError: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\nativei18n.dll: The specified procedure could not be found       startworker       matlabExecutable must be of the form '<release string>=<path to executable>;<release string>=<path to executable>;'. Actual string: %MATLAB_EXECUTABLE%          These errors occur because dynamic libraries from previous versions of MATLAB are being loaded and cause the commands to fail to run correctly.
Workaround: To prevent this issue from occurring, edit your System Path variable and remove any previous versions of MATLAB from the Path.Warning: Incorrectly editing the System Path can cause your system to become unusable. Make a backup of your Path so that you can restore it in the case of an error.?Go to Control Panel -> System and Security -> System -> Advanced System Settings.Click the button labeled "Environment Variables" at the bottom of the dialog box.Under System variables select the "Path" variable and click Edit.Select any previous version of MATLAB and click the "Delete" button.Click OK.Exit and restart any command prompts before starting MATLAB Job Scheduler.

Summary: SimBiology Model Analyzer can hang when loading saved projects with imported data exclusions
Description: Saved projects that include plots of imported data with exclusions might cause the app to hang when loading.

Summary: Test case result includes external input when Include input data in test result?is off
Description: Test case input data used for driving the simulation is saved with the results when?Include input data in test result ?is set to?off   a nd?Stop simulation at last time point ?is?on. ?This causes an increase in the time required to import/export results and create reports.

Summary: On macOS and Linux, MATLAB might hang during the execution of the system or copyfile command
Description: If the system runs out of memory during the execution of the system  or copyfile  command, or if you manually terminate the matlab_helper  subprocess by sending a SIGKILL  signal, then the MATLAB Command Window might hang.
Workaround: To recover from this condition, kill the matlab_helper process, then restart MATLAB.

Summary: No units appear in the unit library of the SimBiology app
Description: If the user-defined unit library contains a unit with an invalid composition, the SimBiology app does not show any units in the unit library.
Workaround: Remove all invalid units from the SimBiology unit library before opening the SimBiology app.

Summary: Component creation from ARXML fails if ClientServerOperation arguments are 64-bit integers
Description: When importing ARXML files in which ClientServerOperation  arguments use a 64-bit integer type, the createComponentAsModel  function fails with the following message: Error using arxml.importer/createComponentAsModel (line 120)Expression 'uint64(1)' for block 'modelName/blockName' returns a Simulink.NumericType object?that has property values that need to be evaluated.This object should be instantiated in a workspace and referenced by name in?Simulink.

Summary: Error when trying to compile an S-function with SLDV support using the Legacy Code Tool
Description: Compilingan S-function using the?Legacy Code Tool may error out with "Internal error generating S-functioninternal analysis file sfcn_main.c "?when all the following conditions are met: The S-function is using a Simulink type such as a?Simulink.AliasType ?whose     corresponding C type is defined as a header file. The S-function is implemented as a macro. The header file defining C type for the Simulink type     is not included in the file defining the macro. The option "supportCoverageAndDesignVerifier " is set to true.  For instance, assuming that the Simulink type MyAliasType  is defined in the C header "my_alias_type.h ", and the header "my_sfunction.h " is defining a macro "my_sfunction ", the following script encounters the error: def = legacy_code('initialize');def.SFunctionName = 'my_sfunction';def.HeaderFiles = {'my_sfunction.h'};def.OutputFcnSpec = 'MyAliasType y1 = my_sfunction(int32 u1)';def.Options.isMacro = true;def.Options.supportCoverageAndDesignVerifier = true;  The problem is caused by some SLDV specific files not including the header "my_alias_type.h " automatically.
Workaround: If the S-function sources or the compilation script can be modified, use any of the following workarounds:Modify the S-function sources so that they include the header for the types used. In the previous example, that would require addition of '#include "my_alias_type.h"' within the header "my_sfunction.h".Modify the Legacy Code script so that the header that defines the type is listed in the HeaderFiles field. In the previous example, that would mean replacement of?def.HeaderFiles = {'my_sfunction.h'} with def.HeaderFiles = {'my_alias_type.h', 'my_sfunction.h'}.

Summary: Compatibility check fails for models using symbolic dimension specification
Description: Compatibility check fails for models using symbolic dimension specification (i.e the parameter  AllowSymbolicDim  is turned on)

Summary: Initial values of RAMs created during HDL code generation of MATLAB Function blocks with MATLAB Datapath architecture have lower precision
Description: During HDL code generation, the RAMs created from persistent variables inside MATLAB Function blocks that have MATLAB Datapath  as the HDL architecture? have less precise initial values in the generated code than the original persistent variable value.?

Summary: Subsystem Reference block's position changes on loading or during synchronization of contents
Description: In certain scenarios, a Subsystem Reference  block changes its position. It can happen during:? Loading of top model which contains the Subsystem Reference  block? Synchronization of Subsystem Reference  block's contents Converting a subsystem to Subsystem Reference  block?

Summary: Code generation incorrect when Parameter Writer writing to Model block parameters is initialized by Model workspace variable
Description: Code generation is incorrect when all of these conditions occur:   A Model block parameter's storage class is set to Auto . An instance parameter of the Model block is initialized by a workspace variable. The Parameter Writer block is writing a value to the parameter of the Model block
Workaround: To obtain the desired generated code, either:Set the storage class of the? of the Model block to Model Default.or?Initialize the Model block parameter directly from the dialog box with the desired value.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when generating code for a model containing C action language Stateflow Chart with shift operation applied to custom storage class
Figure reporter incorrectly scales the snapshot
Description: The Report API Figure  reporter might incorrectly scale the figure snapshot to fit a PDF report page in either of the following cases: The report output type is not specified. The report output type is specified in upper case as PDF .?   The?Figure  reporter might also cause the caption to overflow the page boundaries.
Workaround: Specify the report output type in lower case as pdf.If the figure caption overflows the page boundaries, set the Scaling property to? 'custom' and set the Height and Width properties.?
Fixed In: R2018b Update 7, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 4, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect error message with mutually exclusive signal objects inside a variant subsystem
Description: An incorrect error message is thrown when all the below conditions are met:??A variant subsystem is configured for generating pre-processor conditionals. The variant subsystem has the same signal object used in multiple variant choices. The signal object is specified on an inport block of any subsystem within the choice.  Error message seen: Usage of same signal object for two different choice inport of variant subsystem is not allowed?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model with clock-rate pipelining and streaming optimization might generate incorrect HDL code
Description: The generated HDL code for a model with clock-rate pipelining and streaming or sharing optimizations might be incorrect in certain cases. For example, this issue might occur when a model contains multiple subsystems connected in serial all specified with a non-zero streaming factor. Serial subsystems with streaming may not be scheduled correctly as a part of clock-rate pipelining if a downstream subsystem with streaming is at a lower level of hierarchy than an upstream system with streaming. In this case, the validation model might give assertions during simulation.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 5, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: MATLAB crashes with "RegexMatcher::MatchChunkAt" at the top of the crash dump
Description: MATLAB might crash when two or more of the following conditions are met:  Current Folder browser is open and there are files in the current directory using hex or binary literals (Ex: 0xFF  | 0b1010 ). Code Analyzer is enabled and user is editing a file with hex or binary literals. Tab Completion is used while editing a file.  The crash log for this issue will contain the following function calls: Stack Trace (from fault):  [? 0] 0x00007f85bc566d60 icu_61::RegexMatcher::MatchChunkAt(int, signed char, UErrorCode&)[? 1] 0x00007f85bc56a48c icu_61::RegexMatcher::matches(UErrorCode&)[? 2] 0x00007f85c0ee5d9d fl::i18n::pattern_match::match(std::__cxx11::basic_string<char16_t, std::char_traits<char16_t>, std::allocator<char16_t> > const&) const[? 3] 0x00007f85adb275a8 lf::util::(anonymous namespace)::matchesRegex(mwboost::basic_string_ref<char16_t, std::char_traits<char16_t> >, fl::i18n::pattern_match const&)[? 4] 0x00007f85adb2a206 validateHexSyntax[? 5] 0x00007f85a534a599 ca::ast::TreeBuilder::buildNumberLiteral(ca::core::TreeNode const*)?
Workaround: This crash can be avoided by using one of the following workarounds: Disable Code Analyzer. On the Home tab, in the                        Environment section, click                     Preferences. Navigate to MATLAB > Code Analyzer, then unselect Enable integrated warning and error messages.Disable Tab Completion. On the Home tab, in the                        Environment section, click                     Preferences. Navigate to MATLAB > Keyboard, then unselect Enable tab completion under both the Command Window and Editor headings.Update to MATLAB

Summary: Extra data copy for coding pattern involving function call assigning to multiple persistent variables
Description: When generating code for a MATLAB function containing ?A function call assigning to multiple persistent variables Unbounded varsize data  MATLAB Coder might insert an extra datacopy of the persistent data at the function callsite.?An example function issue_2133056  can be seen below function y = issue_2133056(n)    persistent p    y = 0;    if isempty(p)        p = true;        y = init1(n);    endendfunction y = init1(n)    persistent p data1 data2    if isempty(p)        p = true;        [data1, data2] = init2(n);    end    y = process(data1,data2);endfunction [data1, data2] = init2(n)    coder.inline('never');    data1 = 1:n    data2 = 2:(n+1);endfunction y = process(in1,in2)    coder.inline('never');    y = sum(in1(:) + in2(:));end Code generation with a scalar double input >> codegen -c -config:exe issue_2133056 -args {0} Results in an extra copy after the call-site of init2 . Note the copies from b_data1  to data1  and b_data2  to data2 .   y = 0.0;  if (!p_not_empty) {    p_not_empty = true;    if (!b_p_not_empty) {      emxInit_real_T(&b_data1, 2);      emxInit_real_T(&b_data2, 2);      b_p_not_empty = true;      init2(n, b_data1, b_data2);      i = data1->size[0] * data1->size[1];      data1->size[0] = 1;      data1->size[1] = b_data1->size[1];      emxEnsureCapacity_real_T(data1, i);      loop_ub = b_data1->size[0] * b_data1->size[1];      for (i = 0; i < loop_ub; i++) {        data1->data[i] = b_data1->data[i];      }      emxFree_real_T(&b_data1);      i = data2->size[0] * data2->size[1];      data2->size[0] = 1;      data2->size[1] = b_data2->size[1];      emxEnsureCapacity_real_T(data2, i);      loop_ub = b_data2->size[0] * b_data2->size[1];      for (i = 0; i < loop_ub; i++) {        data2->data[i] = b_data2->data[i];      }      emxFree_real_T(&b_data2);    }    y = process(data1, data2); The generated code without this issue can be seen below. Note the lack of copy   y = 0.0;  if (!p_not_empty) {    p_not_empty = true;    if (!b_p_not_empty) {      b_p_not_empty = true;      init2(n, data1, data2);    }    y = process(data1, data2);  }  return y; This issue can affect the following workflows:? MATLAB only workflows using MATLAB Coder Code Generation from Simulink models containing: MATLAB Function Block, MATLAB Function in Stateflow, and MATLAB System Block

Summary: ASAP2 code generation fails when using enumeration data for lookup table data object
Description: When you use enumeration data types for a lookup table data object (Simulink.LookupTable), code generation aborts:  ### Build procedure for model: 'Test_Model' aborted due to an error. Error:  Simulink Coder Error: Unhandled dataType. DataTypeID = 14 Error: File: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2019b\rtw\c\tlc\mw\asap2lib.tlc Line: 2184 Column: 3 Unable to find RequiredBits within the DataType scope Main program: ==> [00] C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2019b\rtw\c\tlc\mw\asap2lib.tlc:(2184)     [01] C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2019b\toolbox\rtw\targets\asap2\asap2\user\.\asap2userlib.tlc:ASAP2UserFcnWriteStdAxis(381)     [02] C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2019b\toolbox\rtw\targets\asap2\asap2\user\./templates\asap2lookup1d.tlc:ASAP2UserFcnWriteCharacteristic_Lookup1D(271)     [03] C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2019b\rtw\c\tlc\mw\asap2lookuplib.tlc:FcnWriteStandardCharacteristic_Lookup1D(1646)     [04] C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2019b\rtw\c\tlc\mw\asap2lookuplib.tlc:FcnGenerateLookupND(692)     [05] C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2019b\rtw\c\tlc\mw\asap2lib.tlc:FcnLoopThroughNewLookupBlocks(201)     [06] C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2019b\rtw\c\tlc\mw\asap2lib.tlc:FcnLoopThruParamGroups(256)     [07] C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2019b\rtw\c\tlc\mw\asap2lib.tlc:SLibASAP2WriteDynamicContents(21)     [08] C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2019b\rtw\c\tlc\mw\asap2main.tlc:(88)

Summary: Assertion when compiling charts with Model Advisor checks on
Description: If Model Advisor checks are turned on in Stateflow charts with transitions which contain actions, you might see an assertion in the diagnostic viewer when compiling the chart. This assertion might mention variables used by transition actions.
Workaround: Turning off Model Advisor checks eliminates this problem, but you will not see Model Advisor feedback for impacted charts.

Summary: High-Integrity check?Data type selection for index signals incorrectly warns about Multiport Switch block?
Description: The Model Advisor check??Data type selection for index signals ?(ID: mathworks.hism.hisl_0022 ) incorrectly  warns when the indices specified in the Multiport Switch block are of enum data type.
Workaround: Typecast the individual specified indices to integer data type

Summary: Unexpected warning or error message in the Receiver Accumulator (2P) block
Description: In the Receiver Accumulator (2P) block, when the?Liquid volume fraction out of range check  parameter is set to Warning  or Error , the block may erroneously generate the following message: "Liquid volume fraction must be less than or equal to Minimum liquid volume fraction."
Workaround: Set the?Liquid volume fraction out of range check?parameter to None to avoid receiving the warning or error message. Alternatively,?you can disregard the message?"Liquid volume fraction must be less than or equal to Minimum liquid volume fraction" when?Liquid volume fraction out of range check?is set to Warning.Warning messages that read "Liquid volume fraction must be greater than or equal to Minimum liquid volume fraction"? are still valid.

Summary: Simulink model fails to build for all custom ROS 2 messages that depend on non-primitive message types
Description: When building a Simulink model that uses ROS 2 custom messages, certain message types cause the build to fail. If any nonprimitive message type is defined in the custom message defintion, that library is not included when you build the Simulink model. For example, a ROS 2 custom message that depends on std_msgs  library: std_msgs/Header header  bool? enabled  uint16 velocity       Simulink model using this custom message will fail during build. The dependency on the?std_msgs  library is not included when building the Simulink model. Various message types can cause this error, and the error is not restricted to only std_msgs .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model that uses row-major array layout and complex types containing fixed-point data types might generate incorrect results
Description: The generated code might be incorrect when compared to simulation results under all these conditions:? Model configuration parameter Array layout  is set to Row-major . Model contains complex types with fixed-point data types.?

Summary: State Reader/Writer blocks might not work with some S-functions in models created in previous releases
Description: If a model containing an S-Function block with multiple states was created in or before R2018b, and the model is opened in R2019a or the pre-update 5 versions of R2019b, the State Reader and State Writer blocks do not recognize the S-Function block as a valid state owner block. The issue goes away if the state writer is deleted and re-added.

Summary: Test execution time slowdown for baseline test for a model with multi-channel signals
Description: A significant slowdown in test execution time occurs if?the baseline file in a baseline test uses multi-channel signals.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: A block that executes in a conditional input branch with a constant sample time may lead to incorrect generated code
Description: Generated code might produce wrong results when all of following conditions are met: Model configuration parameter 'Conditional input branch execution' is checked Configuration parameter 'Code Generation' > 'Optimization' > 'Default parameter behavior' is set to 'Inlined'  There are blocks that conditionally need input signals, such as Switch or Multiport Switch blocks Upstream blocks driving such blocks have a constant sample time

Summary: "Unrecognized function or variable 'Boolean'" error in roadNetwork function when Simulink is not installed
Description: If you use the roadNetwork  function without Simulink installed, the function might return this error:"Unrecognized function or variable 'Boolean.' " This error occurs because the function validates for Boolean inputs, which is a data type used in Simulink, rather than for logical inputs, which is the equivalent data type used in MATLAB.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Use of Bus containing enum element with a non-zero default value might lead to incorrect code when zero initialization is disabled
Description: Incorrect code might be generated if all these conditions are true:? Model uses Bus object. The Bus contains an element of enum  type. The enum  type has non-zero default value. When the Bus is used in root-level I/O, model configuration parameter Remove root level I/O zero initialization ?is selected or when the the Bus is used in internal data, model configuration parameter?Remove internal data zero initialization  is selected.

Summary: AUTOSAR component model fails to build when referenced submodels use Simulink Accelerator mode
Description: Code generation fails for an AUTOSAR software component model if the following conditions exist:The AUTOSAR component model references submodels. The submodels are configured to simulate in Simulink Accelerator mode.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generation might ignore property set method if class is in a package and uses property validation
Description: The code generator might product incorrect code if all of the following conditions are true:?Your MATLAB code uses a MATLAB class that is contained inside a package. Your MATLAB code defines and uses a property of this class that has a set method. This property uses property validation.  In such cases, the generated code might not include a call to the property's set method, and instead assign the property directly. The generated code still honors the property validation rules.? For example, define the class sampleStates  in a package mypackage :  classdef sampleStates    properties        Samples(1,:) double;    end    methods        function obj = set.Samples(obj,val)            obj.Samples = val + 1;        end    endend Define a MATLAB function that creates an instance of this class and sets the property Samples .? function out = mainx = mypackage.sampleStates;x.Samples = 1;out = x.Samples;end When you call main , MATLAB returns an output 2 . The generated code does not execute the set  method and returns 1 . This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Workaround: This issue has two possible workarounds. Use any one of these:?Modify your MATLAB code so that the property does not have a custom-written?set?method.Modify your MATLAB code so that the property does not use property validation.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 4, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code and MATLAB might behave differently when using addition or subtraction operation that mixes?double?and?int64?or?uint64?inputs
Description: The generated code might produce a wrong answer if your MATLAB code contains an an addition or subtraction that mixes?double ?and?int64 ?or?uint64 ?inputs. This issue occurs?when all of the following conditions are true:? One input is?double ?and the other is?either?int64 ?or?uint64.  If adding, both the  double  and  int  inputs are negative. If subtracting, the first operand is negative and the second is positive.  The?double ?input has absolute value greater than?2^63 .   This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks and MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.

Summary: Test execution fails in SIL/PIL verification mode with parameter overrides
Description: If you configure a test case to execute a block diagram test harness with verification mode set to SIL/PIL, and enable parameter overrides, the test execution fails. An error message indicates that the SimulationInput  object doesn't support overriding parameters in SIL/PIL mode.

Summary: The YOLOv2-based detector sporadically crashes MATLAB when training or performing detection on a batch of images.
Description: The   detect function   (of the yolov2ObjectDetector  object) and the       trainyolov2ObjectDetector            function sporadically produces an error or returns an incorrect result. This behavior occurs when performing detection or training on a batch of images. When the error occurs,       MATLAB?  returns a       CUDA_ERROR_ILLEGAL_ADDRESS    message.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when creating an architecture view
Description: MATLAB crashes when creating an architecture view if the model contains a port whose name has a trailing newline character.

Summary: The?Simulation 3D Fisheye Camera produces a pixelated image
Description: The?Simulation3D Fisheye Camera with?the default parameters?has the field of view45 degrees, what is not typical for a fisheye camera. These parameters produce apixelated image due to the "digital? zoom" of the image captured with?the fisheye camera.
Workaround: In the Simulation 3D Fisheye Camera block, update these parameters:Distortion center (pixels): [640, 360]Image size (pixels): [720, 1280]Mapping coefficients: [320, 0, 0, 0]?

Summary: On non-Windows platforms, the link to function help is broken in error messages for non-built-in MATLAB functions
Description: When a non-built-in MATLAB function is used incorrectly on a non-Windows platform, the first link in the error message (which displays the function name) is broken. For example, consider calling the surf  function with no inputs on a Linux or Mac platform. The first link in the error message is broken. >> surf  Error using surf  (line 49 )  Not enough input arguments.?

Summary: MATLAB might crash when you export data from the Simulation Data Inspector for a virtual bus
Description: MATLAB might crash when you export data from the Simulation Data Inspector for a virtual bus that contains a non-virtual bus or matrix signal if the virtual bus also contains scalar signals.
Workaround: Unzip the attachment and save the?cleanupDataForExport.p?file to a folder on your MATLAB path. Run the?cleanupDataForExport?before exporting this kind of data from the Simulation Data Inspector.The script may take a long time to restructure data with many time points.

Summary: Run with Stepper in Test Manager does not work with an open test harness
Description: If you open a test harness before it is used to run a test case and use?Run with Stepper  in the Test Manager, the simulation buttons for the design model are disabled. This prevents completing the simulation, which causes incomplete test results.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation for model containing Initialize or Terminate blocks
Description: The generated code for a subsystem could compute incorrect results if Initialize or Terminate blocks influence the signals inside the subsystem. The code generation will ignore the Initialize Terminate blocks if they are not present inside the subsystem leading to incorrect result in the target IDE.

Summary: Polyspace Access does not start and User Manager log contains error messages
Description: Polyspace Access might fail to start if the User Manager settings stored in the settings.json  file contain certain special characters. Typically, you configure these settings in the Cluster Operator web interface. When you parse the docker log for the polyspace-usermanager  service, you see error messages similar to these: User repository rejected the User Manager credentials. Ensure they have been configured correctly  or Error: Could not find or load main class Accounts,OU=myinfrasctructure,DC=mygroup,DC=mylocal  Characters that might cause this issue include: ` $ : ; ' " \ &  White space characters such as space, tab, or new line Non-ASCII characters

Summary: Error exporting a System Composer model using systemcomposer.exportModel API
Description: Using the systemcomposer.exportModel  API to export an architecture model returns an error if the model has elements with stereotypes that do not have any properties. You might get this error when trying to use the exportModel  function with stereotypes and no properties attached to model element:     'MATLAB:noSuchMethodOrField'    --------------    Error Details:    --------------    Unrecognized method, property, or field 'parent' for class 'systemcomposer.profile.Stereotype'.        Error in systemcomposer.utils.getPropertyValueMap (line 14)                propValMap = systemcomposer.utils.getPropertyValueMap(prototype.parent); Or you might get this error when trying to use the exportModel function with stereotypes attached to ports:     Error using systemcomposer.utils.exportModelClass/?addPort2table (line 384)     The number of table variables in an assignment must match.     Error in systemcomposer.utils.exportModelClass/?addComp2table (line 322)                 obj = obj.addPort2table(compArch,?compId);     Error in systemcomposer.utils.exportModelClass (line 195)                 obj = obj.addComp2table(childComponents(compItr),?"0");     Error in systemcomposer.exportModel (line 4)         obj = systemcomposer.utils.exportModelClass(modelInfo);

Summary: MATLAB crashes when managing interface elements using the Property Inspector
Description: If you input text field values in the Property Inspector for an interface element without clicking Enter , and instead click away from the text field, MATLAB will crash.

Summary: Reusing Simulink cache file errors out when no replacement model is generated for a custom block replacement rule
Description: Reusing Simulink cache file for Simulink Design Verifieranalysis results in an error  Operands to the || and && operatorsmust be convertible to logical scalar values  when all the followingconditions are true:  The SLDV option BlockReplacement  is set to on.  A custom block replacement rule is specified aspart of the option BlockReplacementRulesList .  Analyzing the model for the first time throws awarning as follows:? None of the active block replacement ruleswere available for replacing blocks in the model ˇ®ModelNameˇŻ. Analysis will beusing the original model 'ModelName'.  The option RebuildModelRepresentation  is set to IfChangeIsDetected  and SLDV analysis is performed again.
Workaround: Disable block replacements by setting BlockReplacement?option to off, whenever the warning:?None of the active block replacement rules were available for replacing blocks in the model 'ModelName'. Analysis will be using the original model 'ModelName'.?is shown.

Summary: The Image Labeler app exports LabelData as structs rather than double arrays.
Description: The  Image Labeler  app? incorrectly exports LabelData  as a struct when the labelingsession contains only labeled ROIs (with no sublabels or attributes). The exported label data should be a double array.

Summary: Assertion Error 'In checkMinimalCompatibility: Unsupported type conversion' when generating code for model containing symbolic dimensions
Description: Generating code for a model that meets all of these conditions might cause an unexpected error:The model contains signals with symbolic dimensions. A Mux or Vector Concatenate block concatenates the signals with symbolic dimensions.  The error is as follows:?? Error using tlc_c (line 199)Unexpected or internal error encountered in "in CGIR: type/TypeConversionTools.cpp line 563: In checkMinimalCompatibility:Unsupported type conversion To reproduce this bug, build the model in the attached zip file  'example_2126122.zip' .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Data Store Read block feeding Stateflow chart with Simulink events might generate incorrect code
Description: Incorrect code might be generated when all the following conditions are met:? Data Store Read block is feeding a Stateflow chart. The Stateflow chart invokes an event that calls a Simulink subsystem external to the chart. This external subsystem reads and write to the same Data Store Memory block.
Workaround: Use any one of these workarounds:Add a Signal Conversion block between the Data Store Read block and Stateflow chart and select?Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization?parameter. This is a preferred workaround because this only excludes the Signal Conversion block from buffer reuse optimization.Disable buffer reuse by clearing?Reuse local block outputs?(BufferReuse) model configuration parameter. This workaround excludes other blocks from buffer reuse optimization.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model with Function-Call Generator block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions:The model contains a Function-Call Generator block. The model contains a Data Store Read block and a Data Store Write block.? In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the?Optimize block operation order in the generated code? parameter is set to?Improved code execution speed.
Workaround: Set?the?Optimize block operation order in the generated code??model configuration parameter to?None.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Global Simulink functions are not called from MATLAB functions within charts that use C as the action language
Description: Stateflow does not execute a call to a global Simulink function from a MATLAB function within a chart that uses C as the action language. Any return values from the attempted call are set to 0.?

Summary: C-API reports incorrect number of model parameters
Description: In the generated C-API files, the C-API field numModelParameters  does not report the correct number of model parameters. It only accounts for shared tunable parameters, excluding parameters that are model arguments.

Summary: S-Function or FMU blocks that do not support multithreading might run on a separate thread if they are in a referenced model
Description: Referenced models might run on separate threads even when they contain an S-Function or FMU block that does not support running on a separate thread. For example, a referenced model containing an S-function might run on a separate thread when you have not explicitly allowed the S-function to be run on a separate thread with this command: ssRuntimeThreadSafetyCompliance(S,?RUNTIME_THREAD_SAFETY_COMPLIANCE_TRUE);

Summary: Entry-point arguments that are MATLAB objects with properties might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB can crash during code generation if all of the following statements about your MATLAB code are true: The entry-point function has an input and an output argument of same name. For example, x = foo(x) .  This argument is an object that is an instance of a MATLAB class. The class has a property that has an initial value. The property's initial value is a cell array containing objects of some other class. There is no usage or assignment of the property anywhere inside the entry-point function.
