Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Integer constants with types int64 or uint64 can generate incorrect VHDL Code
Description: Integer constants with types?int64? or?uint64 ?can get truncated to 32  bit integers when the TargetLanguage  is VHDL .?
Workaround: Use fixed-point constants fi(value, 0, 64, 0) or fi(value, 1, 64, 0).

Summary: Incorrect VHDL test bench generated for large vector constants of type uint64 or int64
Description: Incorrect test bench code is generated when the following conditions are met:  The TargetLanguage  is VHDL  for makehdltb . One of the outputs to DUT need to hold its value as a constant vector throughout the simulation time. Scalar outputs give the correct test bench. One of the constant vector elements have to be a value that cannot be represented using the double  datatype (for e.g. X"FFFFFFFF00000001 "). The constant vector must not be a fixed point data type (fixdt or fi).
Workaround: Use fixed point data types when working with int64 or uint64 types.

Summary: Crash on update diagram when bus object in data dictionary uses enumerated type not visible to all models in hierarchy
Description: MATLAB will crash when you perform an update diagram if the following conditions are true: You have a model hierarchy with at least 3 levels (e.g. top.slx->mid.slx->bot.slx). There are two or more sub-models that link to data dictionaries. You have an enumerated data type defined in a data dictionary and this type is not visible to all sub-models. A sub-model uses a bus object in a data dictionary and this bus refers to the enumerated data type.
Workaround: Create a new data dictionary to share across the sub-models.Move the enumerated type definition to the dictionary.For each sub-model that links to a data dictionary, create a reference from the model's data dictionary to this new data dictionary.?

Summary: Phased Array System Toolbox app?Sensor Array Analyzer errors on?launching
Description: MATLAB might throw an error and become unresponsive when you try to launch the Sensor Array Analyzer app if you do not have certain products installed in addition to Phased Array System Toolbox. Unable to resolve the name matlabshared.application.layout.ScrollableGridBagLayout.?
Workaround: You can work around this issue by installing the Antenna Toolbox, RF Toolbox, or Communication Toolbox in addition to the Phased Array System Toolbox.?

Summary: MATLAB might crash while extending existing test cases with root-level Bus inports
Description: MATLAB crash occurs when extending the existing test cases during the test-generation analysis in the following scenario:The model has at least one root-level Bus inport. The bus contains at least one bus element with Complexity  as complex .  Stack Trace (from fault): [? 0] 0x00007fafe104eb02 std::vector<DV::AnalysisInterface, std::allocator<DV::AnalysisInterface> >::size() const  [? 1] 0x00007fafe104f873 DV::MX::CGTestCaseFromMx::initFromMxArray(DV::Results&, mxArray_tag*, mxArray_tag*, mxArray_tag*)  [? 2] 0x00007fafe1049c44 DV::ExternalLoader::Analyzer::load()?  [? 3] 0x00007fafe101c52c DV::DvInternal::loadTestCases(mxArray_tag*)  [? 4] 0x00007fafe11a6b5e TestComponent::loadTestCases(mxArray_tag*)  [? 5] 0x00007fafe119878a TestComponentloadTestCasesMI::invokeMethod(UDMethodSignature*, int*, void**, int, void**)

Summary: MATLAB crashes when loading and starting a model that uses dashboard blocks on a real-time target
Description: MATLAB crashes when loading and starting a Simulink model that has connected dashboard blocks on a Simulink Real-Time target. The crash stack includes:? sl_hmi.dll+00985532 sl_hmi::DashboardBlock::handleModelStart+00002108   Simulink might issue this warning prior to the crash: Warning: Dashboard blocks are only supported in External mode.?
Workaround: Ensure the model window is closed on the host machine before starting the model on the Simulink Real-Time target.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The cordictanh function returns incorrect values for certain unsigned fixed-point inputs.
Description: For fixed-point inputs, the cordictanh  function uses the data type of the first input argument to determine the data type of intermediate variables. Because some of these variables undergo repeated subtraction,? they can become negative, resulting in incorrect outputs.

Summary: Crossing point of two linearly interpolated signals might not be detected correctly when evaluating temporal assessments
Description: When comparing linearly interpolated signals in a temporal assessment, the time point when the signal becomes greater (or less) than the other signal might not be detected properly. When this happens the overall result of the assessment might silently pass (or fail) causing a test case to silently pass (or fail). This behavior is specific to linearly interpolated signals and does not occur when comparing signals with 'zoh' interpolation.

Summary: MATLAB might crash with masked library blocks having parameter promotion
Description: A Simulink block linked to a masked library block having promoted parameters might cause MATLAB to crash. This behavior is common with promoted parameters having datatype mismatch.  Stack trace snippet:?  Assertion in void __cdecl PromotedParamMgr::add(const class SLBlock *,const class SLPrmDesc *) at l:\bmasklib\matlab\src\simulinkmask\engine\slpromoteprmdesc.cpp line 1143:  true == bIsStyleSame || m_PromotedParamPtrMap.empty()?  [? 5] 0x00007ffcd64253ab PromotedParamMgr::add at l:\bmasklib\matlab\src\simulinkmask\engine\slpromoteprmdesc.cpp:1146 (in L:\Bmasklib\matlab\bin\win64\SimulinkMask.dll)  [? 6] 0x00007ffcd642694a SLPrmPromoteState::addBlock at l:\bmasklib\matlab\src\simulinkmask\engine\slpromoteprmdesc.cpp:1514 (in L:\Bmasklib\matlab\bin\win64\SimulinkMask.dll)  [? 7] 0x00007ffcd62b5a4a SLMask::addPromotedParameter at l:\bmasklib\matlab\src\simulinkmask\engine\slmask.cpp:5928 (in L:\Bmasklib\matlab\bin\win64\SimulinkMask.dll)  [? 8] 0x00007ffcd62b5989 SLMask::addPromotedParameters at l:\bmasklib\matlab\src\simulinkmask\engine\slmask.cpp:5909 (in L:\Bmasklib\matlab\bin\win64\SimulinkMask.dll)  [? 9] 0x00007ffcfd24924a ReferenceBlockInternal::AddPromotedParametersForASubsystemBlock at b:\matlab\src\sl_libraries\update_reference\referenceblock.cpp:2100 (in L:\Bmasklib\matlab\bin\win64\sl_libraries.dll)  [ 10] 0x00007ffcfd24924a ReferenceBlockInternal::AddPromotedParametersForASubsystemBlock at b:\matlab\src\sl_libraries\update_reference\referenceblock.cpp:2100 (in L:\Bmasklib\matlab\bin\win64\sl_libraries.dll)  [ 11] 0x00007ffcfd24f2c4 ReferenceBlockInternal::LibraryBlockCopyToGraph at b:\matlab\src\sl_libraries\update_reference\referenceblock.cpp:2080 (in L:\Bmasklib\matlab\bin\win64\sl_libraries.dll)

Summary: Simulink Web View model might not show the Microsoft Word document included in the DocBlock
Description: When a Simulink Web View model is opened using Internet Explorer version 11, double-clicking a DocBlock  does not open a dialog to download the associated Word document.
Workaround: Open the Simulink Web View model with a different web browser, such as the MATLAB web browser, Firefox, or Google Chrome.

Summary: Incorrect error message generated when mask parameters that are partitioned?inside a For Each subsystem
Description: HDL code generation emits an incorrect error message: Generate parameterized HDL code from masked subsystem is not supported when the data type of the mask parameter is not scalar?  When the following conditions are true:? The model contains a For Each subsystem has mask parameters with?Partition? set to?on? in the?Parameter Partition ?tab of the Block Parameters dialog box The mask parameter is non-scalar  ?The error message is generated even when MaskParameterAsGeneric  is off .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect initialization in generated code for root Outport block driven by Merge or Model block in JMAAB-B model
Description: Generated code may be incorrect under?the following conditions: The model contains a root Inport block with parameter Output function call  selected that is connected to an Asynchronous Task Specification block. The combination of these two blocks constitutes the modeling style known as JMAAB-B. The function-call signal from the output of the root Inport block connects to a destination block that indirectly connects to a root Outport block. The immediate source of the root Outport block is either a Merge block or a Model block.  Under these conditions the root Outport block may have an incorrect initial value in generated code.

Summary: createComponentAsModel?import fails when component and runnable have the same name
Description: When using the AUTOSAR function createComponentAsModel  to import an ARXML component description and create a Simulink model, if the component and one of its runnables have the same name, the import fails with this error: Error using arxml.importer/createComponentAsModel (line 120) Error at element 1 of first input to get_param: block_diagram does not have a parameter named 'BlockType'
Workaround: Before importing, check whether the ARXML component name is distinct from the names of its runnables. If necessary, you can temporarily rename a runnable that has the same name as its component for import and then revert the runnable name within the created Simulink model.

Summary: System Composer models with unsynced profiles may crash on cut/copy/paste/delete of components, ports, and connectors
Description: A System Composer model profile can become out of sync if the profile is edited after the model is closed.?On loading such a model, a diagnostic message pops up suggesting a 'Fix it' workflow for the model. If this suggestion is ignored, then MATLAB may crash on copying, pasting, or deleting model elements that have stereotypes defined in the out-of-sync profile.

Summary: Polyspace analysis stops with error message: Cannot allocate memory
Description: When you analyze a C++ application with Polyspace Code Prover, the analysis might stop during the intermediate language translation phase with an error message similar to this message: ****** GNU C++ 4.6 to intermediate language translation - 4.1.3 (P_ITFP) took x.yyreal, x.yyu + 0s****** GNU C++ 4.6 to intermediate language translation - 4.1.4 (P_CA)System error Cannot allocate memory (nomem: Cannot allocate memory)Error: unable to execute /local/MATLAB/R2019a_server/bin/glnxa64/ps_c_me (errno=12).

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: HDL code generated from a model having Delay block with non-Boolean Enable port might fail to compile
Description: The generated VHDL code from a Simulink model may fail to compile when the following conditions are met: The Simulink model contains an Integer Delay block that has an external Enable port The input to the external Enable port has a data type other than ufix1  or boolean
Workaround: Before generating HDL code, introduce a Data Type Conversion block at the input of the Enable port and convert the input to a boolean or ufix1 type.

Summary: Opening the Requirements Perspective on an architecture model returns an error when a requirement is linked to a component in an architecture view
Description: When you open the Requirements Perspective on an architecture model that has a requirement link to a component in a view, MATLAB displays the following error message: Warning: Error occurred while executing the listener callback for event ReqPerspectiveChange defined for class  slreq.app.PerspectiveManager: Error using slreq.utils.getRmiStruct (line 128)  slreq.getSrcId() does not support input of type systemcomposer.architecture.model.views.ComponentOccurrence
Workaround: Delete the requirement link in the Architecture Views Editor and reestablish the requirement link in the Composition Editor.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Range analysis using Fixed-Point Designer may return incorrect results for models containing stateful blocks on non-English MATLAB
Description: Range analysis using Fixed-Point Designer may return incorrect results when the following conditions are met: Non-English MATLAB The model uses Model blocks The model uses blocks that contain state (such as Data Store Memory blocks) There is a constant rate in the model (such as from a Constant block)  When these conditions are met, a replacement model may be generated for which the relative execution order does not match that of the original model. This leads to the replacement model having a difference in behavior from that of the original model.

Summary: Performance regression when transferring table or timetable data to or from Parallel Computing Toolbox workers
Description: In some circumstances, using table  or timetable  data with Parallel Computing Toolbox workers is significantly slower in R2019b compared to R2019a. This performance regression is most noticeable when returning many?table  or timetable  objects from a parfor  loop in a cell array. For example, the following parfor  loop is much slower in R2019b than in R2019a. N = 5000;out = cell(1, N);t = tic();parfor idx = 1:N    out{idx} = table(42);endtoc(t)

Summary: wvd?function produces incorrect time-vector output when 'NumTimePoints' is specified
Description: When the wvd  function computes the smoothed pseudo Wigner-Ville distribution with the 'NumTimePoints'  name value-pair, the time vector output contains a time range that depends on the value specified in 'NumTimePoints' . The time vector should span the whole signal duration.  For example,  wvd(sin(2*pi*(0:100)/4),100,'SmoothedPseudo','NumTimePoints',100)??% Displays half the correct signal duration of 1 second
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 4, R2020a

Summary: Cannot calibrate the Simulation 3D Fisheye Camera block image
Description: The Simulation 3D Fisheye Camera block image has an?astigmatism?distortion component that prevents calibration. Specifically, the?estimateFisheyeParameters? function cannot estimate a distortion component that the?undistortFisheyeImage  function can correct.
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: Closing MATLAB after running a Model Advisor check might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: Closing a MATLAB session after running the Model Advisor check Check optimization settings  might cause MATLAB to crash. This crash might occur when all of the following conditions are true:The model uses a data dictionary. The model's active configuration set is a reference to a configuration object in the data dictionary.?

Summary: Error when generating a Word report from a template that contains an equation field, grouped content, or a repeated content element
Description: If a template contains certain types of content controls, particularly equations, grouped content, or repeated content elements, the following error occurs when opening a document that uses the template: Error using mlreportgen.dom.Document/openTemplate hole element doesn't specify hole name.  In a Word document template, rich text content controls are used to create holes to be filled by a report generation program. The template can also contain other types of content controls, such as equations, drop-down menus, or citations. The error occurs because the report generation program treats all content controls as holes, instead of ignoring controls that are not rich text controls and generating them unchanged in the output.
Workaround: To avoid the error, in a Word template, do not include equation fields, grouped content, or repeated content elements.

Summary: State owner blocks in masked subsystem are not shown in the State Owner Selector Tree of the State Reader/Writer blocks
Description: Simulink blocks with states are not shown in the?State Owner Selector Tree ?of State Reader/Writer blocks? when the blocks are within a masked subsystem.
Workaround: User interfact workaround:1. Select the?state owner block in the masked subsystem, and right click and drag on it to open a menu;2. Select the item in the menu to create a State Reader/Writer, and cut/paste it in the I/R/T subsystem.Programmatic workaround:1. Select the?state owner block in a given masked subsystem, and get its path (called "ownerBlockPath") in MATLAB command line using "gcb";?2. Set the property "StateOwnerBlock" of a selected State Reader/Writer block using the following command? " set_param(gcb, "StateOwnerBlock", ownerBlockPath) ".

Summary: Unable to create characteristic objects for unpaired Bluetooth Low Energy devices on Windows 10 build 1903
Description: After creating a connection to an unpaired Bluetooth Low Energy device in MATLAB using the ble  function, creating a characteristic  object on the device returns an error stating that the device profile has changed. For example, executing the following code causes an error. >> b = ble('DemoDev')  b =  ??ble with properties:  ???????????????Name: "DemoDev" ????????????Address: "FFE5DBB8917F" ??????????Connected: 1 ???????????Services: [5ˇÁ2 table] ????Characteristics: [10ˇÁ5 table]  Show services and characteristics  >> c = b.characteristic('Heart Rate', 'Heart Rate Measurement')  Device profile has changed. Clear existing ble object with address FFE5DBB8917F and recreate it. ?
Workaround: To successfully create a characteristic object, you must pair your Bluetooth Low Energy device on Windows before using ble to connect to it in MATLAB. You can pair to a device in Windows Settings > Devices > Add Bluetooth or other device.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Math Function block with Function set to?hermitian generates incorrect HDL with matrix input
Description: The generated HDL code for the Math Function block with Function  set to hermitian  is incorrect when : The input to the block is a matrix that uses a fixed-point data type. In this case, a transpose is not performed but?only a complex conjugate. The input to the block is a matrix that uses a complex fixed-point data type. This results in HDL compilation error in ModelSim.

Summary: MATLAB crashes while using Simulink Quick Launch
Description: MATLAB might crash when you try to use the Quick Launch in Simulink. The generated stack trace contains a reference to getCompletionsImpl.

Summary: Unsupported data types in custom C code triggers assertion
Description: If there are any unsupported data types in the C code to be imported into Simulink to use in C Caller, MATLAB Function, MATLAB System blocks and in Stateflow chart, it will trigger an internal assertion.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 3, R2020a

Summary: Updates to stereotypes already applied to behavior models cause MATLAB to crash while trying to run analysis
Description: Changing the stereotype or its properties that have already been applied to behavior models does not update the behavior models correctly. This issue causes MATLAB to crash while trying to run analysis on the model.

Summary: Unable to launch Site Viewer when using custom terrain and buildings data.
Description: Site Viewer throws an error when launched with custom terrain and buildings data. The error message is "Unable to access terrain data 'myterrain'", where 'myterrain' is the name of the custom terrain data previously added using addCustomTerrain . The error occurs when siteviewer  is called with the terrain name and the name of an OpenStreetMap (OSM) file containing buildings data, such as: >> sv = siteviewer("Terrain","myterrain","Buildings","mybuildings.osm")  This bug affects Site Viewer in Antenna Toolbox or Communications Toolbox.?
Workaround: While this issue prevents Site Viewer from being launched with custom terrain and buildings data, Site Viewer may still be launched with buildings data and default terrain:>> sv = siteviewer("Buildings","mybuildings.osm")

Summary: Small memory leak in generated C++ shared libraries when accessed via MATLAB Data API
Description: C++ applications may leak a few bytes of memory on every evaluation of compiled MATLAB functions, if all of the following conditions are true: The compiled MATLAB functions were compiled using Visual Studio Compiler The compiled?MATLAB functions were called using MATLAB Data API  The leaked memory will not be released until the program finishes execution.

Summary: Function name or MATLAB statement is leaked when?feval or eval is called with C++ engine
Description: There is memory leak from feval  or eval  when called with C++ engine.? Only the function name or MATLAB statement is leaked.

Summary: Misleading error message issued if the SerDes IBIS-AMI Manager encounters build error during export.
Description: If the SerDes IBIS-AMI Manager experiences build errors during the export of a IBIS-AMI model, the Manager issues a misleading error to MATLAB? command prompt. The error message reads: extractHTMLText requires a Text_Analytics_Toolbox license.  Error in serdes.internal.rtwbuildWrapper (line 24)  ? ? ? ? msg = extractHTMLText(string(ex.message)+causeMessage);  Error in IbisAmiManager/ExportButtonPushed (line 812)  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? eval("serdes.internal.rtwbuildWrapper(buildTargets,app)")  Error using matlab.ui.control.internal.controller.ComponentController/executeUserCallback (line 356)  Error while evaluating Button PrivateButtonPushedFcn.?  The actual cause of the error is a user introduced issue in the Simulink? model.
Workaround: If you encounter the above error message while exporting a Simulink? model, use the following commands to manually build the .dll/.so portion of the IBIS-AMI model (replace untitled with the model name):rtwbuild(ˇ®untitled/TxˇŻ,'ForceTopModelBuild',true)rtwbuild(ˇ®untitled/RxˇŻ,'ForceTopModelBuild',true)This will allow you to see the actual error message(s) in the MATLAB? command prompt. Once you debug the error(s) and build the model error free, you can revert back to using the SerDes IBIS-AMI Manager export button for building your models.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation results for coding pattern involving While loop with empty body
Description: For MATLAB code that satisfies the following conditions: There exists a while  loop which does not contain any statements inside its body.?? The condition of the while  loop is a compound logical expression involving logical and '&& ' At least one of the logical subexpressions is a function-call with a side effect.  Simulation using MATLAB Function block, MATLAB System block, or MATLAB Function in Stateflow might produce incorrect results. In this situation, the simulation will behave like the while loop never executes or executes once. For an example please see 'issue_model_2093701.slx' in attached issue_2093701.zip. See also Bug Report ID 2067108 ?
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 1, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for Bus Assignment block in a model with the Perform in-place updates for Assignment and Bus Assignment blocks configuration parameter selected
Description: Generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions:The model contains a Bus Assignment block. The model configuration parameter Perform in-place updates for Assignment and Bus Assignment blocks  is selected. ? The input signal to be assigned by the Bus Assignment block feeds into a subsystem or a Stateflow chart. In the subsystem or stateflow block parameters dialog box, on the Code Generation  tab, the Function packaging  parameter is set to Auto  or Nonreusable function .
Workaround: Clear the Perform in-place updates for Assignment and Bus Assignment blocks parameter.

Summary: Edit-time checking dialog not appearing for custom checks that verify block parameter constraints
Description: When edit-time checking is enabled for custom checks that verify user-defined block parameter constraints, the Model Advisor correctly indicates, in the model editor, blocks that violate the check. However, when you select the warning icon, the edit-time dialog does not appear and the following error occurs: Error using edittime.util.getViolationsViolation class not defined for BlockConstraintViolationError in edittime.util.getViolationsError in slSingleSelectionActionConfigError in slSingleSelectionActionConfig - Show complete stack trace?

Summary: "Sync" prefix in Excel spreadsheet does not work correctly if specified for both input and output simulation data
Description: When running a test case that uses an Excel spreadsheet that has a "Sync" prefix, the test produces this error: Prefix 'sync' should not be specified for input signals.

Summary: Error when using C Caller blocks in a library model with Rapid Accelerator or Model Reference Accelerator.
Description: Simulink might produce an error or give incorrect answer while simulating a model in rapid accelerator mode or with model reference in accelerator mode when a C Caller block linked from a library model is used. The C Caller block will try to call the code specified in the Code Generation Custom Code  panel. The error may be of the form "unresolved external symbol".
Workaround: If the Code Generation Custom Code settings are empty in the library model, check the Use the same custom code settings as Simulation Target under Modeling > Simulation Custom Code Settings.If different code generation dependencies are needed, use normal mode simulation.

Summary: Generated code does not compile when an Outport signal name has special characters like '#'
Description: When a signal name contains special characters such as "#" and is associated with the Outport signal name by using the Model Data Editor, the code generater generates an invalid identifier name for the Outport. The resulting generated code is not compilable.
Workaround: Provide valid C identifiers to signal names associated with Outports by using the Model Data Editor.

Summary: RF Propagation functions throw indexing error for certain sites
Description: RF Propagation functions throw an error when used with sites at certain geographic locations. The error message is "Array indices must be positive integers or logical values." The error prevents the usage of the sites with Site Viewer or analysis functions like coverage  and sigstrength . This bug affects RF Propagation functions in Antenna Toolbox or Communications Toolbox.??
Workaround: This issue affects sites at particular geographic locations. Work around the issue by making a small change to the location of the problematic sites. For example, the following code moves the location of transmitter site tx by approximately 1 mm:>> tx.Latitude = tx.Latitude + 1e-8;>> tx.Longitude = tx.Longitude + 1e-8;Repeat the previous RF Propagation function call using the updated site object.

Summary: Favorited buttons in Simulink Toolstrip might not be saved immediately between MATLAB and Simulink sessions
Description: If a you mark a button as a favorite or reorganize your favorited buttons on the Simulink Toolstrip and close MATLAB, those favorites might not appear the next time you reopen Simulink unless the model is also saved.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when using readcell or readmatrix on text file
Description: When reading a file as text using readmatrix or readcell, MATLAB might crash. This is limited to the case where there are no expected variables in the file, which can happen if the format is unrecognized. readcell(filename)?  Or if using import options, the case is detectable. opts = detectImportOptions(filename)  numel(opts.VariableNames) == 0  readcell(filename,opts) % MATLAB might crash
Workaround: Provide one or more variables?opts = delimitedTextImportOptions('NumVariables',1);?readcell(filename,opts)?

Summary: downsample and upsample error out when the input argument is a datetime array
Description: The downsample  and upsample  functions error out when the input argument is a?datetime  array. Consider this example: >> DateStrings = {'2014-05-26';'2014-08-03';'2015-05-26';'2015-08-03';'2016-05-26';'2016-08-03'};  >> t = datetime(DateStrings,'InputFormat','yyyy-MM-dd');  >> y = downsample(t,2,1); Error using downsample (line 45)  Expected X to be either numeric, logical, or char datatype.

Summary: Simulation error when For Each Subsystem block contains Model block in SIL or PIL mode
Description: If your model has a?For Each Subsystem block that contains a Model block with Simulation mode  set to Software-in-the-loop (SIL)  or? Processor-in-the-loop (PIL) , running a simulation produces an error. For example:   Simulink does not support block 'ModelInSIL' inside subsystem 'ParentSystem' because the subsystem contains a For Each block ('ForEachSystem' )  The simulation does not produce an error if the Model block Simulation mode  parameter is set to?Normal  or  Accelerator .

Summary: When saving MATLAB live scripts (.mlx files), MATLAB Drive Connector may unexpectedly sync temporary files
Description: When MATLAB live scripts (.mlx files) are saved, associated, short-lived temporary files are generated within the same folder. These temporary files have names in the format xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx; for example, 69b6-01b7-2ab4-7d00. If MATLAB live scripts are saved in MATLAB Drive while MATLAB Drive Connector is syncing files, these temporary files may appear in the MATLAB Drive Connector Activity window as deleted files.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Lookup table with linear interpolation and custom data type for breakpoint results in mismatch in generated HDL code
Description: A mismatch might occur between the Simulink simulation behavior and the generated HDL code when:? An n-D Lookup Table block is used with Interpolation method  set to Linear  The breakpoint data type is changed from the default Inherit: Same as corresponding input . The issue arises when the chosen breakpoint type has less precision than the corresponding input.
Workaround: Set the breakpoint data type to Inherit: Same as corresponding input to avoid the issue. The generate HDL code in this case does not use additional resources.

Summary: Message period field in a CAN Transmit block is modifiable during simulation time
Description: A CAN Transmit block allows you to edit its Message period value during simulation time. Such edits should not be allowed during simulation.

Summary: Performance regression on Windows? for bitand with vector inputs of type double
Description: There is a performance regression on Windows platforms when bitand  operates on vectors of type double . For example, D = 1:6400;ticfor m = 1:1e5??? k = bitand(D,D);endtoc  In R2019a,  Elapsed time is 9.904751 seconds.  In R2019b, Elapsed time is 15.370698 seconds. The timings were done on a Windows 10, Intel? Xeon? CPU E5-1650 v3 @ 3.50GHz.

Summary: Assertion Error ' In getRule: Rule for expr not found' when generating code for a MATLAB function containing variable sized matrices
Description: When generating code for a MATLAB function containing variable-sized matrices, MATLAB might throw the following error: Assertion at dom_core/ lowering/ scalarize/ ExprToRuleMap.hpp line 36:In getRule: Rule for expr #struct_val(@@%P1,  $"size") not found

Summary: Crash during code generation for AUTOSAR adaptive model saved in R2019a or created from template
Description: MATLAB might crash when generating code for an AUTOSAR model if both of the following conditions are met: The model is configured for the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform. The configuration parameter System target file ?is set to autosar_adaptive.tlc  or a derived system target file. The model was saved in R2019a or was created from the AUTOSAR adaptive software component template.  The crash is caused by an unsupported AUTOSAR schema version, 00045 . The Configuration Parameters dialog box displays the value of Generate XML file for schema version  as 00046 (R18-10) , but the stored value is '00045' . You can?display the stored value by using this command: >> get_param(modelName,'AutosarSchemaVersion')
Workaround: Use this MATLAB command to correct the stored AUTOSAR schema version:>> set_param(modelName,'AutosarSchemaVersion','00046');

Summary: Double-clicking a program in the Documents section of the SimBiology Model Analyzer opens the program with missing tables
Description: When you open a program by double-clicking the program name in the Documents section of the Browser, the program opens but the tables in the program are initialized incorrectly. You might also see erroneous indicators on the program.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Solve method of simscape.multibody.KinematicsSolver class returns incorrect results when frame variables are oppositely aligned
Description: If a KinematicsSolver object contains two oppositely aligned frame variable groups, i.e., ?Group 1 has base frame F1 and follower frame F2?Group 2 has base frame F2 and follower frame F1? and variables in both groups are added to the set of outputs, then the values of the variables in Group 2 returned by the solve function are incorrect.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Memory section parameters not saved for linked subsystem block
Description: The following memory section parameters are not saved correctly for a linked subsystem block, if the value of these parameters in the corresponding library block is different from the default value. RTWMemSecFuncInitTerm? RTWMemSecFuncExecute RTWMemSecDataConstants RTWMemSecDataInternal RTWMemSecDataParameters

Summary: Keyboard shortcut to copy text from the MATLAB Help Browser does not work on Mac
Description: The keyboard shortcut to copy text,??-C, does not work in the MATLAB Help Browser on Mac.
Workaround: To copy the selected text, use the Copy command in the right-click context menu.

Summary: Duplicate display of links with custom link type
Description: A single link displays twice in the Links panel under the following conditions: A custom link type is defined A link specifies the customized link type A requirement which has the link is selected in Requirements browser  The link is incorrectly shown under a base type of the custom link type.

Summary: Run-time parameters in mechanisms with kinematic loops might cause memory corruption and crashes for code-generation targets?
Description: MATLAB might crash and corrupt memory or produce an error when generating code or performing a rapid accelerator mode simulation for a model that meets these conditions:The model must have a least one run-time parameter specified in a Simscape Multibody block. The run-time parameters that are specified in the Simscape Multibody block must also be in a kinematic loop.??  When a model meets the above conditions, this bug typically occurs when there are high-degree-freedom joints in the kinematic loop that have state targets, or for R2019b, when the low degree-of-freedom joints have the?Mode ?parameter set to?Provided by Input . When this bug produces an error, this is the message you get: Error occurred while executing External Mode MEX-file 'ext_comm':ExtTargetPktPending() call failed while checking??for target pktError occurred while executing External Mode MEX-file 'ext_comm':ExtSetTargetPkt() call failed on CLOSE.Ensure target is still running

Summary: Engine crashes without crash log when attached Simulink creates an error message
Description: The engine session crashes without a crash log. This command in Python engine shows the behavior. >>> eng.set_param('SimulinkModel','ErrorCommand','Update', nargout=0)Traceback (most recent call last):  File "", line 1, in   File "C:\Python2.7.16\lib\site-packages\matlab\engine\matlabengine.py", line 71, in __call___stderr, feval=True).result()  File "C:\Python2.7.16\lib\site-packages\matlab\engine\futureresult.py", line 67, in result    return self.__future.result(timeout)  File "C:\Python2.7.16\lib\site-packages\matlab\engine\fevalfuture.py", line 82, in result    self._result = pythonengine.getFEvalResult(self._future,self._nargout, None, out=self._out, err=self._err)matlab.engine.EngineError: MATLAB function cannot be evaluated

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong answers in strided transposed and backward convolution with large filters on GPU
Description: Transposed and backward convolutions produce wrong results when the filter size is larger than 16, the stride is larger than 1 in at least one dimension and executed on GPU.
Workaround: For convolutions layers with filter size larger than 16 and stride larger than 1:layers = [? ? ....? ? convolution2dLayer(filterSize, noOfFilters, 'Name', 'conv', 'Weights', weights, 'Bias', bias, 'Padding', padding, 'DilationFactor', dilationFactor, 'Stride', stride)? ? ... ];Remove the stride from the convolution layer and add subsequent max pooling layer with the original stride and a unit window as follows:layers_workaround = [? ? ...?? ? convolution2dLayer(filterSize, noOfFilters, 'Name', 'conv', 'Weights', weights, 'Bias', bias, 'Padding', padding, 'DilationFactor', dilationFactor)? ? maxPooling2dLayer(1, 'Name', 'maxpool_workaround', 'Stride', stride)? ? ... ];?For dlconv method with filter size larger than 16 and stride larger than 1Y = dlconv(X, weights, bias, 'Padding', padding, 'DilationFactor', dilationFactor, 'Stride', stride);Remove the stride from dlconv and add subsequent maxpool method with the original stride and a unit window as follows:Y = dlconv(X, weights, bias, 'Padding', padding, 'DilationFactor', dilationFactor);Y = maxpool(Y, 1, 'Stride', stride);For transposed convolutions layers with filter size larger than 16 and stride larger than 1layers = [? ? ...? ? transposedConv2dLayer(filterSize, noOfFilters, 'Name', 'transpConv', 'Weights', weights, 'Bias', bias, 'Cropping', cropping, 'Stride', stride)? ? ... ];Remove the stride from the convolution layer and add precedent transposed convolution with the original stride, and weights and cropping as follows:weights_workaround = zeros([stride inputChannels, inputChannels]);weights_workaround(1,1,:,:) = eye(inputChannels,inputChannels);cropping_workaround = [stride(1)-1 0 0 stride(2)-1]layers_workaround = [? ? ...? ? transposedConv2dLayer(stride, inputChannels, 'Name', 'transpConv_workaround', 'Stride', stride, 'Weights', weights_workaround, 'Cropping', cropping_workaround)? ? transposedConv2dLayer(filterSize, noOfFilters, 'Name', 'transpConv', 'Weights', weights, 'Bias', bias, 'Cropping', cropping)];? ? ... ];For dltranspconv method with filter size larger than 16 and stride larger than 1Y = dltranspconv(X, weights, bias, 'Cropping', cropping, 'DilationFactor', dilationFactor, 'Stride', stride);Remove the stride from dltranspconv and add precedent transposed convolution with the original stride, and weights and cropping as follows:weights_workaround = zeros([stride inputChannels, inputChannels]);weights_workaround(1,1,:,:) = eye(inputChannels,inputChannels);cropping_workaround = [0 0; stride-1];Y_intermediate = dltranspconv(X, weights_workaround, 0, 'Stride', stride, 'Cropping', cropping_workaround);Y_workaround = dltranspconv(Y_intermediate, weights, bias, 'Cropping', cropping, 'DilationFactor', dilationFactor);Alternatively, if you are using the transposed convolution for upsampling, consider using max unpooling operation instead of transposed convolution to increase the size of feature maps.

Summary: Incorrect ARXML generated for export function model when Inport feeds both IRT Function and another runnable
Description: An AUTOSAR model generates incorrect ARXML when the following conditions are true: The model uses the export function modeling style. An Inport signal branches, and one branch of the Inport feeds an Initialize, Reset, or Terminate (IRT) Function. Another branch of the Inport signal is connected to a Function-Call Subsystem or another IRT Function.  The ARXML description generated for the runnable mapped to the IRT Function does not describe the variable access corresponding to the branched Inport. For AUTOSAR implicit data access modes, an RTE generator will not generate the macro required by the generated code. This also causes Simulink SIL or PIL mode to fail for implicit data access with an error message similar to the following: modelName.obj : error LNK2019: unresolved external symbol Rte_IRead_Runnable_Init_In1_In1 referenced in function Runnable_Init .\modelName.exe : fatal error LNK1120: 1 unresolved externals

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: cross returns complex conjugated results for complex inputs
Function-call does not execute inside a Simulink based state in a Stateflow chart in Accelerator mode
Description: When you run your model in Accelerator mode, Stateflow does not execute the function-call output signal of a block that is placed inside a Simulink based state.
Workaround: Choose any simulation mode other than Accelerator mode.

Summary: C++ code generated for AUTOSAR adaptive application fails to compile due to missing default or copy constructor for SampleAllocateePtr class
Description: C++ code generated for an AUTOSAR adaptive application fails to compile when the following conditions are true: The model contains a root outport that sends an event. The AllocateMemory  property is set to true for the root outport. Your ara::com  implementation deletes the copy constructor or default constructor for the SampleAllocateePtr  class.

Summary: Under certain conditions, arrays greater than 14KB but smaller than 16KB take longer to clear from the workspace than normal on Microsoft Windows platforms.
Description: Under certain conditions, arrays greater than 14KB but smaller than 16KB take longer to clear from the workspace than normal on Microsoft Windows platforms.

Summary: The lte3DChannel?channel model incorrectly models the?orientation of the antenna arrays.
Description: The?TransmitAntennaArray.Orientation ?and?ReceiveAntennaArray.Orientation ?properties of the?lte3DChannel ?channel model control the mechanical orientation of the transmit and receive antenna arrays, respectively. However, changing these property values does not change the orientation of the antenna arrays correctly and may lead to silent wrong conclusions.??

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect Variant condition propagation with operation on BusElements
Description: Variant condition propagation fails when all the below conditions are satisfied  An operation (example: lookup table  or Add  block) with two inputs.  The inputs being the output signals of a Bus Selector .  The output of the operation is fed to a Variant Source block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: GPU Coder might generate incorrect code if a function has loops with variable unsigned bounds
Description: GPU Coder might generate incorrect code for a function which satisfies all of the following conditions: ?1) The function should have one or more unbounded loops.? ?2) The index value of the above loop evaluates to an unsigned type.? The following function illustrates this:? function y = foo(u,v) y= uint8(ones(255,1)); a = uint8(v);  b = uint8(u); for i=b:a % loop with unsigned integer variable bounds    y(i,1) = uint8(i*i); end? end?  In the above example, if b > a, generated code can produce incorrect value.?

Summary: Shared constants for a model might be generated in const_params.c file when GenerateSharedConstants?parameter is off
Description: The generated code for a model might pool shared constants in the?const_params.c  file in the _sharedutils directory even though GenerateSharedConstants  model configuration? parameter is set to off .

Summary: On macOS 10.15 Catalina computer, simulation of a Simulink Desktop Real-Time model might cause computer restart
Description: After operating system upgrade to macOS 10.15 Catalina, simulation of a Simulink Desktop Real-Time model in external mode might cause the computer to restart or shutdown.

Summary: Delay while setting new device in CAN Configuration block using set_param
Description: There is delay of 5 to 6 seconds when setting a new device in a CAN Configuration block using the set_param  function.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when generating HDL code for models with multiple sin and cos blocks driven by the same signal
Description: MATLAB might crash when?multiple sin and cos blocks?are driven by the same signal and?Floating Point IP Library  is set to Native Floating Point  in the HDL Code Generation > Floating Point  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.

Summary: "MATLABR2019b" name changes to "MATLAB" in Add or Remove Programs afteran update
Description: Updating a MATLABinstallation changes the Add or Remove Programs entry name on Windows platforms from ˇ°MATLAB R2019bˇ± to ˇ°MATLAB.ˇ±

Summary: When the architecture model has requirement links to ports, entering the Requirements Perspective returns an error
Description: If you try to enter Requirements Perspective from your architecture model that has existing requirement links to ports, MATLAB returns the following error message in the Command Window: Warning: Error occurred while executing the listener callback for event ReqPerspectiveChange defined for classslreq.app.PerspectiveManager:Unrecognized method, property, or field 'p_Occurrence' for class'systemcomposer.architecture.model.design.ArchitecturePort'.?

Summary: MATLAB might crash while loading an AUTOSAR model that has mapped signals
Description: MATLAB might crash while loading an AUTOSAR model if both of the following conditions exist: The model was saved using MATLAB R2018b, R2019a or R2019b. The model maps signals to AUTOSAR variables.  One example is when the model has AUTOSAR mapping for signals associated with library blocks, and the model is saved before the library models have been loaded.

Summary: MATLAB crashes on some Linux systems at startup
Description: On some Linux systems, MATLAB crashes at startup when the preferences folder is read-only.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, make the MATLAB preferences folder writable. On Linux systems, for R2019b, the default preferences folder location is?~/.matlab/R2019b.

Summary: Update backlinks action fails when applied to links from Simulink Requirements items to external documents (Word/Excel/DOORS)
Description: Update backlinks  does not work correctly when linking with?entries in?Requirements Sets (.slreqx files).  No error is displayed, and the displayed popup reports the correct number of newly inserted links, but the URLs?embedded in external documents are?incorrect, and on-click navigation fails.

Summary: Broken links from Requirements Editor to DOORS
Description: After?creating a link from a requirement in the Requirements Editor to an object in IBM Rational DOORS by?using the Outgoing Links dialog,?the new link appears in the Links panel at bottom-right. However, the hyperlink does not work correctly, that is, the on-click navigation fails.
Workaround: If you switch into Links View and locate the corresponding link in the left panel, and then click on the destination hyperlink in the Properties pane on the right, navigation works correctly.

Summary: Opening the ex_RobotArch_props model from the System Composer documentation results in an error
Description: Opening the ex_RobotArch_props model referenced in Inspect Components in Custom Views ?from?Getting Started with System Composer ?results in one or both of these errors: Unexpected exception (SystemComposerViews must be set to 1 before creating an architecture model) has been found.  and/or Error using systemcomposer.architecture.model.design.Architecture/isVariantArchitecture. Invalid or deleted object.

Summary: The variant column of the Diagram browser in the SimBiology app does not display variant values
Description: The variant column of the Diagram browser does not display the variant values when the variant content Type ("species" , "parameter" , or "compartment" ) is not spelled all lower-case.? For example, the issue occurs if you specify "Species"  instead of "species" .?

Summary: Turn Coordinator andArtificial Horizon blocks sporadically?display incorrect values whenconnected to output signals?from the same block
Description: When?the Turn Coordinator and Artificial Horizon?blocks are connected to output signals?from the same block, they sporadically display each other's?values.

Summary: Banners and trailers missing from shared utility code generated by parallel building of referenced models
Description: Parallel building of referenced models generates shared utility code (for example, rtwtypes.h ) that does not have banners and trailers. The missing items can include custom banners and trailers that you specify through a code generation template file.

Summary: solve can return an incorrect objective function value
Description: In rare cases, the problem-based optimization solver solve  can return an incorrect objective function value.? Specifically, if the objective starts as a sum of squares, the constant term might be added twice to the final objective function value. For example, this simple minimization problem has a?minimum at x = 0 , y = 0 , with f = 9 . min f(x,?y) = x^2 + y^2 + 9 + x^3  with the constraint x >= 0. However, the following code erroneously returns f = 18 : >> x = optimvar('x', 'LowerBound', 0);>>?y = optimvar('y');>>?p = optimproblem;>>?p.Objective = x^2 + y^2 + 9 + x^3;>>?x0.x = 1; x0.y = 1;>>?[sol, f] = solve(p, x0);>>?f?  f =  ? ?18.0000
Workaround: Obtain the objective function value by using evaluate?on the objective function expression at the solution sol.>> evaluate(p.Objective,sol)ans =? ? 9.0000

Summary: Resizing the figure or container components in GUIDE while the Property Inspector is open causes GUIDE to throw an error: There is no Units property on the AnnotationPane class.
Description: If you open the Property Inspector in GUIDE and then resize the figure or a container component, GUIDE returns this error: Error using getThere is no Units property on the AnnotationPane class.Error in guidefunc>updateChildrenWhenResize (line 2765)????if ~strcmpi(get(children(i), 'Units'), ?'normalized')Error in guidefunc> localSetPixelPos (line 2047)????updateChildrenWhenResize(obj, [where(3)-size(3) where(4)-size(4)]);Error in guidefunc> moveGobject (line 2208)localSetPixelPos(varargin{1}, varargin{4});Error in guidefunc (line 70)  ? ??varargout=moveGobject(varargin{:});  For example, scenarios like these cause GUIDE to error in this way: Create an app in GUIDE Open the Property Inspector for the figure Resize the figure  Or: Create an app in GUIDE Add a panel to the app by dragging one into the GUIDE layout area Drag a component, such as a check box, into the panel Open the Property Inspector for the newly added component (in this case, the check box) Resize the panel?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated PLC code might not have initial values for non-tunable parameters of structure type
Description: Structure type nontunable parameters in the base workspace are supported with PLC code generation. However, if these parameters are generated with struct data type in the generated code, the initial values are missing.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: SimBiology.Scenarios objects imported into the SimBiology Model Analyzer app might produce different results in a datasheet or plot
Description: If you do not set the RandomSeed  property on a SimBiology.Scenarios  object, the object produces different sample values every time you run the generate  function to compute samples. If you import one of these objects into the SimBiology Model Analyzer app, each time you reopen a project or create a new datasheet or plot, the sample values from the imported SimBiology.Scenarios  are different.
Workaround: Instead of importing SimBiology.Scenarios objects into the SimBiology Model Analyzer app, use the Generate Samples program or step to generate simulation scenarios. To inspect samples of a SimBiology.Scenarios object at the command line, use the generate function.

Summary: MATLAB can crash when importing ReqIF files with real number properties
Description: Certain ReqIF files with real number properties could crash MATLAB. To determine if a real number is the problem, open the ReqIF file with a text editor and search for xml tags   ATTRIBUTE-VALUE-REAL, or ATTRIBUTE-DEFINITION-REAL.    ?If the ReqIF file contains such tags, and Simulink Requirement crashes when importing the ReqIF file, apply this fix to allow importing such files.

Summary: The Flux-Based PM Controller block incorrectly calculates the estimated motor torque
Description: The Flux-Based PM Controller block incorrectly calculates the estimated motor torque. Under the block mask, in the Current Controller subsystem, the FluxdSyn  and FluxqSyn  signals that are input to the Calculate Torque subsystem are incorrect. The signals should be switched.
Workaround: To work around the issue, consider switching the?FluxdSyn and?FluxqSyn signals input to the Calculate Torque subsystem. You might need to resolve broken library links.

Summary: Display block shows strings that include new line characters incorrectly
Description: When the input connected to a Display block is a string that includes a new line character, the contents do not display correctly.
Workaround: ?After running the simulation, change the font to any other font to see the string value. To change the font:Right-click the block.Pause on Format in the context menu.Select Font Style for Selection...Choose a new font and click OK.

Summary: System Composer models and data dictionaries saved in R2019a fail to load in R2019b
Description: System Composer models and data dictionaries saved in R2019a might return errors when loaded in R2019b and in some cases cause MATLAB to crash. Following are sample errors that might be returned:  Type 'systemcomposer.architecture.model.views.OccurrenceCatalog' has not been found in?the registry.  Error using systemcomposer.architecture.model.design.Architecture/ isVariantArchitecture. Invalid or deleted object.  Following is a sample stack generated when MATLAB crashes: Stack Trace (from fault):  [? 0] 0x00007f7561883800 R2019b/simulink_plugins/sysarch_app/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwsystemcomposer_architecture_model.so+04397056 _ZN14systemcomposer12architecture5model9interface16InterfaceCatalog19getInterfaceCatalogERKN2mf4zero5ModelEb+00000048  [? 1] 0x00007f7562838502 R2019b/simulink_plugins/sysarch_app/libmwsysarch_app.so+01041666  [? 2] 0x00007f756283a752 R2019b/simulink_plugins/sysarch_app/libmwsysarch_app.so+01050450  [? 3] 0x00007f7562833cc8 R2019b/simulink_plugins/sysarch_app/libmwsysarch_app.so+01023176  [? 4] 0x00007f7562834c62 R2019b/simulink_plugins/sysarch_app/libmwsysarch_app.so+01027170  [? 5] 0x00007f7562835f89 R2019b/simulink_plugins/sysarch_app/libmwsysarch_app.so+01032073  [? 6] 0x00007f7562837c04 R2019b/simulink_plugins/sysarch_app/libmwsysarch_app.so+01039364  [? 7] 0x00007f75628a829d R2019b/simulink_plugins/sysarch_app/libmwsysarch_app.so+01499805  [? 8] 0x00007f75628b33c4 R2019b/simulink_plugins/sysarch_app/libmwsysarch_app.so+01545156  [? 9] 0x00007f75627e2f74 R2019b/simulink_plugins/sysarch_app/libmwsysarch_app.so+00692084?
Workaround: To use R2019a System Composer models and data dictionaries in R2019b, download the attached files and update the models and data dictionaries. Read the documentation in fileList.txt prior to using the patch scripts.For any questions regarding the patch scripts, please contact MathWorks Technical Support.

Summary: CAN pack and unpack blocks do not accept workspace variables for setting MsgLength
Description: The following blocks do not accept workspace variables for setting the value of the MsgLength  parameter: CAN Pack CAN Unpack CAN FD Pack CAN FD Unpack

Summary: MATLAB assertion error or crash when generating code for a modeling pattern with if-elseif-if decision logic with large literal values
Description: MATLAB might produce an unexpected assertion error or crash when generating code for a modeling pattern that meets all of these conditions: It contains?if-elseif-if  decision logic. The condition of one of the if  or elseif  statements contains a literal larger than a 32 bit signed integer. The code generation parameter Convert if-elseif-else patterns to switch-case statements  is enabled.?  When this issue occurs the assertion or crash message contains the following text: In toNativeType: The value 'DataValue{ type={mode='fixpt' signed=false bits=32 slope=1*2^0 bias=0} storage=[SNIP],0,0,0}' could not be converted into requested type {mode='fixpt' signed=true bits=32 slope=1*2^0 bias=0}.' failed? This issue affects the following Embedded Coder workflows MATLAB to C/C++ Code? Simulink to C/C++ Code for models containing flow chart in a Stateflow Chart, a MATLAB function in a Stateflow Chart or a MATLAB Function block  See the attached issue_2082667.zip for example modeling patterns.

Summary: Dashboard blocks may stop working when you display port values for the connected signal
Description: When a Dashboard block is connected to a signal that is not logged, the Dashboard block stops working if you display port values for the signal during simulation.?
Workaround: To continue visualizing the signal using the Dashboard block, you can either:Turn off port value displayMark the signal for logging before running the simulation
