Description: MATLAB might crash generating code from a MATLAB function that meets the following conditions: The? MATLAB function contains an expression that involves indexing into a cell array with variable-size elements. The indexing expression contains unknown bounds or a variable step size.  When MATLAB crashes following assertion message and stack may be displayed: Assertion at dom_core/analysis/dependenceAnalysis/DependenceAnalysis.cpp line 1238:   In weakZeroSivTest: Assertion failed: iST && iST->isSimpleConstant()  Stack Trace (captured): [SNIP] [? 5] 0x00007fcc07195342 /local-ssd/install_/bin/glnxa64/builtins/coder_core/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwemlcoder.so+02085698 [? 6] 0x00007fcc06d959e9 /local-ssd/install_/bin/glnxa64/builtins/coder_core/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcg_ir.so+07068137 _Z23client_assertion_failedPKciS0_+00000025 [SNIP] [ 10] 0x00007fcc033983b4 /local-ssd/install_/bin/glnxa64/builtins/coder_core/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcgir_support.so+01999796 _ZN2CG8analysis18DependenceAnalyzer15getDependenciesERNS0_10AccessDescES3_+00000020 [SNIP] [ 17] 0x00007fcc02bc5b35 /local-ssd/install_/bin/glnxa64/builtins/coder_core/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcgir_xform.so+08837941 _ZNK2CG9transform39ScalarReplacementWithDependenceAnalysis10processFcnEPNS_3FcnE+00000037 [ 18] 0x00007fcc03559d64 /local-ssd/install_/bin/glnxa64/builtins/coder_core/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcgir_support.so+03841380 _ZNK2CG12FcnTransform5applyEPNS_3FcnERKNS_7ServiceE+00000228 [ 19] 0x00007fcc03556f93 /local-ssd/install_/bin/glnxa64/builtins/coder_core/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcgir_support.so+03829651 _ZNK2CG21CompositeFcnTransform10processFcnEPNS_3FcnE+00000067 [ 20] 0x00007fcc03559d64 /local-ssd/install_/bin/glnxa64/builtins/coder_core/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcgir_support.so+03841380 _ZNK2CG12FcnTransform5applyEPNS_3FcnERKNS_7ServiceE+00000228 [ 21] 0x00007fcc03559e5a /local-ssd/install_/bin/glnxa64/builtins/coder_core/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcgir_support.so+03841626 _ZNK2CG12FcnTransform23processScopeWithServiceEPNS_5ScopeERKNS_7ServiceE+00000218 [ 22] 0x00007fcc0355b5c7 /local-ssd/install_/bin/glnxa64/builtins/coder_core/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcgir_support.so+03847623 _ZNK2CG14ScopeTransform5applyEPNS_5ScopeERKNS_7ServiceE+00000167 [ 23] 0x00007fcc02bc5508 /local-ssd/install_/bin/glnxa64/builtins/coder_core/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcgir_xform.so+08836360 _ZNK2CG9transform32ScalarReplacementTransformDriver23processScopeWithServiceEPNS_5ScopeERKNS_7ServiceE+00000104

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Multidimensional root Inport associated to signal object and connected to S-Function might generate incorrect code
Description: Incorrect code might be generated when all the following conditions are met:? Root Inport is connected to S-Function block. The output signal of that root Inport is associated to a signal object (for example, Simulink.Signal ) The signal object has any storage class except "Auto " and "Model default ". Preserve array dimensions  parameter is selected for the signal object.
Workaround: Disable dimension preservation for the root Inports feeding to S-Function blocks.

Summary: Setting the scope of a port to 'Parameter' in a C Caller block causes MATLAB to crash
Description: If the scope of a Caller block port is changed to 'Parameter', MATLAB crashes when 'OK' or 'Apply' button on the Block Parameters dialog is clicked.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Fixed-point numeric type bias resets to 0 on update diagram if used in an evenly spaced breakpoint of lookup table object
Description: Simulink resets the bias value of a Simulink.NumericType object to zero on update diagram when the following conditions are true: The Simulink.NumericType  object or a Simulink.AliasType  object referencing the Simulink.NumericType  object is used as the data type for the breakpoints of a Simulink.LookupTable  object The Simulink.LookupTable  object?Breakpoints specification ?is set to?Even spacing  The? Simulink.NumericType  object has a bias value that is nonzero  This bug impacts anything in the model that references the Simulink.NumericType  object, since the object itself is changed.
Workaround: Replace the Simulink.NumericType object with a Simulink.AliasType object where the data type is an explicitly specified fixed-point type instead of a reference to a numeric type. For example:>>mytype = Simulink.AliasType('fixdt(1,16,2^0,1)');?
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 3, R2020a

Summary: MATLAB sporadically crashes when generating code for a Simulink model containing MATLAB Function or Stateflow blocks
Description: MATLAB might crash when you generate code for a Simulink model containing MATLAB Function or Stateflow blocks. The crash report refers to sf_cg_ctx_destroy. On Windows, the call stack is similar to: [? 1] 0x... cg_ctx (in cg_ir.dll)[? 2] 0x... SF::sf_cg_ctx_destroy (in stateflow.dll)[? 3] 0x... SF::cdr_construct_context (in stateflow.dll)[? 4] 0x... SF::Cg (in stateflow.dll)

Summary: The velodyneFileReader function returns incorrect values for the final frame status and the end-time values
Description: When processing large PCAP point cloud files on a Windows-based platform, the  velodyneFileReader   function returns incorrect values for the final frame status and end time. The  hasFrame method returns  true  instead of false   after the function reads the last frame, and the StartTime   property value is greater than the   EndTime property value.

Summary: Missing code generation report files documenting C or C++ source code on multi-byte paths
Description: When you generate a code generation report on a path that contains multi-byte characters (for example,??? in Japanese), the HTML files that document the source code are not generated.
Workaround: Generate the report to a location that does not have a multi-byte character in the path.

Summary: MATLAB Client for MATLAB Production Server does not support varargin or varargout
Description: MATLAB functions that use varargin  or varargout  can be deployed to MATLAB Production Server. Attempting to call these functions using the MATLAB Client for MATLAB Production Server raises the error "Variable output array "varargout" must be a cell array. "
Workaround: To work around this problem, rewrite the MATLAB function to use cell arrays instead of varargin or varargout.For example, consider a function that returns the sum of a variable number of inputs: function n = sumAll(varargin)    n =  sum([ varargin{:} ]);Call this function from MATLAB:>> n = sumAll(2,3,4,5,6);To deploy this function to MATLAB Production Server so that it can be called by the MATLAB Client for MATLAB Production Server, rewrite the function to use a cell array input instead of varargin:function n = sumAll(numbers)    n = sum([ numbers{:} ]);Then call the function with a cell array:>> n = sumAll( { 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 } );

Summary: An exception is thrown when changing options in the Publish settings pane
Description: When changing any publish setting by using the Publish setting  pane (such as the Outputfile format  setting), an exception is thrown in thread "AWT-EventQueue-0": java.lang.ClassCastException. The Publish setting  pane stops working. It is still possible to publish, but any settings change throws another exception.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when loading model configured with C code mappings and saved with an earlier release of MATLAB
Description: MATLAB can crash when the following conditions are true: You used R2018a, R2018b, or R2019a of Simulink to create and save a model. Model Includes blocks that are linked to a block library. Model is configured with C code mappings. You use R2019b of Simulink to load the model.

Summary: Some docked Simulink components fail to render after being hidden and restored
Description: Some docked Simulink components can appear blank after being hidden and restored. This includes (but is not limited to) the Stateflow Symbols table, the Embedded Coder app, and some elements in the Simulink Property Inspector.
Workaround: Resizing the docked content will cause it to render successfully.

Summary: Opening a standalone chart file (with extension .sfx) that was saved and renamed in R2019a throws an error
Description: Suppose that a standalone Stateflow chart has been saved in 19a as mychart.sfx , and the file is moved to another file folder or renamed. The next time you try to open the file, Stateflow throws an error similar to: Error using open (line 146) Invalid Simulink object name: mychart19a/mychart19a?
Workaround: Rename the file to its original name before opening it.

Summary: DOM? API HTML and HTMLFile objects ignore the CSS height property for table rows
Description:
The DOM API HTML and HTMLFile objects ignore the CSS height property for table rows. For example, the height property in the following script is ignored and generates a warning at the MATLAB command line: import mlreportgen.dom.*htmlTable = '
hello
';domTable = HTML(htmlTable);
Workaround:
Use row entry to specify row height, for example:import mlreportgen.dom.*htmlTable = '
hello
';domTable = HTML(htmlTable);
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 6, R2019b Update 1, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The median?function returns incorrect results for some 3-element and 4-element complex vectors
Description: The?median ?function returns incorrect results for some 3-element and 4-element complex vectors. This issue also occurs with matrices and N-D arrays when the dimension to operate along is 3 or 4 elements in length and the data is complex. In these situations, code generation uses the  'real'  comparison method instead of the  'abs'  comparison method when sorting the complex data.  This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.?

Summary: Inability to insert images into the rich text field in the Requirements Editor, and resulting inability to save the requirement set
Description: Inserting an image using the rich text editor in the Requirements Editor using the Insert Image  button from the toolbar can result in a?broken image. The broken image prevents saving the requirement set.

Summary: Crash while exiting MATLAB when started with -r start_simulink
Description: After you start MATLAB with the option -r start_simulink , the program might crash during exit.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Models configured for concurrent execution and containing blocks with continuous states simulate incorrectly in accelerator mode
Description: If a model isconfigured for concurrent execution and contains blocks with continuous states, simulations in accelerator mode might produce incorrect numerical results.
Workaround: Change simulation mode to Normal or Rapid Accelerator.

Summary: Importing ReqIF created by Simulink Requirements into DOORS Next Generation results in missing images
Description: The following workflow can result in missing images in DOORS Next Generation (DNG) after importing a ReqIF file:? Import a ReqIF file into Simulink Requirements (e.g. requirementsA.reqif ). Export ReqIF file from Simulink Requirements (e.g. requirementsA_export.reqif ) Import requirementsA_export.reqif  into DNG.  The import operation in step 3 can result in missing images.

Summary: YOLO v2 object detector training produces poor results for AlexNet, Inceptionv3, and Squeezenet networks.
Description: Poor detection results while training a YOLO v2 object detector for networks that use a fractional downsampling factor, such as AlexNet, Inceptionv3, and Squeezenet. The floor operation applied on the downsampling factor results inspurious intersections between ground truth data and predicted boxes during training.

Summary: "Alt Gr" key causes access keys to appear in Live Editor toolstrip
Description: If you use the "Alt Gr" key in the Live Editor to type some characters, the access keys appear on the toolstrip. Only the "Alt" key, rather than the "Alt Gr" key, should show the access keys on the toolstrip.
Workaround: Disable the access keys by running the following command in the Command Window:?com.mathworks.desktop.mnemonics.MnemonicsManagers.get.disableThe access keys can again be enabled by running the following command in the Command Window:?com.mathworks.desktop.mnemonics.MnemonicsManagers.get.enable

Summary: MATLAB might crash when calling a Simulink function with large bus input or output from a Stateflow chart
Description: MATLAB might crash when calling a Simulink function with a large bus input or output from a Stateflow chart, and the Simulink function is external to the chart.

Summary: Message Period field in a CAN Transmit block does not not accept a variable
Description: When you use a variable to specify the Message period field in a CAN transmit block, an error results. This occurs whether you specify a variable in the block parameters dialog or by using the set_param  function.

Summary: When comparing MAT files that are equivalent but not identical, the link to the documentation does not work.
Description: When comparing MAT files that are equivalent but not identical, the link to the documentation 'Comparing MAT-Files' does not work.
Workaround: Access the 'Comparing MAT-Files' documentation here:https://www.mathworks.com/help/matlab/matlab_env/comparing-files-and-folders.html#brqxeeu-108

Summary: Simulink functions called from multirate referenced models scheduled with the Schedule Editor might not execute
Description: A Simulink Function block in the top model of a model hierarchy will not execute its contained blocks when all of these conditions apply: The top model is a rate-based model. The Simulink function is global. A rate-based referenced model is scheduled using the Schedule Editor in the top model. This referenced model contains at least three Model blocks: One of these referenced models is an export-function model that contains an exported Simulink function. That exported function makes the only call to the global Simulink function. The other two referenced models contain callers to the Simulink function exported by the export-function model. These two models are rate-based models with different sample times.   The outputs of all related caller blocks are the default initialized values (likely zeros).

Summary: Link with Doors Next Generation (DNG) fails if the server is configured for requirements service section has text other than "rm"
Description: DOORS Next Generation (DNG) service is typically?accessed via a URL such as https://JAZZSERVERNAME:PORT/rm/. When the?DNG server uses a different?section name, for example?https://JAZZSERVERNAME:PORT/rm2/ , Simulink Requirements cannot connect with the DNG service, and cannot create new links.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulation result might be incorrect for a Bus Creator block inside a Model block
Description: Simulation result?might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions:The model contains a referenced model. The referenced model contains a?Bus Creator?block. The simulation mode of the referenced model is set to Accelerator mode.

Summary: Outputs are missing, not updated, or briefly appear and then disappear when a section is run in the Live Editor
Description: When running an individual section in the Live Editor, outputs might be missing, might not be updated, or might briefly appear and then disappear. This typically happens after converting one or more lines of code to rich text.  To fix the issue, run the entire file. Closing and then reopening the file does not fix the issue.
Workaround: Rerun the entire file when the problem occurs.

Summary: Models containing stateful blocks may generate incorrect results on non-English MATLAB
Description: Simulink Design Verifier may return incorrect results when the following conditions are met: Non-English MATLAB The model uses Model blocks The model uses blocks that contain state (such as Data Store Memory blocks) There is a constant rate in the model (such as from a Constant block)   When these conditions are met, a replacement model may be generated for which the relative execution order does not match that of the original model. This leads to the replacement model having a difference in behavior from that of the original model.

Summary: MATLAB might crash after running a model with Simulink Editor side panes open
Description: MATLAB might crash sporadically after simulation when at least one Simulink Editor side pane is open. For example: Open a model with In Bus Element or Out Bus Element blocks at its top level. Open the Model Data Editor pane. Run the model.  The stack trace for the crash mentions?ctx_getBlockByName . The crash is not limited to the above combination, however, it is the most common form reported.

Summary: The Model Advisor check "Usable characters for Inport blocks and Outport blocks" checks the block name of the bus element port instead of port name
Description: The Model Advisor check "Usable characters for Inport blocksand Outport blocks" (ID: mathworks.maab.jc_0211) checks for the usablecharacters in the Inport block name and Outport block names.?In case of Bus Element Port the check checks for the usablecharacters in the block name instead of the port name.?

Summary: Error in slreq.inLinks(DESTINATION) and slreq.outLinks(SOURCE) for certain inputs.
Description: Simulink Requirements APIs slreq.inLinks(DESTINATION)  and slreq.outLinks(SOURCE)  return an error when given an input of type slreq.*.   For example, an input of type slreq.Requirement  results in the following error:  slreq.utils.apiObjToIdsStruct() does not support input of type slreq.Requirement.

Summary: When using Live Editor in MATLAB Online or desktop certain MATLAB apps, like Binning Explorer, error when launched
Description: The following MATLAB apps?error when executed in the Live Editor:  waveletSignalDenoiser, sensorArrayAnalyzer, cameraCalibrator and binningExplorer. Therefore, when using MATLAB Online or desktop, customers cannot launch these apps in the Live Editor.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: SIL/PIL simulations might produce incorrect results for models that generate C++ classes
Description: Software-in-the-loop (SIL) and processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulations might produce results that are wrong if all of these conditions apply:  TargetLang  is set to C++ .  GenerateExternalIOAccessMethods  is not set to None .  The model uses std:strings  or enumerated data types for I/O.

Summary: Signal Editor block settings for nonactive signals are lost when the model is exported to previous release
Description: When a model containing a Signal Editor block is exported to a previous release, the block parameter settings for nonactive signals are not exported. For those signals, the settings are reset to default.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: AUTOSAR generated code for signal might not honor mapped AUTOSAR variable type
Description: The generated code for an AUTOSAR model might incorrectly represent the AUTOSAR mapping of a signal if all of the following conditions exist: A subsystem output signal is mapped for AUTOSAR, as displayed in the Code Mappings editor, Signals/States  tab. The underlying signal within the subsystem, which is connected to the outport of the subsystem output signal, is also mapped for AUTOSAR. One of the two signals is mapped to Auto , while the other is mapped to AUTOSAR variable type ArTypedPerInstanceMemory  or StaticMemory .  Generated code for the signal mapped to an AUTOSAR variable type might not reflect the configured type.
Workaround: Remove the AUTOSAR mapping for the signal that is mapped to Auto.

Summary: In pcbStack, the feed position is picked incorrectly when you specify a single layer of metal with a dielectric layer on top of it.
Description: For a pcbStack described with a single layer of metal and dielectric cover on top?the feed is picked on the top, which is the surface of the dielectric rather than the bottom which is the surface of the metal. There are two consequences of this bug.  1) Due to the feed being picked on the dielectric surface, the results from the solver are incorrect and shows errors in the command window. For example, when plotting the far-field directivity pattern of the structure using the 'pattern' function: Error using surf (line 71) X, Y, Z, and C cannot be complex. Error in em.internal.radiationpattern3D>draw_3d_plot (line 92) surfHdl = surf(axes1,X,Y,Z,MagE, 'FaceColor','interp');? 2) The visualization of the geometry using the 'show' function will display the metal and dielectric layers flipped.?

Summary: sim3d.Engine class errors when you use 3D simulation blocks after updating Windows?? ??
Description: After you install a Windows update that includes .Net 4.8 (528049) , Simulink models that include 3D simulation blocks that interface with the Unreal Engine? display this error at simulation start:? Invalid default value for property 'engine' in class 'sim3d.Engine': No method 'Start' with matching signature found for class 'System.Diagnostics.Process'.   Vehicle Dynamics Blockset??blocks that interface with the Unreal Engine are in the Vehicle Scenarios > Sim3D Simulink block library.?
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 6, R2019b Update 1, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generating code for a subsystem with an array of buses at the input may generate incorrect code or cause unexpected errors
Description: If you generate code for a subsystem by right-clicking on the subsystem and selecting C/C++ Code  > Build this subsystem , and the subsystem meets both these conditions: One of the input ports is an array of buses. That input port is driven by a Delay block.  Then the array of buses will be treated like a scalar, which might either cause incorrect generated code, or an unexpected error during the build process.

Summary: MATLAB or Simulink files become unusable after adding tag in Windows Explorer Details pane
Description: MATLAB and Simulink do not properly handle some file types that are modified by the Windows Explorer Details pane. These file types include SLX, MLX, MLAPP, MLTBX, etc. MATLAB and Simulink are unable to open affected files, but no data is lost. The file can be fully recovered by following the Workaround.?
Workaround: Unzip the attached function, repairfile.m, and repair your file with the following command in MATLAB:  repairedFile = repairfile(sourceFile)

Summary: prob2struct?throws an error when using the?EquationFunctionName name-value pair
Description: prob2struct ?throws the following error when using the EquationFunctionName ?name-value pair. >> s = prob2struct(ep, 'EquationFunctionName', 'myfun')  Error using optim.problemdef.EquationProblem/prob2struct (line 30)The value of 'x0' is invalid. Initial point must be a nonempty struct.  If? you specify an initial point x0 ,?prob2struct ?throws the following error. >> s = prob2struct(ep, x0, 'EquationFunctionName', 'myfun')Error using optim.problemdef.EquationProblem/prob2struct (line 30)  'EquationFunctionName' is not a recognized parameter. For a list of valid name-value pair arguments, see the documentation for this?function.?

Summary: Using serialport on macOS with certain devices might cause MATLAB to hang
Description: When using serialport  to write?to certain devices on a?/dev/tty. ?port on macOS?and clearing the?serialport ?object, MATLAB code execution might hang. For example, when using an Arduino device on the /dev/tty.  port, running the following MATLAB code?results in the Command Window becoming unresponsive:   serialportlist'  ans =        4x1 string array           "/dev/cu.Bluetooth-Incoming-Port"           "/dev/tty.Bluetooth-Incoming-Port"           "/dev/cu.usbserial-A603OQAQ"           "/dev/tty.usbserial-A603OQAQ"    s = serialport("/dev/tty.usbserial-A603OQAQ",9600);   write(s,1,"uint8");   clear s
Workaround: To work around this issue, use the?corresponding?/dev/cu. port instead of the /dev/tty. port.For example, connect to an Arduino device on the /dev/cu. port:  serialportlist'  ans =          4x1 string array             "/dev/cu.Bluetooth-Incoming-Port"             "/dev/tty.Bluetooth-Incoming-Port"             "/dev/cu.usbserial-A603OQAQ"             "/dev/tty.usbserial-A603OQAQ"   s = serialport("/dev/cu.usbserial-A603OQAQ",9600);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Passing single precision arrays to extrinsic functions when using GPU Coder may generate wrong answer
Description: When passing single precision arrays to a function declared as extrinsic, the generated code may produce incorrect results. For example, consider this MATLAB code: function out = foo()    coder.extrinsic('sqrt');    out = sin(single([4 4]));end In MATLAB, foo  returns the correct value of [2 2] .? In the generated code,??foo ?returns the incorrect value of?[0 0] .
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 3, R2020a

Summary: Import from ARXML files?not supported for AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform data types with?'CPP-IMPLEMENTATION-DATA-TYPE' tags?
Description: Importing ARXML files that contain descriptions for AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform components, interfaces, or data types that reference data types with 'CPP-IMPLEMENTATION-DATA-TYPE' tags is not supported. An error messages similar to the following occurs: Cannot resolve reference Type with target????/ company/powertrain/ types/myType????in file/myComponent.arxml:24????to a valid object.?   ?See also?2008032 ?for a related issue.

Summary: Logging Stateflow signals in Simulation Outputs or Temporal Assessments causes test case to fail
Description: If you log Stateflow signals in Simulation Outputs or Temporal Assessments, when you run the test case, it fails and returns this message:? The 'OutputPortIndex' property must be a scalar integer value greater than zero.

Summary: Renaming a data dictionary and linking it back to a model causes ports to lose their Interfaces
Description: When a data dictionary is renamed and relinked to an architecture model, ports lose their interfaces.

Summary: Saving an architecture model with a large number of reference components can cause a freeze
Description: If you save an architecture model with a large number of reference components (approximately >20), MATLAB appears frozen.

Summary: "Component Occurrence must be in closest ancestor" error when creating a custom view for a System Composer architecture model
Description: When creating a custom view for your architecture model, you might receive this error when you click a component: Component Occurrence must be in closest ancestor  This error occurs if you delete all of the previously existing views in your architecture model and then save and close the model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Predefined values for two Synchronous Machine Field Circuit block parameters can yield results that are incorrect or that differ from the results from prior releases.
Description: For R2018b, for the per-unit implementation of the Synchronous Machine Field Circuit block, two Machine Parameters  settings parameters were added for the Fundamental parameters  parameterization. If you do not specify values for the parameters, t  he block uses predefined constants that can yield results that are incorrect or that differ from the results from prior releases.

Summary: Code generation for yolov2ObjectDetector fails if GPU Coder is not installed
Description: If you do not have GPU Coder installed and you attempt to generate code for?yolov2ObjectDetector , you get this error message: Undefined function or variable 'gpucoder'.  This code shows the typical workflow for generating code for?yolov2ObjectDetector ?for 'mkldnn'  and 'arm-compute'  targets.? cfg = coder.config;% This script generates code for MKL-DNN targets. To generate code for % ARM-Compute targets, replace 'mkldnn' by 'arm-compute' in the following line. cfg.DeepLearningConfig = coder.DeepLearningConfig('mkldnn'); cfg.TargetLang = 'C++';codegen -config cfg yolov2_detection -args {imread('highway.png')} For more information about the yolov2_detection  function, see?https://www.mathworks.com/help/deeplearning/examples/generate-cpp-code-for-object-detection-using-yolo-v2-and-intel-mkldnn.html

Summary: When class A restricts property type to class Aand class B contains a class A object, then editing classB can cause MATLAB to stop responding.
Description: Given a class, A,?that has a property whose type is restrictedto an instance of class A, and a class, B, that contains an instance of class A, then modifying the definition of class B while aninstance of class B exists can cause MATLAB to stop responding when MATLAB attemptsto auto-update the instance of class B.            For example, class A has a property,?Prop1,?thatis restricted to be of type?class A. ?      classdef A < handle  ??? properties  ???????Prop1 A %Restrict Prop1 to objects of class A  ??? end  end  Class B has a property,?Prop2, whose default value is an objectof class A.        classdef B  ??? properties  ???????Prop2 = A? %Assign object of class A as default value to Prop2  ???????Prop3 = 10  ??? end  end  Create an instance of class B:    >> obj = B;  Now, if you modify the definition of class B, MATLAB attempts toautomatically update obj to conform to the new definition of class B. However,when you modify the?class B definition file and save it, any referencemade to the existing object of class B can cause MATLAB to stopresponding.        If this problem occurs, the only solution is to end the MATLABprocess.

Summary: Error when checking consistency of requirement link labels using the Model Advisor
Description: If you run the Requirement Links Consistency Checking for a Simulink model using the Model Advisor, the Advisor can return the error: ? ? ?index exceeds the number of array elements   This error can occur when the following conditions are met: You run the 3rd check in the Requirements Consistency  section of the Model Advisor There are library links in the model? Referenced blocks in the library have Requirement links
Workaround: Disable library link in model before running consistency checking.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for C++ std::string in MATLAB Function block?
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for a model that meets all of the following conditions: The model contains a string block. The model contains a MATLAB Function block. The MATLAB Function block contains multiple string operations or functions The model is configured to generate C++ std::string? code.

Summary: Changing target language for model with custom system target file may generate an error
Description: Changing TargetLang  property by using set_param  or changing the?Language ?setting from the Configuration Parameters dialog box might?produce an error. The error occurs when the following conditions are met:? The model uses a custom System Target File. The custom System Target File disables one of the dependent parameters such as the Standard math library .?  When all of the conditions are true, and you attempt to change the parameter through:  The Configuration Parameters dialog box, the error appears momentarily on the dialog and disappears. However, the changed value is applied to the model when you click Apply . The command line by passing the model as the first argument to?set_param ?function, an error related to a dependent parameter is thrown and the value is not applied to the model.?

Summary: External constraints on structure field arrays are not recognized
Description: In come C applications, when you specify external constraints (DRS) on an array field in a structure, the verification does not take into account the init constraint mode in the constraint XML file. The tooltip displays full-range on the content of all elements of the array field, even when the constraint set has restrained each element.

Summary: readtable ignores the specified 'Sheet' index and always reads from the first sheet for some XLSX files
Description: In the example below, readtable  ignores the sheet index provided and defaults to reading always from the first sheet only. >> t = readtable("fails.xlsx","Sheet",2);
Workaround: To workaround this issue, call readtable with the name of the sheet instead of the index.??For example, to read from the second sheet named "Sheet2", use readtable as follows:>> t?= readtable("fails.xlsx","Sheet","Sheet2");

Summary: MATLAB might crash when you delete a block while signal label propagation is enabled
Description: When you delete a block in Simulink, MATLAB might crash if signal label propagation is enabled.
Workaround: On the Debug?tab, clear?Information Overlays > Propagated Signal Labels.

Summary: On a macOS machine, MATLAB crashes when accessing a microphone or web camera
Description: When running on an Apple Macintosh computer running macOS 10.14 or later, if MATLAB tries to access a microphone or web camera the MATLAB process crashes.

Summary: Error message on setting an empty cell array string using set_param for Custom Table Parameter
Description: When mask dialog is open,and you set an empty cell array string using set_param for Custom Table Parameter,example:  set_param( gcb, 'SignalInfo', '{}') The below error message is shown for  SignalInfo which is a Custom Table Parameter   The value of parameter 'SignalInfo' is inconsistent with the column data in block 'untitled/Subsystem'.The dimension of the value must match with the column data for the Custom Table.

Summary: The generateCode method of the simscape.multibody.KinematicsSolver class generates C code that contains a memory leak
Description: When you call the generateCode method on the simscape.multibody.KinematicsSolver class, the generated C code contains a memory leak.

Summary: Unit Delay and Rate Transition blocks not extracted as adapters in an architecture model
Description: When you extract an architecture model from a Simulink model, adapter blocks corresponding to the Unit Delay and Rate Transition blocks are not generated.

Summary: Uploading results to Polyspace Access fails
Description: If you upload results to Polyspace Access from a folder that contains a large number of files, the upload might fail. The Polyspace results folder can contain a large number of files when: You output all your analysis results to the same folder and you do not delete older log files. You run a unit-by-unit code verification that outputs a large number of result sets.?

Summary: MATLAB might crash when generating code using MATLAB Coder or MATLAB Function or System block in Simulink
Description: MATLAB might crash when you generate code using MATLAB Coder or MATLAB Function or System block in Simulink. The stack trace starts with the following: Assertion at b:\matlab\src\cg_ir\export\include\cg_ir\type\type.hpp line 538:  In assertingCast: Failed to cast CG::Type (class CG::Type const * __ptr64) from |matrix| to |class CG::PointerType|

Summary: The pcshow?function throws an error while using the axes toolbar interactions.
Description: The pcshow  function throws an initializePCSceneControl   error while trying to use the axes toolbar interactions. The error occurs only when the rotate3d  option turned off.

Summary: Code inspection fails if any computation in a model evaluates to an Inf value
Description: Simulink Code Inspector fails code inspection if the result of a Simulink, Stateflow or MATLAB computation in the model evaluates to an Inf? numerical value.

Summary: Values of parameters changed in SerDes IBIS-AMI Parameter Manager revert to previous values after single simulation run.
Description: The current value of an AMI parameter associated with a default SerDes ToolboxTM  library block can be updated either in the Block Parameters dialog box or in the appropriate tab (Tx/Rx) of the SerDes IBIS-AMI Parameter Manager dialog box.? When updated from the SerDes IBIS-AMI Parameter Manager dialog box, the value is used for only the first simulation in Simulink. After the simulation is complete, the parameter value reverts back to the previous value with no user feedback.
Workaround: Only update the current value of AMI parameters associated with a default SerDes ToolboxTM library block in the Block Parameters dialog box.? Newly added AMI parameters can be updated safely from the SerDes IBIS-AMI Parameter Manager dialog box.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated HDL code might have unconnected signals for models containing Signal Conversion blocks with output logging enabled
Description: Generated HDL code might have unconnected signals if the following conditions are met:?? The Simulink model contains "Signal Conversion" blocks. The blocks convert a virtual bus to a non-virtual bus or vice versa. The?Output ?checkbox in Data Import/Export > Save to workspace or file  in the Configuration Parameters dialog box is selected.

Summary: Forwarding Stateflow messages that have different data types results in undefined behavior
Description: Calling the? forward ?operator with messages that have mismatched data types will result in undefined behavior in both simulation and code generation. No error message is displayed.

Summary: Range Collection Mode as Derived ranges in Fixed-point Tool fails for export function models
Description: Range collection in Fixed-point Tool with Range Collection Mode ?selected as Derived ranges  fails for Export-function models. The diagnostic viewer shows the error:?Ranges could not be derived for the model.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b

Summary: Simulink Web View?displays as blank pane in code generation report
Description: In a code generation report, the Web View displays as a blank pane if the web?browser is missing a function required by the browser detection JavaScript code.?
Workaround: Open the code generation report using a different web browser, such as the MATLAB web browser, Firefox, or Google Chrome.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 6, R2019b Update 1, R2020a

Summary: Subscripted assignment into properties with class validation is unexpectedly slow
Description: If a class defines a property with class validation, then subscripted assignment into the property is slow. For example, the PropsWithClass  class defines a property Number  with class validation as follows: classdef PropsWithClass   properties      Number double   end   methods      function obj = PropsWithClass(value)          obj.Number = value;       end    end  end Any value assigned to the Number  property must have type?double  or be convertible to type?double . Consider the following code that creates an object of the class PropsWithClass  and initializes the Number  property to a 10000x10000 magic square: >> value = magic(10000);>> obj = PropsWithClass(value); If you execute the following subscripted assignment into the Number  property, the assignment is unexpectedly slow: >> obj.Number(3) = 12; In addition, if the property contains a relatively small amount of data, the slowness might be noticeable inside a for -loop, as in the following example: >> value = magic(3);>> obj = PropsWithClass(value);>> for j = 1:1e6       obj.Number(3) = 12;   end
Workaround: To avoid slow subscripted assignment, remove class validation from the property.

Summary: Incorrect behavior for coverage analysis of MATLAB files with same name in different packages
Description: Unexpected behavior occurs for coverage analysis of a Simulink model which contains calls to external MATLAB files which have the same name but are defined in different packages. Depending on the setup and structure of your model, this bug can manifest in different ways and could cause MATLAB to crash, throw an error during simulation, or produce incorrect coverage results for the external MATLAB files. For example, consider a case where there are two functions named Foo stored in two different subpackages: +topPackage/+subPackage1/Foo.m +topPackage/+subPackage2/Foo.m  A model contains a MATLAB Function block which makes calls to both of these functions:? out1 = topPackage.subPackage1.Foo(in);?  out2 = topPackage.subPackage2.Foo(in);?   Coverage analysis of this model would be affected by this bug.
Products: Requirements Toolbox, Simulink Coverage, Simulink Check

Summary: On macOS 10.15 (Catalina) MATLAB file types are not associated with the installed versions of MATLAB
Description: When installing MATLAB on macOS 10.15 (Catalina), MATLAB file types are not associated with the version of MATLAB being installed. As a result, MATLAB file types (e.g., MATLAB file, MAT-file, Simulink model file) do not open in MATLAB when double-clicked or dragged to the MATLAB icon in the Dock.
Workaround: To work around this issue, use the following steps:Find the MATLAB installation location (e.g., /Applications/MATLAB_R2019b.app) by running the matlabroot command in the MATLAB Command Window.?Open Terminal.app to run the following command (where <MATLABROOT> is replaced by the path found in step 1):??  <MATLABROOT>/bin/maci64/fileassoc --mlroot <MATLABROOT> --products 4 --installConfirm that MATLAB file types are correctly associated WITH MATLAB by double-clicking a MATLAB file or dragging it to the MATLAB icon in the Dock.After this workaround, if MATLAB silently fails to launch, use the following steps:Go to System Preferences > Security & Privacy > Privacy > Files and Folders.Deselect all the checkboxes under MATLAB, then reselect them to turn on Files and Folders access for MATLAB.For more information, see Control access to your files and folders on Mac.

Summary: When using rtwbuild to generate a DPI component for a subsystem containing a model reference, the build fails for cross-platform code generation
Description: When generating a DPI component for a platform different than the host (for example: targeting a Linux toolchain from a Windows machine), and the subsystem contains a model reference, the following error is thrown: error:  The following Configuration parameter option(s) do not match between the models <Parent> and <Child>. Configuration references can help avoid parameter mismatches.    TargetBitPerLong [Use set_param to change settings]:  Parent setting: 64  Child setting: 32   Component:Simulink | Category:Block diagram error?  Following the error suggestion does not fix the issue.?
Workaround: To resolve the issue, you must:1. Restructure your subsystem so that it does not use a model reference.?OR?2. Select a toolchain that matches the host machine.?

Summary: MATLAB crashes when closing a window with App Designer running and debugger stopped
Description: When using the debugger with MATLAB App Designer, MATLAB might crash if the debugger is stopped and you close your app window or quit the debugger.

Summary: Format of ASAP2 file exported during code generation might be incorrect when a model has a n-D lookup table
Description: The format of an ASAP2 file(A2L file) exported during code generation might be incorrect when a model meets both of these conditions:?The Array layout   model configuration parameter is set to Row-major . The model contains a n-D Lookup Table block.  For example, the incorrect part of an A2L file containing 2-D lookup table information is as follows: ? ?/begin CHARACTERISTIC ? ? ? /* Name? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?*/? ? ? T?? ? ? ? /* Long Identifier? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?*/? ? ? "" ? ? ? /* Type? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?*/? ? ? Summary: Inactive signal properties for Signal Editor block inside?subsystem are reset to default after?model is closed and reopened
Description: For Signal Editor blocks, you can set different properties for different signals. When this block is?in a subsystem, the signal properties of an inactive signal are reset to default if the model is saved, closed, and reopened.
Workaround: To recover properties of all signals of a Signal Editor block inside?a subsystem:Unzip the attachment and save the?workaroundForBugReport2068446.p?file to a folder on your MATLAB path.Run this script each time the model containing Signal Editor block is loaded or opened.Input to this script is a cell array of Signal Editor block paths or a vector of Signal Editor block handles.

Summary: Unexpected behavior when editing Custom Gauge blocks or panels after navigating a model hierarchy
Description: After you navigate through a model hierarchy, unexpected behavior can occur when you try to add a block to a panel or edit a Custom Gauge block in a panel. Unexpected behaviors include: When you drag and drop a block into a panel, the block might appear to vanish. When you enter edit mode for a Custom Gauge block in a panel, the block might not enter edit mode or a Custom Gauge block outside of the panel might appear to vanish.
Workaround: To resolve these issues, open the component with the issue in a new window. In the new window, blocks that appeared to vanish now display correctly, and you can enter edit mode for Custom Gauge blocks.To enter edit mode for a Custom Gauge block in a panel, you can also:Add a Custom Gauge block to the component with the issue.Enter edit mode for the added Custom Gauge block.Select the Custom Gauge block in the panel.

Summary: Variant Subsystems not extracted as Variant Components in an architecture model
Description: When you extract an architecture model from a Simulink model, Variant Subsystems are transformed to Components instead of Variant Components.

Summary: Unable to open variant using Simulink Toolstrip
Description: When you navigate inside a locked system, such as a locked library, a library linked subsystem, or a write protected model, the menu items on the?Variant Subsystem > Open  tab of the Simulink Toolstrip are disabled. This prevents opening the variants from this menu.
Workaround: Context-click on the block in the editor, then choose Variant > Open to open the desired variant.

Summary: CAN Unpack block generates unoptimized code block
Description: The CAN Unpack block generates unoptimized code, which has the floor  and ceil  functions. These functions cause implicit data conversion between single and double data type in the generated code.

Summary: Trying to get block handle from block SID inside Subsystem Reference fails
Description: After converting a Subsystem to Subsystem Reference, trying to get a block handle from Simulink Identifier (SID) for a block which is inside a subsystem in Subsystem Reference fails.  The diagnostic viewer shows the below error message:  Object No Longer Exists

Summary: Code generator might crash if the MATLAB code passes a vector input to a method that returns multiple outputs
Description: The code generator might crash if a call to a method in your MATLAB code satisfies both of these conditions: The call passes a vector (for example, 1:n  or a:b:c ) to the method. The method returns multiple outputs to the caller function.  The crash report contains an assertion?at CallExpr.hpp line 16 . For example, suppose that you define a MATLAB class A_class :  classdef A_Class    methods        function [a,b] = a_method(obj, input)            a = 0;            b = input;        end    endend If you attempt to generate code for this function, the code generator might crash: function for_codegen(n)obj = A_Class;[a,b] = obj.a_method(1:n);end
Workaround: In your MATLAB code, save the vector input into a temporary variable and pass this temporary variable to the method.For example, modify the for_codegen?function in the previous section by introducing a temporary variable temp.?function for_codegen(n)obj = A_Class;temp = 1:n;[a,b] = obj.a_method(temp);end
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 6, R2019b Update 1, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB functions performing subtractions with signed integers typecasted as unsigned integers might generate incorrect HDL code
Description: Incorrect HDL code can be generated from MATLAB functions that performs subtractions with signed integers are typecasted as unsigned integers. For example: % in32 is of type int32?  function y_uint32?= foo (in32)  ????uint32_extreme = uint32([0, 4294967295]);  ? ? y_uint32 = uint32_extreme??- uint32(in32);??  end??   The generated code in such cases does not perform saturation of negative values of the signed integer to zero, which results in testbench simulation mismatches.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when opening a bus block dialog or using get_param
Description: MATLAB might crash or appear to hang when you open a Bus Selector, Bus Creator, or Bus Assignment block dialog or invoke get_param(portHandle, 'SignalHierarchy')  on one of those blocks. The generated stack trace contains lines similar to the following: [  0] 0x00000000f05eb4b6                        bin\ win64\ sl_graphical_classes.dll+02405558 SlSigHierInfo::isHierarchyPartiallyKnown+00000006[  1] 0x00000002e0812caa                        bin\ win64\ libmwsimulink.dll+08334506 RTWCGBusParameterBlock::getRTWBlock+00108490[  2] 0x00000002e0812f7f                        bin\ win64\ libmwsimulink.dll+08335231 RTWCGBusParameterBlock::getRTWBlock+00109215

Summary: Using a webcam with the?macvideo adaptor on macOS Mojave or macOS Catalina might crash MATLAB
Description: When using a webcam with the?macvideo  adaptor on macOS Mojave or macOS Catalina, MATLAB might crash. This can happen when using the Image Acquisition Tool or the command line.
Workaround: To work around this issue, use the following steps to start MATLAB from the command line in macOS and allow access to the webcam when prompted.1) Open the Terminal app from the Utilities folder in Applications.2) Navigate to the <MATLAB>/bin folder in Applications, using the following command in the Terminal app:/Applications/MATLAB_R2019b.app/bin3) Start MATLAB, using the following command:./matlab4) At the MATLAB command line, run the following command: imaqhwinfo('macvideo')5) Grant the Terminal application access to the camera when prompted by an alert from macOS. This provides MATLAB access to the camera.?Notes:Subsequent uses of the capture system do not cause the alert to appear again because macOS remembers the permission settings. These settings can be changed in System Preferences > Security & Privacy > Privacy > Camera.Restricted users cannot access media devices in macOS Mojave or macOS Catalina. For such users, no device is detected.

Summary: Using a USB webcam on macOS Mojave causes MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB might crash when you create a webcam object on macOS Mojave.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, use the following steps to grant camera access to the Terminal application:1) Open the Terminal app from the Utilities folder in Applications.2) Navigate to the <MATLAB>/bin folder in Applications using the following command in the Terminal app.? ? ? ? /Applications/MATLAB_R2019b.app/bin3) Start MATLAB using the following command.? ? ? ? ./matlab? ?4) Create the wecam object in MATLAB with the following command:? ? ? ? ?w = webcam5)?Authorize camera access to the Terminal application when prompted. Granting access allows MATLAB and the webcam to work as expected.Notes:Once you grant/deny the camera access to the Terminal, the system prompt will not appear for subsequent launches of MATLAB from the Terminal. You can change the permission settings in System Preferences > Security and Privacy > Privacy > Camera.Restricted users cannot access media devices in macOS Mojave. For such users, no device will be detected.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: While loop with empty body incorrectly omitted in generated code
Description: For MATLAB code that satisfies the following conditions:? There exists a while  loop which does not contain any statements inside its body.?? The condition of the while  loop is a compound logical expression involving logical and '&& '. At least one of the logical subexpressions is a function-call with a side effect.  The code generator might incorrectly omit the while  loop in generated code.? For an example, see the file issue_example_2067108.m in the attached issue_2067108.zip.? function out = issue_example_2067108(idx)    global globalState;    globalState = 0;    while(globalState < idx  && ...          updateGlobalState())    end     out = globalState;end function out = updateGlobalState()    coder.inline('never');    global globalState;    globalState = globalState + 1;    if(globalState > 100)        out = false;        return;    end    out = true;end  The call to updateGlobalState  in the while  loop condition has a side-effect of incrementing globalState . In the generated code, the while  loop and the call to this function are incorrectly omitted. >> codegen issue_example_2067108 -args {0} -globals {'globalState',0}real_T issue_example_2067108(const emlrtStack *sp, real_T idx){  (void)sp;  (void)idx;  globalState = 0.0;  globalState_dirty |= 1U;  return globalState;} Instead the code generator should have produced real_T issue_example_2067108(const emlrtStack *sp, real_T idx){  boolean_T exitg1;  globalState = 0.0;  globalState_dirty |= 1U;  exitg1 = false;  while ((!exitg1) && (globalState < idx)) {    if (*emlrtBreakCheckR2012bFlagVar != 0) {      emlrtBreakCheckR2012b(sp);    }    if (!updateGlobalState()) {      exitg1 = true;    }  }  return globalState;} This issue can affect MATLAB Coder only code generation Simulink Coder code generation of models containing a MATLAB Function block, a MATLAB System block, or a MATLAB function in Stateflow?  See also Bug Report ID 2093701
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 1, R2020a

Summary: Model default storage class unexpectedly preserved for model argument exported to earlier release?
Description: If you export amodel argument with storage class Model default  to release R2018b or earlier,the storage class is preserved even though Model default  is not supported for any release before R2019a. In this case, running a simulation in Accelerator mode might result in a warning message similar to: Ignoring the storage class specified for model argument 'Param' in model workspace of 'modelName'. To prevent this warning, set the storage class of 'Param' to 'Auto'.?  with an error message similar to: Unable to perform assignment with 0 elements on the right-hand side
Workaround: Clear the storage class of model argument after exporting the model. By assigning variable 'modelName' with the name of the model containing model arguments, users can run the following lines of MATLAB code to fix the issue.?>> orgArgStr = get_param(modelName, 'ParameterArgumentNames')>> set_param(modelName, 'ParameterArgumentNames', '')>> set_param(modelName, 'ParameterArgumentNames', orgArgStr)>> save_system(modelName)

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code when the output of a Rate Transition block uses a? Reusable storage class
Description: The behavior of the generated code for a model may differ from its simulation behavior when it contains a Rate Transition block whose output uses a Reusable  custom storage class.
Workaround: Do not use the Reusable storage class for the output of a Rate Transition block

Summary: Reordering lifelines in the Sequence Viewer block causes MATLAB to hang
Description: In the Sequence Viewer block,?you can reorder lifelines by dragging them. To change the order of a lifeline within its current hierarchical level, you can drag its header symbol so that it occupies a new horizontal position in the header pane.? If you reorder any lifeline which is not at the top level of the lifeline hierarchy, this causes MATLAB to hang.

Summary: Incorrect geometry visualization and meshing with polygonal PCB board shape in pcbStack
Description: Using pcbStack? to create a single layer of metal on top of a dielectric substrate with the BoardShape  property assigned to an antenna.Polygon  object, shows the following inaccuracies in:  The geometry visualized if the board shape specified as a polygon is not centered at the origin of the coordinate system. The results from an analysis because the mesh constructed is incorrect. In particular, the bottom face of the substrate is also covered with metal when only the top should have metal on it.?

Summary: Incorrect table formatting in Simulink Requirements report
Description: In Simulink Requirements, you can use tables in a requirement Description  or Rationale  field. Depending on the table style, table borders might not appear in a report, resulting in inconsistent table formatting between the report and the Requirements Editor.

Summary: Placing a table UI component in a grid layout manager causes App Designer .mlapp file corruption
Description: When an App Designer app has a table UI component in a grid layout manager, the app becomes corrupted.? The file corruption occurs in either of the following situations: 1.? Loading an app that contains a table UI component in a grid layout manager.? The following error is shown:  "Unable to open <path to MLAPP file> because it is corrupt, or has an unrecoverable issue. "    2.? Designing an app that contains a table UI component in a grid layout manager.? This impacts interactive gestures, including re-positioning of the table component, copy/paste, etc.? When designing the app, no errors or warnings are shown.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch between simulation and code generation results for bit operations in variant conditions having normal MATLAB variables
Description: When variant condition expressions contain  bitcmp  or  bitshift  operations with normal MATLAB variables whose data type is not specified (like A = 4 etc.), simulation?uses the assumed type  uint64  for the operand whereas C preprocessor?uses the largest available integer type (example  int64 ). This causes mismatch between simulation and code generation results.

Summary: Favorited gallery buttons persist when switching between Simulink and Stateflow.
Description: When you favorite a gallery button in Simulink, the button appears in the Favorites category at the top of the gallery. You can then favorite the Stateflow gallery button that has the same name, and the two identically named buttons appear side-by-side.?Both buttons are functional, but the expected behavior is that only one should be present.
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: Aerospace Blockset Turn Coordinator and Artificial Horizon flight instrument blocks may display incorrect values when connected to discrete signals
Description: Aerospace Blockset Turn Coordinator and Artificial Horizon flight instrument blocks sporadically exchange first and second input values in the block displays when connected to discrete signals.

Summary: MATLAB crash during code generation for AUTOSAR model with multiple bus inports
Description: MATLAB crashes during code generation for an AUTOSAR model if all of the following conditions exist: In the model, a Simulink bus inport connects to more than one block, either at the top level or inside a subsystem. In the Code Mappings editor, the Simulink bus inport is mapped to AUTOSAR data access mode ExplicitReceive . The Simulink bus inport is one of multiple bus inports mapped to the same AUTOSAR receiver port. The bus inport is not the first bus inport in the AUTOSAR receiver port mapping.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code for a model behaves differently from normal mode simulation when Block reduction parameter is selected
Description: The code generated for a model might behave differently from normal mode simulation when the model meets the following conditions:?In the model configuration parameter dialog box, the Block Simulation Target > Advanced parameters > Block reduction ?parameter is selected.? An atomic subsystem has no "direct functional connection" between any of its Inport and Outport blocks during normal mode simulation, but block reduction leads to a direct functional connection between a pair of its Inport and Outport blocks when code is generated. This typically occurs when blocks that do not reduce during simulation do reduce during code generation (e.g. the Reshape block).  Turn on display of execution order to determine whether a direct functional connection exists between a pair of Inport and Outport blocks. If no blocks along a signal path from an Inport to an Outport displays their execution order, then all blocks on that path are either inherently virtual or reduced during code generation, and a direct functional connection between a pair of Inport and Outport blocks exists via that path.? If any blocks along that path display their execution order, then that path does not provide a direct functional connection between the Inport and Outport blocks.
Workaround: Clear the model configuration parameter Simulation Target > Advanced parameters > Block reduction.

Summary: The nrCDLChannel?channel model incorrectly models the?orientation of the antenna arrays.
Description: The?TransmitAntennaArray.Orientation ?and?ReceiveAntennaArray.Orientation ?properties of the?nrCDLChannel ?channel model control the mechanical orientation of the transmit and receive antenna arrays, respectively. However, changing these property values does not change the orientation of the antenna arrays correctly and may lead to silent wrong conclusions.?

Summary: Error or crash loading Embedded Coder Dictionary created with Japanese encodings
Description: When a coder dictionary is created in a Simulink Data Dictionary on a machine with Japanese encodings, opening it in the Embedded Coder Dictionary dialog box in a new MATLAB session causes MATLAB to crash in R2018b.? In R2019a, this causes an error and the Embedded Coder Dictionary dialog box is not loaded.

Summary: Active toolstrip tab is not set correctly upon first model opening
Description: When you save a model, the currently active toolstrip tab is restored as the active tab the next time you open the model. However, the Simulink toolstrip will set the Simulation tab active when the following conditions are true: The model is the first model you open after restarting MATLAB. The model has been configured via a toolstrip app to target a hardware board.   ?For example, the following apps configure the hardware board: Simulink Real-time app Desktop Real-time app "Run on hardware board" app "Run on custom hardware" app Robot Operating System (ROS) app

Summary: On the App Designer Start Page, clicking on the Interactive Tutorial from Examples: General does not open the tutorial.
Description: On the App Designer Start Page, clicking on the Interactive Tutorial  from Examples: General  does not open the tutorial.There is no error or warning that is displayed.
Workaround: To open the interactive tutorial, use either of the following options:?On the first slide at the top of the App Designer Start Page, click the Start Tutorial button.From the Command Window, run this command and navigate to the documentation page called "Create and Run a Simple App Using App Designer":?docsearch('Create and Run a Simple App Using App Designer')Then, click Open Example from the documentation page.

Summary: Validation model generation from a non-top level DUT generates ambiguous error message
Description: Validation model generation from a non-top level DUT which is present under a masked subsystem generates ambiguous error message as follows: Index exceeds the number of array elements (0)

Summary: Packet-Based ADSB Transceiver example RF mode support in hardware.
Description: The Packet-Based ADS-B Transceiver example in theSoC Blockset produces incorrect results when deployed to the supported hardwareplatforms, Xilinx? Zynq? ZC706 evaluation kit andZedBoard?. In simulation, the model correctly simulates two planes with locationinformation and correctly displays the expected results. When deployed to ahardware platform, the model shows multiple incorrect planes without locationinformation. Only the simulation half of this example can be used successfully.     ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: PLC Coder generates wrong code for the Discrete-time Integrator block using unsupported integrator methods
Description: PLC Code generation does not support the following integrator methods of the Discrete-time Integrator block: 'Integration: Backward Euler',? 'Integration: Trapezoidal', 'Accumulation: Backward Euler', 'Accumulation: Trapezoidal'

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results when building a referenced model that contains a Bus Creator block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect when building a referenced model that meets all of these conditions:The referenced model contains a Bus Creator block that directly connects to a referenced model output.? The signal that connects the Bus Creator block to the referenced model output contains a signal object.? In the Bus Creator Block Parameters dialog box, the Output as nonvirtual bus  parameter is selected.?
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block between the Bus Creator block and the referenced model output. In the Signal Conversion block parameters dialog box, set the Output parameter to Signal copy.

Summary: Signal names specified on a signal line may not appear in the generated C-API file
Description: When generating C-API files, a signal name specified on a signal line from a subsystem may be discarded and not appear in the list of rtwCAPI_Signals  structure when the following conditions are met: The output signal line coming out of the Subsystem is named. A test point is set on the signal line.? The Subsystem's Function packaging  property is marked as Auto .

Summary: MATLAB performance slows down from jcef_helper memory consumption
Description: The MATLAB  jcef_helper  processes increase memory consumption over time, slowing down MATLAB responsiveness.

Summary: Models exported from R2019b to previous versions generate an error when re-opened in R2019b
Description: When you export a model from R2019b to a previous version, re-opening that exported model in R2019b generates an error. This occurs for models with any of the following blocks: CAN Pack  CAN Unpack  CAN FD Pack  CAN FD Unpack   The model opens correctly in earlier versions. Also, the exported model opened and saved in the earlier version will open correctly in R2019b.
Workaround: To work around this issue, open and save the exported model in the earlier version of Simulink. Then you can open that resaved model in R2019b correctly.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code is generated when State Reader block reads from a Delay block in a model with row-major array layout
Description: Incorrect code is generated when all of the following conditions are true in a Simulink model: Delay block's output port is connected to State Reader block's input port. Delay block's Delay length  value is greater than 1. Model configuration parameter Array layout  is set to Row-major .  The generated code for State Reader block reading Delay block's state value is incorrect as it does not consider row-major array layout of the Delay block's state value. For example: Delay Block's delay length = 6? Delay Block's Input port or Initial Condition dimension = 2 * 5? ?? Incorrect code where the row-major array layout was not taken into consideration: ? ? ? ?memcpy(&stateReader.TerminateInnerOut[0], &stateReader.Delay_DSTATE[0],?(uint32_T)(60U * sizeof(real_T))); ? Correct code to read Delay block's state value by a State Reader block, where the row-major layout is considered:     int32_T i;    int32_T i_0;    int32_T i_1 = 0;    for (i = 0; i < 10; i++) {          for (i_0 = 0; i_0 < 6; i_0++) {            stateReader.TerminateInnerOut[i_1] = stateReader.Delay_DSTATE[(int32_T)((int32_T)(i_0 * 10) + i)];            i_1++;         }? ? ? ? ?}

Summary: matlab -r does not support?non-ascii command line arguments
Description: MATLAB does not process non-ascii command line arguments in the  -r  switch. For example,  % matlab -r?"disp('?')"  displays the following character in MATLAB:    >>?

Summary: Generated code might not compile for non-scalar input?Initialize Function, Reset Function, and Terminate Function blocks?in referenced model
Description: Generated code might not compile if all these conditions are true:? The model contains a model reference. The referenced model contains Initialize Function, Reset Function, and Terminate Function blocks. The Initialize Function, Reset Function, and Terminate Function blocks have non-scalar input.?

Summary: StartTime and StopTime for test case results incorrect after importing result set
Description: The StartTime  and StopTime  properties for test case results both display as 31-Dec-1969  after importing a result set. This incorrect date might impact the Simulink Requirements verification status, which depends on these properties.

Summary: equilibrate errors for complex sparse arguments
Description: The?equilibrate  function errors when called with a complex sparse matrix.
Workaround: Call equilibrate on the absolute value of a sparse complex matrix instead. The result is equal to that called on the original matrix.?For example, instead of calling equilibrate(A), call equilibrate(abs(A)).

Summary: MEXCUDA does not support Visual Studio 2019 as a host compiler
Description: ?Attempting to use Visual Studio 2019 as a host compiler to compile?MEX-functions for GPU computations results in the following warning: ?Warning: The selected C++ compiler is not supported for CUDA compilation. Searching for a supported compiler.  MEXCUDA then attempts to compile using an available supported compiler.??
Workaround: To use Visual Studio 2019 as a host compiler for MEXCUDA, use the attached custom configuration files. Specify the filepath to the configuration file using the option -f and the name of the file.To compile a MEX-function from a CUDA C++ file, use the following steps:      Download the attached zip file    Unzip the downloaded file into the same location as the source file to be compiled?    Execute the following command, replacing mexGPUExample.cu with the name of the file to be compiled:      mexcuda -f 'nvcc_msvcpp2019.xml' mexGPUExample.cu       If you are compiling with dynamic parallelism (the -dynamic option) use the alternative options file (nvcc_msvcpp2019_dynamic.xml) as follows:        mexcuda -dynamic -f 'nvcc_msvcpp2019_dynamic.xml' mexGPUExample.cu        Change log :November 20, 2019: attachment_2059103_19b_win64_2019_11_27.zip: initial version

Summary: Large threshold for crossing timer or counter in unit level design effects performance of test generation analysis
Description: Performance time for test generation analysis is impacted in smaller unit level designs when threshold steps for timer or counter is large.  The analysis completes much faster when threshold is kept relatively smaller (few thousand steps).

Summary: Duplicated signals in the Signal Editor hierarchy?cause tree reordering
Description: When the?Signal Editor hierarchy?has two or more?signals with the same name, duplicating?one of these signals from the toolstrip or context menu does not create a new signal. Instead, it reorders the signals in the tree.

Summary: MATLAB may crash or assert with messages "Critical Section Interleaved" or "Critical Section Statements out of order"
Description: MATLAB may crash or assert when simulating or generating code from a Simulink model that meets any of the following conditions:? ?The model contains one or more Rate Transition blocks (might be hidden). ?The model configuration parameter Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer  is set to on.  ?The model simulation mode is either Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator.  Following two stack trace messages might be seen:"Critical Section Interleaved " "Critical Section Statements out of order "

Summary: Incorrect address of model parameter argument in C-API
Description: When using a model parameter argumentin a model, the access to the parameter in the model_InitializeDataAddr? methodof C-API may be incorrect.  Insteadof: / * Initialize Data Address */  static void model_InitializeDataAddr(void* dataAddr[], ?InstP_model_T* model_InstP) {  ? dataAddr[0] = (void*) (&top_17b_P->p1);?  }?The generatedcode might incorrectly access the parameteras:  dataAddr[0] = (void*) (&model_M->model_InstP_ref->p1);  Thiscode fails to compile as the model_M  variable isn't passed intothe?InitializeDataAddr  function.?

Summary: Segmentation violation when switching from multitasking to single tasking mode with Code Mappings editor open
Description: A segmentation violation?occurs when performing an?update diagram on a model with all of these conditions:The Code Mappings editor is open. In the Code Mappings editor, the Entry-Point Functions  tab is selected. On the Solver  pane, the configuration parameter Treat each discrete rate as a separate task  checkbox is changed from selected to cleared. In other words, the setting is changed from multitasking execution to single-tasking execution.?
Workaround: Switch to another tab in the Code Mappings editor when performing the update diagram.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Sum block generates incorrect HDL Code when the List of signs parameter has the value minus (-)
Description: When a Sum block has the?List of signs ?parameter set to the value minus (-) , the generated HDL Code uses sum of elements followed by unary minus.?This results in a mismatch from the Simulink block behavior.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when simulating a model with C Caller block in Rapid Accelerator mode or through a Model block in Accelerator mode
Description: MATLAB can crash when simulating a model containing a C Caller block in Rapid Accelerator mode. The crash can also occur when simulating a model containing a C Caller block through a Model block in Accelerator mode. In either crash scenario, it happens when the C Caller block's Function name parameter points to an nonexistent C function name. The crash stack when simulating in Rapid Accelerator mode is similar to the following: [  0] 0x0000000256f482a0 CCFunctionCallerBlock::checkRapidAccelTarget at b:\matlab\toolbox\shared\slcc\src\slcc_blocks\src\ccfunctioncallerblock.cpp:945[  1] 0x0000000256f40aae CCFunctionCallerBlock::EvalDlgPrms at b:\matlab\toolbox\shared\slcc\src\slcc_blocks\src\ccfunctioncallerblock.cpp:991[  2] 0x000000013d411ecb BlockEvalPrmsCompilePhase at b:\matlab\src\simulink\sl_obj\blockevalprms.cpp:681[  3] 0x000000013d4121c9 CoreBlockEvalPrms at b:\matlab\src\simulink\sl_obj\blockevalprms.cpp:806 The crash stack when simulating through a Model block in Accelerator mode is similar to the following: [  0] 0x000000000603804c CCFunctionCallerBlock::checkMdlRefTarget at b:\matlab\toolbox\shared\slcc\src\slcc_blocks\src\ccfunctioncallerblock.cpp:929[  1] 0x0000000006030aa6 CCFunctionCallerBlock::EvalDlgPrms at b:\matlab\toolbox\shared\slcc\src\slcc_blocks\src\ccfunctioncallerblock.cpp:989[  2] 0x00000002291a1ecb BlockEvalPrmsCompilePhase at b:\matlab\src\simulink\sl_obj\blockevalprms.cpp:681[  3] 0x00000002291a21c9 CoreBlockEvalPrms at b:\matlab\src\simulink\sl_obj\blockevalprms.cpp:806

Summary: Code generation fails when specifying a variable-size table input argument with variables having the categorical, duration, or table data types
Description: When generating code, using coder.typeof  to specify a variable-size table with variable number of rows might fail. In particular, if the table contains variables having the categorical  or duration  data types, or if the table is a nested table, then the following error message is returned: coder.resize is not supported for a coder.ClassType object.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model build of Simulink Real-Time model that includes vector or matrix root inport data may generate wrong answer
Description: When you build a Simulink Real-Time model that receives vector or matrix data --multiple entries per time step-- through root level inports, the generated code produces a? wrong answer. The wrong answer is that the data is not arranged properly. This issue can be difficult to locate and diagnose.

Summary: Model build of Simulink Real-Time model that includes padded bus root inport data fails during code generation
Description: When you build a Simulink Real-Time model that includes padded bus root inport data, the model build fails during code generation. The build error message is: Unable to perform assignment because the left and right sides have a different number of elements.   In generated code, a Simulink Bus appears as a C struct. When the Bus contains different data types, the code generator forms a padded struct in generated code. For example, a Bus that contains an int8 ?data type and a double ?data type has byte padding in the generated code and will encounter this bug.

Summary: Invalid error for Data Store Memory block specifying symbolic dimensions
Description: An invalid error message might be reported when simulating or generating code for a model containing a Data Store Memory block when all of the following conditions are true: The Data Store Memory block resolves to a Simuink.Signal  object. The?Simuink.Signal?object specifies symbolic dimensions. The configuration parameter Allow symbolic dimension specification ?is cleared.  The error message is similar to: The 'Dimensions' parameter of the Data Store Memory block 'model/Data Store Memory' must match the symbolic dimensions of the object 'MySignal' that it resolves to. To disable symbolic dimension propagation, search for 'Allow symbolic dimension specification' in the Configuration Parameters dialog box and clear the checkbox.  The error message is invalid because?Allow symbolic dimension specification ?is already cleared.
Workaround: Set the dimensions of the?Simuink.Signal?object to be their numeric, non-symbolic equivalent.

Summary: Pedestrian model outputs incorrect velocity estimation when the sampling interval changes
Description: When calling the move  method on phased.BackscatterPedestrian  or backscatterPedestrian , the resulting velocity of body segments will be incorrect if the duration of the next walking interval changes in consecutive calls. For example: [bppos,bpvel,bpax] = move(pedestrian,t1,0);??[bppos,bpvel,bpax] = move(pedestrian,t2,0);

Summary: In Signal Analyzer, fitting spectrum plots to view for complex-valued signals results in inconsistent limits between the spectrum view and the spectrogram view
Description: In Signal Analyzer, if you zoom into a spectrum plot of a complex-valued signal and then reset the view by clicking?Fit to View , the resulting spectrogram and spectrum views in the app have different frequency-axis limits.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model with a Data Store Memory block that is written to from inside a subsystem and read from outside the subsystem
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: A?Data Store Write block?is in a subsystem and writes to a Data Store Memory block. A Data Store Read block reads from the same Data Store Memory block and connects to an inport of the subsystem. The Data Store Read block is used and evaluated after the subsystem is evaluated.? In the subsystem block parameters dialog box, the?Function Packaging ?parameter is set to?Reusable function .  In the generated code the Data Store Memory value is incorrectly updated after the subsystem function call.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 7, R2019b, R2020a

Summary: MATLAB might crash when generating code for Multiply-Accumulate block with matrix inputs
