Description: MATLAB might crash when generating HDL code for a Multiply-Accumulate block with: HDL Architecture  set to Parallel  in the HDL Block Properties dialog box? Operation Mode  is set to Vector  in Block Parameters dialog box? Inputs to the block are matrices

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model with a nested?For Each?subsystem block
Description: Generated code might be incorrect for a modelthat meets the following conditions: The model contains a For Each subsystem block nested inside another For Each subsystem block. The?Function Packaging ? parameter of theinner For Each?subsystem block is set to Reusable . In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the?Pass reusable subsystem outputs as ?parameter is set to? Structure reference .

Summary: BridgeMode property cannot be set to 'Half' or 'Full'
Description: Setting a bridge channel BridgeMode  property value to 'Half'  or 'Full'  silently fails, and its value remains at the default value of 'Quarter' , even though the display reflects the requested change. The issue affects the following example: s = daq.createSession('ni');ch = addAnalogInputChannel(s, "cDAQ1Mod2","ai0","Bridge")ch =Data acquisition analog input channel 'ai0' on device 'cDAQ1Mod4':             BridgeMode: Unknown       ExcitationSource: Internal      ExcitationVoltage: 2.5NominalBridgeResistance: 'Unknown'                  Range: -0.025 to +0.025 VoltsPerVolt                   Name: ''                     ID: 'ai0'                 Device: [1x1 daq.ni.CompactDAQModule]        MeasurementType: 'Bridge'          ADCTimingMode: '' s.Channels(1).BridgeMode = 'Half'ch =Data acquisition analog input channel 'ai0' on device 'cDAQ1Mod4':             BridgeMode: Half       ExcitationSource: Internal      ExcitationVoltage: 2.5NominalBridgeResistance: 'Unknown'                  Range: -0.025 to +0.025 VoltsPerVolt                   Name: ''                     ID: 'ai0'                 Device: [1x1 daq.ni.CompactDAQModule]        MeasurementType: 'Bridge'          ADCTimingMode: ''  Despite the fact that the BridgeMode  property value is reported as 'Half' , its actual value is 'Quarter' .
Fixed In: R2018b Update 5, R2019a Update 5, R2019b Update 1, R2020a

Summary: Chapter reporter ignores page number format from custom template
Description: For chapters other than the first chapter of a PDF report, the Report API Chapter  reporter ignores the page number format from a custom template.
Workaround: Customize the page number format for each chapter by using the?Chapter?reporter's?Layout property. For example:ch = mlreportgen.report.Chapter("MyChapter"); ch.Layout.PageNumberFormat = "A";

Summary: Create test for model component wizard errors if the model uses Simulink.Signal' objects
Description: When the Create test for model component wizard generates tests for a subsystem containing Simulink.Signal elements, this error message occurs : Subsystem1: The Simulink signal object 'sigObj1' for output port 1 of 'mSigObjects/? Subsystem1/? Gain' has logging information set. The LoggingInfo field of the Signal object is only supported for Data Store Memory and must not be set for signals.

Summary: Vehicle Body 3DOF block in dual track mode neglects track width in wheel force calculation
Description: If you set the Vehicle Body 3DOF block parameter Vehicle track  to Dual , the block neglects the track width in the wheel force, FzF  and FzR , calculations. If you set?Vehicle track ?to?Dual ,?the block correctly calculates the wheel forces.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated for coding pattern with the same structure read and written in the same assignment
Description: Simulink might generate code with incorrect read/write ordering for a MATLAB statement containing: A structure variable written as the destination of an assignment A field of the same structure read in the same statement The structure contains multiple fields  For example, see the attached model issue_2054062_model in attached issue_2054062.zip. This model contains a MATLAB function with the statement: s1 = A(s1.f2);? In this statement s1.f2 is read before writing to s1. But the generated code is  s1_f1 = A[s1_f2].f1;s1_f2 = A[s1_f2].f2;s1_f3 = A[s1_f2].f3;s1_f4 = A[s1_f2].f4; Note that the last two statements read s1_f2 after it has been written. Instead the value of s1_f2 must be stored in a temporary like the following: temp = s1_f2 s1_f1 = A[temp].f1;s1_f2 = A[temp].f2;s1_f3 = A[temp].f3;s1_f4 = A[temp].f4; This issue can affect a model containing:?  MATLAB Function Block MATLAB Function in Stateflow MATLAB System Block   When generating code or running rapid accelerator simulation.
Workaround: This issue is due to an optimization within the code generator that explodes structure access into multiple variables representing individual structure fields. This optimization is not properly accounting for the nested structure access in the assignment:s1 = A(s1.f2); As a workaround, this optimization can be disabled using coder.ceval:s1 = A(s1.f2); coder.ceval('(void)',s1);In the generated code, the structure s1 will not be exploded into its fields and the issue does not occur.s1 = A[s1.f2];(void)s1;

Summary: Double-clicking a commented Bus Creator block might crash Simulink
Description: Double-clicking?a commented Bus Creator block might crash Simulink.

Summary: Design error detection analysis may run out of memory or report an internal error
Description: When analyzing a model for design errors, memory usage may increase after each processed design error check. If the model is large and has many design error checks, Simulink Design Verifier may run out of memory or report an internal error. The error message reported in the summary window is as follows:? Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier back end.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 5, R2019a Update 5, R2019b, R2020a

Summary: After saving or running an existing .mlapp file in App Designer, recent code changes cannot be undone by pressing?Ctrl+Z.
Description: After saving or running an existing .mlapp  file in App Designer, recent code changes cannot be undone by pressing?Ctrl+Z .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: strip removes only '' in the generated code
Description: In generated code, strip  removes only ''. ? It does not remove other white space characters, such as tab and new line.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model containing Enabled Synchronous Subsystem or Resettable Synchronous Subsystem and?Single output/update function?parameter set for referenced model with states might generate incorrect C code
Description: Embedded Coder might generate numerically incorrect code when a model meets all the following conditions: In the referenced model, Model configuration parameter Single output/update function ?parameter is selected. The model contains synchronous semantics. The model has states.  AND  In the top model, Model configuration parameter Single output/update function ?parameter is selected. The referenced model is inside an Enabled Synchronous Subsystem or a Resettable Synchronous Subsystem.  The results obtained from running this C code might differ from simulation results.??

Summary: Adding tests for missing coverage from test case results produces aggregated results only for added tests
Description: From test case results, if you auto generate tests using the Add tests for missing coverage button , when you run the test case, the?aggregated coverage results only show the coverage for the newly added tests.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Linear time-varying Simscape blocks such as Variable Capacitor generate incorrect HDL implementation model
Description: When the Simscape model contains linear time-varying blocks such as Variable Capacitor or Variable Inductor, the Simscape HDL Workflow Advisor generates an incorrect HDL implementation model.? ?

Summary: App Designer message panel in GUIDE causes high idle CPU usage and might cause MATLAB to become unresponsive
Description: When GUIDE is open and the App Designer message panel is displayed, the following issues can occur: The computer's idle CPU usage increases by 10 - 30% potentially impacting the performance of all running applications on the system. Some charting interactions, such as trying to add a data tip, can cause MATLAB to become unresponsive or the action to not take place.
Workaround: Clear the "Show App Designer message panel" check box in the MATLAB GUIDE preferences.

Summary: Values of UserData parameter values lost when disabled library links are broken
Description: If a model containing a disabled library link is loaded, and a block inside the library link has a non-empty UserData parameter value, the the UserData parameters must be loaded from the SLX file before breaking the library link.
Workaround: Ensure that the values of UserData parameters for blocks inside the disabled library link are loaded from the SLX file before breaking the link.? Use the "get_param" command to load the? UserData parameters. For example:? get_param(Simulink.findBlocks(bdroot),'UserData');The same issue will affect other operations which cause the value of the SID parameter for a block to change.

Summary: Polyspace analysis stops in pre-analysis phase with error: Internal error: *** Assertion failure ***
Description: When verifying a complex C application, Polyspace can stop during the pre-analysis phase with this error message: **** Bug-finder - Pre-analysis - 4 (P_MA) - timeout - **** Bug-finder - Pre-analysis - 4 (P_MA) took xxxx.yyreal,? ?xxxx.yyu + zs (xxx.yygc)**** Bug-finder - Pre-analysis - 5 (P_G)Internal error: *** Assertion failure ***
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder Server, Polyspace Bug Finder

Summary: Aerospace Toolbox function aeroReadIERSData does not support reading of IERS finals2000A.all?files
Description: Aerospace Toolbox function aeroReadIERSData  expects the first 10 rows of Earth orientation parameter data in IERS data files to be valid IERS provided data, not predicted data.? The following criteria must be met, given the file format described in?readme.finals2000A :? First 10 rows of column 17 must equal 'I' First 10 rows of column 58 must equal 'I' First 10 rows of column 96 must equal 'I'
Workaround: If data for dates between January 1973 and January 1992 is not required, use finals2000A.data files.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Temporal logic operators in standalone Stateflow chart incorrectly evaluated as true when used in hierarchy
Description: In a standalone Stateflow chart for execution in MATLAB, if the chart contains multiple temporal logic operators at different levels of state hierarchy, the transition might be incorrectly evaluated as true sooner than expected if the step  function or an input event is called while one of the timers is pending. For example, if a transition between two child states is guarded by after(3,sec) , and a transition between their parent and another state guarded by after(4,sec) , the transition exiting the parent might incorrectly be taken sooner than the one between the children.

Summary: The Simulink Toolstrip does not render correctly when using a per-application DPI override on Windows
Description: The Simulink Toolstrip might fail to render as expected when moved between monitors on Windows 10.? When encountering this problem, the Simulink Toolstrip renders at an inappropriate size--for example, it might only cover a quarter of the expected area. ?The problem occurs when the following conditions are true:  The affected system has two or more monitors The monitors have differing DPI scaling values A per-application DPI scaling override for MATLAB is applied
Workaround: Open Windows Explorer and navigate to your MATLAB install directory.Right click "matlab.exe" and select "Properties".View the "Compatibility" tab.Click the button labeled "Change high DPI settings".Deselect the checkbox labeled "Override high DPI scaling behavior."Click "OK" to close this dialog.Click "OK" to close the matlab.exe Properties dialog.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Model compatibility check produces inaccurate warning message if a Stateflow state uses unsupported MATLAB code
Description: When you run the model compatibility check Check usage of Stateflow MATLAB action language  , the subcheck Check for unsupported MATLAB code in Stateflow transitions  might produce a warning about unsupported MATLAB code on a Stateflow transition when it should instead warn about a Stateflow state.To resolve this bug, Simulink Code Inspector identifies whether the check target is a state or transition and produce an accurate warning message.

Summary: Table iterations with logged signal set cause errors in Test Manager
Description: If you use signal logging overrides with iterations for signals inside model references or?observers, this error occurs:Specified Key is not present in this container

Summary: Update diagram might fail for a top model when a referenced model uses an enumeration type defined in its own data dictionary
Description: Update diagram fails for a top model when all of the following conditions are true: The top model and a model it references each link to their own data dictionary. The data dictionary for the top model does not link to the data dictionary for the referenced model. The referenced model uses a Simulink.Bus  object that is not used by the top model. This Simulink.Bus  object refers to an enumeration type definition defined in the referenced model's data dictionary.  The error message incorrectly reports that the parent model is using the enumeration type defined in the referenced model's data dictionary.
Workaround: Create a shared data dictionary, such as Shared.sldd.Create the enumeration type definition in?Shared.sldd.For the referenced model that?uses the enumeration type, create references from?data dictionaries?used by this?model reference to Shared.sldd.Remove the?same?enumeration type definitions?from data dictionaries?other?than the one in Shared.sldd.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: AUTOSAR generated code might write uninitialized value if IRV feeds back to runnable
Description: AUTOSAR generated code might write an uninitialized value to an IRV if all of the following conditions exist: The model contains a subsystem that is mapped to an AUTOSAR runnable. The subsystem writes to a signal that is mapped to an AUTOSAR IRV. The subsystem reads from the IRV signal that it writes.  Under these conditions, the generated code might write an uninitialized variable.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Missing function calls for variable-size signals propagated from root input to root output ports of model in reference hierarchy that specifies SIL or PIL
Description: Consider a model A , which contains a Model block with block parameter Simulation mode  set to  Software-in-the-loop (SIL)  or  Processor-in-the-loop (PIL) . The Model block references model  B , which in turn references model  C . The generated code for model  B ? can differ from the code that is generated if the Model block parameter Simulation mode  is set to Normal  or Accelerator  and all of these conditions apply to model C :  There are root input or output ports that have variable dimensions.  The model configuration parameter Propagate sizes of variable-size signals  is set to Infer from blocks in model .  The variable dimensions of at least one root output port are propagated from the variable dimensions of a root input port.   When the Model block parameter Simulation mode  is set to  Normal  or Accelerator , these function calls might be present in the generated code for model B :    modelC_SetDims(...)   modelC_FinalizeDims(...)   When the Model block parameter Simulation mode  is set to Software-in-the-loop (SIL)  or  Processor-in-the-loop (PIL) , the function calls are not present in the generated code for model B . You might observe incorrect simulation results.
Workaround: For each referenced model below the model referenced by the Model block with parameter Simulation mode set to? Software-in-the-loop (SIL) or Processor-in-the-loop (PIL), set the model configuration parameter?Propagate sizes of variable-size signals? to During Execution.Alternatively:Delete the code that was generated with the Model block configured for SIL or PIL.For each model in the model reference hierarchy that references the model with variable-size signal propagation, set the simulation mode to normal or acceleration.Regenerate code.

Summary: Polyspace analysis stops after compilation with error: A segmentation fault occurred in "ps_cxx_fe_ia64.exe"
Description: When verifying a complex C++ application, Polyspace can stop during the compilation phase with an error messages like one of the following: Internal error: assertion failed at: ".../src/expr.c", line 5541  Error: A segmentation fault occurred in "ps_cxx_fe_ia64.exe"

Summary: Broken traceability linkfor Delay block with Reset type?set to None?following a Lookup Table
Description: Delay block with?Reset type ?set to?None ?following a Lookup Table maps to a ROM in thegenerated HDL code, which causes a broken link in the traceability report.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect initial value for model argument in generated code when argument is written by Parameter Writer block
Description: Simulink generates code with incorrect initial values for the model arguments of a Model block when the following conditions are true: The model argument of the Model block is written by a Parameter Writer block. Simulation mode  for the model is set to Accelerator .

Summary: Code inspection fails for a model containing Boolean datatype that is imported from C99 math library
Description: A Simulink model that is configured to use C99 math library fails code inspection if the model uses a Boolean datatype that is imported from stdbool.h.

Summary: Observer Port blocks observing non-virtual bus with different data type elements might produce incorrect output
Description: If a non-virtual bus being observed by an Observer Port block has bus elements of different data types, the Observer Port block output might not match the signal being observed. In the non-virtual bus output of the Observer Port block, one or more bus elements near the end of the bus object structure will be constantly zero, regardless of their actual value in the system model. No error or warning is issued during model compile or simulation.?

Summary: Parallel build generates makefile with wrong optimization flags when 'BuildConfiguration' for referenced model is 'Faster Runs'
Description: If your model contains a model reference hierarchy and the?'BuildConfiguration' ?parameter for a referenced model is set to?'Faster Runs' , then building the referenced models in parallel generates a makefile with the wrong optimization flags. The generated makefile contains the optimization flags for 'Faster Builds'  instead of the flags for?'Faster Runs' .  The parallel worker uses the correct optimization flags to build the referenced model binaries. However, if you build the referenced model binaries with the generated makefile, the incorrect optimization flags are used.  ?
Workaround: Insert the correct optimization flags by manually modifying the generated makefile.

Summary: Signal Analyzer does not plot spectra of complex-valued signals generated by extracting regions of interest from timetables
Description: In Signal Analyzer, if you import a timetable with complex-valued and then generate a new signal by extracting a region of interest, the app produces an empty spectrum plot.

Summary: Inadvertentchanges to properties of a copied reporter, when the property is a reporter orDOM object
Description: Some Report API reporters have properties that are set to reporter or DOM object values. When a reporter is copied using the copy method, only the references to the reporter or DOM objects in these properties are copied. Any modifications to the reporter or DOM object properties of one reporter also affect the other reporter because the properties of both reporters reference the same reporter or DOM object. The consequences of this issue vary based on the reporter. For example, mlreportgen.report.MATLABVariable  has a property, ParagraphFormatter , that is a DOM Paragraph object used to format the value of the MATLAB variable. In the following code, both MATLABVariable  reporters display paragraphs in italics, even though only the copied reporter's ParagraphFormatter  property is modified: import mlreportgen.report.* mlVar1 = MATLABVariable('x'); mlVar2 = copy(mlVar1); mlVar2.Variable = 'y'; mlVar2.ParagraphFormatter.Italic = true;     Other reporters such as slreportgen.report.Diagram  and slreportgen.report.SimulinkObjectProperties  add content directly to properties that are reporter objects. Adding copies of these reporters to a report results in the same content being added to the report for each reporter, despite any modifications to copied reporters. For example, the following code creates a report with two identical tables that both report the properties of the block? vdp/Mu :  import slreportgen.report.* load_system('vdp'); rpt = Report('exampleReport','pdf'); obj1 = SimulinkObjectProperties('vdp/Mu'); obj2 = copy(obj1); obj2.Object = 'vdp/x1'; add(rpt, obj1); add(rpt, obj2); close(rpt); rptview(rpt);
Workaround: After copying a reporter, use the clone method to copy DOM object properties:chTitle = mlreportgen.dom.Text('example title');?rptr1 = mlreportgen.report.Chapter(chTitle);?rptr1Copy = copy(rptr1);?rptr1Copy.Title = clone(rptr1.Title);?Use the?copy?method to copy reporter object properties:load_system('vdp');?rptr2 = slreportgen.report.SimulinkObjectProperties('vdp');?rptr2Copy = copy(rptr2);?rptr2Copy.PropertyTable = copy(rptr2.PropertyTable);??

Summary: Adding or removing several components from an App Designer app causes a slowdown in App Designer performance
Description: Adding or removing components repeatedly in an App Designer app causes a memory leak in the Component Browser. As the leaked memory increases, App Designer performance slows down. The issue can be reproduced and quantitatively analyzed by adding and removing a button repeatedly 50 times, which causes 9.28 MB of memory leak.
Workaround: To work around the slowdown caused by the memory leak, restart MATLAB.

Summary: HDL coding standard report shows violation for Check for non-integer constants when floating-point library is specified
Description: In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the HDL Code Generation > Global Settings > Coding standards  tab, when you set HDL coding standard  to Industry  and generate code, the HDL coding standard report shows a violation for the check Check for non-integer constants  even when?Floating Point IP Library  is set to Native Floating Point  or other target floating-point libraries.

Summary: Error "Undefined function 'isvalid'" for code that calls uifigure and axes in debugger
Description: MATLAB returns an error when both of the following conditions are true: You run the debugger using dbstop if error , or you use the Pause on Error  option Your code creates an axes either in App Designer, or in a figure created with the uifigure  function  The error returns the following message: Undefined function 'isvalid' for input arguments of type 'double'.  Error in matlab.graphics.interaction.graphicscontrol.ControlManager/removeControl (line 62)  if(isvalid(this)) %Makes sure that this object is still valid and not destroyed ealier.  Error in matlab.graphics.shape.internal.ScribeStackManager/createLayer (line 62)  ? ? ? ? ? ? set(hOverlay,'Parent',matlab.graphics.primitive.world.Group.empty);  Error in matlab.graphics.shape.internal.ScribeStackManager/getLayer (line 81)  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? hLayer = matlab.graphics.shape.internal.ScribeStackManager.createLayer(hViewer,layerName,[]);  Error in matlab.graphics.internal.CanvasSetup.createScribeLayers (line 13)  ? ? ? ? ? ? if? isempty(cM.getLayer(canvas,'overlay'))  Error in matlab.graphics.controls.HTMLCanvasPlugin/createCanvas (line 14)  ? ? ? ? ? ? can = hObj.Factory.createCanvas();
Workaround: Refrain from either the dbstop if error option or the Pause on Error option in the debugger. Instead, set a standard break point in the debugger to step through your code.

Summary: Zoom-in mode does not work after restoring the original view of plots with legends and linked axes
Description: Zoom-in mode does not work after restoring the original view of plots with legends and linked axes. For example, create two subplots with legends and link the axes.?  ax1 = subplot(2,1,1);legend(ax1)ax2 = subplot(2,1,2);legend(ax2)linkaxes([ax1 ax2])  Turn on zoom-in mode, zoom into one of the plots, and then restore the original view of the plot. When you do subsequent zoom operations, they have no effect.?  Additionally, if you restore the original view of the plot and then zoom by scrolling, MATLAB??throws an error such as ˇ°Invalid or deleted objectˇ±.?

Summary: MATLAB crashes when evaluating a block parameter with the model name as its value
Description: For masked blocks, changing the value of the parameter to the name of the model results in a crash. Updating the diagram results in a similar crash.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for?a model containing a?Unit Delay block inside a?For Each Subsystem block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a Unit Delay block inside a For Each subsystem block. In the For Each Subsystem Block Parameters dialog box, the Function packaging  parameter is set to  Inline .  In the For Each Subsystem Block parameters dialog box, the Minimize algebraic loop occurrences ?parameter is selected .  In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Single output/update function ?parameter is selected .   In the generated code, the input buffer of the Unit Delay block may be incorrectly localized into a single scalar type variable without considering the slice instances.?
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 7, R2019b, R2020a

Summary: Schedule Editor comparison in Model Comparison tool is missing.
Description: Contents of Schedule Editor are not compared in the Model Comparison tool. This does not affect the comparison of other features of the model.?

Summary: Incorrect expected results for Simulink Check test case SLCHK_SL_Q010
Description: In test case SLCHK_SL_Q010  for model DesignMergeBlkUsage_WARN.slx , the expected results are missing the results for one of the sub-checks. Expected results for test model DesignMergeBlkUsage_WARN.slx  should be "Failed, Failed, Failed, Failed, Failed, Failed, Warning".?

Summary: Simulating a referenced model that inherits a constant sample time might produce an error
Description: Simulink might display an error when you simulate a model hierarchy in accelerator or rapid accelerator mode or build a model reference simulation target when all of the following conditions are true:? A model hierarchy contains a referenced model that inherits a constant sample time from its parent model. The parent model has a sample time that is a union of two or more discrete sample times and has a constant sample time.  The message in the diagnostic viewer will have some combination of the following warnings and/or errors: differs in levels of indirection  different types for formal and actual parameter  pointer mismatch for actual parameter  too few arguments to call

Summary: Mismatch between actual result and expected result for Simulink Code Inspector qualification test
Description: The expected result details for Simulink Code Inspector qualification test Variant_neg  are incorrect. The correct details are: (CODE, Variant2.c:99, UNABLE_TO_PROCESS),?  (CODE, Variant2.c:100, UNABLE_TO_PROCESS),?  (CODE, Variant2.c:101, UNABLE_TO_PROCESS),?  (BLOCK, ModelVariants1/Variant_ref1, FAILED_TO_VERIFY),?  (BLOCK, ModelVariants2/Variant_ref3, FAILED_TO_VERIFY),?  (BLOCK, ModelVariants3/Variant_ref2, FAILED_TO_VERIFY),?  (BLOCK, Variant Subsystem2/Subsystem2/Gain, FAILED_TO_VERIFY)?

Summary: Simulink Check qualification test case SLCHK_HI_Q076 fails unexpectedly
Description: Simulink Check test case?SLCHK_HI_Q076?  in the DO Qualification Kit fails with the following error when Simulink tries to compile model?hisf_0003_WARN.slx ?because of an unsupported data type: Error:  ? ? { l_bool = (l_u8*l_u16) ^ l_i64 } ? ?? Stateflow supports access to multi-word fixed point variables only as inputs and outputs to nested MATLAB functions, MATLAB-based Truth Table functions, and Simulink functions. Direct access to variable 'l_i64' in the Stateflow action language is therefore illegal in the above context.?  Occurred in chart 'hisf_0003_WARN/sfChartC_12Warnings', '% Warn...l_i64 }'.
Workaround: 1. Copy the matlabroot/toolbox/qualkits/do/slchk folder and subfolders to a location where you have write access.2.? In the /tests directory, open model hisf_0003_WARN.slx.3. Open the Model Explorer.?4.? Under sfChartC_12Warnings, change the data type of local variable l_i64 to int32, then apply.5. Save the model.?

Summary: Aerospace Blockset functions computeLongitudinalFlyingQualities and computeLateralDirectionalFlyingQualities?may misclassify response modes against MIL-F-8785C standards under certain flight conditions
Description: Aerospace Blockset functions computeLongitudinalFlyingQualities  and computeLateralDirectionalFlyingQualities ?misclassify response modes when the roots of the computed characteristic equation meet one of these response criterion: Longitudinal or lateral-directional root(s) with negative undamped natural frequency Lateral-directional?root(s) with negative time constant Longitudinal short period mode with damping ratio less than 0.3 or greater than 2.0 Longitudinal complex conjugate roots where the root with the higher frequency of oscillation has the lower damping ratio

Summary: The Model Advisor check "Check for comparison operations in Stateflow charts" does not display the "Inconclusive situations" warning for some variables defined in header file that are used in logical expressions.
Description: The Model Advisor check "Check for comparison operations in Stateflow charts" (ID: mathworks.maab.na_0013) checks for post-compile data types in Stateflow when comparison operations are performed. The issue happens when the action language of the Stateflow chart is set to "C" and some transitions involving several macros defined in header file.  For example, the comparison operation [ in_data > TEN ] is reported but [in_data > ZERO && in_data <= TEN] is not reported. In this particular case ZERO and TEN are macros defined in a header file (so they do not have a data type).?

Summary: DOM API ignores VAlign format for formal table header entries in PDF output
Description: For PDF reports, report generation ignores the VAlign  format for header table entries of a formal table represented by an mlreportgen.dom.FormalTable  object. For example, in the table generated from this script, content is not vertically aligned to the middle of header entries.  import mlreportgen.dom.*d = Document('output','pdf');open(d);  t = FormalTable({'Col1','Col2'}, {'entry1','entry2'});t.Border = 'solid';t.RowSep = 'solid';t.ColSep = 'solid';  t.Header.TableEntriesStyle = { ...??? Height('1in'), ...??? VAlign('middle') };append(d,t);  close(d); rptview(d.OutputPath);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for?a model containing an?output port of a reusable MATLAB Function block connected to a Terminator block
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for a model that meets all of the following conditions: The model contains a MATLAB Function block. The MATLAB Function block has the block parameter Treat as atomic unit ?enabled and Function packaging ?parameter is set to Reusable function . The MATLAB Function block has more than one input and output ports.? The same variable is used for both an input and an output of the MATLAB Function block and the output port is connected to a Terminator block.??  In the generated code the input port data is not passed.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect numerical results might occur for a model containing a referenced model with at least two outputs feeding a Concatenation block
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for a model that contains a referenced model with at least two outputs feeding the same Concatenation block.
Workaround: Move the Concatenation block inside the source referenced model or inside a new reusable subsystem. Both of these strategies will avoid the generated code containing a call to a model reference function with an array (with offsets) as arguments.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect row-major code generation for Product of Elements block in All Dimensions mode
Description: The code generator might generate incorrect row-major code for a model when all of these conditions are true: Model configuration parameter Array layout  is set to Row-major . Model configuration parameter Use algorithms optimized for row-major array layout  is not selected. Model contains Product of Elements block with Multiply over  parameter set to All dimensions . For the Product of Elements block, Accumulator data type  is different than input data type.
Workaround: Don't use row vectors or leading 1's when generating row-major code when Multiply over parameter set to All dimensions and Accumulator data type is different than input type. For example:Instead of feeding 1x2 matrix to Product of Elements block, use an array of size 2.Instead of feeding 1x3x4 matrix to Product of Elements block, use a matrix of 3x4.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for?a model containing a?Unit Delay block inside a?For Each Subsystem block
Description: The generated code contains an incorrect initialization of a Unit Delay block state for a model that meets all of these conditions: The Unit Delay block is inside a For Each subsystem. The Initial Condition  of the Unit Delay block initialization is specified by a parameter instead of a fixed value. In the For Each Subsystem block parameters dialog box, the?Parameter Partition ?is selected .
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 6, R2019b Update 2, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Complex to Real-Imag block with constant input and test-pointed output generates uninitialized code
Description: The code generated for a Complex to Real-Imag in your Simulink model is uninitialized if the following conditions are met: One of the output ports of the Complex to Real-Imag block is marked as a test point. The input to the Complex to Real-Imag block?is fed from a Constant block.?
Workaround: The workaround would be to remove the test-point.

Summary: Assertion error during code generation when assigning a Stateflow variable with custom code variable
Description: Stateflow might throw an assertion error during code generation when all of the following conditions are true: A Stateflow chart contains two transitions originating from one junction and ending on the another junction Both transitions assign the same Stateflow variable The first transition assigns the Stateflow variable with a custom code variable, for example, with the transition label [in > 0] {out = customCodeVar;}  The second transition assigns the Stateflow variable with a non custom code variable or a constant, for example with the transition label {out = 1.1;}  The custom code variable is defined with a type alias, for example typedef float MyReal32_T  The option Import custom code  is selected on the Simulation Target pane of configuration options  The assertion error is similar to: CGIR assertion 'SaneConditionalExprs violated: conditional expr output type and false expr type must match' failed in 'invariant/SaneConditionalExprs.cpp:71'?

Summary: slreq.createLink(src, dest)?errors if one input is?slreq.Reference, or when?performing Clear Issue action
Description: When importing Requirements from .reqif file with the default options, you get a hierarchical collection of slreq.Reference objects. If you later want to create a link from or to?such an?object, an error might display at the MATLAB?command prompt: "Invalid argument in a call to slreq.createLink", or other similar error to do with invalid input argument type. Additionally, when using "Clear Issue" action for the link flagged with a change issue (due to changes with the linked requirement), a dialog box might appear which says "DATESTR failed converting date number to date vector".

Summary: Invalid parameters in a real-time test case cause MATLAB crash when refreshing parameters
Description: In a real-time test case, if some parameters are not valid real-time parameters, clicking the refresh parameter button crashes MATLAB. For example, invalid real-time parameters might be in the parameter override table after converting a test case to a real-time test case.

Summary: Simulink Web View displays a blank screen when using Firefox 68+
Description: Starting in Firefox 68+, Firefox no longer allows local files to access other files in the same directory.? This change prevents Simulink Web View from loading the required files.
Workaround: ?Simulink Web View cannot be viewed in Firefox 68+ because of local file security restrictions. To allow local file access:Type about:config into the Firefox location bar.Search for privacy.file_unique_origin and set value to false.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Reciprocal block with ShiftAdd architecture can generate incorrect HDL code for all rounding modes with saturation mode cleared
Description: For the Reciprocal block with ShiftAdd  specified as the Architecture  in the HDL Block Properties dialog box, if you clear the Saturate on Integer Overflow  check box, all?Integer rounding mode  options can generate incorrect HDL code.?
Workaround: Either use Linear architecture or use Zero as the Integer Rounding?mode?and?Saturate on Integer Overflow?check box selected when using the?ShiftAdd?architecture.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Embedded Coder might generate incorrect code for a model with MATLAB System and Data Store Memory Read blocks
Description: Embedded Coder might generate incorrect code for a MATLAB System block under all the following conditions: The MATLAB System block has a?updateImpl  method. The MATLAB System block writes to a globlal MATLAB variable in the?updateImpl  method. The global MATLAB variable is read by a Data Store Memory Read block.  The value read by the Data Store Memory Read block might be off by one timestep when compared to the simulation result.

Summary: Incorrect Variant condition propagation with operation on a BusElement and a normal signal
Description: Variant condition propagation fails when all the below conditions are satisfied  An operation (example: lookup table or Add block) with two inputs.  One input being the output signal of a Bus Selector and the other being a non-bus capable input.  The output of the operation is fed to a Variant Source block.

Summary: Simulink models with blocks that have custom state names might be incorrectly reordered in the linearization result when using numerical perturbation
Description: When linearizing a model that has blocks with custom state names using numerical perturbation, the state names in the linearization result might be incorrectly reordered.
Workaround: To work around this issue, prevent truncating names of states when linearizing a Simulink model using numerical perturbation. At the command line, set the?UseFullBlockNameLabels?option to on using linearizeOptions:opt = linearizeOptions;?opt.UseFullBlockNameLabels = 'on';?In the Linear Analysis Tool, on the Linear Analysis tab, click More Options.?Then, in the Options for exact linearization dialog box, select?Label input/output channels and states with full block path.

Summary: Reading corrupted multiframe GIF files using imread throws error: Unable to perform assignment
Description: Reading certain corrupted multiframe GIF files using?imread  might throw the following?error in MATLAB: Unable to perform assignment because the size of the left side is 255-by-636 and the size of the right side is 181-by-542.

Summary: MATLAB might crash while generating HDL code for Product of Elements with adaptive pipelining
Description: MATLAB might crash while generating HDL code for vector inputs to Product of Elements block when Adaptive pipelining  is enabled in?HDL Code Generation > Optimization > Pipelining  pane of Configuration Parameters dialog box.

Summary: Commented out Probe block?might crash MATLAB
Description: Commented out Probe block?might crash MATLAB if the following are true: Probe sample time  check box is selected. Probe block is commented out after update diagram of the model.

Summary: Unable to view or edit properties of objects using Property Inspector, or unable to open multiple Property Inspector windows
Description: When you double-click on some objects in the Workspace Browser, the Property Inspector opens instead of the Variables Editor. In the Property Inspector, you may not be able to view or edit property values that are arrays or objects. Only one Property Inspector window can be open at a time, so you cannot see the properties of multiple objects at once using the Property Inspector.
Workaround: View or edit property values in the Variables Editor instead of the Property Inspector by using the openvar?function. For example, create a ParquetInfo object. Then, view its properties using the Variables Editor.info = parquetinfo('outages.parquet');openvar('info')

Summary: Models containing Entity Unbatcher block might return an error?about invalid data type
Description: When a model with Entity Unbatcher block is compiled, a compilation error like the following might be generated: Error Attempt to use invalid data type id -10
Workaround: To change the block compilation order, try renaming any SimEvents block.?

Summary: Control subsystem as DUT generates incorrect error message.
Description: Enable,  Trigger,  and Reset Ports are not supported in the top level system for HDL code generation and incorrectly generates an internal error instead of valid error message. internal error during hdlcore.block_comp/ validate?

Summary: Simulating a Model Predictive Controller or Adaptive Model Predictive Controller block with both online horizons and signal previewing can crash MATLAB
Description: MATLAB can crash when you simulate a Model Predictive Controller or an Adaptive Model Predictive Controller Simulink block under the following conditions: Online horizons are enabled by selecting the Adjust prediction horizon and control horizon at run time  parameter. Signal previewing is enabled by connecting a signal with multiple rows to either the reference (ref ) or measured disturbance (md ) input ports.

Summary: Rebuilding a test harness might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: If you manually modify the interface to the conversion subsystems in the test harness before rebuilding it, MATLAB might crash.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Streaming logged data directly to SDI with selective logging may produce wrong results?if Simscape model contains variables with ExternalAccess set to none
Description: Simscape variables with (ExternalAccess = none)  should not be displayed in simlog  and in the Simulation Data Inspector. If a Simscape model contains such variables,?then enabling selective logging and choosing to record data in Simulation Data Inspector may produce wrong results.
Workaround: To work around this issue, do one of the following:?In the Model Configuration settings, disable Record data in Simulation Data Inspector when Log simulation data is set to Use Local Settings.When Record data in Simulation Data Inspector is selected, set?Log simulation data?to All.If you have control over the Simscape file, set the ExternalAccess attribute of the variables?to observe instead of none.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for modelling pattern consisting of matrix accesses that use logical indexing
Description: The simulation and code generation of Simulink model with MATLAB Function block can produce incorrect results when following conditions are satisfied: The code contains a loop in which one matrix (A ) subscripts and grabs elements from another matrix (B ). The loop contains writes to all elements of A .  Consider the mdl_example.slx  model in the attached file. The model contains a MATLAB Function block with the following MATLAB code: function B = example( in )    B = zeros(1,400);    A = (4:13);    for i=1:10        B(A) = in;        A(i) = 8;    endend Simulating the model produces incorrect output. Executing the generated code from the Simulink model also produces incorrect output.?The incorrect generated C code is below. In this code, the write to 'A ' and the indexing operation using 'A ' inside the loop are missing.? void example(const double in[10], double B[400]){  int i;  memset(&B[0], 0, 400U * sizeof(double));  for (i = 0; i < 10; i++) {    B[7] = in[i];  }} See also Bug Report ID 2006614.

Summary: Installer fails?when using Add-On Explorer to install products if you set proxies in MATLAB
Description: If you set proxies inMATLAB, the installer fails when you try to install additional products from Add-On Explorer.

Summary: Undefined type in generated AUTOSAR code for inter-runnable variable data type
Description: In an AUTOSAR model, if a data type is used only by an inter-runnable variable (IRV), generated code does not define the data type. This prevents compilation of the code.

Summary: Missing SIL stub definitions for structure field data types in AUTOSAR generated code
Description: In an AUTOSAR model, if a data type is used only by a structure field (e.g., Simulink.BusElement ), generated stub files for SIL processing omit the type definition. The missing type causes compilation errors during SIL or PIL simulation.?The issue is avoided if the structure field data type is used by another exported AUTOSAR element (for example, an S-R interface data element).

Summary: autosar.api.syncModel fails to map imported ArTypedPerInstanceMemory or StaticMemory to signal if SHORT-NAME matches Stateflow port name
Description: Function autosar.api.syncModel  fails to automatically map an imported ArTypedPerInstanceMemory or StaticMemory to a signal if all of the following conditions are true: The AUTOSAR model contains a Stateflow chart. The chart contains a data specification set to Input or Output. The Stateflow port name matches the imported AUTOSAR element SHORT-NAME.  Under these conditions, autosar.api.syncModel  generates a warning and does not perform the mapping.

Summary: autosar.api.syncModel does not map imported?AUTOSAR elements to signals, states, and data stores in inactive variants
Description: autosar.api.syncModel ?fails to map?imported ArTypedPerInstanceMemory or StaticMemory?elements?to signals, states, and data stores in inactive variants.
Workaround: Use the AUTOSAR Code Mappings editor to map signals, states, and data stores in inactive variants.

Summary: Imported AUTOSAR DataConstraint referenced by ImplementationDataType and ApplicationDataType can generate incorrect data types
Description: AUTOSAR code generation generates incorrect data types if all of the following conditions exist: The model imported AUTOSAR elements, including ImplementationDataTypes, ApplicationDataTypes, and DataConstraints, from ARXML files. An ImplementationDataType and an ApplicationDataType reference the same DataConstraint. The DataConstraint references a PhysicalConstraint but not an InternalConstraint.

Summary: Missing SIL stub definitions for matrix element data types in AUTOSAR generated code
Description: In an AUTOSAR model, if a data type is used only by a matrix element, generated stub files for SIL processing omit the type definition. The missing type causes compilation errors during SIL or PIL simulation. The issue is avoided if the matrix element data type is used by another exported AUTOSAR element (for example, an S-R interface data element).

Summary: AUTOSAR XML import fails for descriptions that use true?and false?as built-in values
Description: If the ARXML importer imports a description that uses true ?and?false  as literal values (for example, <VALUE>false</VALUE> ), the importer generates the following error: Error using autosar.mm.util.FormulaExpression/resolveSymbol    The symbol false could not be found when parsing expression false

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Deep learning network that uses MKL-DNN v0.14 on AVX-512 CPUs can generate wrong output for certain convolution operations
Description: Generated code for deep learning networks that uses the MKL-DNN target library version 0.14 can generate incorrect output if all of the conditions below are met:The target processor uses Intel AVX-512 ISA extensions. The input tensors to a convolution layer have height and width that are multiples of 224. The padding right parameter to the convolution layer has a non-zero value. The output filters are multiples of 16 to the convolution layer.  For example, if the input tensor dimensions are 3-by-224-by-224 (c,h,w), the padding parameters are [0 0 0 1] (top, bottom, left, right), and the filter dimensions are 3-by-3-by-3-by-16 (h,w,i,o), then the convolution layer produces incorrect output.

Summary: Various cases of growing a gpuArray using linear indexing assignment results in an error
Description: Using a linear index to assign a value to an empty gpuArray, or increasing the size of a gpuArray by assignment using linear indexing results in the following error:? Error using gpuArray/subsasgn  Attempt to grow array along ambiguous dimension.?   The error occurs in various cases where MATLAB cannot determine the dimension along which to grow the array. The following representative examples produce the error:?? Example 1:  g  is a 0-by-0 array.? g = gpuArray([]);  g(2) = 3;  Example 2:  ?g ?is a non-square, non-vector array with an empty dimension. g = gpuArray.zeros(2, 0);  g(2) = 3;  Example 3:  g ?is a 1-by-1-by...by-N array.? g = gpuArray.zeros(1, 1, 1, 2);?  g(3) = 3  Example 4:  g  is a gpuArray inside a dlarray. Error occurs during assignment inside a call to dlfeval.  a = dlarray(randn(5,'gpuArray'));  loss = dlfeval(@modelGradients,a);  function g = modelGradients(a)  ? ? for i = 1:3  ? ? ? ? g(i) = mean(a, 'all') + i;?  ? ? end  end ?
Workaround: To avoid this error, either preallocate the gpuArray or construct the gpuArray in advance.For? example:g = zeros(1,'gpuArray');g(2) = 3;If you encounter this error in the example?Sequence-to-Sequence Classification Using 1-D Convolutions, replace the maskedCrossEntropyLoss function with the following function:function loss = maskedCrossEntropyLoss(dlY, dlT, numTimeSteps)numObservations = size(dlY,2);loss = zeros([1, 1], 'like', dlY)for i = 1:numObservations    idx = 1:numTimeSteps(i);    loss(i) = crossentropy(dlY(:,i,idx),dlT(:,i,idx),'DataFormat','CBT');?end?end

Summary: Code inspection fails for arithmetic shift operations when the Casting Modes model configuration parameter set to Standards Compliant
Description: When the Casting Modes  model configuration parameter is set to Standards Compliant , code inspection fails to verify the explicit type cast in the generated code for arithmetic shift operations.

Summary: Setting session Rate after a single scanmeasurement causes later single scans to return stale values
Description: While using the session interfacewith National Instruments hardware (e.g., NI USB-6218), setting the session Rate  property after calling inputSingleScan  will result in future calls to inputSingleScan returning stale values. For example, s = daq.createSession('ni');addAnalogInputChannel(s,'Dev1','ai0','Voltage');s.Rate = 2000;% The following measurement value is correct:data1 = inputSingleScan(s);% After changing the session Rate, subsequent inputSingleScan measurements will return buffered (stale) values.s.Rate = 1000;dataStale = inputSingleScan(s);

Summary: Simulink might crash when string data type is used in multi-instance export-function submodel in accelerator mode
Description: Simulink might crash if a referenced model: Has a Simulink string signal Is a multi-instance export-function model Runs in accelerator mode?
Workaround: Run the submodel in normal mode.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results with PLC Ladder Diagram simulations
Description: Incorrect simulation results when using nested function blocks inside a PLCladder diagram when same variable within a single routine block is written to several times.  Pleasenote that no error or warning message will be displayed for such cases.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Use of selector block to output an array of bus results may result in incorrect HDL code
Description: Using a selector block to create or output an array of bus from a bus can result in incorrect generated code.
Workaround: The standard modeling practice is to use a vector or matrix concatenate block to create an array of bus. Selector blocks are recommended to extract individual buses from array of buses.

Summary: SLCI compatibility checker errors out when prod method is used in the MATLAB function block if the dimension used for prod method is a parameter.
Description: When running SLCI compatibility checker on a model with MATLAB function block, the check "Check Usage of Code in MATLAB Functions" fails with the error message "Abnormal exit: PROD does not work with char inputs". This is because the prod method used in the MATLAB function block uses a parameter for the dimension, which was unsupported by SLCI. To solve this problem, SLCI resolves this parameter when being used for the dimension.

Summary: createInputDataset?function might error if some?model parameters are stored in data dictionary or model workspace
Description: Defining StartTime  and StopTime ?model parameters as variables in a data dictionary or model workspace?causes the?createInputDataset  function to throw an error that mentions:?? Unrecognized function or variable 'SIMULATION_STOP_TIME'?

Summary: MATLAB crashes or errors when subsystem mask accesses value of InputPartitionDimension of?underlying For Each subsystem
Description: Release?2018a and newer:? During model compilation, MATLAB crashes when initialization code of a masked subsystem accesses the value of the?InputPartitionDimension  parameter of?the underlying For Each subsystem.? The crash occurs if the following conditions are true during model compilation: A?For Each subsystem is under a masked subsystem The For Each subsystem has at least one partitioned port and one non-partitioned port The masked subsystem is hidden and the mask initialization code accesses the InputPartitionDimension ?parameter of the For Each subsystem, for example:  get_param(ForEach(i),'InputPartitionDimension');  Release?2017b: When the conditions mentioned above are true, the model compilation results in a diagnostic error message: Invalid port initial dimension.? Dimension of output port 1 of '<model name>/For Each Subsystem/Implicit Slice Selector' has not been set.?

Summary: Incorrect normal mode simulation results might occur when model argument data type is uint64 or int64
Description: Normal mode simulation might produce an incorrect result when all of these conditions apply.? A model argument has a data type of uint64  or int64 . For this model argument, the top model specifies a value that has a fixdt(0, 64, 0) ?or fixdt(1, 64, 0)  data type respectively.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: In Restricted mode, blocks with conditional visibility of ports or parameters might produce wrong answers
Description: When a model is in Restricted mode, ?opening a block dialog (or accessing the Property Inspector for a block) triggers the block to ignore the actual values of its control parameters and use?the default values from the Simscape file instead.?As a result, themodel loads and simulates correctly in Restricted mode, but opening a blockdialog box or accessing the Property Inspector can cause the block to use thewrong parameter settings.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Interpolation Using Prelookup block might have previously unused?Valid index input may reach last index check box incorrectly selected when upgrading models from releases prior to R2014b
Description: The Interpolation Using Prelookup block might have a previously hidden and unused?Valid index input may reach last index   check box incorrectly selected?when upgrading models under these conditions: Models being upgraded from version R2014a or earlier Interpolation Method  parameter set to Flat . Valid index input may reach last index? parameter selected (even though it is hidden). For example,?you might have done this by: Programatically?selecting the check box using set_param(gcb, 'ValidIndexMayReachLast', 'on') . Setting the Interpolation Method  parameter to Linear , selecting the visible?Valid index input may reach last index  check box, and setting the??Interpolation Method  parameter back to Flat .   Under these conditions, upgraded version models output results assuming the check box is selected. Previous version models output results assuming the check box is cleared.
Workaround: After the upgrade, clear the Valid index input may reach last check box. Programatically clear the check box?using?set_param(gcb, 'ValidIndexMayReachLast', 'off').
Fixed In: R2018b Update 5, R2019a Update 5, R2019b, R2020a

Summary: Compiler that supports only the C89 standard does not compile deployed Simscape C code
Description: If the deployed Simscape C codes are not pre-compiled for a C compiler that supports only the C89 standard, the compiler will fail to compile the deployed Simscape C codes during code generation.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code may be generated for multiple similar Prelookup or n-D Lookup Table blocks?when?inputs, breakpoints, or fractions contain same fixed-point data types with different word lengths
Description: Incorrect code may be generated for models with multiple Prelookup or n-D Lookup Table blocks?when one of these sets of conditions are true: The blocks?have fixed-point data types for both inputs and breakpoints. Each block input and breakpoint?has different fixed-point data types. The blocks?have the same configuration except that: The data?types of the inputs differ only by word length but have the same?container word length . For example: Block 1 data type of?input: fixdt(1,12,3)  Block 2 data type of input:?fixdt(1,14,3)  The data? types of the breakpoints?differ only by word length but?have the same?container word length . For example: Block 1 data type of?breakpoint: fixdt(1,15,3)  Block 2 data type of breakpoint:?fixdt(1,16,3)    ?Or The blocks?have fixed-point data types for fractions. The blocks?have the same configuration except that: The data?types of the fractions differ only by word length but have the same?container word length . For example: Block 1 data type of?fraction: fixdt(1,12,3)  Block 2 data type of fraction:?fixdt(1,14,3)    ?

Summary: Tool-coupling FMU?does not simulate if the exported Simulink project contains referenced projects
Description: Tool-coupling FMU exported from Simulink might?not simulate in Simulink or other FMU import tools if the following conditions are true: The Simulink project?from which?FMU is exported has references to other Simulink projects During FMU export, Include referenced project  check box is selected  An example error message from Simulink FMU Import block: Log from FMU: [category:logStatusError, status:fmi2Error] fmi2Instantiate: Error using slproject.loadProjectCould not load the project atC:\work\slprj\_fmu\12345abc\testFMU\resources\obj\987654xyz  Unable to find a definition folder for the project in resources/project.?   Error in fmuhelper

Description: MATLAB may crash while editing a Simulink model that meets all of these conditions:  Using a subsystem with a self-modifying mask  Adding a line programmatically to an already connected port in the self-modifying mask code  The Edit-Time Errors & Warnings  diagnostic is turned on.   ?The stack trace constains a call to 'SLEditTimeDisplay::CheckMismatches'  similar to the following: --------------------------------------------------------------------------------                Access violation detected at Thu Jun 20 10:21:53 2019--------------------------------------------------------------------------------? Summary: Incorrect results might occur for loop that contains a read from and a write to a variable of scalar type
Description: Incorrect code might be generated from MATLAB code that meets all of these conditions: Inside a for  loop, a variable is read from and written to. This variable initialized before the loop.?? The variable is of a scalar type. (that is, int32 ,?int64 )  When the above conditions are met, the generated code might not contain a computation that is performed inside the loop.? For example, the attached script contains this MATLAB code: for Out4 = 3:5  ? ? ? Out2 = transpose( (Out2) .* (P3( 1:1:1, 1:1:1 )) );  end ? In the following incorrect generated code,? *Out2  is assigned an incorrect value because one iteration of the loop is skipped:? ? *Out2 = 6.0 * (real_T)P3 * (real_T)P3;??  Here is the correct generated code:? ? *Out2 = 6.0 * (real_T)P3 * (real_T)P3 * (real_T)P3;  To reproduce the bug, unzip the attached??exampleForBugReport2035181.zip file. ? In the MATLAB command window, enter the following command: run generateCode.m?

Summary: Removing a stereotype when multiple stereotypes inherit from a common parent stereotype may cause MATLAB to crash
Description: Removing one of the stereotypes from the element that contains two or more stereotypes inheriting from a common base stereotype removes all the properties in the hierarchy of the applied stereotype. As a result, the common set of the properties are deleted. The sibling stereotype, which remains applied to the System Composer element expects the property to exist and can cause MATLAB to crash if the Property Inspector window is open.?If the stereotype is removed through the API, attempting to inspect the element by selecting it in the canvas returns command-line errors while trying to build the Property Inspector.?

Summary: Enumeration class errors for AUTOSAR component model when enumerated type in data dictionary types outports connected to Signal Invalidation blocks
Description: An AUTOSAR component model generates enumeration class errors if the model uses enumerated types stored in a Simulink data dictionary to type Outport blocks connected to Signal Invalidation blocks.?The following errors might occur when viewing the outport Communication  attributes in the Property Inspector view for the Code Mappings editor, validating the AUTOSAR mapping of the model, or generating code: Cannot define enumeration class 'myEnumeratedType' because it was previously defined by a data dictionary.Cannot clear enumeration class 'myEnumeratedType' because it was created by a data dictionary.
Workaround: Move the definition of the enumerated type out of the data dictionary and into the base or model workspace.

Summary: Generated code for top model that references model that uses zero-crossing detection is different when Model block is in SIL or PIL mode
Description: Consider a model that c ontains a Model block that is configured for SIL or PIL mode simulation. In addition, the referenced model  uses zero-crossing detection.  The code you generate for the model can differ from the code that you generate when the Model block is configured for normal mode simulation.  When the Model block parameter Simulation mode  is set to Software-in-the-loop (SIL)  or Processor-in-the-loop (PIL) , this line?is not present in the model .h  file:  #include "zero_crossing_types.h

Summary: Error during compilation of C++ file : shared_ptr.h, line 285: error: argument of type "int" is incompatible with template parameter
Description: When verifying a C++ application, the following error can appear during the Polyspace compilation phase: .../shared_ptr.h, line 285: error: argument of type "int" is incompatible with template parameter of type "std::enable_if<std::is_constructible<std::__shared_ptr<_Tp, 2>, std::unique_ptr<_Yp, _Del>, std::__sp_array_delete>::value, void>::type *"  |? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? _Constructible<unique_ptr<_Yp, _Del>, __sp_array_delete>* = 0>|? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ^?
Workaround: To work around this issue, define the following macro in the project:?? _GLIBCXX_USE_DEPRECATED=0
Products: Polyspace Code Prover Server, Polyspace Code Prover

Summary: Creating multiple direct connections between a pair of system ports may result in a crash
Description: This problem arises when the user:  Drags from one side of the system box to another to create two directly connected system ports such as InBus and Outbus. Set the element field of OutBus, for example, to B2. Drags a second time from the first system port to the second. This action will either be unsuccessful or cause MATLAB to crash, depending on the drag direction.  Example of element remapping:InBus.A1 -> OutBus.B2InBus.A2 -> OutBus.B1

Summary: New link from MATLAB code to Simulink Requirement is not resolved
Description: If you use the MATLAB Editor context menu to link from a code selection to a requirement in the Requirements Editor or Requirements Browser, the link is created and you can navigate from the MATLAB code to the requirement. However, the link is not displayed in the Requirements Editor or Requirements Browser when you select the linked requirement. Also, in the Links View, the link is displayed as "unresolved."

Summary: Simulating tool-coupling FMU on MATLAB session with shared engine causes MATLAB to become unresponsive
Description: MATLAB might become unresponsive, when simulating a Simulink model, if the following conditions are true: Simulink model contains FMU Import block that loads a tool-coupling FMU exported from MATLAB Before running simulation, MATLAB session engine is shared via a command?such as?shareMATLABForFMUCoSim ?or?matlab.engine.shareEngine .

Summary: Error might occur when executing a test case in a previous release if Parallel Computing Toolbox is not installed
Description: Executing a test case in a previous release might produce the following error if Parallel Computing Toolbox is not installed:? Unable to resolve the name parallel.internal.apishared.JobPathHelper.getUserAddedPathEntries?

Summary: Spectrum Estimator block cannot restore and run from a Simulink logged state
Description: If a Simulink model logs its final states and uses them asinitial conditions for subsequent runs, the model errors out when one or bothof the following conditions are met:  The model has a Spectrum     Estimator block with Output frequency vector  parameter selected.  The model has a Spectrum     Estimator block with Output effective RBW  parameter  selected.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Morphological operations using single datatype yield incorrect Inf result?with C/C++ code generation
Description: Generating C/C++ code for morphological operations, such as imdilate  and imerode  using?the single  datatype, returns incorrect results that contain Inf . Note: These morphological functions?yield correct results in the MATLAB environment.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, edit the images.internal.coder.morphop.m file by executing the following command:  edit(fullfile(matlabroot,'toolbox','images','images','+images','+internal','+coder','morphop.m'))Find the line that contains the word?realmin and change that to -realmax. In other words, modify the file from this:  B(B <= realmin('single')) = single(-Inf);to this:  B(B <= -realmax('single')) = single(-Inf);

Summary: Several moving statistics functions may produce slightly different results when run multiple times on the same data.
Description: The following functions may not produce identical results when run on the same data multiple times: movmean  movprod  movstd  movsum  movvar?   The results from each function call are numerically equivalent, but may differ by factors on the order of eps(y) , where y  is the returned value.

Summary: Simulation Data Inspector comparison results can be incorrect for string, boolean, and enumerated signals using linear interpolation
Description: When you use the Simulation Data Inspector to compare string, boolean, or enumerated signals, the results can be incorrect if all of these conditions are met: The signals do not share identical time vectors. The Baseline  signal interpolation method is set to linear . The Baseline  signal synchronization method is set to union .  Under these conditions, the comparison algorithm can generate incorrect interpolated signal values for use in the comparison.
Workaround: When you compare string, boolean, or enumerated signals in the Simulation Data Inspector, use zoh, or zero order hold, interpolation. You can modify the interpolation method for a signal in the Properties table on the Inspect pane in the Simulation Data Inspector or by programmatically setting the InterpMethod for the Simulink.sdi.Signal?object corresponding to the signal.

Summary: Validation of AUTOSAR mapping might error out unnecessarily with mapped signals
Description: The mapping validation for an AUTOSAR model might unnecessarily error out if all of the following conditions exist: A subsystem output signal is mapped for AUTOSAR, as displayed in the Code Mappings editor,?Signals/States ?tab. The underlying signal within the subsystem, which is connected to the outport of the subsystem output signal, is also mapped for AUTOSAR. One of the two signals is mapped to?Auto , while the other is mapped to AUTOSAR variable type?ArTypedPerInstanceMemory ?or?StaticMemory .  The error message reports a signal configuration conflict. For example: Conflicting signal configuration. The code generation properties in code perspective for signal on output port 1 of 'autosar_swc/SS1/Sum' conflicts with signal on output port 1 of 'autosar_swc/SS1' since both outputs map to the same signal.
Workaround: Remove the AUTOSAR mapping for the signal that is mapped to?Auto.

Summary: Slow model compilation for large array of buses inside nonvirtual bus
Description: Updating the diagram, simulating, and generating code for a model might be slow when a nonvirtual bus contains a large array of buses.

Summary: Discrete FIR Filter HDL Optimized returns simulation error
Description: When simulating the Discrete FIR Filter HDL Optimized block, you may receive this error: 'Nontunable property 'numMults' may only be assigned once.'.

Summary: App Designer components are incorrectly positioned
Description: When loading a saved app in App Designer, components inside Panel  objects that are parented to GridLayout  objects are incorrectly placed. They might be shifted vertically in either direction. However, when you run the app, the components are positioned correctly. Repositioning the components in App Designer and re-saving the app does not fix the issue.
Workaround: To work around the issue, use the following steps to replace the panel with a tab group and a tab:1. In App Designer, move the panel for which the content is incorrectly positioned, to a temporary location, for example below the uifigure.2. Add a tab group and tab where the panel was previously positioned.3. Move the content of the panel to the tab. To do so:a. In the Component Browser, right-click on any component.b. Check Include component labels in Component Browser.c. Locate the panel in the Component Browser and multi-select all the components inside the panel by holding the Shift key.d. Place the mouse over the selected components in the canvas, and drag them into the tab. If you are unable to drag them directly into the tab, first drag them outside the panel to a temporary location, then drag them into the tab.4. Delete the panel.

Summary: In Ubuntu 18.04, the Polyspace user interface is stuck at 83% when opened
Description: When you open the Polyspace user interface in Ubuntu 18.04, the loading process stops at 83% and does not show further progress. You are not able to open the Polyspace user interface in any other way.
Workaround: To continue the loading process and open Polyspace, kill the processes starting with jxbrowser. The Polyspace user interface will open but the Start Page and context-sensitive help will not be available.
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder, Polyspace Bug Finder Server

Description: ?In the ConfigurationParameters dialog box, changing the value of ˇ®Hardware Implementation >Target hardware resources > FPGA design > Memory diagnostics levelˇŻ andthen closing the dialog box produces the following error message:  ?"The first input to get_param must be of type 'double','char' or 'cell'  "    and the new value for? 'Memory diagnostic level'is not saved.  In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, after setting 'HardwareImplementation > Hardware board' to a new hardware board, the model errorsat 'Update Diagram' or in simulation with the following error instructions:  "The diagnostic setting of 'No debug' in the model configuration parameters and the logged signal state of 'Basic diagnostic signals' do not match. This is likely because the setting was changed but not saved in the configuration settings.  To fix this issue:   1. Choose your desired setting.  Apply."    Theseinstructions will not resolve the error. This is likely because the setting was changed but not saved in the configuration settings. To fix this issue:   1. Open the configuration parameters to Hardware Implementation / Hardware board settings / Target Hardware resources / FPGA design (debug)?   2. Choose 'Basic diagnostic signals', Apply. 3. Choose your desired setting. Apply."    Theseinstructions will not resolve the error.

Summary: Model Advisor exclusions not working for shipped library-linked blocks
Description: Right-clicking on a shipped library-linked block (example: PID Controller) and selecting Model Advisor  > Exclude all instances of library block  does?not exclude the block from Model Advisor.

Summary: SIL/PIL block build failure during parallel build with separate Simulation cache folder and Code generation folder
Description: During an SIL or PIL simulation, when the top model is built, the build of an SIL or PIL block fails when all the following conditions apply: Separate folders are specified for the Simulink preferences CacheFolder  and CodeGenFolder . For the top model, EnableParallelModelReferenceBuilds  is set to on . A parallel pool is running.  The build process produces an error that states the build folder cannot be found.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, take either of the following actions:Specify a serial build by setting EnableParallelModelReferenceBuilds to off.Specify the same folder for?CacheFolder and CodeGenFolder.

Summary: svd?might fail to converge when operating on complex-valued matrices
Description: The three-output syntax of svd  might fail to converge when the input matrix is complex: ?>> [U,S,V] = svd(A)  Error using svdSVD did not converge.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Thefunctions afterEach and afterAll for parallel.Futures do not work for deployedapplications
Description: WtihafterEach and afterAll you can specify functions to execute on completion of aparallel.Future object returned by parfeval or parfervalOnAll. However, indeployed applications the functions that you specify in afterEach and afterAllare not triggered on completion of the parallel.Future, unless you callafterEach or afterAll after the future completes.

Summary: MATLABJob Scheduler parpool workflows can slow down or stall
Description: In parallel pools onMATLAB Job Scheduler clusters, MATLAB can unnecessarily resend data between clientand workers when the following conditions occur: There are a large number of     workers in the parallel pool. There is a slow connection     between the client and workers. There are large amounts of     data being transferred between client and workers.  This situation cancause any parallel functionality that uses parpool, such as parfor, parfeval,and spmd, to slow down and potentially stall.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code reuse of subsystems containing Unit Conversion blocks
Description: The code for two subsystems containing Unit Conversion blocks might be incorrectly reused in the generated code. The incorrect reuse could happen even if the Unit Conversion blocks in the two subsystems are converting different units (for example, cm to m in the first subsystem and cm to feet in the second subsystem). When the incorrect reuse occurs, the generated code contains incorrect code for the unit conversion in one of the subsystems.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model containing multiple?Reusable?custom storage class with a?branched root Inport
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: A pair of root-level Inport and Outport signals contain the same?Reusable ?custom storage class specification. The root Inport signal is branched and feeds into an atomic subsystem. The Function Packaging  parameter in the Block Parameters of a subsystem is set to Auto  or Reusable function . The subsystem is reused in the generated code.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Generated code for deep learning network that uses MKL-DNN v0.14 and certain dimensions to depthconcatenation layer can cause exceptions
Description: Generated code fordeep learning networks that uses the Intel MKL-DNN target library version 0.14 cancause exceptions under these conditions:   The network has a depth concatenation layer.   The input tensors to the depth concatenation layer have channel dimensions that are multiples of 8 and non-multiples of 8.   For example, ifthe input tensor dimensions are 5x24x24 (c,h,w) and 8x24x24 (c,h,w), then the depth concatenation layer can causean exception.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Embedded Coder might generate incorrect code for a MATLAB System block
Description: Embedded Coder might generate incorrect code for a MATLAB System block under all the following conditions: The MATLAB System block has a?updateImpl  method. The MATLAB System block inherits from?matlab.system.mixin.Nondirect  class The MATLAB System block has a?isInputDirectFeedthroughImpl  method that returns false.  The output of the MATLAB System block might be off by one timestep when compared to the simulation result.

Summary: Current object is an empty array after right-clicking on data tips
Description: If you right-click on a data tip that appears as you hover over a data point, then the current object returned by gco  is an empty array.
Workaround: Disable default interactions, including data tip interactions, using the?disableDefaultInteractivity?function.

Summary: Simulink Code Inspector compatibility checker incorrectly reports a dimension and datatype mismatch for unsupported MATLAB action language usage in a Stateflow chart.
Description: If a Stateflow chart uses MATLAB action language and has a condition such as "x ==false" or "after(1, sec)", Simulink Code Inspector compatibility checker incorrectly reports an incompatibility that the condition has a missing data type or dimension.
