The above syntax is not a Simulink Code Inspector supported subset of MATLAB action language and will also be reported as an incompatibility by another compatibility check.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Software-in-the-loop (SIL) simulation might generate incorrect answer for root Outport block with constant source during C++ code generation
Description: Software-in-the-loop (SIL) simulation might generate incorrect answer if all these conditions are true: The model contains a root Outport block. The root Outport block has a constant source. The model configuration parameter Language  is set to C++ . The model configuration parameter Code interface packaging  is set to C++ class . In the Configure C++ class interface dialog box, Function specification  is set to I/O arguments step method .
Workaround: Insert a Rate Transition block before the root Outport block.

Summary: Specifying an invalid DBC-file in set_param for the CANdbFile parameter on pack and unpack message blocks does not throw an error
Description: Specifying an invalid DBC-file when using the command-line function set_param ?for the CANdbFile parameter on any of the following blocks should throw an error, but does not. CAN Pack CAN Unpack CAN FD Pack CAN FD Unpack  For example, the following command should error when filename.dbc  is invalid: set_param(gcb,'CANdbFile','filename.dbc')

Summary: Dependency reports take a long time to complete
Description: When creating a dependency report for a folder, MATLAB takes a long time to complete the report.

Summary: Compatibility check may fail for models containing Data Store Memory blocks
Description: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check fails? with the message  Checking Compatibility Failed ? when all the following conditions are met: The model contains?Data Store Memory blocks  The model contains blocks having?a constant sample time and the?BlockType ?equal to?S-Function  A replacement model gets generated   Additionally,? when these conditions are met,?you will encounter the following error?while executing? sldvblockreplacement :  $PRODUCT$ has detected an S-Function with a constant sample time. $PRODUCT$ does not support S-Functions with this configuration.

Summary: Comparing two large LinkSet files can result in an error
Description: Simulink Requirements may throw following error when you compare two large LinkSet files:  com.mathworks.toolbox.rptgenxmlcomp.exception.XMLComparisonException: Error occurred in server thread; nested exception is: java.lang.UnsatisfiedLinkError: com.mathworks.jmi.NativeMatlab.PostMatlabRunnable(JZLjava/lang/Runnable;)

Summary: Temporary variable assignment before declaration with Variant Sinks and Variant Subsystem
Description: When a non-virtual block connected to a Variant Subsystem's inport has same condition as the inport of an active choice, local variables are assigned before they are declared as shown below:  void try2_step(void)  {? ?  #if Z == 1 || Z == 2  rtb_VariantMerge_For_Variant_So  = try2_U.In2;  {  ? ? real_T rtb_VariantMerge_For_Variant_So ;  ? ? #if Z == 2  ? ? rtb_VariantMerge_For_Variant_So = 1.0;  ? ? #endif  ? ? try2_B.VariantMergeForOutportOut3 = rtb_VariantMerge_For_Variant_So;  ? ? }  ? ? try2_Y.Out2 = try2_B.VariantMergeForOutportOut3;  ? ? #endif  }

Summary: Performance regression for large FIS creation and ANFIS tuning
Description: Performing the following operations on large fuzzy systems (number of rules greater than or equal to 250) takes significantly longer than in previous releases of MATLAB: Tuning fuzzy inference systems using the anfis  function Tuning fuzzy inference systems using?the Neuro-Fuzzy Designer  app Creating fuzzy inference systems using the?genfis ?function

Summary: Windows users who are not part of their network domain unable to save existing SLX files in certain network folders
Description: On Windows, users who are not part of their network domain might not be able to save existing SLX files in certain network folders, if the folders are shared from either a Windows NT based file system or Samba file system. New SLX files are not affected by this issue.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for a deep learning network using the ARM Compute Library can produce wrong output for multiple observations
Description: When targeting ARM platforms using the ARM Compute Library, the code generated for a deep learning network can produce an incorrect output if the input to the the?predict ?method contains multiple observations or a stack of images. In other words, if the input image is an array of size h-by-w-by-c-by-N, where?N > 1 .? This behavior is applicable only for code that is generated by using the codegen  command. ?Consider the following SqueezeNet example.? function out = test(in)%#codegenpersistent net;if isempty(net)??? net = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('squeezenet','squeezenet');endout = net.predict(in);   end ? The code configuration object for an 'exe'  target is shown. cfg = coder.config('exe');  cfg.DeepLearningConfig = coder.DeepLearningConfig('arm-compute');  cfg.DeepLearningConfig.ArmArchitecture = 'armv7';  codegen -config cfg test -args {rand(227,227,3,2)} -d arm_compute  Since the fourth input size dimension is greater than one, the generated code produces incorrect results.?
Workaround: In R2019b, the software throws an error if there are multiple input images to the predict method. To perform inference for cases where the number of images in the input image stack is greater than one, use the 'MiniBatchSize' argument of the predict method. For the SqueezeNet example shown earlier, modify the entry-point function as shown.function out = test(in)%#codegenpersistent net;if isempty(net)??? net = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('squeezenet','squeezenet');endout = net.predict(in,'MiniBatchSize',1); end

Summary: 3-D ground truth images incorrectly transposed along the height and width dimensions when passed to the output loss layer
Description: When training a 3-D image-to-image semantic segmentation CNN, the ground truth labeled images dispatched to the loss layer are transposed along the height - width dimensions. This results in incorrect training and predictions.

Summary: Function Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct might return incorrect value when a virtual bus contains a nonvirtual bus
Description: Function Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct  might return an incorrect value for a virtual bus when all of these conditions apply: The virtual bus contains only nonvirtual buses. The data types of the nonvirtual buses are the same.

Summary: Error when extracting the architecture of a Simulink model with inactive variants
Description: Exporting a Simulink model to a System Composer architecture model returns an error if the Simulink model has Variant Subsystem blocks with inactive variant choices. The error has the following form: Warning: File '<model name>_tempExportWorkflow.slx' not found.  > In systemcomposer.internal.arch.exportToArch (line 139)  ? In systemcomposer.extractArchitectureFromSimulink (line 12)  Error using systemcomposer.extractArchitectureFromSimulink (line 14)  Invalid Simulink object name: <block path>  Caused by:  ? ? Error using systemcomposer.internal.arch.exportToArch>addInterfaceToPort (line 583)  ? ? <block name>?is not a SubSystem block. - Show complete stack trace?

Summary: Building AUTOSAR model that contains root inport with enumerated data type and QueuedExplicitReceive data access mode crashes MATLAB
Description: Building an AUTOSAR model crashes MATLAB if all of the following conditions are true: The model contains a root inport for which the AUTOSAR data access mode is set to QueuedExplicitReceive . The model reads an enumerated type value at the root inport. An enumeration class defines the enumerated data type. The root inport connects to a Stateflow chart that is configured to receive a message.  Under these conditions, building the model crashes the MATLAB session.

Summary: Model Advisor Check Issues metrics incorrectly counts passed model advisor configuration parameter checks as issues?
Description: The metric engine counts Model Advisor checks of model configuration parameter settings as issues even if they pass. in addition, the metric engine does not count as issues Model Advisor checks that warn or fail and do not contains links to blocks in the model. This bug pertains to these two metrics:??mathworks.metrics.ModelAdvisorCheckIssues.hisl_do178  ?mathworks.metrics.ModelAdvisorCheckIssues.maab   The Metrics Dashboard displays the incorrect number of issues.
Products: Simulink Check, Requirements Toolbox, Simulink Coverage, Simulink Design Verifier

Summary: MATLAB crashes during property proving analysis on model with Assertion blocks
Description: If the Simulink Design Verifier option DVAssertions  is set to EnableAll or DisableAll and the model contains Assertion blocks, property proving analysis might result in a MATLAB crash.
Workaround: Set the DVAssertions option to UseLocalSettings and use the Enabled block parameter of Assertion blocks to enable or disable individual blocks. Then, perform property proving analysis.?

Summary: Simulation Manager hangs when SimulationInput?object contains a model or a block parameter value which is not a character vector or a numerical value
Description: If a SimulationInput? object contains a model or a block parameter value which is not a character vector or a numerical value and 'ShowSimulationManager ' option is used with sim  or parsim , the Simulation Manager window opens up in a hung state and eventually throws the following error message after several minutes: ?Timed out while waiting for Simulation Manager to open  For example, the following code reproduces the behavior (it uses a fixed-point numeric object) in = Simulink.SimulationInput('vdp')in = in.setBlockParameter('vdp/Mu', 'Gain', fi(pi))out = sim(in, 'ShowSimulationManager', 'on')  Press ctrl-c on the MATLAB command prompt and fix the parameter value by adding single quotes around the parameter value? in = Simulink.SimulationInput('vdp')in = in.setBlockParameter('vdp/Mu', 'Gain', 'fi(pi)')out = sim(in, 'ShowSimulationManager', 'on')
Workaround: In most cases the model and the block parameter value that?setBlockParameter expects?is a character vector. Fix the parameter value by changing it to a character vector and run the simulations again.?In cases where that is not possible, use the?setPreSimFcn?to specify the model or block parameter value.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: AUTOSAR generated code might write uninitialized value if Bus Creator block output writes to root outport
Description: AUTOSAR generated code writes a partially initialized struct  value to a root outport if all of the following conditions exist: The model contains a root outport for which the AUTOSAR data access mode is set to either ExplicitSend  or ImplicitSend . The model contains a Stateflow chart that outputs multiple signals. The signals are fed into a Bus Creator block. The bus signal output of the Bus Creator block is written to a root outport. The chart logic partially writes the bus signal in different states. The chart is not configured as Treat as atomic unit  with Function packaging  set to Reusable function .  Under these conditions, the generated code writes a partially initialized variable to the root outport.
Workaround: In the Stateflow chart block parameters:On the Main tab, select Treat as atomic unit.On the Code Generation tab, set Function packaging?to Reusable function.

Summary: Actor trajectory creation in driving scenarios is slow
Description: When specifying a large number of waypoints in the trajectory function (or the path function in previous releases), the function executes slowly. The slowdown is most evident in scenarios with a large number of actors.

Summary: Unclear error when creating a project in a folder containing a Resources folder.
Description: Unable to create a project in a folder that contains a resources folder with uppercase characters. Users encounter an unclear error: "The category with uuid FileClassCategory does not exist in the project".
Workaround: To avoid this issue,rename the folder to resources all lowercase.rename or move the folder.create the project in a parent folder.

Summary: The Order Spectrum Dialog in the Diagnostic Feature Designer throws an unexpected error when using parallel computing
Description: In the Diagnostic Feature Designer app, when the Use parallel computing  option is checked in the Computation Options dialog, the Order Spectrum Dialog throws an error on deserialization issues after you click OK .

Summary: MATLAB does not start properly on some Mac systems
Description: MATLAB sometimes does not start properly on Mac systems, either hanging or generating an error such as the following in the command window: Error using connector.internal.doEnsureServiceOnError in Connector: Did not start the server. Desired port was: 31515. Last error was: Server did not respond to initial request: 127.0.0.1:31714

Summary: Unable to start real-time application when communicating with more than four UDP multicast addresses
Description: A real-time application that communicates with more than four UDP multicast addresses is unable to start execution on the target computer. The error that appears on the target computer is: ERROR: UDP add multicast member error : EMCASTFULL? ? ERROR: Execution cannot be started ??

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Log10 block returns a wrong numeric value in accelerator mode with non-inline subsystem packaging
Description: Setting to reusable  or non-reusable  subsystem packaging causes incorrect results in accelerator mode. For example, Log10  block's imaginary outputs are incorrectly computed as inf .
Workaround: These are possible workarounds:?Change the subsystem code generation packaging to in-line.??Use other modes such as rapid accelerator or normal mode.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Difference between simulation and code generation results when a model contains an Atomic subsystem with reusable function packaging
Description: Simulation and code generation results may be different when all the following conditions are met: The model contains an Atomic subsystem with the Function Packaging  option set to Reusable function . An Outport block, Signal Specification block, or Inport block with a specified sample time is directly connected to an output port of the Atomic subsystem, or through virtual blocks. The sample time specified on the Outport block, Signal Specification block, or Inport block is different from the compiled sample time without the specification.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block outside the Atomic subsystem. The Signal Conversion block is only required on output ports matching the problem description.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for?a model containing nested reusable subsystems
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains? nested reusable subsystems. In the subsystem Block Parameters dialog box, the?Function packaging ?parameter is set to  Reusable function .  The signal that leaves the input port of the subsystem has a  Reusable  custom storage class.  The subsystem contains a child subsystem with the??Function packaging parameter  is?set to  Reusable Function.  The signal that enters the output port of the child subsystem has the same  Reusable  custom storage class.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 5, R2019b, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Constant comparison in MATLAB code can generate incorrect HDL code
Description: In the following cases, the generated HDL code might incorrectly return true or false: 0 < u?  0 >= u?  MAX_CONSTANT <= u?  MAX_CONSTANT > u   ?where MAX_CONSTANT  is the maximum value that can be represented using the data type specified for u .?
Workaround: Rewrite the relational expression by moving the constant in the left hand side to the right hand side. For example, rewrite:? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?0 < u? ? ? ? ? ?as ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? u > 0

Summary: MATLAB might crash when virtual bus crosses atomic subsystem boundary
Description: MATLAB might crash when all of these conditions apply: A virtual bus contains a nonvirtual bus. This virtual bus crosses an atomic subsystem boundary. A Signal Conversion block converts this virtual bus to a virtual bus. A Bus to Vector block converts this virtual bus to a vector signal. This vector signal connects to another atomic subsystem.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Transition guards in standalone charts for execution MATLAB are ignored when preceded by comments
Description: In standalone charts for execution in MATLAB, event triggers and transition conditions are ignored when preceded by a comment. For example, a transition label of the form: % comment??[condition]?  is evaluated as if the transition were unconditional.?
Workaround: Use one of the following syntaxes instead:[?% comment??condition?]?or[condition] % comment??

Summary: Roundtripping between IBM DOORS and Simulink Requirements using ReqIF might fail
Description: Roundtripping is the process of exporting, importing, and updating requirements between IBM DOORS and Simulink Requirements. Roundtripping follows the process: Export content from IBM DOORS into a ReqIF file. Import the ReqIF file into Simulink Requirements. Unlock and edit the requirement data in Simulink Requirements. Export from Simulink Requirements into a ReqIF file, using the previous import settings. Re-import the ReqIF file into IBM DOORS and have IBM DOORS merge/update changes to the requirement data.   Step 5 may result in duplicate elements.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might not call all instances of a referenced model that contains a Reset Function block
Description: In the generated code, the function that defines a reset event in the top model might call only one of multiple referenced model instances when all of these conditions apply: The top model contains a Reset Function block and at least two Model blocks that reference the same model. The referenced model is an export-function model. The referenced model contains a Reset Function block and at least one Simulink Function block. The top model contains only one Function Caller block, which calls one of the instances of the Simulink Function block in the referenced model.

Summary: Reporting on a vector of objects can cause an error
Description: If you use the mlreportgen.report.MATLABVariable  reporter to report on a variable, report generation ends with an error when both of these conditions are true: A property of the variable or of a descendant of the variable in a hierarchy is set to a vector of objects. The DepthLimit  property value of the reporter causes the reporter to report the vector of objects as inline text instead of expanding it.  In the following example, an mlreportgen.report.MATLABVariable  reporter reports on a variable ax  that is an Axes  object. This example causes an error because the Children  property of ax  is set to a vector of Line  objects and the DepthLimit  property of the reporter is set to 0.plot(rand(5))ax = gca;rpt = mlreportgen.report.Report("test");rptr = mlreportgen.report.MATLABVariable(ax);rptr.DepthLimit = 0;add(rpt,rptr);close(rpt);rptview(rpt);

Summary: Incorrecttraceability between Polyspace Bug Finder Tool Operational Requirements (TOR)and Tool Requirements (TR)
Description: The TOR-to-TR traceability matrix (qualkitdo_bugfinder_HLR_OR_LSR_trace.xlsx) contains the following incorrect links: HLR-OR-LSR     Trace  HLR-22-BFIncorrect       trace to OR-410 and OR-411? Missing       trace to OR-310, OR-323, OR-353, OR-354, OR-367, OR-396, and OR-398?         OR-HLR     Trace  OR-310Missing       trace to HLR-22-BF        OR-323Missing       trace to HLR-22-BF        OR-353Missing       trace to HLR-31-3-BF        OR-354Missing       trace to HLR-31-3-BF        OR-398Missing       trace to HLR-22-BF        OR-410Incorrect       trace to HLR-2-BF and HLR-22-BF        OR-411Incorrect       trace to HLR-2-BF and HLR-22-BF??

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code is incorrect when passing NaN to the MATLAB isinf function
Description: ?Incorrect code generation might occur when passing NaN to the MATLAB isinf  function under the following conditions: The MATLAB code contains a call to isinf . The value passed to isinf  is NaN . The NaN  value from (2) is computed. For example, a function call or an expression could compute the NaN  value. Simply calling isinf(NaN)  is unlikely to result in incorrect code generation.  Consider the following MATLAB function: function out = foo()  ? ? x = rand(1);  ? ? if isinf(rem(x, 0))  ? ? ? ? out = 1;  ? ? else  ? ? ? ? out = 2;  ? ? end  end  T he call to isinf  in the  if  condition satisfies condition 1.  rem(x, 0)  returns the value NaN  satisfying condition 2.  A function call computes  the value NaN  satisfying condition 3.? The generated code for this function is:? real_T foo(const emlrtStack *sp)  {  ? real_T out;  ? (void)sp;  ? b_rand();  ? out = 1.0;  ? return out;  }?  The generated code incorrectly determines that isinf  of the value NaN  is true.? The correct code is:? real_T foo(const emlrtStack *sp)  {  ? real_T out;  ? (void)sp;  ? b_rand();  ? out = 2.0;  ? return out;  }?

Summary: Assertion during code generation when Stateflow chart and MATLAB Function block call the same external function
Description: An assertion error occurs during code generation when all the following conditions are met: The model contains a Stateflow chart with C as the action language. The model contains a MATLAB Function block. The model has the setting Import custom code  enabled. The Stateflow chart and the MATLAB Function block both call the same external function. The Stateflow chart is from a library model with the setting Use local custom code settings (do not inherit from main model)  disabled.  The assertion is similar to the following:? ?failed assertion in b:\matlab\src\rtwcg\rtwcg_utils\managedcustomcodescope.cpp at line 243:?  ? ? In getSpecialScopeImpl: Assertion failed: scope->parent() == fCCScope?
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 4, R2020a

Summary: Unrecognized function of variable 'cmdToUrl' error when exporting to ReqIF
Description: When exporting Simulink Requirement Set to ReqIF, export may fail with the message: Unrecognized function or variable 'cmdToUrl'  when the following conditions are met: The export links  option is enabled. There are links with Simulink Test Cases.

Summary: Virtual worlds fail to open because of missing libGLU.so.1 library
Description: On the Red Hat 8 operating system, virtual worlds may fail to open. The error message is "Cannot load Simulink 3D Animation client module: libGLU.so.1: cannot open shared object file: no such file or directory".
Workaround: As a workaround, install the libGLU.so.1 library from Red Hat 8 software repositories. This can be done by issuing the command yum install mesa-libGLU with administrator privileges from the Red Hat 8 command prompt.Alternately, or if you are unable to install the missing libGLU.so.1 library, please run MATLAB with software OpenGL using matlab -softwareopengl .

Summary: Symbol 't' resolves to intrinsic function instead of function argument in standalone charts for execution in?MATLAB
Description: In standalone charts for execution in MATLAB, if you use the symbol 't ' for a function argument, the value of t  always resolves to the current execution time of the chart, rather than the value passed to the function. For example, a function of the form: function y = foo(t)? ? y = t;end  returns the execution time, not the argument value.

Summary: Anonymous entity with fixed-point data type at control port of Entity Gate block causes?MATLAB crash
Description: If the control port of the Entity Gate block receives an anonymous entity with fixed-point data type, MATLAB crashes.

Summary: n-D Lookup table block run-time object reports incorrect number of DWorks for certain block configurations
Description: The n-D Lookup table block run-time object reports incorrect number of DWorks under these conditions: Tunable table size is enabled in the n-D Lookup table block. This is enabled by one of the following: Set Data specification ?parameter to?Lookup table object ?and select the?Support tunable size ?check box in the?associated Lookup table object? Set Data specification ?parameter to?Table and breakpoints ?and select the?Support tunable table size in code generation ?check box.  Number of table dimensions  parameter is greater than or equal to 3  for homogeneous-data algorithm majority and greater than or equal to 2  for mixed-data algorithm majority. An?example of homogeneous-data algorithm majority?is setting Array Layout  parameter to Column-major  and clearing the?Use algorithms optimized for row-major array layout  check box?in the model configuration parameter settings. An example of mixed-data algorithm majority is setting?Array Layout ?parameter to?Column-major ?and selecting the?Use algorithms optimized for row-major array layout ?check box?in the model configuration parameter settings.   Under these conditions, the number of DWorks reported is?one less than the correct value. For more information on run-time objects, see?https://www.mathworks.com/help/simulink/ug/accessing-block-data-during-simulation.html .

Summary: SIL/PIL coverage analysis fails for complex MCDC expressions
Description: If the generated code has more than one MCDC expression that contain too many conditions then the SIL/PIL simulation generates the following error: Build procedure for model: <model name> aborted due to an error.  The process cannot access the file because it is being used by another process.
Workaround: Disable the expression folding optimization:set_param(<model>, 'OptimizationCustomize', 'on', 'ExpressionFolding', 'off')
Products: Simulink Check, Requirements Toolbox, Simulink Coverage

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model block generating C++ class interface inside a For Each Subsystem block can lead to wrong answer
Description: The code generator can generate incorrect code for a model when all of these conditions are true: The model is configured to generate C++ code, and the model configuration parameter?Code interface packaging ?is set to?C++ class . The model has a For Each Subsystem block, which contains a Model block. The referenced model's configuration parameter?Total number of instances allowed per top model ?is set to?Multiple , and its Code interface packaging ?is also set to?C++ class .?   When these conditions exist, the code generated for the model can lead to incorrect results.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might be incorrect when the storage class of an output signal of a Variant Subsystem is set to Reusable
Description: Generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The storage class of an output signal of a Variant Subsystem is set to Reusable. ? The block parameter?Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate preprocessor conditionals  of the Variant Subsystem is turned on.
Workaround: Clear the block parameter?Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate preprocessor conditionals?of the Variant Subsystem.

Summary: Incorrect rate limits for NI devices with CJC compensation
Description: NI sessions containing channels from devices, such as the NI USB-9211A, with built-in CJC compensation using internal channels for auto-zero and CJC compensation, might report upper rate-limits and default rates that are too high. For example, s = daq.createSession('ni');ch = addAnalogInputChannel(s,'Dev1',0,'Thermocouple');ch.ThermocoupleType = 'K';data = startForeground(s); NI Error -200081:Sample rate exceeds the maximum sample rate for the number of channels specified.Reduce the sample rate or the number of channels. Increasing the convert rate or reducing thesample delay might also alleviate the problem, if you set either of them.Number of Channels: 2Sample Rate:  12.0Maximum Sample Rate:  6.0Task Name: _unnamedTask<112>Status Code: -200081
Workaround: To work around this issue, set the session rate to the maximum sample rate listed in the error message.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model with a Data Store Memory block driven by an?Assignment block?within a While Iterator Subsystem
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: An Assignment block is within a While Iterator Subsystem. A Data Store Read block drives an input port Y0 of the Assignment block. A Data Store Write block is connected to the an output port Y of the Assignment block.  During Simulink simulation, a local copy of a variable for the Data Store Read block?is updated for every iteration but not the generated code leading to an answer mismatch. For an example see issue_example_model_2016375.slx  in attached folder?issue_example_2016357.zip . The incorrect generated code for issue_example_model_2016375/While Iterator Subsystem/If Action Subsystem/Assignment is as follows:         global_index += issue_example_model_2016375_P.Constant_Value;      global_data[(int32_T)global_index - 1] = i; The correct generated code is as follows:?       global_index += issue_example_model_2016375_P.Constant_Value;      if (WhileIterator_IterationMarker[0] < 2) {        WhileIterator_IterationMarker[0] = 2U;        for (i = 0; i < 3; i++) {          Assignment_g[i] = global_data[i];        }      }      Assignment_g[(int32_T)global_index - 1] = s1_iter;      for (i = 0; i < 3; i++) {        global_data[i] = Assignment_g[i];      } Note that variable for the Data Store Read block?global_data  is updated during every iteration.

Summary: TableEntriesStyle property settings are not applied to?InternalLink or ExternalLink objects in tables in Word and HTML reports
Description: Some formatting objects in the TableEntriesStyle  property of a DOM API Table , FormalTable , or  MATLABTable  object are not applied to InternalLink  and ExternalLink  objects in the table.  For HTML reports, placing an HAlign  formatting object in the TableEntriesStyle  property or setting the TableEntriesHAlign  property of the table does not affect the horizontal alignment of link objects.  For HTML and Word reports, formatting objects in the TableEntriesStyle  property that modify the appearance of text, such as FontSize , FontFamily , Color , and Bold , have no effect on the link text.
Workaround: For HTML ReportsFor HAlign, wrap link objects in DOM Paragraph objects. For example:import mlreportgen.dom.*el = ExternalLink('https://www.mathworks.com/','MathWorks');linkParagraph = Paragraph(el)tbl = Table({'Table entry 1',linkParagraph;'Table entry 3','Table entry 4'});tbl.TableEntriesHAlign = 'center';For text formatting objects, set the Style property of the link object to include the text formatting objects in the TableEntriesStyle property. For example:import mlreportgen.dom.*tableEntriesStyle = {FontSize('20pt'),Bold(true)}; % Create an external link and set its style to match the table entries styleel = ExternalLink('https://www.mathworks.com/','MathWorks');el.Style =  tableEntriesStyle;tbl = Table({'Table entry 1',el;'Table entry 3','Table entry 4'});tbl.TableEntriesStyle = tableEntriesStyle;For Word ReportsSet the Style property of the DOM Text object that is the child of the link object to include any text formatting objects in the TableEntriesStyle property. For example:import mlreportgen.dom.*tableEntriesStyle = {FontSize('20pt'), Bold(true)}; % Create an external link and set its style to match the table entries styleel = ExternalLink('https://www.mathworks.com/','MathWorks');el.Children(1).Style =  tableEntriesStyle;tbl = Table({'Table entry 1',el;'Table entry 3','Table entry 4'});tbl.TableEntriesStyle = tableEntriesStyle;

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Unexpected behavior of model rebuild when using structure array with enumeration
Description: When a model uses a structure array with enumeration, the following unexpected rebuild behavior can occur:? For a structure array with multiple elements using the same enumeration value, switching between a serial build and a parallel build can unexpectedly trigger the model to rebuild, even though the structure array has not been modified. For a structure array with multiple elements using different enumeration values, changing the enumeration value of an array element to a value already used by another array element does not trigger a rebuild as expected.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB action language Stateflow charts that perform floating point operations may generate incorrect HDL Code
Description: Stateflow charts may generate incorrect HDL code when the following conditions are met:? The chart is a MATLAB action language chart Variables or parameters used in the chart are of single or double types Floating Point IP Library  is set to  Native Floating Point   ?Note? that floating point types specified at the inputs are correctly detected and reported.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for?a model containing a?Unit Delay block connected to a?reusable subsystem output
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a subsystem. In the subsystem Block Parameters dialog box, the?Function packaging ?parameter is set to? Reusable function .  The subsystem contains a Unit Delay block which directly connects to a subsystem output port.  In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Pass reusable subsystem outputs as  parameter is set to  Individual Arguments .  In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the?Optimize global data access ?parameter is set to  Use global to hold temporary results ?or? Minimize global data access .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Message Receive block may not set a correct initial valuewhen non-scalar message data type is different than initial value data type
Description: Message Receive block may set an incorrect initial valuewhen all the following conditions are met:  The initial value is a matrix or vector  The initial value data type is different than message data type   When these conditions are met, the MessageReceive block may set an incorrect initial value and this may produce wrong results for simulation and code generation.
Workaround: Change the initial value type to match the message data type. For example, if the message data type is int32 and its dimension is [2, 1], an acceptable initial value in Message Receive block is int32([10, 10]).

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Use of Constant block with a pass-through block might lead to incorrect code when the signal on the output is set to a custom storage class
Description: Incorrect code might be generated if all these conditions are true: Model contains a Constant block. The Constant block is connected to a block that has an empty output function when the sample time is constant, e.g. Probe block. The signal on the output is set to a custom storage class.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Constant output from Zero Crossing block might lead to incorrect code when the signal on the output is set to a custom storage class
Description: Incorrect code might be generated if all these conditions are true: Model contains a Zero Crossing block. The Zero Crossing block has constant sample time. The output signal from the Zero Crossing block is set to a custom storage class.

Summary: Python Engine does not display error message for unsupported struct arrays if number of elements is 2
Description: Python Engine converts MATLAB scalar struct types to Python dict types but does not support converting struct arrays to a Python type. However, for struct arrays with 2 elements, the appropriate error message is not displayed. Instead, only the first element of the struct is converted to a Python dict object, silently dropping the second element in the process. For example, in MATLAB:??   >> sta(1).label = 'a';   >> sta(1).data = magic(2);    >> sta(2).label = 'b';    >> sta(2).data = magic(3);   In Python, calling MATLAB to bring sta  into Python:   >>> eng = matlab.engine.connectMatlab();    >>> s = eng.workspace['sta']   >>> s   {'data': matlab.double([[1.0,3.0],[4.0,2.0]]), 'label': 'a'} Without warning, the second element in the struct array is missing.

Summary: Simulink.Signal object property InitialValue on root outport of referenced model is ignored in certain cases
Description: Simulink can produce incorrect numeric results when each of the following conditions are true: Simulink.Signal  object is resolved for signal feeding root outport of referenced model. Simulink.Signal  object has property InitialValue  and a custom storage class specified. Referenced model is conditionally executed. For example, the model is triggered or enabled.? Top model is set to accelerator or rapid accelerator simulation mode, or the parent model is a referenced model that uses an accelerated simulation mode.
Workaround: There are 2 ways to work around this problem:Specify the?InitialOutput property?on the outport block of the referenced model instead of specifying the InitialValue property on the?signal object.Simulate the top model and referenced models in normal simulation mode.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 7, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 4, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch between simulation and code generation results might occur?for CORDIC atan2 function in Trigonometric Function block?when block input is negative minimal value of its data type
Description: Mismatch between simulation and code generation results might occur for a Trigonometric Function block under these conditions: Function ?parameter is set to atan2.  Approximation method  parameter is set to CORDIC. Data type of both inputs is signed integer or signed fixed-point. Value of either input is the negative minimal value of its data type. For example, the negative minimal value for int8 is?-128 .
Fixed In: R2018b Update 5, R2019a Update 5, R2019b, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect proven out of bound array index on read of a C extern array field
Description: Code Prover incorrectly shows a proven (green) Out of bound array index  (OBAI) check when a C?extern  array field is read. If the size of the array is unspecified, the check should be unproven (orange). The following code demonstrates the issue: extern unsigned int array[];  unsigned int foo(int value) {  ? ? ? ? ?return array[value]; // OBAI check is green and should be orange  }?

Summary: S-function compilation fails when HeaderFile is not found for bus object
Description: The S-function compilation may fail when your model consists of S-Function Builder block that references a Bus and has following configurations: The Bus is not specifying an header file On the Libraries pane, no header file is specified in the External function declarations  field for the referenced bus On the Build Info pane, Enable support for Design Verifier  is enabled  For example, when a bus object without HeaderFile  "MyBus " is defined, and that the External function declarations  field contains the following:extern my_function(MyBus *inputbus); The S-function compilation fails as it cannot find a definition for "MyBus ".
Workaround: Include the generated header file containing the bus definition. This header is typically named with the name of the S-function followed by "_bus.h"? For example, if the S-function is "myFunction", fix the error by including this file at the beginning of the External function declarations field:#include "myFunction_bus.h"extern my_function(MyBus *inputbus);

Summary: MATLAB crashes when creating analysis model if component linked to Simulink behavior model
Description: MATLAB crashes when trying to create an analysis model from the UI or API. The crash occurs when the architecture model contains one or more components that are linked to a Simulink behavior model.

Summary: Code generation may fail with a For-Iterator,Variant subsystem and a Merge block
Description: Code generation errors out when all below conditions are met :  ?A variant subsystem?with GeneratePreprocessorConditionals  on, contains a For-Iterator subsystem inside one of the choices  The For-Iterator subsystem drives the output of the variant subsystem, which is connected to a Merge block   An example error message:?? The signal from 'model/block1/blocksys_two/If Action Subsystem1/Variant Subsystem/VARIANT_ONE/Subsystem/Assignment' output port 1 is required to be persistent, hence this signal cannot be connected to a Merge block?

Summary: Skip button ignored during the Find Dependencies step of Bus Refactoring
Description: If you modify a bus name or a bus element name in a project using the Bus Editor, then the project performs an analysis to evaluate the impact of the change. You see a progress dialog with a skip button. If you click the skip button immediately after the dialog is shown, the dialog might close without cancelling the operation. MATLAB might be left busy for some time, and then you see the results showing the impact of the change.?
Workaround: Wait for the analysis of the first file to start before clicking skip. The progress dialog closes and MATLAB finishes analyzing the current file before cancelling the operation.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for Identify Matrix block
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for a model that meets both of these conditions:  An Identity Matrix block (from the DSP System Toolbox block set) directly feeds into the Root Outport block. The signal at the output of the Identity Matrix?block has a non-Auto custom storage class (for example, ExportToFile ).

Summary: dsp.HDLChannelizer example errors on HDL code generation
Description: The HDLChannelizer8  function presented in the "Create Channelizer for HDL Generation" example, on the dsp.HDLChannelizer reference page, fails HDL code generation with the following error: Failed to compute constant value for nontunable property 'FilterCoefficients'.
Workaround: The error is because the coefficients from the dsp.Channelizer object are not marked as constant values for code generation.? Use this code for the function instead: function [yOut,validOut] = HDLChannelizer8(yIn,validIn)%HDLChannelizer8 % Process one sample of data using the dsp.HDLChannelizer System object% yIn is a fixed-point scalar or column vector. % validIn is a logical scalar value.% You can generate HDL code from this function.  persistent channelize8;  coder.extrinsic('tf');  coder.extrinsic('dsp.Channelizer');    if isempty(channelize8)    % Use filter coeffs from non-HDL channelizer, or supply your own.      channelizer = coder.const(dsp.Channelizer('NumFrequencyBands',8));    coeff = coder.const(tf(channelizer));    channelize8 = dsp.HDLChannelizer('NumFrequencyBands',8,'FilterCoefficients',coeff);  end      [yOut,validOut] = channelize8(yIn,validIn);end

Summary: Integration between MATLAB and Polyspace breaks after MATLAB is updated
Description: If you have previously linked a MATLAB and Polyspace installation, after updating MATLAB, you can no longer access the Polyspace functions. The issue happens because some parts of the MATLAB-Polyspace integration are reset when MATLAB is updated.
Workaround: After updating MATLAB, uninstall the previous link between MATLAB and Polyspace and repeat the steps to link the two installations.See the documentation for details:?https://www.mathworks.com/help/bugfinder/ug/integrate-polyspace-with-matlab-and-simulink.html.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Subscripted assignment from a 64-bit integer into a fi object can result in wrong answer
Description: When you assign a 64-bit integer into a fi object, it can give a wrong answer. For example:Create a 64-bit unsigned fi object equivalent to an unsigned 64-bit integer: >> a = fi(0,0,64,0);  ?Create a 64-bit integer with a specific bit pattern: >> b = typecast(uint32([2359331; 2490405]),'uint64');  ?Assign the 64-bit integer into the fi object: >> a(:) = b;  The stored integer of a should be the same as b, but it is not. >> storedInteger(a)   ans =? uint64 ? 10696208031154212 >> b   b =? uint64 ? 10696208031154211
Workaround: Cast the right-hand side to the type of the left-hand-side.  Following the steps of the problem statement above:Assign in the 64-bit integer into the fi object using a cast on the right-hand side.>> a(:) = cast(b,'like',a);The stored integer of a is correctly the same as b.>> storedInteger(a)ans =? uint64 ? 10696208031154211 >> bb =? uint64 ? 10696208031154211

Summary: svd can return invalid NaN values in the singular vectors of some rare matrices.
Description: For a few rare matrices, [U,S,V] = svd(A) ?returns the?singular vectors in the columns of U  and V  with incorrect NaN  entries. >> [U,S,V] = svd(A);>> any(isnan(V(:))) | any(isnan(U(:)))  ans =??  ? ? ? 1?

Summary: Property Inspector hangs for graphics objects with thousands of children
Description: If you open the Property Inspector for an object with thousands of children, the Property Inspector may hang or be slow to respond. For example, the Property Inspector may hang if you open it for an Axes object with thousands of plotted lines.
Workaround: View or edit properties of objects with thousands of children using the command line.

Summary: Template makefile build process generates file-specific makefile rules that use absolute paths
Description: If you use the template makefile approach, the build process compiles source files by using file-specific makefile rules. The rules use absolute paths instead of tokenized paths, e.g.?$(START_DIR)/path  .  The use of absolute paths might produce build errors if:  Your workflow involves a makefile relocation that causes the absolute path to become invalid.  An absolute path contains a space.
Workaround: If a space in a source file path causes a build error, move the file to a folder on a file path that does not contain a space.If your build folder name contains a space, then change your build folder to a folder that does not have a space in its file path.

Summary: Roundtripping between IBM DOORS Next Generation and Simulink Requirements using ReqIF might fail
Description: Roundtripping is the process of exporting, importing, and updating requirements between DOORS Next Generation and Simulink Requirements. Roundtripping follows the process: Export content from IBM DOORS Next Generation into a ReqIF file. Import the ReqIF file into Simulink Requirements. Unlock and edit the requirement data in Simulink Requirements. Export from Simulink Requirements into a ReqIF file, using the previous import settings. Re-import the ReqIF file into IBM DOORS and have IBM DOORS merge/update changes to the requirement data.   Step 5 can result in an error such as "Import failed" .

Summary: Application does not synchronize PTP (IEEE 1588) clock to grand master PTP device
Description: A Simulink Real-Time application does not synchronize its Precision Time Protocol (PTP) clock with other parties when the following conditions are true: The application includes an IEEE 1588 Real-Time UDP  block.  The block parameter Destination IP address of transmit packets  is set to Standard PTP multicast .   If the block parameter Slave Only  is set, the protocol remains in the Initializing  state (value 4) instead of the Slave  state (value 9). If the block parameter Slave Only  is not set, the protocol enters the Master  state (value 6) instead of the Slave  state (value 9), even when a third party device (for example, Grand Master Clock) is in Master  state.
Workaround: Set the block parameter "Destination IP address of transmit packets" to "Specify" and use the address "255.255.255.255". Note that third party PTP applications in the network must be configured in the same way.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 5, R2019a Update 3, R2019b, R2020a

Summary: Scale to fit fails for tall images
Description: The DOM API ScaleToFit format fails to scale images that are too tall to fit between the top and bottom margins of a page. This issue occurs in both Microsoft Word and PDF output. The following script illustrates the issue. import mlreportgen.dom.*d = Document('repro_pdf_portrait', 'docx');open(d);plo = d.CurrentPageLayout;plo.PageMargins.Top = '4in';plo.PageMargins.Bottom = '4in';plo.PageMargins.Left = '0.25in';plo.PageMargins.Right = '0.25in';% b747 ~= 5x6 inchesimg = Image(which('b747.jpg'));img.Style = {ScaleToFit};append(d, img);close(d);rptview(d);
Workaround: Scale the image in your MATLAB report generation program:import mlreportgen.dom.*d = Document('repro_pdf_portrait', 'docx');open(d);plo = d.CurrentPageLayout;plo.PageMargins.Top = '4in';plo.PageMargins.Bottom = '4in';plo.PageMargins.Left = '0.25in';plo.PageMargins.Right = '0.25in';% b747 ~= 5x6 inchesimg = Image(which('b747.jpg'));%img.Style = {ScaleToFit};scaletofit(img, '8in', '3in');append(d, img);close(d);rptview(d);function scaletofit(img, maxWidth, maxHeight)    maxWidth = mlreportgen.utils.units.toPixels(maxWidth);    maxHeight = mlreportgen.utils.units.toPixels(maxHeight);    imgWidth = mlreportgen.utils.units.toPixels(img.Width);    imgHeight = mlreportgen.utils.units.toPixels(img.Height);    imgAspectRatio = imgHeight/imgWidth;    if imgHeight > maxHeight        imgHeight = maxHeight;        imgWidth = imgHeight / imgAspectRatio;    end    if imgWidth > maxWidth        imgWidth = maxWidth;        imgHeight = imgHeight * imgAspectRatio;    end    img.Width = sprintf('%4.1fpx', imgWidth);    img.Height = sprintf('%4.1fpx', imgHeight);end

Summary: Toolbox project files sometimes fail to open
Description: Toolbox project files (.prj ) created while packaging a toolbox might fail to open. When this occurs, MATLAB displays the error:?Not a valid project .? Restarting MATLAB usually resolves the issue.? This error does not prevent packaging new toolboxes.

Summary: Opening app in App Designer returns the warning: This canvas does not support painters
Description: When opening an app in App Designer, the MATLAB command window might return several warnings with the message:This canvas does not support painters   In most cases,?the app was created in a version of MATLAB that is older than R2019a.
Workaround: Stop the warnings in your MATLAB session by pressing Ctrl+C.

Summary: Training a convolutional neural network with large 3D input data results in a crash
Description: MATLAB crashes when training a network with a large 3D input data size or a huge number of observations (for example, of the order of 1e+03). You might see an error like the following:An unexpected error occurred during CUDA execution. The CUDA error was:an illegal memory access was encountered

Summary: Ideal Fixed Gear Transmission block mask has two incorrect parameter descriptions
Description: On the Ideal Fixed Gear Transmission block mask, these parameter descriptions are incorrect Initial gear number, G_o ?(appears as autoblks_sharedautoblkFixedGearTransblkPrm_G_o) Initial neutral gear input rotational velocity, omegaN_o  (appears as autoblks_sharedautoblkFixedGearTransblkPrm_omegaN_o)  The issue is cosmetic and has no?functional impact on the block operation or performance.

Summary: surrogateopt checkpoint file?becomes very large
Description: Because of a bug in the save  function, a surrogateopt  checkpoint file can grow at every function evaluation. The size of the checkpoint file can far exceed the size of the saved data.

Summary: CDFX-file import fails if the file name extension contains uppercase characters
Description: When using the cdfx  function to import CDFX-files, the import fails if the filename extension contains any uppercase characters. The resulting error message does not clearly indicate the source of the issue.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, change the CDFX-file name extension to all lowercase characters.

Summary: Building a simulation target MEX-file can generate an error message
Description: When a model code generation target language is set to C++ , model simulation might generate the following error message in the Command Window:   Error(s) encountered while building simulation target MEX-file for model 'my_model'.  ...?  error C2065: 'static_cast': undeclared identifier  ... This is caused by blocks that use simulation through code generation creating simulation C code that contains C++ static_cast . Blocks that might trigger this error include the C Caller block, MATLAB System block, and Dataflow Subsystem block.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, change the model Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Target selection >? Language from C++ to C.

Summary: Setting a format in the Style property of a PowerPoint (PPT) API object causes MATLAB to crash
Description: Setting a format in the Style  property of a PPT API object causes?MATLAB to crash if there is a corresponding format property. For example, the following code causes MATLAB to crash because the Style  property is used instead of the Bold   property. para = mlreportgen.ppt.Paragraph();  para.Style = {mlreportgen.ppt.Bold(true)};?
Workaround: Do not specify the format in the Style property when there is a corresponding format property. For example, to specify that the text of a paragraph is bold, set the Bold property instead of specifying the Bold format in the Style property.para = mlreportgen.ppt.Paragraph();para.Bold = true;

Summary: The ocvMxArrayFromVector function MEX file build produces a linking error
Description: The interface to the OpenCV library does not export the ocvMxArrayFromVector  function properly during the MEX build. The linking process produces the following error:  'ocvMxArrayFromVector': identifier not found ?
Workaround: To workaround this bug:Define the ocvMxArrayFromVector function locally based on the input data type. Copy and paste one of the seven definitions below: mxArray *ocvMxArrayFromVector_double(const std::vector<real_T> &v){?? ?mxArray *out = mxCreateNumericMatrix(v.size(), 1, mxDOUBLE_CLASS, mxREAL);?? ?std::copy(v.begin(), v.end(), reinterpret_cast<real_T *>(mxGetData(out)));?? ?return out;}mxArray *ocvMxArrayFromVector_single(const std::vector<real32_T> &v){?? ?mxArray *out = mxCreateNumericMatrix(v.size(), 1, mxSINGLE_CLASS, mxREAL);?? ?std::copy(v.begin(), v.end(), reinterpret_cast<real32_T *>(mxGetData(out)));?? ?return out;}mxArray *ocvMxArrayFromVector_uint8(const std::vector<uint8_T> &v){?? ?mxArray *out = mxCreateNumericMatrix(v.size(), 1, mxUINT8_CLASS, mxREAL);?? ?std::copy(v.begin(), v.end(), reinterpret_cast<uint8_T *>(mxGetData(out)));?? ?return out;}mxArray *ocvMxArrayFromVector_uint16(const std::vector<uint16_T> &v){?? ?mxArray *out = mxCreateNumericMatrix(v.size(), 1, mxUINT16_CLASS, mxREAL);?? ?std::copy(v.begin(), v.end(), reinterpret_cast<uint16_T *>(mxGetData(out)));?? ?return out;}mxArray *ocvMxArrayFromVector_uint32(const std::vector<uint32_T> &v){?? ?mxArray *out = mxCreateNumericMatrix(v.size(), 1, mxUINT32_CLASS, mxREAL);?? ?std::copy(v.begin(), v.end(), reinterpret_cast<uint32_T *>(mxGetData(out)));?? ?return out;}mxArray *ocvMxArrayFromVector_int8(const std::vector<int8_T> &v){?? ?mxArray *out = mxCreateNumericMatrix(v.size(), 1, mxINT8_CLASS, mxREAL);?? ?std::copy(v.begin(), v.end(), reinterpret_cast<int8_T *>(mxGetData(out)));?? ?return out;}mxArray *ocvMxArrayFromVector_int16(const std::vector<int16_T> &v){?? ?mxArray *out = mxCreateNumericMatrix(v.size(), 1, mxINT16_CLASS, mxREAL);?? ?std::copy(v.begin(), v.end(), reinterpret_cast<int16_T *>(mxGetData(out)));?? ?return out;}mxArray *ocvMxArrayFromVector_int32(const std::vector<int32_T> &v){?? ?mxArray *out = mxCreateNumericMatrix(v.size(), 1, mxINT32_CLASS, mxREAL);?? ?std::copy(v.begin(), v.end(), reinterpret_cast<int32_T *>(mxGetData(out)));?? ?return out;}

Summary: Logging data for multiple signals in Simulink using block port handles consumes excessive memory
Description: Logging multiple signals in a model using block port handles can result in a memory leak on the order of gigabytes. You must close and start MATLAB again to recover the memory.?
Workaround: Instead of logging signals using block port handles, like this:for m = 1:numel(blocks)    portHandle = get_param(blocks{m},'PortHandles');    set_param(portHandle.Outport(1),'DataLogging','on')enduse the InstrumentedSignals property for the model to specify logged signals in a single call to?set_param:?Get the Simulink.HMI.InstrumentedSignals object that specifies logging for the model.instSigs = get_param(mdl,'InstrumentedSignals');Add a Simulink.HMI.SignalSpecification object containing the block path and output port number for each additional signal you want to log.?sigSpec = SimulinkHMI.SignalSpecification;for m = 1:numel(blocks)? ? ?sigSpec.BlockPath = blocks(m);? ? ?sigSpec.OutputPortIndex = 1;? ? ?instSigs.add(sigSpec);endSpecify the new value for the?InstrumentedSignals property. set_param(mdl,'InstrumentedSignals',sigs)?

Summary: Update diagram operation for models may be slower during dimension propagation
Description: When a large model with muxed signals or virtual buses is instructed to update the diagram or to run the simulation, the model update time might significantly increase due to the signal conversion blocks when compared to the model runtime in previous releases. If this occurs, Simulink might take longer to run simulation or update diagram, showing "Compiling: Propagating frame data and dimensions" on the bottom left corner of Simulink window.
Workaround: Specify the output dimension of all Signal Conversion blocks in the model explicitly.?

Summary: The Vehicle Body 1DOF Longitudinal block incorrectly determines the loads on the front and rear axles
Description: The Vehicle Body 1DOF Longitudinal Wheel block incorrectly determines the loads on the front and rear axles. The axle loads do not include drag forces, grade forces, or forces externally applied at the vehicle center of gravity.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible when configuring function prototype control for referenced model
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of the following conditions: The model references a model that specifies that the input and output arguments of the step function use the same variable name. The referenced model is a variant of a variant subsystem. On the variant subsystem block parameters dialog box, the parameter Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate preprocessor conditionals  is selected.?
Workaround: On the variant subsystem block parameters dialog box, clear the Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate preprocessor conditionals.

Summary: readtable?errors reading text files with duplicate variable names with length greater than or equal to namelengthmax
Description: readtable  throws an error when you try to read a text file containing duplicate variable names with length greater than or equal to namelengthmax . For example, given a text file data.csv  with the following contents:  SomeReallyLongTableVariableNameGreaterThanOrEqualToNameLengthMax,SomeReallyLongTableVariableNameGreaterThanOrEqualToNameLengthMax1,23,4  readtable  will error as follows:  >> filename = "data.csv";>> t = readtable(filename, opts);Error using readtable (line 216)Expected a cell array of valid variable names.Note: readtable detected the following parameters:'Delimiter', ',', 'HeaderLines', , 'ReadVariableNames', true, 'Format', ''
Workaround: Using detectImportOptions with readtable. For example:>> filename = "data.csv";>> opts = detectImportOptions(filename);>> t = readtable(filename, opts);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect Code Generation: Uninitialized variable passed to AUTOSAR?Rte_Write call when subsystem signal branches to root outport and another subsystem
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for an AUTOSAR model when all of the following conditions are true: An output signal from a subsystem branches. One branch of the signal feeds a root outport. The AUTOSAR data access mode of the root outport is ExplicitSend . The other signal branch feeds another subsystem.  In the generated code, an uninitialized stack variable is passed as an argument to the Rte_Write  function.
Workaround: Place a Signal Conversion block on either branch of the branched subsystem output signal.

Summary: Error at start of external mode simulation on Arduino when using XCP on Serial and non-default code generation folder
Description: An external mode simulation on the Arduino target produces an error at the start when these conditions apply:?  Configuration parameter?Transport layer  is?XCP on Serial .  Simulink preference?Code generation folder  is not empty or? CodeGenFolder  is set by?Simulink.fileGenControl .         The error message is:  Executing the 'Connect' command produced the following error:   DWARF Parser internal error:? Parser::initialize failed to open file
Workaround: Reset the code generation folder to the default:In the Simulink Preferences dialog box, clear the?Cache folder and?Code generation folder fields.Reset Simulink.fileGenControl settings by running the command?Simulink.fileGenControl('reset').?Alternatively, run the Simulink external mode simulation on a Linux or Mac computer.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated for Switch block whose signal source is a Constant block inside an enabled subsystem
Description: The code generated from a model may be incorrect if all of the following conditions are true: The model contains a Switch block whose signal source is a Constant block that is inside an Enabled Subsystem block.? In the Constant block parameters dialog box, the Sample time  parameter is set to Inf . The model configuration parameter Default parameter behavior  is set to Inlined .
Workaround: Set the sample time of the Constant block that is inside the Enabled Subsystem block to -1, or clear the model configuration parameter Block reduction.

Summary: LTE HDL SIB1 Recovery example failing to decode PDSCH due to an error in DCI decoding
Description: For DCI format1C, the DCI message is correctly decoded, but the DCI Format output signal is cleared before the next stage in processing can read the value. The DCI format parameter calculated is now registered on condition of successful decode, and held until the decoder is reset.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Shift operations incorrectly cast to unsigned type
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for shift operations matching the following situation:? The Casting mode  is set to Standards Compliant . The result of the bitshift operation is assigned to a floating point variable or casted to a floating point type. The value of the bitshift operation is a negative value.  In the incorrect generated code, the result of the bitshift is cast to an unsigned type and then cast to the floating point data type. An additional cast to a signed type is required to properly represent negative values. Note that the cast to unsigned is required to comply with MISRA. For example, when generating code for the following function with standards casting mode enabled?    function issue_example_2008845(x)       global out      out = double(bitshift(int64(1), int64(x)));  end The bitshift operation generated is?   out = (double)(unsigned long)((unsigned long)1L << (unsigned long)(long)x); Instead an additional cast to a signed integer type is needed prior to casting to double  out = (double)(long)(unsigned long)((unsigned long)1L << (unsigned long)x);

Summary: Incorrect warning for Discrete-Time Integrator block for Model Advisor MAAB check db_0140:Display of basic block parameters
Description: When you run Model Advisor MAAB check for db_0140 :Display of basic block parameters , the following error message is shown for a? Discrete-Time Integrator ?block??: The following blocks use and fail to display nondefault values:?  Block : untitled/Discrete-Time Integrator   Parameter : SampleTime  Expected Value :1  Actual Value : -1  Please note that the default SampleTime ?block parameter for the?Discrete-Time Integrator ?block is -1.

Summary: Tool-coupling FMU fails to simulate if current working directory is Windows UNC path
Description: Tool-coupling FMU exported from Simulink fails to simulate in FMU import tools, if the import tool unzips FMU to Windows UNC path (for example,?\\localhost\shared_folder\) . Specifically, if the import tool is Simulink, tool-coupling FMU fails to simulate when the MATLAB current working directory is UNC path. An example error message from Simulink FMU Import Block: Log from FMU: [category:logStatusError, status:fmi2Error] fmi2Instantiate: Error using cd Cannot CD to C:\work\localhost\shared_folder\slprj\_fmu\12345abc\testFMU\resources (Name is nonexistent or not a directory).?  Error in supplied FMU: An error occurred in function 'fmi2Instantiate' for block 'model/FMU' during simulation. For more information, see the FMU troubleshooting documentation.?

Summary: Simulink might crash when extracting signals from an element of a nonvirtual bus that a virtual bus contains
Description: Simulink crashes when all of these conditions apply: A virtual bus has multiple levels of hierarchy and contains a nonvirtual bus. A Bus Selector block selects a multidimensional signal from this nonvirtual bus. A Demux block extracts signals from this multidimensional signal.
Workaround: For R2018b, unzip and run the attached script every time you start Simulink. The script enables Simulink to convert a nonvirtual bus into a virtual bus when the nonvirtual bus is contained in a virtual bus.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 5, R2019a Update 4, R2019b, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect ARXML data type tag?'IMPLEMENTATION-DATA-TYPE' generated for AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform
Description: When generating code for a model configured for the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform, data types are exported to ARXML files using the tag 'IMPLEMENTATION-DATA-TYPE'. This tag is used in the AUTOSAR Classic Platform?to export data types. For the?AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform, the correct tag is 'CPP-IMPLEMENTATION-DATA-TYPE'.? ?See also?2073966 ?for a related issue.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: HDL Reciprocal block might produce wrong answer for fixed-point inputs
Description: HDL Reciprocal block might produce mismatched?answers during?normal mode simulation and?code generation? when the following conditions are true: The input value is zero The input type is fixed-point Number of iterations  is set to 1

Summary: Error when reverting an architecture model file with unsaved changes,?using MATLAB source control integration for Git or SVN
Description: It is not possible to revert a file whileediting a Simulink architecture model under Git or SVN source control fromwithin MATLAB. With unsaved changes, users encounter an error when discarding local changes or reverting the file to an older revision.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, save or close the model before reverting changes.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model with a?Bus Assignment block and?an?Assignment block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a?Bus Assignment block block and an?Assignment?block.  An?Assignment ?block connects to the?assignment input ports?of the?Bus Assignment ?block. The?Assignment ?block and?Bus Assignment block are inside ?the same?For-Iterator ?or?While-Iterator ?subsystem.
Workaround: Clear the model configuration parameter Perform in-place updates for Assignment and Bus Assignment blocks.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulation results might be incorrect for a model consisting of cascading Bus Selector and Bus Creator blocks
Description: Simulation results might be incorrect in both normal and accelerator modes for a model that meets these conditions: A Bus Selector block selects more than one element of a bus.? The elements are permuted and sent to a Bus Creator block of the same bus object type. The model configuration parameter Element name mismatch  is not set to error .
Workaround: In the model configuration parameters dialog box, set the Element name mismatch diagnostic parameter to error.

Summary: Initial condition dx/dt?field appears on the x pane of the block dialog for Second-Order Integrator block.
Description: The initial condition value of dx/dt  state in the Second-Order Integrator block can be provided on the block dialog using the Initial condition dx/dt? edit field. This field should normally appear on the dx/dt  pane of the block dialog as is expected and documented. However, it is on the x   pane of the block below the State name x  field.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Saturation block can give wrong answers in simulation and code generation when input and output data types are integer types with different signs
Description: The Saturation block can give wrong answers in simulation and code generation when all of the following are true: The input and output data types are both integral types. The input and/or output data type has the same number of bits as the largest hardware integer size in Configuration Parameters  > Hardware Implementation  > Device details . The input and output types have different signedness. Not all values of the input type can be represented by the output type.  For example, an input type of int8 and an output type of uint32 meet these conditions when the largest hardware integer size is 32 bits.
Workaround: 1.?Set the Output data type parameter of the Saturation?block Signal Attributes tab?to Inherit: Same as input.2.?Connect the output of the Saturation block to a Data Type Conversion block if a data type change is needed.

Summary: Order spectrum dialog in the Diagnostic Feature Designer app selects the wrong tacho signal.
Description: This problem pertains to the Diagnostic Feature Designer app.  In the Tacho Information section of the order spectrum dialog, the user can select among three forms of rotation-speed input using radio buttons. Each radio button has an associated dropdown menu.  If the user selects one signal A for radio button 2, but then selects radio button 3 and uses a different signal B, the app still uses signal A to compute the order spectrum even though the current radio button selection points to signal B.  For example, suppose that radio button 2 has dropdown selection "Vibration_TSA/Data" and radio button 3 has dropdown selection "Tacho/Data".? Changing radio buttons from 2 to 3 does not change the signal that the dialog processes. The dialog still processes "Vibration_TSA/Data", which is incorrect. The dialog should process "Tacho/Data".

Summary: The "Xilinx System Generator for DSP with HDL Coder" example does not generate HDL code
Description: HDL Code Generation for the example "Using Xilinx System Generator for DSP with HDL Coder " fails when using Xilinx Vivado System Generator for DSP. The example was configured to be used with Xilinx ISE System Generator.
Workaround: In order to make the demo model work, the user needs to perform the following modifications on the model:Navigate to the subsystem: "hdlcoder_slsysgen/SLandSysGen/sysgendut" and change the device setting through the "System Generator" block to a device supported by Xilinx Vivado (e.g.: Virtex7).Expand the subsystem: "hdlcoder_slsysgen_edit/SLandSysGen/sldut" by right-clicking on the subsystem and then choosing: "Subsystem & Model Reference > Expand Subsystem". Alternately, it can also be done by selecting the subsystem and using the shortcut-key combination: Ctrl+Shift+GNavigate to the "Choose synthesis tool" option in "Configuration Parameters > HDL Code Generation > EDA Tool Scripts > Synthesis scripts" and modify it to "Xilinx Vivado" instead of "Xilinx ISE".The user can also download the attached "hdlcoder_slsysgen_modified.zip" file. This file contains modified demo models for releases starting from R2015a to R2019a.

Summary: MATLAB Job Scheduler workers run out of memory after increasing JOB_MANAGER_MAXIMUM_MEMORY in mjs_def file
Description: Worker nodes of a MATLAB Job Scheduler cluster can run out of Java heap memory if: The JOB_MANAGER_MAXIMUM_MEMORY setting in the mjs_def file (on the headnode) has been increased significantly from its default value of 1024m *without* increasing the value of WORKER_MAXIMUM_MEMORY from its default value of 1024m (on the worker nodes) A worker node in the cluster has a large number of MATLAB workers  The default values for JOB_MANAGER_MAXIMUM_MEMORY and WORKER_MAXIMUM_MEMORY support up to several hundred workers per worker node.? If you increase the JOB_MANAGER_MAXIMUM_MEMORY value by a factor of N *without* changing the WORKER_MAXIMUM_MEMORY value, then the number of workers supported on a worker node decreases by a factor of N.? If the number of workers on a worker node exceeds this value, then the worker node can run out of memory. You can identify this issue by the following error messages in the workergroup-service-log file, which you can find on the worker nodes in the folder specified by LOGBASE in the mjs_def file: 2019 05 01 09:32:18.516 UTC | 0 | Thread: NativeIpcFevalProvider fEvalExecutor-0, terminated with java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space: 2019 05 01 09:32:18.516 UTC | 0 | Exception: java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space 2019 05 01 10:02:46.215 UTC | 0 | Thread: PeerSession fListenerExec-0, terminated with java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: GC overhead limit exceeded: 2019 05 01 10:02:46.215 UTC | 0 | Exception: java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: GC overhead limit exceeded MATLAB clients can see the task fail in a number of ways, including the following error message: Error Report: Job errored or did not reach the state 'finished'.  Cannot rerun job because at least one of its tasks has no rerun attempts left (The task has no rerun attempts left.).  Original cancel message:  TaskEvaluator is already evaluating a task.?
Workaround: As a workaround, make sure that if you increase the JOB_MANAGER_MAXIMUM_MEMORY setting from its default value of 1024m in the mjs_def file (on the headnode), then you also increase the WORKER_MAXIMUM_MEMORY setting by the same amount (on the worker nodes).Note: In R2019a, there is usually little benefit in increasing the JOB_MANAGER_MAXIMUM_MEMORY from its default value of 1024m.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for MATLAB code consisting of matrix accesses that use logical indexing
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for MATLAB code consisting of the following elements: The code contains a loop in which one matrix (A) subscripts and grabs elements from another matrix (B). The loop contains writes to all elements of A.  For example, incorrect generated code occurs for the following MATLAB code:? function B = example( in )    B = zeros(1,400);    A = (4:13);    for i=1:10        B(A) = in;        A(i) = 8;    endend The incorrect generated C code is below. Notice the write to 'A ' and the indexing operation using 'A ' inside the loop are incorrectly removed.? void example(const double in[10], double B[400]){  int i;  memset(&B[0], 0, 400U * sizeof(double));  for (i = 0; i < 10; i++) {    B[7] = in[i];  }} See also Bug Report ID?2041681.

Summary: Creating or deleting a new port in a model to which a reference component connects may cause incorrect connections to be made to ports on the reference components
Description: In some scenarios,?creating a new port or deleting a new port in a model that a reference component connects leads to incorrect connections to ports on the reference components. For example, consider a model where connection A connects to port P1 on reference component C1. Now, if a new port, P2, is created above P1 in the model that C1 refers to, then connection A would now be connected to P2 instead of P1.

Summary: Error when invoking the getChoices API on a Variant Component that contains multiple Reference Components
Description: Invoking the getChoices  API on a Variant Component that contains multiple Reference Components causes the following error: Error using horzcat?Dimensions of arrays being concatenated are not consistent.

Summary: On Linux and Mac platforms,?MATLAB classifies class definition files as script files when accessed by soft link
Description: MATLAB incorrectly classifies a class definition file referenced by a soft link as a script file, which makes the class inaccessible.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when copying figures with complicated content
Description: MATLAB?  might crash due to a Java?  exception when you copy the content of a figure, and there is not enough memory to store the copy. Here are a few situations that might cause this problem:The figure contains many different or complicated graphics objects The figure uses transparency or custom lighting effects You are using the figure to visualize a large amount of data
Workaround: It might be possible to avoid the crash by either:Copying the figure as a bitmap image, rather than as a vector graphicIncreasing the memory available for the Java heapTo increase the memory available for the Java heap, perform these steps:Open MATLAB Preferences from the Environment?tab in the Toolstrip.Select MATLAB > General > Java Heap Memory.Use the slider to increase the Java heap size, and click OK.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Assertion when generating HDL code for models containing Gain blocks with complex floating-point Gain parameter
Description: HDL code generation from a model containing Gain blocks can result in an assertion, if the following conditions are met: Input signal to the block is of single  or double data? type Input signal is not complex-valued but the Gain  parameter has a complex value Gain  value is not a power of two Floating Point Library  is set to Native Floating Point   Under these conditions, you will see this assertion during code generation: Assertion failed:? \matlab\src\cgir_hdl\dom_pir_core\port.cpp:483:|nfp_out_im|: name was not uniquified on comp: HDL_Coder_ERROR" RefNum="c46" UserName="nfp_gain_pow2_single_1" RTTIClass ="class pir::NetworkInstComp" Network="n0"/>??
Workaround: Breaking the complex valued constant into and real and imaginary parts in separate gain blocks would avoid the assertion.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong answer when assigning to a variable of Complex type while its being read on RHS with an aliased handle class object
Description: Incorrect code generation might occur for MATLAB code when the following conditions are true: A MATLAB function creates at least one handle class object and its alias. A class property is a complex  array. The MATLAB function contains an assignment from the aliased object to the handle class variable in a loop (the variables from condition 1).
