Consider the following example??where the function?createMCOSAlias ?returns an alias (or reference) to the input variable:??  function [cv] = example_2005746(ca)  persistent handle_ca  if isempty(handle_ca)  ? ? handle_ca = handleVar;  ? ? handle_ca.value =? ca;  end    handle_alias_ca = createMCOSAlias( handle_ca);  global P1  for k1 = 12:43  ? ? for k2 = 1:22  ? ? ? ? handle_ca.value( k2 + 1, k1 - 7 ) =...  ? ? ? ? ? ? cast( handle_alias_ca.value( 6, 33 ).* P1( 1, 1 ), 'double' );  ? ? end  end    ca = handle_alias_ca.value;  end  In this?MATLAB code, the handle class object,?handle_ca? and?its alias,? ?handle_alias_ca ?satisfy condition 1. The input to the function is a complex array (ca ) which is assigned to the variable of handle class object, which satisfies condition 2. Finally, the assignment inside the loop from?handle_alias_ca  to?handle_ca  satisfies condition 3. The??generated C code shown below is incorrectly?reading the value of??handle_alias_ca->value[869].re  which was written in the statement above it. Instead it should have used the original before write in previous statement.? handle_alias_ca = createMCOSAlias(&handle_ca);??d = P1.re;??d1 = P1.im;??for (k1 = 0; k1 < 32; k1++) {? ??for (k2 = 0; k2 < 22; k2++) {? ? ? i = (k2 + 27 * (k1 + 4)) + 1;? ? ? handle_ca.value[i].re = handle_alias_ca->value[869].re * d -? ? ? ? handle_alias_ca->value[869].im * d1;? ? ? handle_ca.value[i].im = handle_alias_ca->value[869].re * d1 +? ? ? ? handle_alias_ca->value[869].im * d;? ? ? if (*emlrtBreakCheckR2012bFlagVar != 0) {? ? ? ? emlrtBreakCheckR2012b(sp);? ? ? }? ? }  The generated C code for the above function should be the following where the writes to a real and imaginary part are stored in the temporary variables 're' and 'im' before the reads occur as is shown below: handle_alias_ca = createMCOSAlias(&handle_ca);  ? d = P1.re;  ? d1 = P1.im;  ? for (k1 = 0; k1 < 32; k1++) {  ? ? for (k2 = 0; k2 < 22; k2++) {  ? ? ? re  = handle_alias_ca->value[869].re * d - handle_alias_ca->value[869].im * d1;  ? ? ? im  = handle_alias_ca->value[869].re * d1 + handle_alias_ca->value[869].im * d;?  ? ? ? i = (k2 + 27 * (k1 + 4)) + 1;  ? ? ? handle_ca.value[i].re = re;  ? ? ? handle_ca.value[i].im = im;  ? ? ? if (*emlrtBreakCheckR2012bFlagVar != 0) {  ? ? ? ? emlrtBreakCheckR2012b(sp);  ? ? ? }  ? ? }  To reproduce this bug, see the example in the attached zip file  'example_2005746.zip' .

Summary: Predicting responses for a sequence-to-sequence deep neural network returns sequences of incorrect length when the inputs are 2-D image or 3-D image sequences
Description: Responses with wrong sequence lengths are returned when using classify, predict, classifyAndUpdateState, or predictAndUpdateState when all of the following conditions apply: The network has a sequenceInputLayer and takes 2-D or 3-D image sequences as input The network is set up for sequence-to-sequence tasks (e.g. all LSTM and BiLSTM layers use OutputMode 'sequence')  The resulting output sequences should have the same length as the input sequences (if the SequenceLength parameter is set to 'longest' or a fixed integer) but instead they all have the same incorrect length.

Summary: Model Workspace dialog box does not display MATLAB code?that exceeds?16382?characters.
Description: If you specify text that exceeds 16382 characters in the MATLAB Code  box of the Model Workspace dialog box, the?MATLAB Code ?box displays the dimensions of the specified code instead of the code. For example, if you enter 16383 characters in the?MATLAB Code ?box and click?Apply , the?MATLAB Code ?box displays?<1x16383 char> .
Workaround: To view the text, in the MATLAB command window, enter these commands.model_workspace_object = get_param(bdroot, 'ModelWorkspace');model_workspace_object.DataSource = 'MATLAB Code'; matlab_code_text = model_workspace_object.MATLABCodeTo verify that the text is not truncated, enter this command.length(matlab_code_text)

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Sharing with certain configurations of enabled subsystems results in a mismatch in the validation model
Description: The sharing optimization in HDL Coder using atomic subsystems and enabled subsystems can result in a mismatch in the validation model. The following two configurations incorrectly perform sharing. Sharing of atomic subsystems inside an enabled subsystem with synchronous semantics (state control block set to synchronous) Sharing of atomic subsystems which are also enabled subsystems (classic or synchronous semantics)  In R2019a update 5, HDL Coder avoids performing the incorrect optimization which leads to a mismatch in the validation model.

Summary: surrogateopt ignores a new MaxTime option when resuming from a checkpoint file.
Description: When you run?surrogateopt ?from a checkpoint file and pass a new MaxTime  option, surrogateopt  does not use the new value of MaxTime .
Workaround: Modify the checkpoint file so that surrogateopt can use the new value of MaxTime. To do so, run the following function on the checkpoint file. The function loads the data, modifies the data, and saves the data back to the checkpoint file.Step1. Save the fixCheckPointMaxTime function (listed below) to a file?named 'fixCheckPointMaxTime.m' in the current folder. Step 2.?Run fixCheckPointMaxTime(chkPointFile, MaxTime), where chkPointFile is the checkpoint file name, and MaxTime is the new maximum time.Step 3. Run surrogateopt(chkPointFile, options).?function fixCheckPointMaxTime(chkPointFile, MaxTime)???%?Load checkpoint file data, extract options?? load(chkPointFile,'BlackboxModelConstantData')?? options = BlackboxModelConstantData.options;?? ???% Override options.MaxTime?? options.MaxTime = MaxTime;?? BlackboxModelConstantData.options = options;?? % Save data?? save(chkPointFile, 'BlackboxModelConstantData', '-append')end

Summary: MATLAB crashes when a connected OPC UA server shuts down or stops
Description: If connected to an OPC UA server, which then shuts down or stops working, MATLAB crashes either immediately or after some time has elapsed.

Summary: MATLAB ncinfo errors out with certain OPeNDAP endpoints in Windows
Description: On Windows systems, certain OPeNDAP endpoints return an NC_EINVAL  error from the NetCDF library. For example:   ncinfo('https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/thredds/dodsC/namanl/201609/20160929/namanl_218_20160929_0000_000.grb') Returns this error:   Error using netcdflib  The NetCDF library encountered an error during execution of 'open' function - 'Invalid argument (NC_EINVAL)'.

Summary: Incorrect power scaling of HE-STF in HE TB packet
Description: The power of the high-efficiency short training field (HE-STF) is incorrectly scaled for a high-efficiency trigger-based (HE TB) waveform. This problem occurs when generating 802.11ax HE TB waveforms by using the heTBWaveformGenerator  function in the "802.11ax Packet Error Rate Simulation for Uplink Trigger-Based Format" example.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation and Accelerate/Rapid simulation for Parameter Writer block assessing a Model block with trigger/bus element port
Description: The problem occurs for multiple-level nested models with a Parameter Writer block in a lower level of a model assessing a Model block with a trigger/enable port or bus element port. The code-generation is not correct, and Accelerator/ Rapid mode simulation produce wrong answers.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Event persists after an exception interrupts the chart execution
Description: In standalone Stateflow charts executed in MATLAB, input events can persist after an exception interrupts the execution of the event function. For example: A chart is triggered by input event E. While processing E, an exception is thrown that interrupts the execution of the event function. The next time the chart is invoked (by step ?or by another event), E will still be active.?  This behavior can produce unexpected results. For example, on(E) ?state actions?and transitions guarded by E will be valid during the next execution step.

Summary: Simulink might crash or have unexpected state changes when updating multiple windows simultaneously
Description: When two or more Simulink windows are open with pending updates, and you close one of those windows, Simulink might crash or have unexpected state changes.

Summary: Merging data dictionary files within a GitHub project sets property HasAccessToBaseWorkspace to true
Description: The property HasAccessToBaseWorkspace? for a Simulink data dictionary ?is automatically set to true ?when the following conditions are true: You are working in a Windows environment. You use GitHub to manage your Simulink data dictionaries (.sldd  files). You merge .sldd  files from different GitHub branches within MATLAB.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 3, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: matlabFunction might return value of a subexpression of the input
Description: A MATLAB function file generated from?matlabFunction? might return the value of a subexpression of the input, instead of the input as a whole, if optimization is turned on (which it is by default).
Workaround: Disable the code optimization by setting the value of Optimize to false:f =  matlabFunction(r,'File','myfile','Optimize',false);

Summary: Remote batch analysis fails when the number of workers is greater than the number of licenses
Description: When running remote analyses, if the number of workers on the server side is more than the available number of licenses, some jobs fail. The initial jobs are queued but the later jobs are cancelled instead of being queued until licenses are available. The job manager log contains a message like this: Error: Remote execution has failed Error: State: cancelled (Cannot rerun task because there are no rerun attempts left (The task has no rerun attempts left.). Original cancel message: Unable to delete file: C:\WINDOWS\TEMP\MJS\Checkpoint\GNB-machine.com_worker06_mlworker_log\matlab\work\tp7dbda96b_15bc_456e_be2a_b88c597356d6\Polyspace_R2019a_goodpractice_05_07_2019-15h07.log  This is causing:  Failed to delete C:\WINDOWS\TEMP\MJS\Checkpoint\GNB-machine.com_GNB-machine.com_worker06_mlworker_log\matlab\work before task execution.) Error: Job State: finished

Summary: Duplicate namespaces incorrectly flagged for AUTOSAR adaptive service interfaces
Description: In an AUTOSAR adaptive software component, multiple service interfaces can specify the same C++ namespace. However, AUTOSAR validation incorrectly prevents services interfaces from using the same namespace by displaying an error message similar to the following: Service Interfaces Interface1 and Interface2 both define Namespace Interface. Namespaces must be unique to avoid compilation errors.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: System objects requesting mixed-majority generated code might produce incorrect results
Description: If your MATLAB code uses a column-major System object before a row-major System object, then in the generated code, the row-major object might incorrectly use the column-major shared utility functions.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulation results may vary between Rapid Accelerator mode and Normal mode for?a model containing a For Iterator Subsystem
Description: Simulation in the Rapid Accelerator mode may not match the Normal mode?for a model that meets the following conditions: There is an Assignment blockconnected to a Unit Delay block in a For Iteratorsubsystem. The?Initial Condition ?of the Unit Delay block initialization may or may not be zero.
Workaround: For releases prior to R2018b, unzip the attachment and save the fix_2002452.p file to a folder on your MATLAB path. Run this script in each MATLAB session before opening your model.

Summary: Code generation fails for a model with For Each Subsystem and a MATLAB function block
Description: Code generation may fail for a model that has a MATLAB function block within a For Each subsystem. It may generate an error message:? Failed assertion: numIndices == 1 || numIndices == aMatrixType->numDimensions()??

Summary: Code generation error for a model involving For Each Subsystem block, Simulink.Parameter object, and nested bus
Description: Simulink might fail to generate code for a model involving a For Each Subsystem block if all of the following conditions are satisfied: The For Each Subsystem block has at least one partitioned mask parameter.? The value of the partitioned mask parameter is set to a field access expression of a Simulink.Parameter object whose type is multi-level nested bus with at least three levels in the bus hierarchy.? The partitioned mask parameter is not used by any of? the blocks inside the For Each Subsystem.  In this case, an error message similar to the following might be issued: %exit directive: Simulink Coder Fatal: invalid SLTypeIdx -1 for struct?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for isa might give a wrong answer when its first argument is a parameter specified using?coder.Constant
Description: The code generated for isa ?might give a wrong answer when all of the following conditions are true:  The first argument of isa  is a parameter of an entry-point function. While specifying input types, the value of this parameter is defined to be a constant during code generation by using?coder.Constant.  The constant value of this parameter is an instance of one of these classes:  quaternion  categorical  ClassificationKNN  ClassificationLinear  classreg.learning.classif.CompactClassificationDiscriminant  classreg.learning.classif.CompactClassificationECOC  classreg.learning.classif.CompactClassificationNaiveBayes  classreg.learning.classif.CompactClassificationSVM  classreg.learning.regr.CompactRegressionEnsemble  classreg.learning.regr.CompactRegressionGP  classreg.learning.regr.CompactRegressionSVM  RegressionLinear  ExhaustiveSearcher  KDTreeSearcher    For example, define the MATLAB function foo :  function result = foo(arg)result = isa(arg, 'quaternion'); Generate a MEX function for foo ?by running these commands:  value = quaternion;codegen foo -args coder.Constant(value) Run the generated MEX function: foo_mex(value)? foo_mex ?might give a wrong answer ( logical?0  instead of logical?1 ).

Summary: Linearization error?when model uses Simscape run-time parameters and linearization configured to not recompile model for all parameter grid points
Description: When you linearize a model that uses Simscape run-time parameters and the linearization is configured to not recompile the model for all parameter grid points, the software generates an error similar to the following and leaves the model stuck in a compiled state: R2016a and R2016b:?Reference to non-existent field 'SimscapeMaskParameter1'  R2017a through R2019a:?Reference to non-existent field 'SSCPrm__ParamName' , where ParamName  is the Simscape run-time parameter name  This error occurs when linearizing a model using any of the following: linearize   function slLinearizer   interface slTuner   interface Linear Analysis Tool
Workaround: To terminate the compiled state?of the Simulink model, at the MATLAB command line, type:eval([ModelName '([],[],[], ''term'')']);To linearize a model that uses Simscape run-time parameters, configure the software to recompile the model for each parameter grid point.When using the?linearize?function or an?slLinearizer interface, set the AreParamsTunable?option to false using linearizeOptions.opt = linearizeOptions;opt.AreParamsTunable = false;When using an slTuner interface, set the?AreParamsTunable?option to?false?using slTunerOptions.opt = slTunerOptions;opt.AreParamsTunable = false;When using the Linear Analysis Tool, in the Options for exact linearization dialog box, select Recompile the model when parameter values are varied for linearization.

Summary: Incorrect solution for a thermal model with a radiation boundary condition
Description: For a thermal model with constant emissivity, radiation boundary conditions?might be?set incorrectly when specified as a number. This leads to an incorrect thermal solution.
Workaround: To ensure that radiation boundary conditions are?set correctly,?specify them?as a?function handle. For example, specify emissivity as an?anonymous function handle @(region,state)0.9 instead of?specifying?it as a value 0.9.

Summary: GPZ parameter in CTLE block does not accept complex data
Description: When you try to enter a gain pole zero matrix with complex values in the Gain pole zero matrix ?parameter of the CTLE block, the block errors out. This is because the parameter only accepts real valued matrix. You must replace CTLE blocks in your models with new CTLE blocks from the SerDes Toolbox/Datapath Blocks library, because those library links are broken.

Summary: MATLAB might crash on exit for models with masks
Description: The stack trace generated by MATLAB includes these lines:  [ 5] 0x00000000fb0a3a71 `anonymous namespace'::mwJavaAbort at b:\matlab\src\jmi\jmi\javainit.cpp:1386  [ 6] 0x00000000697388ad [unknown function]  [ 17] 0x000000001760d15a inDestroyWS at b:\matlab\src\m_interpreter\mi_api\exportedapi.cpp:841  [ 18] 0x00000003623cb418 `SLMaskStaticInfo::Instance'::`2'::`dynamic atexit destructor for 'aStaticMaskInfo''

Summary: Concatenate blocks in "Vector" mode may result in cryptic code generation error
Description: When using any Concatenate block (Vector Concatenate or Matrix Concatenate blocks), configured in "Vector" mode, the following error message may be thrown during HDL code generation. Evaluation of elaborate function on class hdldefaults.Concatenate failed with the error message: MATLAB:assertion:failed: Assertion failed.  This happens when the "Concatenate dimension" field has been populated with a number greater than 1. This field is only visible when the block is configured in "Multidimensional array" mode. If the block is then switched to "Vector" mode, this error is encountered.?
Workaround: Run the following command to change the "Concatenate dimension" field to 1 for the offending blocks.>> set_param(<block_path>, 'ConcatenateDimension', '1')

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When UDP Configure block Local IP Address parameter value provided as string, incorrect address used in generated code
Description: When the UDP Configure block Local IP Address  parameter value is provided as string, the UDP Send block and UDP Receive block use an incorrect IP address in generated code. The blocks use an incorrect IP address when the address is directly entered as a string (for example, '10.10.10.15' ) or when the address is provided as a variable holding a string (for example, localIP = '10.10.10.15' ).

Summary: MATLAB crashes when copying/pasting/deleting or inlining a component that contains subcomponents linked to a System Composer architecture model
Description: If you perform any of the following actions on a component that contains subcomponents linked to a System Composer architecture model, then MATLAB might crash: Cut/copy-paste Drag-and-drop Undo after delete Inline Component?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model with a For Each subsystem block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a For Each  subsystem. The signal driving the partitioned?input port of a For Each Subsystem?also drives an Outport  block. The Outport  block is inside a reusable subsystem, or it is ?a root-level Outport block? of a referenced model? block.  ?

Summary: Errors when building a model containing subsystems that have attached library function interfaces
Description: When you build a model that reuses library code, you may observe this error: The function name '$N$R' for code generation in subsystem 'aSubsystem' is not a valid function name  This is an incorrect error as models containing library subsystems that have attached function interfaces permit the "$N$R " name. This error might occur when a model is loaded into memory and then the subsystem is instantiated in the model from a library.?

Summary: Configuration parameters Array layout?and Use algorithms optimized for row-major array layout?are ignored
Description: Simulink Design Verifier considers?Array layout  to be 'Column-major'  and?Use algorithms optimized for row-major array layout ?to be?'Off' , irrespective of their actual values. This may result in incorrect analysis results. ?

Summary: Error when using thingSpeakRead?or?thingSpeakWrite in a deployed application
Description: The MATLAB functions?thingSpeakRead  and?thingSpeakWrite  fail to execute in a deployed? application, generating the following error message: FATAL ERROR: Undefined function 'thingSpeakRead' for input arguments of type 'double'.? ?or?? FATAL ERROR: Undefined function 'thingSpeakWrite' for input arguments of type 'double'.??

Summary: CUDA error during under-determined GPU solve (MLDIVIDE with wide matrix, or MRDIVIDE?or?QR with tall matrix)
Description: For matrix sizes larger than 3071 columns, mldivide  (or backslash '\') with gpuArray   inputs can generate an error. The error usually refers to CUDA_ERROR_ILLEGAL_ADDRESS. This error can also occur for an equivalent call to?mrdivide , and when calling?qr ?with large tall rectangular matrices. After receiving this error, it is no longer possible to use any GPU device without restarting MATLAB. This issue is limited to certain GPU devices and NVIDIA driver versions. Example output is: c = gpuArray.rand(2080, 4096) \?gpuArray.rand(2080, 1);? Warning: An unexpected error occurred during CUDA execution. The CUDA error was:CUDA_ERROR_ILLEGAL_ADDRESS?Warning: An unexpected error occurred during CUDA execution. The CUDA error was:CUDA_ERROR_ILLEGAL_ADDRESS?Warning: An unexpected error occurred during CUDA execution. The CUDA error was:CUDA_ERROR_ILLEGAL_ADDRESS?Warning: An unexpected error occurred during CUDA execution. The CUDA error was:CUDA_ERROR_ILLEGAL_ADDRESS?Warning: An unexpected error occurred during CUDA execution. The CUDA error was:CUDA_ERROR_ILLEGAL_ADDRESS?Error using??\ ?Call to Real Double QR (construct Q factor) on the GPU failed with error status: an illegal memory?access was encountered.
Workaround: For?mldivide?this problem occurs for under-determined system matrices of size M-by-N where M < N and M?and N do not lie in the same range 1:3071, 3072:10239, or 10240:inf. Equivalently, for?mrdivide?and?qr?it may occur for N-by-M matrices with the same conditions.The following function can be used to replace mldivide by padding with zeros:function X = mldivideWithFix(A, B)ranges = [0 3072 10240];m = size(A,1);n = size(A,2);if m >= n    X = A\B;else    rangem = find(m >= ranges, 1, 'last');    rangen = find(n >= ranges, 1, 'last');    if rangem == rangen        X = A\B;    else        rangemin = ranges(rangen);        % Pad A and b with rows of zeros where necessary        padding = rangemin - m;        A = [A; zeros(padding, n, 'like', A)];        B = [B; zeros(padding, size(B,2), 'like', B)];                % Solve using LQ factorization and restrict to the        % full rank square system        [Q,R] = qr(A');        X = Q(:,1:m)*(R(1:m,1:m)'\B(1:m,:));    endendend?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Modifying tunable parameters of the Counter block in a Dataflow subsystem during simulation may cause unexpected behavior.
Description: Modifying the Maximum Count tunable parameter of the Counter block in a Dataflow subsystem during simulation may cause incorrect results.
Workaround: Move the Counter block outside of the Dataflow subsystem to modify the Maximum Count parameter during simulation.

Summary: add_block?and replace_block functions cause port placement and connectivity issues forEntity Store, Resource Acquirer, and Resource Releaser blocks
Description: Entity Store, Resource Acquirer and Resource Releaser blocks have the following issues when added to a model.? Using the?add_block  function causes?the blockoutput port?to appear on top of the block instead of on the right side ofthe block. Using?the replace_block  function causes?the block portsto be?unconnected.
Workaround: Open the block dialog. In?the Statistics tab, enable any statistic.Click Apply.

Summary: Model Metrics Dashboard fails to display detailed data for compliance metrics
Description: When you click on a gauge widget for a Model Advisor Check Compliance metric in the Model Metrics Dashboard, the table may not display the detailed data for large models.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for multiple calls to functions writing to global data inside of an if statement within at least two level loop nests
Description: Incorrect generated code might occur for MATLAB code that satisfies all of the following the conditions:? The MATLAB function contains at-least one for  loop.? The for -loop contains a call to a function which writes to some global data.? The for -loop contains at least one if  statement. The if  statement contains a while loop.? The if  statement contains another call to a function which writes to the same global data.  In the generated code, the order of the calls inside the for loop is incorrectly changed which might cause incorrect results.  For example, in the below MATLAB function: function [IA1, IA2, IA3, IA4] = funToCall(IA1, IA2, IA3, IA4)?????for P3 = 8:9?????????for P4 = 5:7?? ? ? ? ? ? funOnPath2( IA3( 1:1:1, ?2:1:2 ) );?????????????if cast( IA2( 6 ).f1, ?'logical' )?????????????????P1 = 3;?????????????????while P1<=5?? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? funOnPath4();?????????????????????P1 = P1 + 1;?????????????????end?????????????end?????????end?????end? end  function funOnPath4    coder.inline( 'never' );    global P12    P12( P23, 1 ) = [ cast( P22( P21, P23 ), 'uint64' ) ]; end ? function funOnPath2(IA3_1_1_1_2_1_2_)    coder.inline('never');    global P12    P12( 1, 1 ) = IA3_1_1_1_2_1_2_( 1 ).f2( 1:1:1, 1:1:1 );end funOnPath2  and funOnPath4  write to the global variable P12.  In the incorrect generated code, the writes to the global data in  funOnPath4  executes before the writes to the global data in  funOnPath2 . Following ?is the incorrectly generated C code. void funToCall(const unsigned short IA1[40], const struct0_T IA2[6],  const struct1_T IA3[2], const struct2_T IA4[5]){  int P3;  (void)IA1;  (void)IA4;  if (!isInitialized_funToCall) {    funToCall_initialize();  }  for (P3 = 0; P3 < 2; P3++) {    if (IA2[5].f1 != 0UL) {      funOnPath4();      funOnPath4();      funOnPath4();    }    funOnPath2(IA3[1].f1, IA3[1].f2);    funOnPath2(IA3[1].f1, IA3[1].f2);    funOnPath2(IA3[1].f1, IA3[1].f2);  }}

Summary: Call to mpiexec results in error message "libudev.so.1 not found" on Red Hat? Enterprise Linux? version 6.x or CentOS version 6.x
Description: When you use MATLAB Parallel Server? with workers on Red Hat Enterprise Linux version 6.x or CentOS version 6.x, cluster validation can fail with a message that refers to "libudev.so.1". This only occurs for schedulers other than MATLAB? Job Scheduler.  When the failure occurs, you can see a text similar to the following in cluster validation results:? /opt/matlab/r2019a/bin/glnxa64/mpiexec:error while loading shared libraries: libudev.so.1: Cannnot open shared object file:No such file or directory

Summary: Comparison between signed and unsigned integer inside MATLAB Function results in non-synthesizable HDL code
Description: In MATLAB Function block, comparing two variables with signed and unsigned integers can lead to unexpected cast to double types. The generated HDL code for such a design will contains real types, which makes the logic unsupported for synthesis.
Workaround: Convert both inputs explicit to signed or unsigned types.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible when configuring function prototype control for a protected referenced model
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following criteria:  The model references another model that specifies that the input and output arguments of a step function use the same variable name.  The referenced model is protected.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Error or memory corruption might occur when returning a fixed-size cell array to a function that specifies the array as variable-size
Description: An out-of-bounds error or memory corruption might occur for generated MEX and standalone code under these conditions: Your MATLAB code completely assigns a fixed-size cell aray in a loop. The cell array is returned as output to another function that specifies the array as variable-size.  Under these conditions, the generated code might not define the size of the cell array before the loop assignment.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink Coder might generate incorrect code for parameter partitions in For Each subsystem
Description: Simulink Coder might generate incorrect code under the following scenario:The model contains a masked For Each subsystem block. The For Each block configures a parameter partition for a multi-dimensional mask parameter. The parameter partition is used in a Lookup, Prelookup or any other block that would use the starting address of the parameter partition.  Simulink Coder will use the base address of the mask parameter instead of the parameter partition as the starting address.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a

Summary: surrogateopt errors when resuming from a checkpoint file.
Description: When you run  surrogateopt  in parallel and create a checkpoint file,  surrogateopt   can throw an error when you run again (resume)?from the resulting checkpoint file. The error begins:  Error using globaloptim.bmo.restoreSolver (line 95)    Matrix index is out of range for deletion...[more error lines appear ]  The error is due to the checkpoint file being unreadable.
Workaround: The checkpoint file can be repaired so surrogateopt can read it without error.?Run the following function on the checkpoint file. The function loads the data, modifies the data, and saves the data back to the file.function fixCheckPointFile(chkPointFile)load(chkPointFile,'SurrogateSolverData')TrialsArray = SurrogateSolverData.TrialMgr.TrialsArray;id = [TrialsArray.pending];if any(id)??? for ii = 1:length(id)??????? if id(ii)??????????? TrialsArray(ii).waiting = true;??????????? TrialsArray(ii).pending = false;??????? end??? end??? % TrialsArray is changed, save to file??? SurrogateSolverData.TrialMgr.TrialsArray = TrialsArray; ??? save(chkPointFile,'SurrogateSolverData','-append')end?

Summary: mlreportgen.report.Figure?reporter might produce images that are cropped or not centered in PDF documents
Description: The mlreportgen.report.Figure  reporter produces images that are cropped or not centered when both of these conditions are true: The snapshot format is SVG. The screen resolution is not 96 pixels per inch.
Workaround: Try printing the figure to an image file and then inserting the image file into the report by using an mlreportgen.dom.Image object.% Print the figure, where fig is the handle to your figure print(fig,'-dsvg','my_snapshot.svg'); % Create image reporter and set scale-to-fit to true imgr = mlreportgen.dom.Image('my_snapshot.svg'); imgr.Style = {mlreportgen.dom.ScaleToFit(true)};

Summary: Generated C++ code might contain an extra unnecessary set of parentheses for a static_cast
Description: The generated C++ code for a static_cast  might contain an extra unnecessary set of parentheses as follows: ?static_cast<uint16_T>((rtDWork.temporalCounter + 1U));  With this fix, the generated code is as follows:?  static_cast<uint16_T>(rtDWork.temporalCounter + 1U);

Summary: Nominal friction scaling factor, mu?is not visible in the Vehicle Body 3DOF block mask
Description: In the Vehicle Body 3DOF block mask, the?Nominal friction scaling factor, mu ?parameter is not visible when you do both of these: Set the Axle forces  parameter to External longitudinal forces  or External longitudinal velocity.  On the Input signals  pane, clear External friction.
Workaround: You can do either of the following:?Use set_param?command to set the?Mu?block parameter, orOn the?Input signals?pane, select?External friction to create the input port Mu. Connect the input port Mu?to a Constant block with the friction value.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: AUTOSAR Variants specifying direct expressions may generate incorrect short names
Description: Conflicting short-names c0  and c1  for VARIATION-POINT-PROXY  generated  by Simulink in the RTE compatible C code for the following scenarios:  Model reference hierarchy:  The variant blocks?within the referenced model use direct expressions. For example V==1   The variant?blocks in the top model use?different direct expressions compared to the referenced model. For example  W==1   Rte_cfg.h  generated for the top model does not contain the short names specific to the referenced model    Individual model components:  Model1 and Model 2 having different variant conditions may generate same short name cXX  as VARIATION-POINT-PROXY during AUTOSAR code generation.
Workaround: Usage Simulink.Variant instead of direct expressions for AUTOSAR variants can workaround the behavior.

Summary: Error when using models with powergui discrete solver in rapid accelerator or code generation mode
Description: Simulating a Specialized Power Systems model in rapid accelerator or code generation mode stops and generate an error if, the powergui block has this configuration: In the Solver  settings, Simulation type  is set to Discrete.  In the Preferences ?settings,?the?Automatically handle Discrete solver and Advanced tab solver settings of blocks  check box is selected.

Summary: Edit-Time checking in a Japanese localization environment incorrectly identifies block name violations for Inport and Outport blocks
Description: When you select Analysis > Model Advisor > Display Advisor Checks in Editor   in a Japanese localization environment, the Model Advisor incorrectly identifies block name violations in Inport and Outport blocks.

Summary: Downsample block errors when used in a Model Block
Description: When the Downsample block is used inside a Model block, simulating the model throws an error, ˇ°This block is not supported inside an enabled subsystem in its current parameter settingsˇ±.  The error occurs regardless of the Downsample block parameter settings. The error occurs when a Downsample block is used inside a Model block, whether or not it is contained in an enabled subsystem.

Summary: Wrong line numbers in traceability comments in the generated code for charts that use MATLAB as the action language
Description: For charts that use MATLAB as the action language, the line numbers in traceability comments might not be computed correctly. ?Consider this snippet of generated code:     // Entry 'State1': '<S1>:1'     // '<S1>:1:2' a = 1;     sftest1_Y.Out1 = 1.0;? The comment? // '<S1>:1:2' a = 1;  indicates that C code statement?sftest1_Y.Out1 = 1.0; ?corresponds to the statement?a = 1;?on line 2 of the entry action in state State1 . However, the statement a = 1; might be on a different line in the entry action of the state.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Referenced models that use multitasking and an asynchronous?sample time might produce incorrect linearization results
Description: If you have a referenced model that uses multitasking and an asynchronous sample time, Simulink might produce incorrect results for linearization.

Summary: S-function builder incorrectly adds line breaks in Library pane
Description: In the Library/Object/Source files  box of the Library  pane of S-function builder, S-function builder automatically inserts a new line at any whitespaces. If a space is added between one of the INCLUDE_PATH, LIBRARY_PATH, or SRC_PATH keywords and an actual path, upon reopening the dialog, the line might be broken into two lines, causing a compile failure because the path cannot be parsed.
Workaround: Remove the whitespace. For example, change ? ? ?INCLUDE_PATH ./include to ? ? ?INCLUDE_PATH./include?

Summary: Unable to set the value of a custom attribute for an imported requirements reference
Description: If you import an external document as read-only references, you can later unlock the imported item and change its attributes (for example, summary, description). Attempting to modify the value of a custom attribute in an imported reference can result in the following error: "No appropriate method, property, or field 'setAttribute' for class 'slreq.Reference'".

Summary: Outlet temperatures of Condenser Evaporator (2P-MA) may cross under certain conditions
Description: Under typical steady-state flow conditions, the temperatures of the hot fluid and cold fluid approach each other but do not cross. However, the outlet temperature of the hot fluid may become less than the outlet temperature of the cold fluid under either of the following conditions: The flow rates of both fluids are zero for an extended period of time. Heat transfer is from the moist air side to the two-phase fluid side (an evaporator operating condition), water vapor is condensing on the moist air side, and the two-phase fluid side has relative large vapor zone and small mixture zone.

Summary: Security Issue: C++ shared libraries might allow third-party access to global variables
Description: C++ shared libraries using the MATLAB Data API interface that are produced with MATLAB Compiler SDK might allow C++ code written by a third party to read or write global variables used in the source MATLAB code.
Workaround: As an alternative to using global variables, you can use a variable in the entry functions and pass it to subsequent functions as needed.

Summary: Security Issue: Python packages might allow third-party access to global variables
Description: Python packages produced with MATLAB Compiler SDK might allow Python code written by a third party to read or write global variables used in the source MATLAB code.
Workaround: As an alternative to using global variables, you can use a variable in the entry functions and pass it to subsequent functions as needed.

Summary: IgnoreDataTypes property of Simulink.sdi.constraints.MatchesSignalOptions is misspelled
Description: Attempting to assign the IgnoreDataTypes  property of a Simulink.sdi.constraints.MatchesSignalOptions  instance generates an error, because the property is misspelled in the class definition.

Summary: Relational boundary coverage is unintentionally reported for Abs and Relational operator blocks in case of complex input
Description: Relational boundary coverage metric is unintentionally reported for Abs and Relational operator blocks when input(s) to these blocks is of complex type.

Summary: Code generation fails when BuildConfiguration is set to non-standard value for build process with custom toolchain
Description: Code generation fails when these conditions apply:  The  Toolchain  parameter of the model is set to the name of a custom toolchain.  The custom toolchain definition uses addBuildConfiguration  to define a build configuration with non-standard name, that is, the name is not Faster Builds , Faster Runs , Debug , or Specify .  The BuildConfiguration  parameter of the model is set to the name of the non-standard build configuration.   You see this error:   ?Build configuration must be one of allowed values

Summary: Duplicate SerDes blocks with similar names but differentlengths result in corrupt parameter parsing.
Description: When you use multipleinstances of the same SerDes block in a SerDes system, blocks with identicalinitial strings but of different lengths (for example CTLE and CTLE1) are notuniquely identified when parsing their respective control parameters. Asa result, the system ignores the control parameters for the block with theshorter name (CTLE in this case) and uses the default values for all parametersin that block.
Workaround: Name each block withunique names that have the same string lengths (for example CTLE1, CTLE2 etc.) whengenerating the SerDes system in the SerDes Designer App.

Summary: Incorrectly formatted requirement descriptions when importing rich text content from IBM DOORS Next Generation using ReqIF
Description: When importing a ReqIF file exported from IBM DOORS Next Generation into Simulink Requirements, some requirement descriptions may be formatted incorrectly, if the description includes rich text content such as: HTML tables, which may lose some row and column formatting, or Embedded images, which may not be displayed.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect?results are possible for a model containing?Switch and Data Store Memory blocks
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets these conditions:? The model contains Switch, Data Store Memory, Data Store Read, and Data Store Write blocks. The Switch block's control port connects to a Constant block.  The relative execution order of the Data Store Read and Data Store Write blocks might be different in simulation than in the generated code.
Workaround: Choose either of these workarounds:Specify the priority of Data Store Read and Data Store Write blocks to enforce their relative execution order.Disable the elimination of the Switch block by deselecting the?Block reduction?model configuration parameter.?

Summary: Exceptions when comparing Simulink models with changes in both MATLAB function block code and annotation text
Description: If there are changes in bothMATLAB function block code and annotation text in the Simulinkmodel comparison, you see a Java exception in the MATLAB command windowafter you select both of these sets of changes in the comparison report.You cannot see the details of both the annotation changes and theMATLAB function block changes. To see the 'other' set of detailed changes, you must close and rerun the comparison.

Summary: Simulation Data Inspector renders large signals incorrectly when zoomed in
Description: When you plot a large signal on a time plot in the Simulation Data Inspector that is zoomed in, the plot renders the signal incorrectly and shows incorrect data values.?

Summary: Polyspace report not generated on Windows machine with polyspace-report-generator command
Description: When calling polyspace-report-generator  from the command line on a Windows machine, a report is not generated.  However, no error message is displayed.
Workaround: To generate a report, add the .exe extension to the command name. For example, call:? polyspace-report-generator.exe
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder Server, Polyspace Bug Finder

Summary: Syntax errors in transition statements may result in unexpected results
Description: A transition condition that has incorrectly ordered square brackets and parenthesis, followed by an action, is not caught as a syntax error. The transition is treated as unconditional. For example, a transition labeled by:  [ (x==0) && (x==1] ) {x=2;}   is treated as if the label is {x=2;}

Summary: Incorrect error when Parameter Writer block accesses Model block associated with variant conditions
Description: If a Parameter Writer block in your top model accesses a parameter in a Model block, and that Model uses variants, you get the following error when you update your model: The Parameter Writer block ''{BlockPath}'' cannot write to the Model block ''{BlockPath}'' because one of the model arguments has a variant condition associated with it.  This occurs even if the model arguments are not associated with any variant conditions.?

Summary: Code generator?might not?honor data alignment when AdvancedOptControl parameter is set to -SLCI
Description: The code generator does not honor the data alignment requirement, producing incorrect code, when?all of the following conditions?are true: The model configuration parameter AdvancedOptControl ?is set to -SLCI. The code generator uses a code replacement library to replace an operation or function with a custom function. The corresponding?code replacement?entry specifies an alignment requirement with the AlignmentBoundary  property. Code replacement occurs in the generated code.  If you are using a compiler on a target that uses instructions which produce a crash when data alignment requirement is not satisfied, then the compiled generated code from Simulink model (or Matlab function) might also produce a crash.

Summary: FormalImage reporter incorrectly scales an image
Description: The FormalImage reporter might incorrectly scale an image to fit a report page when the following conditions are true: The report program adds a FormalImage object to a Chapter object.   The layout specified by the Chapter object is different from the layout specified by the Report object.  For example: import mlreportgen.report.*import mlreportgen.dom.*rpt = Report('bug1','pdf');img = FormalImage('Image',which('ngc6543a.jpg'));img.ScaleToFit = true;img.Caption = 'Cat''s Eye Nebula';ch = Chapter('Title','Image Bug');ch.Layout.Landscape = true;add(ch,PageBreak);add(ch,img);add(rpt,ch);close(rpt);rptview(rpt);
Workaround: A report program can scale the image instead of relying on the FormalImage reporter to do the scaling:import mlreportgen.report.*import mlreportgen.dom.*rpt = Report('bug1', 'pdf');img = FormalImage('Image',which('ngc6543a.jpg'));img.Caption = 'NGC6543a';img.ScaleToFit = false;scale(img, 9, 6);ch = Chapter('Title','Image Bug');ch.Layout.Landscape = true;add(ch, PageBreak);add(ch, img);add(rpt, ch);close(rpt);rptview(rpt);function scale(formalImg,toWidth,toHeight)    import mlreportgen.dom.*    import mlreportgen.utils.*        domImg = Image(formalImg.Image);        domImgHeight = units.toInches(domImg.Height);    domImgWidth = units.toInches(domImg.Width);        imgAspectRatio = domImgHeight/domImgWidth;    if domImgHeight > toHeight        domImgHeight = toHeight;        domImgWidth = domImgHeight / imgAspectRatio;    end    if domImgWidth > toWidth        domImgWidth = toWidth;        domImgHeight = domImgHeight * imgAspectRatio;    end    formalImg.Width = sprintf('%0.2fin',domImgWidth);    formalImg.Height = sprintf('%0.2fin',domImgHeight);    end

Summary: Code?generator does not honor data alignment?requirement for Simulink.Parameter that is passed by pointer
Description: The code generator does not honor a data alignment requirement when all of the following conditions?are true: The model contains at least one block that directly uses block parameter for computation, such as a Gain block. The block parameter is configured to pass-by-pointer in generated code. One way to achieve this configuration is by associating it with a Simulink.Parameter and setting its storage class to ImportedExternPointer . The code generator uses a code replacement library (CRL) to replace an operation or function with a custom function. An operation or function on the parameter is replaced with a custom function. The corresponding?code replacement?entry specifies an alignment requirement with the AlignmentBoundary  property.  If you are using a compiler on a target that uses instructions which produce a crash when data alignment requirement is not satisfied, then the compiled generated code from Simulink model (or Matlab function)?can also?produce a crash.

Summary: Running the Simscape to HDL workflow on a model with an Inport on the root level may error out
Description: When running the final step, "Generate implementation model", in Simscape to HDL advisor, you may encounter the following error if the model has an Inport block at the top level. ?Failed  ?block_diagram does not have a parameter named 'Position'?
Workaround: Avoid inports and outports from the top level of the model.

Summary: Incorrect simulation result in normal mode when output port of a Reset or Terminate Function block is initialized by Initialize Function block
Description: Your model is subject to this bug when the following conditions are met: Model contains a Reset or Terminate Function block with at least one output port.? The Reset or Terminate Function block output port is driving a root Outport block. The Reset or Terminate Function Outport block for the output port is written to by a State Writer block in an Initialize Function block within the same model. The value written by the State Writer block is different from the value specified in the Reset or Terminate Function Outport block parameter Initial output .  The correct simulation result is when the State Writer block overrides the initial output value of the Reset or Terminate Function Outport block. However when the above conditions are met, in normal simulation mode, the initial output value of the Outport block actually prevails. There is no problem with the generated code.

Summary: MATLAB crash or assertion error when generating code for data with more than 10 dimensions
Description: MATLAB might crash or display an assertion error when generating code for data with more than 10 dimension. The assertion diagnostic contains the following text: Assertion at ...\matlab\src\cgir_support\dom_core\analysis\range\RangeValueFactory.cpp line 459:In getMatrixSizeRange: Assertion failed: numElements > 0 && numElements <= MAX_MATRIX_ELEMENTS??   In release R2018b of MATLAB the message might instead be:Assertion at dom_core/analysis/range/RangeValueFactory.cpp line 551:aNumDims >= 0

Summary: Hilbert spectrum output of the Hilbert-Huang transform (HHT) is incorrect
Description: The Hilbert spectrum output, P ,  of hht ?is incorrect. The plot of the Hilbert spectrum obtained when hht ?is called with no output arguments is correct, but returning the Hilbert spectrum results in the incorrect output. ?

Summary: MATLAB might crash when using variable size gpuArray inputs with the DynamicMemoryAllocation property enabled
Description: MATLAB might crash when using variable bound gpuArray? as input and the code configuration object containing one of the following properties: ?DynamicMemoryAllocation  is set to? 'AllVariableSizeArrays ' ?DynamicMemoryAllocation  is set to 'Threshold ' and DynamicMemoryAllocationThreshold  is set to a value less than the size of the variable  The stack trace contains the following: [  9] 0x00007fe8d61d4812 /home/user1/Desktop/matlab/bin/glnxa64/builtins/coder_core/../../../../   bin/glnxa64/libmwcgir_gpu.so+00534546   _ZNK2CG9transform22GpuMemSyncMinimization4doitEPNS_3FcnE+00003906[ 10] 0x00007fe8d61d5758 /home/user1/Desktop/matlab/bin/glnxa64/builtins/coder_core/../../../../   bin/glnxa64/libmwcgir_gpu.so+00538456   _ZNK2CG9transform22GpuMemSyncMinimization12processScopeEPNS_5ScopeE+00000216[ 11] 0x00007fe8d74e0287 /home/user1/Desktop/matlab/bin/glnxa64/builtins/coder_core/../../../../   bin/glnxa64/libmwcgir_support.so+03736199   _ZNK2CG14ScopeTransform5applyEPNS_5ScopeERKNS_7ServiceE+00000167

Summary: MATLAB crash might occur during code generation from MATLAB code with nested for-loops where the inner loop might be conditionally executed
Description: In presence of nested for-loops where the inner loop has a variable sized bound, and is conditionally executed, MATLAB might crash during code generation. For example, consider the following MATLAB code that contains a copy to matrix B ?from matrix A ?within the inner?for ?loop as follows: function [B] = example_1991581(A, in)? ?B = A + 1;? ?global g? ?if in > 5? ? ? n = 5;? ?else? ? ? n = 8;? ?end? ?for i = 1:15? ? ? ?if in? == 3? ? ? ? ? ?B( 1:1:n, 1:1:1 ) = A( 1:1:n, 1:1:1 );? ? ? ?else? ? ? ? ? ?g = g+1;? ? ? ?end? ? endend  This might result in an assertion similar to the following:? CGIR assertion 'In forLoopOfVariableSizedCopy: Assertion failed: compatibleLoop(loop, ?forEntry)' failed in '../ ../ src/ cgir_xform/ dom_core/ lowering/ memFcn/ ReplaceWithMemCall.hpp:330'? To reproduce this bug, run the?run_memcpy_example_1991581.m ?file for the attached example memcpy_example_1991581.m  in??'example_1991581.zip' .
Workaround: Set?Use memcpy for vector assignment?to?Off, or, set the?Memcpy threshold (bytes)?parameter to a very high value such as?int32(intmax).

Summary: Model cannot set code generation folder structure to reuse library code
Description: When you try to reuse library code in a model, the model uses the value specified in Simulink.filegen.CodeGenFolderStructure.TargetEnvironmentSubfolder . In certain cases, despite setting the correct Code generation folder  structure  via the Simulink preferences or via the Simulink.fileGenControl  API, building the model generates a warning or error depending on the value of Behavior when pregenerated library subsystem code is missing ?(PregeneratedLibrarySubsystemCodeDiagnostic ) model configuration parameter. The error is as follows: The subsystem 'SubsystemA'?cannot reuse library code because it is using 'ModelSpecific' code generation folder structure. Choose 'TargetEnvironmentSubfolder' code generation folder structure to enable the subsystem to reuse library code.?

Summary: Code generator?might not?honor data alignment for structure field for MATLAB function or MATLAB Function block
Description: The code generator does not honor the data alignment requirement when the following conditions are met:?  ?The code generator uses a code replacement library     (CRL) to replace an operation or a function with a custom function. The code is generated for MATLAB code or Simulink models with at least one MATLAB Function block. At least one operation or function written in MATLAB function or MATLAB Function block is replaced with a custom function. At least one input to the replacement function is a field of a structure. The corresponding?code replacement?entry specifies an alignment requirement with the AlignmentBoundary  property.? ??   If you are using a compiler on a target that uses instructions which produce a crash when data alignment requirement is not satisfied, then the compiled generated code from Simulink model (or Matlab function) might also?produce a crash.

Summary: Models with Pipe (2P) or 3-Zone Pipe (2P) block might cause simulation to stall or fail
Description: Simulation might stall or fail for models with a Pipe (2P) block from the Simscape Foundation library or a 3-Zone Pipe (2P) block from the Simscape Fluids library. This occurs because?the Gnielinski correlation  equation, which is?used to calculate the heat transfer, may become singular under some conditions, such as low flow rate.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model with a?Bus Assignment block?or an?Assignment block
Description: The results of an Accelerator mode simulation might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a?Bus Assignment block?or?an?Assignment?block.  The input to the assignment input port of the Bus Assignment block is one or more signals from a bus.? The signal that connects to the assignment input ports of the?Bus Assignment block  or the U-input data port of the?Assignment block? drives a root-level output port.
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, deselect the Perform in-place updates for Assignment and Bus Assignment blocks parameter.

Summary: Code generator?might not?honor data alignment when CRL replacements are triggered inside a Simulink Function Block
Description: The code generator might not honor the data alignment requirement when all the following conditions are met:?? The code generator uses a code replacement library     (CRL) to replace an operation or a function with a custom function. The model contains at least one Simulink Function block. At least one operation or function inside the Simulink Function block is replaced with custom function. The corresponding?code replacement?entry specifies an alignment requirement with the AlignmentBoundary  property.  If you are using a compiler on a target that uses instructions which produce a crash when data alignment requirement is not satisfied, then the compiled generated code from Simulink model (or Matlab function) might also produce a crash.

Summary: In serdes.CDR and serdes.DFECDR?System objects, the property ReferenceOffset? changed from units of ˇ°fraction of? reference clockˇ± to ˇ°parts-per-million (PPM)ˇ±
Description: The units of property ReferenceOffset ?in?serdes.CDR ?and?serdes.DFECDR? System objects?changed from ˇ°fractionof reference clockˇ± to ˇ°parts-per-million (PPM)ˇ±, which is more widely used inindustries. If you manually changed the value of ReferenceOffset ?property fromits default zero value, double check the value and the unit to avoid unexpectedsimulation results.?

Summary: Folder deletion error during PDF report generation
Description: PDF report generation randomly fails with a folder deletion error. This error generally occurs when the report contains a diagram snapshot whose elements include hyperlinks to enable diagram-based navigation. The error message is similar to: "Cannot remove directory: ?"  followed by the path of the intermediate folder.?

Summary: Entity Queue block with nest bus input and Priority queue type incorrectly sorts entities
Description: An Entity Queue block incorrectly sorts entities in the queue when all of these conditions occur: Queue type  parameter is  Priority  Data type at the input port of the queue block is nest bus A bus element of the nest bus is specified as Priority source  parameter for queue to sort entities  Under these conditions, the Entity Queue block incorrectly always uses the first bus element of the nest bus to sort entities.

Summary: latex(symbolicExpression) can create code that does not work with 'Interpreter','latex'
Description: For symbolic expressions containing the following functions, the result of calling latex(symbolicExpr)  cannot be used in figures with 'Interpreter','latex' . erfc  airy  expint  ei  vpaintegral   This bug affects all places rendering LaTeX in figure windows, including the text  command, legend entries, titles, and axes tick marks.
Workaround: Use replace(latex(symExpr),'\text{','\mathrm{').

Summary: Simulink Code Inspector fails to verify non-inlined graphical function call inside Stateflow transition condition
Description: Simulink Code Inspector fails to verify non-inlined graphical function calls inside Stateflow transition conditions, for example: [ foo(u1) ]{? ˇ­? }  where:? function out = foo(in) ? ? out = boolean(in);?end?    ? ? ? ?

Summary: Compilation fails when custom code does not comply with TargetLangStandard
Description: Compilation with gcc or MinGW fails when custom code does not comply with ?the version of the C/C++ standard selected by Standard math library? (TargetLangStandard) .  When you specify the Standard math library  ( TargetLangStandard ) for gcc and MinGW builds, the software adds compiler flags to enforce the specified language standard.? For example, if you set?Standard math library  (TargetLangStandard)  to C89/90 (ANSI)  , the software specifies: For C code:  -ansi -pedantic  For C++ code: - std=c++98 -pedantic     If the build includes, for example, custom C++ code that uses C++11 features, then the code might fail to compile. This issue impacts standalone code generation.
Workaround: If your model is configured to use a toolchain for building code:In Configuration Parameters > Code Generation, set? Build configuration to Specify.Under Toolchain details, in the C Compiler and C++ Compiler Options fields, replace C_STANDARD_OPTS and CPP_STANDARD_OPTS with your required compiler flags, omitting -pedantic and any flag that specifies a language standard. You can determine default values for C_STANDARD_OPTS and CPP_STANDARD_OPTS by inspecting a previously generated makefile.
Products: Embedded Coder, MATLAB Coder, Simulink Coder

Summary: U suffix on integer literals might be missing from the generated code causing a??MISRA 10.6?violation
Description: The generated code might contain a MISRA 10.6 violation for a model that contains a signal with an unsigned integer data type that is smaller than int16  and contains a custom storage class.For this modeling pattern, MISRA compliant code should contain a U  suffix in the assignment to the signal variable with the custom storage class. The MISRA violation occurs because the integer literal in the assignment statement is missing the U  suffix.

Summary: Comments and justifications on global structured variables might not be imported
Description: Comments and justification on results in the Global variables ?category might not be imported to subsequent runs. The issue applies only to global variables having fields, for instance, structures and unions.
Products: Polyspace Code Prover Server, Polyspace Code Prover

Summary: Compiling code generated from a Simscape model fails with unknown type name 'bool' error
Description: Compilation of generated code from Simscape model fails with the following error in the diagnostic viewer: <matlabroot>/toolbox/physmod/simscape/engine/core/c/win64/ssc_core_c8d83e88.c:2045:33: error: unknown type name 'bool'

Summary: MATLAB might crash on Windows machines without Microsoft Visual C++ 2010 Redistributable installed
Description: On machines that do not already have the Microsoft Visual C++ 2010 Redistributable installed, MATLAB's supporting licensing and installer applications may fail, causing MATLAB to crash.

Summary: 'Marker' property of stacked plots not saved or copied for plot type 'scatter'
Description: The stackedplot  function can return a StackedLineChart  object that has properties of the stacked plot. If you save or copy a StackedLineChart  object, and the plot type of a plot is 'scatter' , then the 'Marker'  property of the plot reverts to the default circle setting in the loaded or copied object.

Summary: Custom TMF builds throw timeout error when building shared utilities code
Description: For template makefile builds, the build process uses your custom template makefile to create the shared utilities makefile. This will cause the build process to throw an error when all of the following conditions are true:  Generate code only is turned off in the model configuration parameters The?BUILD_SUCCESS  message (e.g. "### Created:") is output during the call to make for rtwshared.mk  the call to make for rtwshared.mk does not generate rtwshared.lib   The error message is as follows:? Timeout waiting for creating of sharedLibrary, rtwshared.lib
Workaround: Update your template makefile so that the make variable PRODUCT is always actually created as a result of the call to make. Alternatively, if your build process does not actually use the generated makefile you can disable makefile generation by setting the Model Configuration parameter?GenerateMakefile to false.

Summary: MATLAB crashes after interpreted mode simulation with a MATLAB System block that accesses a Data Store Memory block
Description: A System object in a MATLAB System block accesses Data Store Memories by using global variables within System object methods. If the System object via a MATLAB System block accesses a Data Store Memory block in interpreted mode and the Data Store Memory contains a fixed-point type whose characteristics (signedness, word length, fraction length, etc.) are different than any fixed-point type used at the inputs or outputs of the MATLAB System block, MATLAB will crash.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Passing?bus or structure type data to Simulink functions in event actions of SimEvents blocks may produce incorrect results
Description: Calling Simulink functions from event actions of SimEvents blocks might produce incorrect results when the following conditions are met: A data of bus or structure type is passed to the Simulink function The bus or structure type contains fields which are non-double

Summary: MATLAB does not honor the MATLAB proxy settings for a connection to dws.mathworks.com during startup
Description: MATLAB does not honor the MATLAB proxy settings for a connection to dws.mathworks.com . This connection occurs on MATLAB startup to perform a check for product updates.  This bug does not affect system proxy settings outside of MATLAB that are configured.

Summary: Uncompilable code when Parameter Writer access Model block inside For-Each subsystem
Description: Simulink generates uncompilable code when the following conditions are true: You have a Model block inside a For-Each subsystem. A Parameter Writer block accesses an argument of that Model block. The accessed parameter has the Model Default storage class. The Parameter Writer block is not inside the same For-Each subsystem as the Model block.  Compiling the generated code leads to errors like the following:? error C2039: 'InstP_Model': is not a member of 'InstP_top_T'  or? error: "InstP_Model" undeclared (first use in this function).?

Summary: Resizing the Volume Viewer app generates a warning
Description: Resizing the Volume Viewer app before it has completely initialized, generates warnings. These warnings cause no issues with the usage of the app: Warning: Error occurred while executing the listener callback for event SizeChanged defined for class matlab.ui.Figure: Output argument "pos" (and maybe others) not assigned during call to "images.internal.app.volview.View/computeViewportPosition". Error in images.internal.app.volview.View/manageVolumeFigureResize Error in images.internal.app.volview.View>@(hobj,evt)self.manageVolumeFigureResize() ...
Workaround: To avoid this issue, do not resize the app until it is completely initialized.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when opening Simulink on Red Hat Enterprise Linux 6
Description: If the user environment does not contain the LD_LIBRARY_PATH environment variable, MATLAB might crash when opening Simulink.
Workaround: Set your LD_LIBRARY_PATH environment variable by entering these commands in a command terminal:cd (matlabroot)LD_LIBRARY_PATH=export LD_LIBRARY_PATHbin/matlabIf setting the LD_LIBRARY_PATH variable resolves the crash, you can make the fix more permanent by entering these commands in a command terminal: cd ~touch .bash_profileprintf 'LD_LIBRARY_PATH=\nexport LD_LIBRARY_PATH' >> .bash_profile. ~/.bash_profile If you are using non-standard terminal settings, you might need to modify a different start-up script, such as ~/.profile.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Find Nonzero Elements?block?generates incorrect code with fixed-point data types that cannot represent zero values
Description: In the generated code, the?Find Nonzero Elements?block incorrectly excludes some non-zero elements when the following conditions are true: The input to the Find Nonzero Elements block is of fixed-point type.? The fixed-point type cannot represent the value zero exactly, e.g. fixdt(0,8,1,1) . The input signal contains?nearest to zero values that the fixed-point type can represent, e.g. fi(1,0,8,1,1).   The incorrect code causes?a mismatch in behavior between normal mode simulation and all code generation targets (accelerator mode, rapid accelerator mode, standalone code generation, and so forth).?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect initialization of state for Unit Delay block that reuses the same variable for its state and input signal in generated code
Description: The generated code contains an incorrect initialization of a Unit Delay block state for a model that meets all of these conditions: The input to the Unit Delay block is not a conditionally-executed subsystem.? The input to the Unit Delay block is a constant value which does not equal the value of the Initial condition  parameter of the Unit Delay block. In the generated code, reuse occurs between the Unit Delay block state and its input signal.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model with a?Bus Assignment block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a?Bus Assignment block.?  The input to the assignment input port of the Bus Assignment block is one or more signals from a bus. An Assignment  block also connects to the?assignment input ports?of the Bus Assignment  block. The Assignment  block has a Y0-input initialization?signal .  The Assignment  block and Bus Assignment block are inside  the same For-Iterator  or While-Iterator  subsystem.
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, deselect the?Perform in-place updates for Assignment and Bus Assignment?blocks parameter.

Summary: Code generation error when model has Model block in SIL/PIL simulation mode connected to?Merge block
Description: A code generation error occurs under these conditions:? A top model or a model in a reference hierarchy contains a Model block with?Simulation mode ?set to?Software-in-the-loop (SIL) ?or?Processor-in-the-loop (PIL) ?mode. the Model block is connected to a Merge block.  The error is:  You cannot connect the signal from Model Reference block?'<model>/ <block>' output port <port number> to a Merge block when the Model block is in Software-in-the-Loop (SIL) or Processor-in-the-Loop (PIL) simulation?mode. To workaround this limitation, ?first make sure that no errors occur when?the Model block is in Accelerator simulation mode. Next, ?restore the Model?block simulation mode to its previous setting. Then, ?if the Model block is?inside a conditionally executed subsystem that is connected to the Merge block, add a Signal Conversion block (Signal copy) between the Model block and the?Outport of the conditionally executed subsystem. Alternatively, ?if the Model?block is connected directly to the Merge block, ?add an Enabled Subsystem?between the Model block and the Merge block that passes the signal straight?through. Configure the Enable Port of the new Enabled Subsystem so that only?one Merge block input is active at a time.

Summary: MATLAB cannot access Amazon S3 when Federated Login Authentication is required
Description: Accessing an Amazon S3 bucket using credentials based on AWS Federated Authentication issues the error: Cannot find files or folders matching: 's3://mybucket/path/to/folder' . This affects all functions that can read from cloud storage, including datastore , readtable  and imread .

Summary: Import Tool returns an error when importing a file with duplicate column headers
Description: When you use the Import Tool to import a file with duplicate column headers as a Numeric Matrix, Cell Array, or String Array, MATLAB?? returns the following error: Two or more variable names are the same. Rename them so that they are unique.
Workaround: Change the Output Type to Table, and convert to matrix or cell array afterwards at the command line, using table2cell or table2array.

Summary: Indirect assignments for Variant Subsystem choice output connected to ground block
Description: A Variant Subsystem output connected to a constant parameter in one choice?and to a ground block in another choice, may generate the below code with indirect assignments.    For example :    void In1(void)?  {?  #if a == 1?      ? mdl1_B.VariantMergeForOutportOut1 = 1.0;?      ? mdl 1_B.VariantMergeForOutportOut2 = 1.0;?      ? mdl 1_B.VariantMergeForOutportOut3 = 1.0;?    #endif?      ? mdl1_Y.Out1 = test1_18b_B.VariantMergeForOutportOut1;?    ? mdl1_Y.Out2 = test1_18b_B.VariantMergeForOutportOut3;?    ? mdl1_Y.Out3 = test1_18b_B.VariantMergeForOutportOut2;?    }    Where, the expected code is :  ?  void In1(void)?  {?    #if a == 1?    ? mdl1_B.Out1 = 1.0;?    ? mdl1_B.Out3 = 1.0;?    ? mdl1_B.Out2 = 1.0;?    #endif? ??  }

Summary: Simulink Coder build process fails on Linux for models containing UDP Send or UDP Receive?blocks when certain GCC C/C++ version compilers are used.
Description: When Simulink Coder is used with models containing UDP Send or UDP Receive blocks, and compiled using some of the recent versions of GCC C/C++ compilers such as GCC 4.9.x on Ubuntu 16.04.6 LTS, the build process fails. These versions of GCC C/C++ compiler require the link flags -lm -ldl to appear last in the build command line. The gcc command line did not have these link flags listed last, and the link phase of the build fails. Errors will indicate undefined references to functions such as 'dlsym' and 'dlcose', and that the build fails to generate all binary outputs.
Workaround: Manually call the gcc build command in a Linux shell with the link flags?-lm -ldl only appearing at the very end of the gcc build command line.

Summary: Incorrect objective status reported when one or more referenced models have Assertion or Proof Objective blocks
Description: Property proving analysis may report incorrect objective status when the model has one or more model references in the below scenarios:  The objectives that are considered for analysis may be incorrectly reported as Undecided due to runtime error ?if the referenced model satisfies either or both these conditions: ? ?Assertion block with the?Stop simulation when assertion fails ?parameter set to ON  ? Proof Objective block with the?Stop simulation when the property is violated ?parameter set to ON  The objective status of the Assertion block in the referenced model may be incorrectly reported as Undecided with counterexample .?
Workaround: Workaround for scenario 1 above :Before invoking Simulink Design Verifier analysis in the property proving mode, turn off the block parameter Stop simulation when assertion fails?for the Assertion blocks and the block parameter Stop simulation when the property is violated?for Proof Objective blocks in the referenced model(s).?
Products: Simulink Design Verifier, Requirements Toolbox, Simulink Coverage, Simulink Check

Summary: Incorrect port name displayed on Property Inspector after undo/redo
Description: Changing the name of a port on a component in the Composition Editor and performing an undo or redo causes a mismatch between the name of the port in the Composition Editor and the name displayed on the Property Inspector.?

Summary: Connector error on MATLAB startup
Description: If you have Wireshark installed, MATLAB might throw the following error when starting up: Error in Connector: Did not start the server. Desired port was: 31515. Last error was: Error while starting socket: Net Exception: Address already in use:  127.0.0.1:31714, ?10048?
Workaround: Uninstall or disable Wireshark.?

Summary: Build with template makefile fails when using rtwoptions to customize DOWNLOAD or MAKECMD macros
Description: When you build code using a makefile generated from a customized template makefile, you might see one of these errors:?  syntax error near unexpected token `|'  | was unexpected at this time   The build process failure occurs under these conditions:  The system target file has a custom rtwoptions  structure that defines  makevariable  Your template makefile uses?  makevariable  to modify one of the DOWNLOAD  or MAKECMD  macros.

Summary: Empty signal hierarchy in bus ports generates an error during HDL code generation
Description: In Simulink, an array of bus can be packaged inside a virtual bus. When an empty bus signal hierarchy is present inside a bus, HDL Coder generates an error during code generation. Assertion failed: . . / . . / src/ cgir_hdl/ pir_transforms/ CoreCompLowering. hpp:97:?

Summary: Slow simulation of Frequency Response Estimator block with Bode plot display
Description: In the Frequency Response Estimator block, selecting Display Bode plot  creates a plot of estimated frequency response that updates during simulation. For faster sample times, frequent refreshing of the Bode plot can cause significant simulation slowdown. The block ignores the Refresh plot every N*Ts seconds  parameter, which is intended to limit the refresh rate.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, clear Display Bode plot. To view estimated frequency responses during simulation, connect a Display block to the frd output port of the Frequency Response Estimator block.

Summary: VGG-16 and VGG-19 pretrained deep neural networks cannot be used in compiled applications
Description: It is not possible to use VGG-16 and VGG-19 deep neural networks in compiled applications made with MATLAB Compiler or App Designer's "Standalone Desktop App" option. The compilation process will work without issue, but when the vgg16() or vgg19() function call occurs in the compiled code, you see an error.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: C Caller blocks with Mux blocks as inputs generate incorrect code
Description: C Caller block receiving an input signal from a Mux block generates incorrect code. The expected generated code should combine the input signals to the Mux block into one vector signal and call the C Caller block's function once with the combined vector signal. The incorrect generated code creates a call to the C Caller block's function for each input signal to the Mux block.
Workaround: Replace Mux blocks with Vector Concatenate blocks as inputs to C Caller blocks.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 5, R2019a Update 4, R2019b, R2020a

Summary: Generating code for a model that contains a Stateflow Chart produces an error that mentions tlc
Description: Generating code for a model that meets the following conditions results in a  tlc  error:  The model contains a Stateflow Chart that uses the C action language with string inputs.  The model configuration parameter Import Custom Code  is selected.    Generating code results in a build failure and a message similar to the following:  Main program:==> matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc:FcnGenRateGroupedCodeFromModule(5237)    matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc:FcnGenerateCodeFromModule(5119)    matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc:SLibFcnGenBodySysFcn(256)    matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc:SLibFcnGenBodyFcnCache(501)    matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc:SLibFcnGenBodySysFcnEntry(608)    matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\commonbodlib.tlc:FcnGenBodySysCache(1594)    matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\commonbodlib.tlc:SLibGenBodyCache(5940)    matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\formatwide.tlc:<NONE>(55) ?

Summary: Polyspace Access users cannot view results
Description: Polyspace Access might not load all the LDAP users you specify in the Cluster Operactor (COP) settings. The affected users can log in to Polyspace Access but are unable to view any results. You cannot manage or change the project permissions of these users. This issue occurs when the number of users that Polyspace Access attempts to retrieve from the LDAP server exceeds the maximum results size that the LDAP server returns. For instance, if you configure the?LDAP Base DN ?in the COP setting to include 150 users, but the maximum results size of the LDAP server is 100 users, Polyspace Access loads only 100 out of 150 users.
Workaround: Use a more restrictive?LDAP Base DN?in the Cluster Operator settings. A more restrictive Base DN improves performance and reduces the likelihood that a Polyspace Access request to load users will exceed the LDAP server's maximum results size.?For example, this base DN only lists a domain component (DC)  DC=MyCompany,DC=comTo make the base DN more restrictive, add an organizational unit (OU):  OU=Users,OU=Development,DC=MyCompany,DC=comPolyspace Access will retrieve only users from the Development organization, as opposed to all users under MyCompany.

Summary: Error with Simulink Coverage when using a toolchain for the CodeWarrior compiler
Description: The following errors might appear when using a CodeWarrior-based toolchain:  ? ?? line xxx: error: function call is not allowed in a constant expression  ? ? ? ? ? #if __option(double_is_4_bytes) == 1?   ? ? Warning: File "..." not instrumented for coverage because of previous error???
