Summary: Symbolic dimensions assertion when realmax is specified as the maximum value in a parameter range
Description: MATLAB might assert when building a model under the following conditions: You create a dimension in a version of MATLAB prior to R2016a. The dimension uses realmax  as the maximum value in a parameter range.? You specify a port dimension as realmax .? You upgrade MATLAB to R2016a or later and try to build the model.?  When you build the model, you get this error: CGIR assertion 'In narrow: Assertion failed: aOther.getMin() <= newMax' failed in 'util_prim/SymbolicSizeRange.cpp:45'
Workaround: In the Model Configuration Parameters dialog box, clear the?Allow Symbolic Dimension Specification?parameter. This will allow the build to proceed without any assertions.

Summary: Power spectrum computation using the Welch method in Diagnostic Feature Designer may return wrong results
Description: Any changes made to the power spectrum computation options, such as the choice of window and overlap, are ignored when computing using the Welch method.

Summary: Dot indexing error when importing requirements from DOOR module without mapping
Description: The Map Attributes dialog box is a part of Import configuration that allow you to map DOORS user attributes to custom attributes in Simulink Requirements, or to skip?certain attributes you do not want to import. If you do not need to make any adjustments and proceed to Import step without opening the Map Attributes dialog, or if you open the dialog and close it without clicking OK , an error pops up after requirements?import. All requirements are imported correctly, but the popup displays?Unable to import , and the imported Requirements Set remains unsaved.
Workaround: It is safe to close the error popup, but you must save the imported requirements. Because of the error,?the destination Requirement Set remains unsaved until you manually save it.

Summary: Model blocks with Schedule rates with set to Schedule Editor using the code perspective causes an incorrect error
Description: When using the code perspective, Model blocks with the Schedule rates with  parameter set to Schedule Editor ?errors on update diagram with the following error: "Unable to create partition in model with code mappings for block '<block>'. Code mappings are established by using the AUTOSAR target, customizing function prototypes, and using the code perspective."  This error also prevents the?Schedule rates with ?option from being set to Schedule Editor ?in such models.

Summary: HDL code generation from windowing functions fails
Description: MATLAB throws an error when you try to generate HDL code from MATLAB functions containing windowing functions such as parzenwin , tukeywin , triang , gausswin ,? and nuttallwin.

Summary: UITable CellEditCallback and DisplayDataChangedFcn: Programmatically changed table array Data in response to an interactive cell edit is not updated in the UITable
Description: When the table array Data  of a UI Table  is programmatically changed in response to an interactive cell edit via CellEditCallback  or DisplayDataChangedFcn  callback functions , only the interactively edited cell displays the correct updated value. The programmatically edited cells do not get updated in the UI Table  display, even though the Data  property prints the correct updated values to the Command Window. In the following code example, the values in the Double  column should always be double of the corresponding values in the Single ?column. Interactively editing a cell triggers the doubling behavior in the CellEditCallback . f = uifigure;?  tableData = array2table([1 2; 2 4; 3 6], 'VariableNames', {'Single', 'Double'});  uit = uitable(f, 'Data', tableData, 'ColumnEditable', [true false]);  uit.CellEditCallback = @myCellEditCallback;    function myCellEditCallback(src, evt)  ? ? src.Data.Double = src.Data.Single*2;  end  Now notice that editing the first cell in the Single  column does not change the corresponding value in the Double  column of the UI Table ; however, printing the?Data   variable in the Command Window shows that the data itself has the doubled value as expected: >> uit.Data  ans =  ? 3ˇÁ2 table  ? ? Var1? ? Var2  ? ? ____? ? ____  ? ? ?10? ? ? 20?  ? ? ? 2? ? ? ?4?  ? ? ? 3? ? ? ?6? ?
Workaround: Use a combination of other UI Table and UI Figure callbacks to mimic the behavior of editing.EXAMPLE 1: When the edited cell is an Edit Field?There are a few cases that signify that a cell edit is completed:The return key is releasedThe user has clicked elsewhere in the tableThe user has clicked elsewhere in the figureFor each of these cases, set up the corresponding callback on the UI Table or UI Figure, and then call the UI Table CellEditCallback function from it to update the UI Table display.?1. Return key released: Use the UI Figure WindowKeyReleaseFcn,?passing in the?UI?Table?as a parameter.f.WindowKeyReleaseFcn = {@UIFigureWindowKeyRelease, uit};function UIFigureWindowKeyRelease(src, evt, srcTable)? ? key = evt.Key;? ? % Trigger myCellEditCallback if 'return' is released? ? if strcmpi(key, 'return')? ? ? ? myCellEditCallback(srcTable, evt);? ? endend2. User has clicked elsewhere in the table (i.e. selection has changed): Use the UI Table CellSelectionCallbackuit.CellSelectionCallback?= @UITableCellSelection;?function UITableCellSelection(src, evt)? ? % Put a drawnow before the call to?myCellEditCallback?to process? ? % any pending callbacks first. This ensures that the Data is ? ? % updated from?myCellEditCallback?before evaluating.? ? drawnow? ??myCellEditCallback(src, evt);end3. User has clicked elsewhere in the figure:?Use the UI Figure?WindowButtonDownFcn,?passing in the?UI?Table?as a parameter.f.WindowButtonDownFcn?= {@UIFigureWindowButtonDown, uit};function UIFigureWindowButtonDown(src, evt,?srcTable)? ? drawnow? ??myCellEditCallback(srcTable, evt);endEXAMPLE 2: When the edited cell is a Check BoxThe check box interactions are different, so they require a different approach. When a check box is clicked during an interactive edit, use a flag to indicate that an edit has occurred, and then use the UI Figure WindowButtonMotionFcn callback to trigger the Data of the UI Table to update and change the flag to indicate that the edit is complete. This callback occurs when the mouse moves.To demonstrate this example, the original table Data from the Problem area will be altered to have check boxes in its first column.tableData2 = cell2table({false 0; true 2; true 2}, 'VariableNames', {'Single', 'Double'});uit.Data = tableData2;?1. Use setappdata?on the UI Figure to initialize the editFlag variable to false at the beginning of the file to indicate that no edit is currently occurring.setappdata(f, 'editFlag', false);2. Create a changeFlag function that uses setappdata?to change the editFlag?to true, indicating a current edit. Use this as the UI Table?CellEditCallback.uit.CellEditCallback = @changeFlag;?function changeFlag(src, evt)? ? % Set app data on the UIFigure?? ? setappdata(src.Parent, 'editFlag', true);end3. Create an updateData function that calls myCellEditCallback when the editFlag is true. Use this as the UI Figure WindowButtonMotionFcn, passing in the UI Table as a parameter.?f.WindowButtonMotionFcn = {@updateData, uit};?function updateData(src, evt, srcTable)? ??editFlag = getappdata(src, 'editFlag');? ? % Only trigger myCellEditCallback the first time the? ? % mouse moves after the edit for performance purposes? ? if editFlag == true? ? ? ? myCellEditCallback(srcTable, evt);? ? ? ? setappdata(src, 'editFlag', false);?? ? end?end?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect array indexing when using S-function blocks
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for an AUTOSAR model when all of the following conditions are true: A root Inport signal feeds to a S-function block. The root Inport?signal has matrix as its data type. The AUTOSAR data access mode of the root Inport is ImplicitReceive .  In the generated code, return value from Rte_read  call might be treated as a pointer to scalar element, rather than pointer to a vector/matrix. This leads to incorrect array indexing.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when opening a model containing Test Sequence blocks in a Variant Subsystem
Description: MATLAB might hang and eventually crash when you load a model with Variant Subsystems if all of the following conditions are true: The variant subsystem contains multiple Test Sequence blocks? The model was created in a release prior to R2018a Before saving that model in the prior release, you recently renamed one or more of the ports of the Test Sequence block(s)

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The generated code does not call the overloaded end method of a class
Description: If A  is an instance of a class MyClass , an expression such as A(2:end) ?produces a call to the overloaded end  method of?MyClass  in MATLAB. The generated code does not produce a call to the overloaded?end ?method of?MyClass . ?Instead, the generated code uses the default value 1 as if the class did not have an?end  method. For example, define the MATLAB class VerboseEnd : classdef VerboseEnd    properties        Data    end        methods        function obj = VerboseEnd(data)            obj.Data = data;        end                function ind = end(obj,k,n)            disp('end');            ind = 2;        end    endend Define the function testClassEnd ?that creates an instance of  VerboseEnd ?and then calls the  end  method.  function x = testClassEndobj = VerboseEnd([1,2,3;4,5,6]);x = obj(1,end);  Run?testClassEnd ?in MATLAB. The overloaded end  method displays 'end'  and returns 2 . This causes an indexing error because  obj ?is a scalar (contains only one object). MATLAB produces this error message:? Index in position 2 exceeds array bounds (must not exceed 1).?  However, if you generate a MEX function for?testClassEnd ?and run the MEX,? testClassEnd_mex? does not display 'end'  and returns obj . This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.

Summary: Stateflow or the MATLAB Function block might generate C source files when the target language is set to C++ during simulation
Description: If there is no custom code specified and the target language is set to C++, during simulation Stateflow or the MATLAB Function block might generate C source files and use the C compiler. This results in a compile or link error.
Workaround: Change the target language of the Simulink model to C++ by following these steps:Open the model in Simulink.In the Simulink editor, select Simulation > Model Configuration Parameters.In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, select Code Generation?pane.In the Target selection section, set the Language to C++ and click Apply.In the?Configuration Parameters?dialog box, select?Simulation Target?pane.Add a space character (" ") under Additional build information > Include directories.Click OK and save the model.Alternatively, run the command below on the command line and re-save the model:set_param(<modelName>, 'SimUserIncludeDirs',' ')??

Summary: The serdes.DFECDR tap weight adaptation algorithm converges to nonideal values.
Description: The serdes.DFECDR tap weight adaptation algorithm previously used the MATLAB function fix ?instead of round ?which leads to less accurate tap values.  The issue impacts all DFE tap weights regardless of the magnitude. But it is most noticeable for DFE tap weights that are less than 50 mV since the deviation is a significant portion of the true tap value.
Workaround: To avoid the issue, set theresolution of the DFE tap step size to the ideal value of zero.? If you are using a serdes.DFECDR System objectTM, set the property EqualizationStep? to 0. If you are using a DFECDRlibrary block, set the parameter Adaptive step size (V) to 0.

Summary: The logic analyzer appears to contain no signals for certain demo models
Description: The display area of the logic analyzer appears to contain no waveforms for some demo models.
Workaround: Click the waveform list area and scroll the mouse wheel.

Summary: Base workspace of parallel workers might not be reset after parallel build of model reference hierarchy
Description: When building referenced models in parallel, you can initialize MATLAB workers through the ParallelModelReferenceMATLABWorkerInit  configuration parameter. If you set the parameter to Copy Base Workspace  , the base workspaces of the workers might still contain client workspace variables after the build process is complete. The base workspaces of the workers might not be reset to their initial state.

Summary: get_modelref_global_variable_checksum error during parallel build
Description: Parallel building of a model reference hierarchy might fail with this error: Error using get_modelref_global_variable_checksum   Invalid Simulink object name: <temp_name>    <temp_name>   is a temporary filename.? This error is likely to occur if the previous parallel model reference build failed.
Workaround: Before you start another parallel build, run this command:pctRunOnAll('Simulink.ModelReference.internal.NewSystemForGlobalVarChecksum.getInstance(''delete'');');?Alternatively, restart the pool of MATLAB workers in your parallel computing environment.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when simulating Model blocks with Schedule rates with set to Ports in rapid accelerator mode
Description: In some cases where a referenced model uses modified library blocks or is from a release before R2016b, the model file might be missing information needed for compilation.  MATLAB might crash when you compile a model that meets these conditions:? If you reference a model mentioned above. The Model block has the?Schedule rates with  parameter set to Ports . You simulate in rapid accelerator mode without first loading the referenced model.
Workaround: Load any referenced models before simulating in rapid accelerator mode. Models re-saved in R2019a will no longer need to be loaded before requesting a simulation.

Summary: Incorrect size of structure from sizeof with Polyspace options -compiler diab and -target tricore
Description: Polyspace calculates the size in bytes of some structures incorrectly when you use analysis options?-compiler diab ?and?-target tricore.  For example, on this structure with a mix of basic type of different sizes: typedef struct {  unsigned int field1;  unsigned long long field2;  char field3;  char field4;} MyStruct; Polyspace calculates the size as 24 bytes (8 bytes alignment). However, the size of the structure with the selected compiler and target should be reported as 16 bytes (4 bytes alignment).
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder, Polyspace Bug Finder Server

Summary: Incorrect enums size when using polyspace-configure on diab toolchain with option -Xenum-is-best
Description: When you run polyspace-configure  on a build system that uses the diab compiler with the option -Xenum-is-best , the resulting options file does not take into account the alignment size of enums.?A Polyspace Bug Finder analysis using this options file might result in a false negative or a compilation error due to the enum size. For instance, the size of MyStructEnum  below should be 2U: typedef enum {A0,? B1,? C2,? D3} Enum1;typedef enum {AA0,? BB1,? CC2,? DD3,?              AAA0,? BBB1,? CCC2,? DDD3,?              AAAA0,? BBBB1,? CCCC2,? DDDD3,?              AAAAA0,? BBBBB1,? CCCCC2,? DDDDD3} Enum2;typedef struct {  Enum1 a1;  Enum2 a2;} MyStructEnum; However, when you run polyspace-configure  and use the resulting options file with Polyspace Bug Finder, the analysis calculates the size of?MyStructEnum  to be 4U.
Workaround: After you run polyspace-configure, open the resulting options file and add option -enum-type-definition with value 'auto-signed-first' or 'auto-unsigned-first'.
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder Server, Polyspace Bug Finder

Summary: Model Advisor check?Check usage of Merge block?incorrectly produces a warning for From block of a Goto and From block combination that connects a conditional subsystem to a Merge block
Description: The Model Advisor check Check usage of Merge block  (ID: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0659 and mathworks.hism.hisl_0015) should only flag blocks that prevent direct connection from conditional subsystems to Merge blocks. Goto and From block combination can be used to connect a conditionally executed subsystem and a Merge block which is a special case of direct connection, but the check incorrectly produces a warning for the From block as it is not graphically connected to the Goto block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Global Simulink Functions called by multiple MATLAB Function blocks or Stateflow Charts might lead to wrong answer
Description: The code generator might generate incorrect code for a model when all of these conditions are true: The model contains one or more Simulink Function blocks whose?Function visibility   is set to global . The model contains multiple MATLAB function blocks or Stateflow Charts that call the global Simulink Functions. In the generated code, multiple calls to the Simulink Functions reuse the same pointer to a variable to store the output arguments of the Simulink Functions.  When these conditions exist, the result of one call to a Simulink Function may be overwritten by a subsequent call to a Simulink Function, which might lead to incorrect results.

Summary: Error when running a deployed application with velodynelidar
Description: When deploying an application with MATLAB Compiler using the velodynelidar  constructor with the default calibration file, the deployed application errors out with this message:   File ....toolbox\shared\pointclouds\utilities\velodyneFileReaderConfiguration\HDL32E.xml does not exist.
Workaround: To work around this issue, manually add the calibration files to the deployed application. Add the calibration file path as a name-value pair argument in the?velodynelidar?constructor. For example:lidarObj = velodynelidar('HDL32E','CalibrationFile','C:\utilities\velodyneFileReaderConfiguration\HDL32E.xml');In the "Files required for your application to run" section of the?Application Compiler, add the calibration XML files.Package the application in Application Compiler.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink Design Verifier may sometimes miss to report impact of approximation on Design Range Check or Out of Bound Array Access Errors
Description: When Simulink Design Verifier performs analysis using approximations such as rational approximation, it reports impact of the approximation in the analysis result. But it may sometimes miss to report approximation impact on Design Range Check or Out of Bound Array Access Errors for Stateflow or MATLAB variables or Simulink signals when the corresponding variable or signal has multiple assignments.

Summary: Generating HDL test bench from MATLAB functions may fail for input arguments of the type double in the absence of Simulink
Description: When generating HDL test bench for MATLAB functions using HDL Coder app, MATLAB errors out if the following conditions are met:  Any input argument is of the type double?  Simulink is not installed.  ?The error message is:? Undefined function 'boolean' for input arguments of type 'double'.
Workaround: The workaround to this is to define a custom function called "boolean" on the MATLAB path that takes in a value and outputs that value typecasted as "logical". For example:?function y = boolean(u)? ? y = logical(u);??end?

Summary: Table in Signal Labeler does not resize when Signal Analyzer app window is resized
Description: The table that shows attribute labels for plotted signals in Signal Labeler does not resize when the Signal Analyzer app window is resized. For that reason, some label values might not be visible after resizing.

Summary: Model block SIL or PIL simulation produces error for AUTOSAR software component with model workspace parameters mapped to SharedParameter
Description: If you run a Model block software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation for an AUTOSAR software component that has model workspace parameters mapped to AUTOSAR parameters of type?SharedParameter , the simulation produces an error. For example:  SIL or PIL component "mymodel" references AUTOSAR calibration parameter "Model workspace parameter 'myparam'" which cannot be initialized. The parameter is not initialized in the generated code and is not supported for SIL/PIL dynamic parameter initialization. To avoid this error, only use parameters that SIL/PIL can dynamically initialize (see SIL and PIL tunable parameter support documentation).
Workaround: To test the generated code for the AUTOSAR software component, you can run a top-model SIL or PIL simulation, which does not produce the error.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 7, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 6, R2020a

Summary: Updated representation of column names that contain special characters in imported data
Description: In the R2018b release, the Database Toolbox changed the representation of column names that contain special characters. For example, you import data from a database that contains a table with a column named Cod. Difetto .  Database Toolbox imports this column name as Cod_Difetto_1  in R2018b. In prior releases, Database Toolbox imported this column name as Cod_Difetto .

Summary: Expression folding regression for custom S-function
Description: The generated code does not contain expression folding for a custom S-function with TLC implementation that meets these conditions: The custom S-function block connects to a Switch block or other Simulink block that supports conditional branch execution. The custom S-function block does not support conditional branch execution. The custom S-function block enabled expression folding through its TLC implementation.  For the custom S-function block, the generated code does not contain an expression output and store this output in a temporary variable.?
Workaround: To work around this issue, the custom S-function should set the?SS_OPTION_CAN_BE_CALLED_CONDITIONALLY S-function option in its C implementation.

Summary: Enabling Saturation on Synchronous Machine pu Standard block gives unstable results
Description: When you select Simulate saturation  in the Parameters settings for the Synchronous Machine pu Standard block under Simscape > Electrical >Specialized Power Systems > Fundamental Blocks > Machines  sub-library, the simulation results are unstable.

Summary: Incorrect compiled properties on the output of Merge?block with?inactive?input
Description: Incorrect compiled properties on the output port of the Merge block when all the following are satisfied: Input port of Merge block is inactive and Merge block is active Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate preprocessor conditional  is unselected on the Variant block The?Variant block propagates condition to?the Merge block  For Example:if 'Model/Merge' block is a Merge block with 2 inports and 1 outport then after simulation   >>pH =?get_param('Model/Merge' , 'PortHandles'); %Port handles for merge block>>outport_handle = pH.Outport;>>get_param(outport_handle,? 'CompiledBusType'); % Incorrect

Summary: Crash when updating model containing Initialize, Reset, or Terminate Function block inside function-call subsystem hierarchy
Description: Your model can be subject to this bug if it meets the following conditions:? The model contains a Function-Call Subsystem block. Within the above function-call subsystem hierarchy, there is an Initialize, Reset, or Terminate Function block with at least one input port. The input port of the Initialize, Reset, or Terminate Function block is connected to a root Inport block. Along the signal path from the root Inport block to the input port of the Initialize, Reset, or Terminate Function block, the signal is combined and uncombined at least once using a Mux and a Demux block, respectively.

Summary: Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator simulation build error in mdlPeriodicOutputUpdate and mdlOutputs functions
Description: Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator simulations are affected under all these conditions: The model has a Model block. The Model block references a model containing only Constant, Initialize, Reset, and Terminate blocks.  Build error is generated because an output function for the referenced model is not generated, as it does not contain a discrete rate.Following is an example of the error message: Error using coder.internal.mexbld (line 258)?/tmp/mex_482447257479_11308/modelRef_msf.o: In function `mdlPeriodicOutputUpdate':?modelRef_msf.c:(.text+0x299): undefined reference to `modelRef'?/tmp/mex_482447257479_11308/modelRef_msf.o: In function `mdlOutputs':?modelRef_msf.c:(.text+0xedd): undefined reference to `modelRef'? collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status Here,?modelRef  is the name of the referenced model.
Workaround: Insert a Discrete block, e.g. Unit Delay block, in the referenced model that contains only Constant, Initialize, Reset, and Terminate blocks.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for conversion of MATLAB strings to doubles does not validate commas as thousands separators
Description: Code generated for?the conversion of?a MATLAB string to a double value is more lenient with its treatment of included commas than MATLAB execution.  For example, suppose that?you generate code for this MATLAB code fragment.  s = "1234,56";y = double(s)?  This code returns NaN  when evaluated in MATLAB. The generated code returns the double value 123456 . For string arguments, the generated code?for?the function double  has the same behavior as the generated code for the function str2double .   This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function Blocks and MATLAB System Blocks in Simulink, and Stateflow charts.?

Summary: Security Issue: Polyspace Access R2019a might allow users to move or rename projects without permission
Description: Security updates are available for Polyspace Access R2019a.  A user without the proper permissions might be able to move or rename Polyspace Access projects. Download the?update ZIP file , and follow the update instructions?here .

Summary: MATLAB might crash during code generation with an assertion error
Description: During code generation, MATLAB might crash with the following error: Assertion at dom_core/ analysis/ loopAnalysis/ RegularSectionBuilder.cpp line 874: In weakUnionComponent: Assertion failed: aComp1->type() == aComp2->type()
Workaround: For MEX mode, setting the coder configuration object (cfg) as?cfg.ResponsivenessChecks = 1;?might prevent the issue from occurring.

Summary: In the Stateflow Symbols pane, the filter toolbox can respond incorrectly when entering text
Description: The Symbols pane can respond incorrectly when pasting or manipulating text in the filter textbox.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for referenced models
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions:  The model contains referenced models with Boolean  input     and/or output signals  The model configuration parameter Pack Boolean data into Boolean bit fields  is enabled. The model configuration parameter Reuse global block outputs  is enabled or the Optimize global dataaccess  parameter is set to Use global to hold temporary results .  The incorrect generated code might contain a read from an uninitialized variable or a write to a variable that never gets use.

Summary: Block parameter modification using mask initialization may cause MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB might crash with a Simulink mask block in the below scenario:  The mask initialization code generates an error message following the selection of a specific option in the mask dialog.  After the error message, the mask initialization code modifies a block parameter (example : block position) using  set_param .   Stack trace includes the below lines:  [ 0] 0x00000001b692292a bin\win64\m3i.dll+00665898 M3I::Deviant::commitTransaction+00000474?[ 1] 0x00000001b6b5da17 bin\win64\m3i.dll+03004951 M3I::NSInterface::ModelIntf::commitTransaction+00000023?[ 2] 0x00000001e9876631 bin\win64\glue2.dll+00812593 GLUE2::Command::commitTransactionsAndHandlePostDo+00000113
Workaround: Add a new function example?delayedParamSet,as below :function delayedParamSet(blk, value)t = timer;t.StartDelay = 0.01;t.TimerFcn = @(~,~) set_param(blk, <SOME_PARAM>, value);start(t);endThen inside the mask initialization callback, change the existing?set_param(gcb, <SOME_PARAM>, <VALUE>)?to?delayedParamSet(gcb, value)The introduced timer causes the parameter setting to occur just after the mask initialization callback completes, thus avoiding the crash.

Summary: Text is illegible during IME input
Description: When using an IME for text entry, the text sometimes appears illegible (black text on a black background) until the IME is closed.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: In the generated code,?str2double?might return NaN when the input contains commas that are not thousands separators
Description: For code generation, the str2double  function only allows commas in valid locations as thousands separators. If the input argument contains commas in any other locations, str2double ?can return a ?NaN  output.
Workaround: Before calling?str2double,?remove all commas from the input value.

Summary: Coding Rules panel in Polyspace Metrics stuck on "Loading data" screen
Description: In Polyspace Metrics, if the uploaded results contain certain types of coding standard violations, the Coding Rules  panel gets stuck with a?Loading data ?message. The coding standard violations in the results do not get loaded on this panel. The issue applies to results containing AUTOSAR C++14, CERT or ISO/IEC TS 17961 violations only.?
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder Server, Polyspace Bug Finder

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: HDL Coder generated code is incorrect for models with constant values propagated to Add blocks
Description: HDL Coder can generate incorrect code for a model if the following conditions are met: There is an Add block used in any of the following modes: ++, +-, -+ or --. A constant value is propagated to all inputs of the Add block.  This issue is more pronounced for fixed-point computations. The incorrect HDL code may be detected when you generate the validation model and then simulate the validation model with an exhaustive test bench.?
Workaround: If your design is affected by this issue, run the attached P-coded file to disable the constant optimization. The syntax for running the P-coded function is as follows:?>> turnOffConstantOptimization('<modelName>');

Summary: Points inserted using Signal Editor?user interface Draw Signal may be offset when inserted via touchscreen
Description: Points inserted using Signal Editor?user interface Draw Signal  may be offset when inserted via touchscreen. The issue occurs for some desktop and laptop touchscreens.

Summary: Incorrect results for multi-rate Simulink message queue during concurrent execution
Description: Models configured for concurrent execution that contain blocks or components that send and receive messages in the same queue with different rates might have incorrect output data.

Summary: Printing sometimes fails in the Live Editor and Help Browser on Linux
Description: When printing in the Live Editor or Help Browser on Linux, MATLAB sometimes throws an error and printing fails.

Summary: Simulink Coder might fail to generate code for a model
Description: Simulink Coder might fail to generate code for a model displaying the following error message: tlc/ lib/ blkiolib.tlc Line: 3502 Column: 50? Unable to find VarGroupIdx within the GlobalBlockOutput scope

Summary: Simulink produces an assertion similar to:?!StructType::isa(aDstType)
Description: Whenyou generate code for a model, Simulink might throw anassertion error if all of the following conditions are true: An output port block of a conditionally executed subsystem specifies '0' ?for its initial output. This output port block connects to a virtual bus that contains a nonvirtual bus. A bus object does not define the virtual bus.  The assertion is similar to:?!StructType::isa(aDstType) .

Summary: Missing inclusion of multiword_types.h file in generated code leads to compilation errors when using parallel build for a model reference hierarchy
Description: When using parallel workers for building a referenced model hierarchy that is using multiword types may result in build errors during compilation due to the missing inclusion of multiword_types.h  file.

Summary: jsondecode?might change the name of a field which is a MATLAB keyword in the context of a JSON object
Description: The?jsondecode  function changes the name? of a field which is a MATLAB keyword in the context of a JSON object, if any other field in the JSON object is not a valid MATLAB identifier. For example, this command:  >> t = jsondecode('{"start":10,?  "end":20,?  "_height":100}')?  incorrectly modifies the end  field name.? ?t =struct with fields:start: 10?xEnd: 20x_height: 100  The correct value should be:? t =struct with fields:start: 10?end: 20x_height: 100

Summary: MATLAB crashes for some locales when Open button is pressed from MATLAB Home tab
Description: If the locale set by the user has a script designator along with language and region, MATLAB throws an Invalid Locale Name ?error and crashes when the user selects the Open  button from the MATLAB Home  tab. The structure of a locale name is <language-script_region>  For example, these locales throw an error:? sr-latn_BA  zh-hans_SG  Example crash snippet is as below: Exception in thread "AWT-EventQueue-0" java.util.MissingResourceException: Can't find bundle for base name mldatx:general, locale sr-latn_BA?  at java.util.ResourceBundle.throwMissingResourceException(ResourceBundle.java:1564)  at java.util.ResourceBundle.getBundleImpl(ResourceBundle.java:1387)  at java.util.ResourceBundle.getBundle(ResourceBundle.java:890)  at com.mathworks.fl.i18n.XMLMessageSystem.getBundle(Unknown Source)  at com.mathworks.fl.i18n.XMLMessageSystem.getBundle(Unknown Source)  at com.mathworks.toolbox.shared.mldatx.MLDATXFileInfo.getCatalogString(MLDATXFileInfo.java:109)  at com.mathworks.toolbox.shared.mldatx.MLDATXFileInfo.getExtension(MLDATXFileInfo.java:60)?

Summary: Security Issue: Released security update availablefor the FlexNet License Manager
Description: Flexera recentlyreleased security updates for the FlexNet License Manager, which is used byMathWorks Software. More information is available in CVE numbers: CVE-2018-20031 , CVE-2018-20032 , CVE-2018-20033 , and CVE-2018-20034 .These vulnerabilities affect only the License Server, not MATLAB.
Workaround: A software update from Flexera can be downloaded from the MathWorks License Manager download page. The patched version is FlexNet version 11.16.2.1.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Erroneous memory reuse of inner MATLAB system object in context of cell arrays
Description: When using MATLAB system objects in the context nesting them via a cell-array, the system objects state may be shared for two cell-array elements. This issue can occur when the following conditions are met: Define your own custom MATLAB system objects (i.e. class inherits from matlab.system) Define a cell-array property with private access that can contain other MATLAB system objects An outermost MATLAB system object that contains a cell-array property with private access that can contain other MATLAB system objects  See the attached BugReport1902005Details.zip  for examples that demonstrate this issue. ? ? ? ? ? ?? ??
Workaround: Avoid using cell-arrays with MATLAB system objects and instead use structures. For an example, see the A_withWorkaround.m file in?BugReport1902005Details.zip.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generator?does not?honor data alignment in generated code when multiple operations or functions with different data alignment requirements access the same data
Description: The code generator does not honor the data alignment requirement, and produces incorrect code, when?all of these conditions?are met: A model contains multiple operation or function blocks that?access the same data.  At least one operation or function has a data alignment requirement originated from code replacement. At least one operation or function has no data alignment requirement or specifies a different data alignment requirement than the other functions or operations.  In the generated code, multiple functions or operations with different alignment requirements access the shared data through a pointer.  When generated code does not honor data alignment requirement, if compiled with a compiler that uses instructions that require data alignment, it can cause numeric results different from simulation or crashes.

Summary: MATLAB might not?find a new class definition after multiple path changes
Description: After a folder containing a class definition is removed from the path and there is still an existing instance of the class, and a new folder containing a class with the same name is added to the path, MATLAB might fail to find the new class definition. Instead, MATLAB continues to use the previous definition, which is no longer on the path. The following reproduction steps demonstrate this behavior:? % dir1?classdef EnumClass?????enumeration?????????a?????????b?????????c??????????end?end?% dir2?classdef EnumClass?????enumeration?????????a?????????b?????????c??????????????e?????????f?????end?end?>> addpath dir1?>> o = EnumClass.a;?>> enumeration(o);?Enumeration members for class 'EnumClass':?????a?????b?????c?>> rmpath dir1?>> addpath dir2?>> enumeration(o);?Enumeration members for class 'EnumClass':?????a?????b?????c?????e?????f?>> rmpath dir2?>> addpath dir1?>> enumeration(o); % Incorrectly uses the class definition in DIR2?Enumeration members for class 'EnumClass':?????a?????b?????c?????e?????f?
Workaround: Call clear all after making changes to the MATLAB path.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generator does not honor data alignment in generated code when replacing operations or functions in a referenced model
Description: The code generator does not honor the data alignment requirement, and produces incorrect code, when?all of these conditions?are met: The model contains a referenced model. In the referenced model, at least one operation or function accesses a constant specified in the top model?with?a constant block.  The operation or function is replaced with a code replacement library (CRL) custom function. The corresponding?code replacement?entry specifies a data?alignment requirement?with?the AlignmentBoundary  property. In the generated code, the replacement function directly accesses the variable that represents the constant specified by top model constant block.  When generated code does not honor data alignment requirement, if compiled with a compiler that uses instructions that require data alignment, it can cause numeric results different from simulation or crashes.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generator?does not?honor data alignment when replacing operations or functions with code replacement library custom functions?that access a subset of data in a subsystem
Description: The code generator does not honor the data alignment requirement, and produces incorrect code, when?all of these conditions?are met: Model contains a subsystem that accesses a subset of matrix data from top model.  In the subsystem, at least one operation or function that access the subset of matrix data?is replaced with a code replacement library (CRL) custom function. The corresponding?code replacement?entry specifies an alignment requirement with the AlignmentBoundary  property.  When generated code does not honor data alignment requirement, if compiled with a compiler that uses instructions that require data alignment, it can cause numeric results different from simulation or crashes.

Summary: Variable-step simulation of a Simulink model containing one or more?FIR Decimation?blocks?may cause MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB might crash when you run a model containing a FIR Decimation block, and all of the following conditions are true:  The model uses a variable-step solver The?Rate options ?parameter on the?block is set to?Allow multirate processing; ?the sample time of the output port of the block is?K ?times slower than that of the input port where K  is the integer value specified for the Decimation factor  parameter The greatest common denominator of all the discrete sample times in the model is a small number relative to the magnitude of the individual sample times.  To help determine if the greatest common denominator of all the discrete sample times in the model is a relatively small number, switch the model to a fixed-step solver and then press Run. If the condition is present, Simulink throws an error similar to:? The computed fixed step size (...) is 1000000.0 times smaller than all the discrete sample times in the model... You can fix this error either by switching to a variable step solver...

Summary: Model with a root bus inport may throw an error during code generation
Description: When you generate code for a model which has a root inport set to a non-virtual bus data type, you may see the below error in the MATLAB Command Window or Diagnostic viewer: Internal error while creating code interface description file: codeInfo.mat. Aborting code generation

Summary: MATLAB might crash for Stateflow charts that contain enumerated data
Description: If you simulate a Stateflow chart that contains enumerated data while the chart is open, MATLAB might crash. The generated stack trace contains lines similar to the following: [ 22] 0x000000003bd62aa1 bin\win64\libemlrt.dll+00338593 emlrtLocationLoggingPushLogR2017a+00004385 [ 23] 0x000000003bd62812 bin\win64\libemlrt.dll+00337938 emlrtLocationLoggingPushLogR2017a+00003730 [ 24] 0x000000003bd3df04 bin\win64\libemlrt.dll+00188164 emlrtCallMATLABR2012b+00001236 [ 25] 0x000000003bd36918 bin\win64\libemlrt.dll+00157976 emlrtCreateEnumR2012b+00000136 [ 26] 0x0000000187cec525 bin\win64\sf_runtime.dll+01033509 sf_mex_create_enum+00000101??

Summary: cd?command fails for NTFS paths/network drive letter paths having junctions
Description: On Windows, the MATLAB?cd?command fails in the below scenarios :    NTFS network path has directory junctions either to directories in different hard disk partitions, or physical drives on a remote machine.   The directory junction, or symbolic link, is created using the Windows? mklink? command.  For example, junction1?is a directory junction to folder?Z:\folder1?on the Z: drive on a remote machine. Diagnostic error shown is as below:   cd \\RemoteMachine\ShareFolder\junction1?   Error using cd    Cannot CD to \\RemoteMachine\ShareFolder\junction1 (Name is nonexistent or not a directory).   NTFS network path is mounted as a drive letter path and has a directory junction to directories on different hard disk partitions or physical drives on a remote machine.    For example,?junction1?is a directory junction to folder?Z:\folder1?on the Z: drive on a remote machine and?the shared network path is mounted to drive letter Y: such that Y:\ is equivalent to \\RemoteMachine\ShareFolder      Diagnostic error shown is as below:               cd? Y:\junction1               Error using cd                Cannot CD to??Y:\junction1? (Name is nonexistent or not a directory).?                  Note: Make sure Z:\ drive/partition does not exist on your local machine

Summary: Unintended change of execution order of inner transition after using "OClock" programmatic API
Description: The "OClock" transition property is intended to change the visual layout of the transition (direction of the source or destination).  It may also change the execution order (even if the execution order is explicit) under the following conditions: Multiple transitions that start or end at the inner boundary of a state Change "OClock" using one of the following APIs on one of the transition objects. For example: transitionObj.SourceOClock = 15 transitionObj.DestinationOClock = 45

Summary: Foreground streaming operations might fail at low scan rates
Description: A startForeground  operation with National Instruments hardware might fail at low scan rates. For example, using the NI USB-6351, a session running at 100 Hz might fail with the following message: s = daq.createSession('ni');addAnalogInputChannel(s,'Dev1',0:15,'Voltage');s.Rate=100;startForeground(s); Warning: Error occurred while executing the listener callback for event DataWritten defined for class asyncio.InputStream:Unable to perform assignment because the left and right sides have a different number of elements.
Workaround: For devices that support faster sampling, running at a higher rate avoids this issue.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation with Function Packaging configured for a conditional non-virtual Subsystem
Description: Incorrect code may be generated when a Variant Source is connected directly to an Inport of a non-virtual Subsystem Below are the scenarios where the incorrect code generation may happen:? Non-virtual Subsystem contains more than one input port. A multiple input Variant Source is directly connected to Inport of a non-virtual Subsystem. Condition on the input port is not same as the subsystem's condition. The non-virtual Subsystem has Function Packaging  parameter set to?Reusable function/Nonreusable  function.  ?Example:Consider a Variant source inside the non-virtual subsystem. The subsystem block's condition is (for example: A==1||A==2). However, Inport1 directly connected to Variant source within has condition A==1. Inport2 connected to Variant source has condition A==2.

Summary: Failure to import DOORS module with tables: Dimensions of arrays being concatenated are not consistent
Description: Simulink Requirements fails to import a?module?of?IBM Rational DOORS requirements if this?module contains a table of requirements, and?one or more cells were?previously deleted?from the?original DOORS table.? The import function throws the following error message:  Dimensions of arrays being concatenated are not consistent

Summary: Linearizing a model with an analysis point on a duplicate inport generates an error
Description: Linearizing a model with an analysis point placed on the output of a?duplicate inport  block can generate the following error. For R2017b and earlier releases:  Error using linearize  Matrix index is out of range for deletion.  For R2018b and R2019a:?  Error using linearize  Assertion failed.?
Workaround: To remove the error, use one of the following workarounds:Insert a nonvirtual block, such as a gain block with unit gain, directly downstream from the?duplicate inport. Place the analysis point on the output of the inserted block, and remove the analysis point from the?duplicate inport. Doing so puts the?duplicate inport?on the linearization path if it is reachable by a linearization output.Place the analysis point on the input signal of the subsystem one level up from the?duplicate inport, and remove the analysis point from the output of the?duplicate inport. Doing so puts the master and?duplicate inport?on the linearization path if they are reachable by a linearization output.

Summary: MATLAB Crash while editing Simulink models
Description: In rare circumstances, MATLAB might crash when you add, copy, rename, or remove Simulink blocks, or during Simulation or Update-Diagram. The generated stack trace refers to: getBlocksWithName  or? removeBlockFromNameCaches?

Summary: Assertion failure in rtwcgtlc.hpp:421 during code generation for Stateflow chart
Description: MATLAB might throw an error when generating code for a model that meets all of the following conditions: The model contains a Stateflow chart that defines and uses a graphical function. In the State Properties dialog box, the?Function Inline Option ?parameter is set to?Auto  or Function .  The assertion will produce the following error message followed by a stack trace:  Code generation assertion '!varInfo->flags.isObjPtr2 || (RTW_CG_INPORT_VAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_OUTPORT_VAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_INPORT_NONAUTOSIMPLE_CGVAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_ROOT_INPORT_SIZE_VAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_ROOT_OUTPORT_SIZE_VAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_INPORT_CSC == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_OUTPORT_CSC == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_DWORK_HSTRUCT_CGVAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_DWORK_CSC == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_DWORK_NONAUTOSIMPLE_CGVAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_DWORK_LOCAL_SCRATCH == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_OUTPUT_CSC == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_OUTPUT_NONAUTOSIMPLE_CGVAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_CONSTANT_OUTPUT_HSTRUCT_CGVAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_OUTPUT_LOCAL_CGVAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_OUTPUT_HSTRUCT_CGVAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_CSTATE_CGVAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_CSTATE_DISABLED_CGVAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_DERIVATIVE_CGVAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_CSTATE_ABSOLUTE_TOLERANCE_CGVAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_NSZC_VAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_ZCE_VAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_INPUT_EXPR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_AGGREGATE_VAR == varInfo->varType)' failed in 'b:\ matlab\ src\ rtwcg\ export\ include\ rtwcg\ rtwcgtlc.hpp:421'   The file path at the end will change depending on the system, but will include 'matlab\src\rtwcg\export\include\rtwcg\rtwcgtlc.hpp:421'.
Workaround: There is no way to precisely?determine which Stateflow functions cause the assertion failure except through trial and error. A possible work around this issue is to modify the?Function Inline Option?of all Stateflow functions to Inline.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Processing entities carrying bus or structure type data with event actions or MATLAB Discrete-Event System block may produce incorrect results
Description: Processing entities might produce incorrect results when the following conditions are met: ?Entities carry data of complex bus or structure type with non-double data type elements ?Entities are processed with event actions or MATLAB Discrete-Event System block (with the?Simulate using? parameter is set to 'Code generation ')  For instance, processing an entity carrying nested bus with non-double data type elements by Event Actions might produce wrong results.
Workaround: If an incorrect result is produced by MATLAB Discrete-Event System block, set the?Simulate using?parameter to 'Interpreted execution'.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: preExit method provided by the simevents.SimulationObserver?is not called?when entities depart from the MATLAB Discrete-Event System block
Description: preExit ?method provided by the simevents.SimulationObserver ?object is not invoked if you set the Simulate using  parameter of the MATLAB Discrete-Event System block to  Code generation .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation results with variants and data store readers/writers
Description: Incorrect Simulation results for a variant model when all the following conditions are satisfied :  The model has connected data store readers and writers.  The connected data stores has any variant blocks on the signal path with the following configuration :  Analyze all choices and Generate Preprocessor conditionals  turned on.  Contains at least?two variant choices.   The variant conditions on the data store readers and its corresponding writers are different.

Summary: Bus propagation fails for subsystem that graphically modifies itself during update
Description: Updating a model might produce an error if all of the following conditions apply: The model contains a subsystem that graphically modifies itself during update. A bus propagates across the subsystem.  The error message looks like: Cannot propagate non-bus signal from output port 1 of <model_name/subsystem_name> to input port 1 of <model_name/Out1> because the destination block requires a bus signal.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 5, R2019a Update 4, R2019b, R2020a

Summary: Generating a MEX with many source files fails during linking on Windows
Description: If you try to generate a MEX file from a large number of source files (usually more than 500) on Windows, code generation might fail during linking and throw an error.

Summary: Simulink does not provide correct consistency check for settings between two Outport blocks connected through model reference boundary
Description: Simulink either throws a false warning or no warning when all of the following conditions are true: The option Underspecified initialization detection  is set to 'Classic' . The model contains a Model block. In the referenced model, a root Outport block is driven directly (or indirectly through virtual blocks) by a conditionally executed subsystem. For the rest of this bug report, the Outport block corresponding to the conditionally executed subsystem output will be referred to as the source  Outport block. In the top model, the output port of the Model block that is driven by the source Outport block in the referenced model drives a destination  Outport block of a conditionally executed subsystem.  Without model reference, i.e. if both the source and destination Outport blocks were in the same model, Simulink will throw an error if the settings Initial output  and Output when disabled ?(if applicable) for both Outport blocks differ. However, in the model reference case described above, Simulink either throws a false warning when the two Outport blocks have the same settings or throws no warning or error when they are different.

Summary: Degraded performance when running large loops with many iterations in live scripts
Description: Code in the Live Editor runsslower than code at the command prompt. In most cases, the performance change is not significant. However, you may notice a difference when running large loops with many iterations.??

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Sample time inherited by a referenced model can cause mismatch between simulation and code generation results
Description: Code generation results differ from simulation results, when all of the following conditions are true: Top model includes a referenced model. The referenced model inherits a periodic sample time that is slower than the base rate of the top model. The referenced model includes a block that specifies sample time inf  and drives a root outport that inherits the specified sample time inf . The top model and referenced model are configured such that parameters are tunable (model configuration parameter Default parameter behavior  is set to Tunable) . You generate code for the top model.  Even if model parameter Periodic sample time constraint  is set to Ensure sample time independent , the r eference model can pass the check and the code generation results differ from simulation results.
Workaround: Consider one of these approaches: Set model configuration parameter Default parameter behavior to Inlined in the top and referenced models.In the referenced model,?set Periodic sample time constraint to Specified and explicitly specify the sample time that you want the rmodel? to use.Insert a Unit delay block that drives the referenced model outport that uses the sample time specified as a parameter. ?

Summary: Test Sequence block or Stateflow chart breakpoint prevents resuming test case execution
Description: If you set a breakpoint in a Test Sequence block or Stateflow chart in a model, running the model from a test case in Simulink Test Manager pauses at the breakpoint. However, all the debugging buttons (Continue, Step, Stop, etc.) are disabled in the toolbar, making it impossible to continue or terminate the simulation.

Summary: Reading?DiskLoggerFrameCount property of videoinput while logging data to disk?causes MATLAB to hang
Description: Reading the DiskLoggerFrameCount  property of a?videoinput ?object?while logging data to disk causes MATLAB to hang. For example, executing the following code causes the hang: vid = videoinput('gentl', 1, 'Mono8')vid.FramesPerTrigger = inf;vw = VideoWriter('myFilenmae.avi','Grayscale AVI');vid.DiskLogger = vw;vid.LoggingMode = 'disk';start(vid)for ii = 1:10    pause(1)    vid.DiskLoggerFrameCount end Similar behavior occurs when reading the FrameCount  property of the DiskLogger VideoWriter ?object. Because of this issue, the?Logging Data to Disk ?example in Image Acquisition Toolbox documentation might not work as expected.

Summary: Inconsistent tunable parameter behaviors between block dialog and command line in rapid accelerator mode and external mode
Description: If a block parameter's value is set to a workspace variable, it cannot be modified in the block dialog during simulation of a model in rapid accelerator mode or external mode. However, if you use?set_param ,?the value changes but it is reflected only on the Simulink canvas. The change does not affect the currently-running simulation results.

Summary: Unable to install an OPC Foundation simulation server for OPC UA
Description: Several examples showing OPC UA features require the installation of OPC UA simulation servers from the OPC Foundation. As of June 2018, these servers are no longer available.
Workaround: To work around this issue, you can install an OPC UA simulation server from Prosys OPC Ltd.Download the Prosys OPC UA Simulation Server tool free of charge from https://www.prosysopc.com/products/opc-ua-simulation-server/.To install the Prosys OPC UA simulation server, follow the installation instructions provided with the software.When you have started the server, you might want to reduce the number of ports used by the server by turning off HTTPS endpoints in the Endpoints tab of the Prosys OPC UA Simulation Server tool.Modify the code provided in toolbox examples to make use of the Prosys OPC server. This typically involves changing the port number argument for the opcua function to 53530 for the Prosys default installation, as well as node names and IDs as applicable. Use browseNamespace to view this information in the Prosys namespace.

Summary: Test harnesses for a model might not load correctly after the associated harnessInfo file is merged
Description: Test harnesses stored externally?might fail to load when all of these conditions occur: Multiple test harnesses are present in a model Two or more test harnesses are edited by different users and submitted to source control The <modelname>_harnessInfo.xml  file, which maintains metadata about the test harnesses, is manually merged?  Subsequent harness operations, including opening or updating metadata, can fail with warnings similar to the following: The test harness <test harness name> was saved externally but the owner block?<path to the block that owns the harness> was not found in <model name>. This  test harness will not be created. Re-save <model name> to update the harnessInfo metadata file <harness info file name>.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Discrete FIR Filter block with Adaptive Pipelining causes mismatch in validation model
Description: When using the Discrete FIR Filter block, a numeric mismatch can occur after HDL code generation if Adaptive Pipelining  is enabled and non-zero values are set on the?MultiplierInputPipeline  or MultiplierOutputPipeline  HDL block property options. The block then reports more latency than is actually introduced during optimizations (i.e block reports 8 cycles but actually only has 6 pipeline stages in the generated code).
Workaround: MultiplierInputPipeline or MultiplierOutputPipeline options need not be specified when using Adaptive Pipelining. Adaptive pipelining introduces the appropriate pipelines around multipliers for proper DSP mapping and frequency. Set these options to 0 if using Adaptive pipelining.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect dead logic reported for multiport switch having constant array as control input
Description: Simulink Design Verifier reports incorrect dead logic when model has multiport switch with constant array/vector as control input.Example: Consider a multiport switch with 4 inputs. For such a multiport switch if the control input is connected to [1 2 ] i.e a constant array/vector, then Simulink Design Verifier incorrectly reports truncated input(2) value as unreachable .

Summary: MinMax Running Resettable block?does?not?hold maximum or minimum value with variable-step solver and continuous sample time
Description: During simulation, the MinMax Running Resettable block might?not output maximum or minimum values when all of these conditions?exist: Solver type is?variable-step. Sample time?is continuous.

Summary: Missing local variables in generated code when Variant Subsystems are inside Function-Call Subsystem
Description: For models with ert targets, Variant Subsystem may generate missing local variables when all of the following conditions are met:  Variant Subsystem is inside a Function-Call Subsystem.  Variant Subsystem is inside the Function-Call Subsystem that connects to another Variant Subsystem.  Variant Subsystem is configured to generate pre-processor conditionals. In Function-Call Subsystem, function-call trigger is from a Stateflow chart.  In Variant Choice blocks, Function packaging ?is set to  Auto/Inline .
Workaround: Set the Function packaging of Variant Choice blocks to Reusable function/Nonreusable function.Disable the Reuse local block outputs?or BufferReuse in Configuration Parameters of the model.

Summary: Live Editor throws system configuration error message when opened on Linux
Description: Recent Linux distributions do not include the libgconf-2.so.4 library, which is required by the embedded browser used within MATLAB to display the Live Editor and some other user interfaces.  When this library is unavailable, opening the Live Editor results in a dialog with the message "The Live Editor is unable to run in the current system configuration." Other user interfaces that rely on the embedded browser, such as the Simulink Start page or the Help browser, operate with limited functionality instead of throwing an error message. ?For a similar issue see?Bug Report?1885601 ?
Workaround: Ubuntu users can install the required library by running this command:sudo apt-get install libgconf2-4?

Summary: Inconsistent behavior for model using data dictionaries with the same name in different folders
Description: A model might behave inconsistently when using multiple data dictionaries with the same name. For example, consider two dictionaries with the same name in different folders. If you open and compile a model in the folder of the first dictionary, the model reads values from that dictionary. If you then change to the second folder, or change the MATLAB path to include second folder, the model is inconsistent about which dictionary it uses.

Summary: MATLAB may crash when running models in External mode
Description: A MATLAB crash may occur when running models in External mode. The crash is more likely to happen for models configured with smaller time steps or that run for long time. The issue occurs only on the Windows operating system with certain newer CPU types, and is related to an incompatibility between the real-time binary code generated for these CPUs and the real-time kernel.
Workaround: Follow these steps to work around this issue:Open the compiler configuration file? ? ?edit(fullfile(toolboxdir('sldrt'),'rtw','sldrtclang.mk'))Navigate to line 34, which starts with?LLC :=.?Add the compiler option -mattr=-avx to the end of the line.Save the file. You need administrator privileges to save the file.Rebuild all models affected by this issue before running them.

Summary: Baselines for iterations not captured programmatically
Description: Baselines for iterations are not being captured or saved when you use Simulink Test's programmatic interface.
Workaround: To capture baselines for iterations, select "Capture Baselines for Iterations" in the Test Manager.

Summary: Uncompilable code when Simulink Function has a return argument and contains a Variant Subsystem block
Description: The code generator generates uncompilable code when the following conditions are true:? The model contains a Simulink Function block. The C/C++ return argument  ?field of the Simulink Function block's Configure C/C++ Function Interface  ?dialog box ?is not set to void . The Simulink Function block contains a Variant Subsystem block. The Variant Subsystem block's Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate preprocessor conditionals  option is selected.  Under these conditions, compiling the generated code leads to errors similar to the following: error: conflicting types for simulink_function_foo ?

Summary: Compatibility checks fail when Remove reset function parameter is not selected even though code inspection passes
Description: If the model configuration parameter Remove reset function  is not selected, the compatibility checks fail and recommend that you select this parameter even though code inspection passes. Starting in R2018b, verification does not depend on the Remove reset function  parameter setting, so the compatibility checks should not recommend that you select this parameter.

Summary: Test generation fails for Bus containing vector of enumerations as elements
Description: Test generation analysis fails when all the below conditions are satisfied:  The model contains non-virtual buses  The bus contains vector of enumerations as elements. For example,   classdef    BasicEnum < Simulink.IntEnumType     enumeration??????           ZERO(0)                                            ONE(  1)                end          end     The analysis fails with the below message in the Diagnostic Viewer window: Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier abstract interpretation back end.  Error during analysis: Stopeed Due to errors  Additionally, the following message is reported in the Results Summary window:?  Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier abstract interpretation back end.An unexpected internal error occurred in Simulink Design Verifier. If you can reproduce this problem, please report it to MathWorks by copying this log contents and including the .dvo file contained in the directory:   <path to log file >

Summary: MATLAB crashes when a?parfor-loop contains an anonymous function that references a nested function
Description: MATLAB crashes when a parfor -loop contains an anonymous function that references a nested function. The crash occurs prior to the execution of the parfor -loop. The crash can occur upon saving the MATLAB file or when setting a breakpoint.
Workaround: The body of a parfor-loop cannot reference a nested function. However, it can call a nested function by a function handle. To avoid the crash, create a function handle to the nested function outside of the body of the parfor-loop. Then, use feval to invoke the function handle in the parfor-loop body.This example shows how to fix the code in the original?function. The workaround?function correctly invokes the nested function by using?nestedHandle.function original?parfor i = 1:10? ? fh = @() nested(i);? ? fh()end? ? function nested(n)? ? ? ? disp(n)? ? endend?function workaroundnestedHandle = @nested;parfor i = 1:10? ? fh = @() feval(nestedHandle,i);? ? fh()end? ? function nested(n)? ? ? ? disp(n)? ? endend

Summary: Initial output values of conditionally executed subsystems are ignored
Description: Simulink Design Verifier may ignore the initial output value of a conditionally executed?subsystem, if the initial output value is inherited from a signal object and the model is set to Simplified initialization mode.
Workaround: Specify the required initial output values of conditionally executed subsystems by using the dialog parameters of Outport blocks.

Summary: Dynamic field references to dynamically introduced nested structures within an anonymous functions crashes MATLAB
Description: A nested structure is a structure that contains another structure in one of its fields. A dynamically introduced structure is a variable introduced at runtime with a load , eval , evalin , or assignin  statement, or a call to a script. A dynamic field name reference is defined by a variable or an expression. When a dynamic field reference to a dynamically introduced structure is used inside an anonymous function, MATLAB can crash. For example, the following code will result in a MATLAB crash. function functionName()   load('fileName.mat');   % Dynamically introduce the structName variable.   dynamicExpression = 'fieldName';    fh = @(x) structName.(dynamicExpression).fieldName(x);end
Workaround: Instead of dynamically referencing a nested structure from within an anonymous function, create a temporary variable to index into the structure and use that variable to access the structure field inside the anonymous function. For the example above, instead of creating the dynamic reference within the anonymous function, usetemp = structName.(dynamicExpression);fh = @(x) temp.fieldName(x);

Summary: Using the dot operator on dynamic field names in nested functions might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: If you use the dot operator on a dynamic ?field name in a nested function and if the nested function does not define the struct, MATLAB crashes. Consider the following example. function main()    function nested(key)    myStruct.(key).member();    endend In a nested function, a dynamic field name key  accesses myStruct , and myStruct   is not defined within the nested function. This use of the dot operator on the dynamic field name crashes MATLAB.
Workaround: Replace the chained call to member with two lines of code.  For example, instead of myStruct.(key).member() use the following code.tmp = myStruct.(key);step = tmp.member();

Summary: Using end in index expression with function call can result in incorrect behavior or inaccurate error message
Description: If you use end  in an index expression that also includes a function call, in some instances MATLAB can produce incorrect results or an inaccurate error message.  In these instances, MATLAB does not correctly calculate the end  index.  For example, the following code results in the inaccurate error message "Error using ceil Not enough input arguments. " A = [1 2 3;     4 5 6];A(1, end:-1:ceil(end/2)); And the following code, when placed in a script, displays 2  instead of [2 2; 2 2] . ones; M = [2, 3]; M(ones(end))
Workaround: Replace use of end with an explicit calculation of the array size or length. For example, the following code evaluates without error.A = [1 2 3;     4 5 6];n = size(A, 2);A(1, n:-1:ceil(n/2))And the following script contents produces the correct results.M = [2, 3];n = numel(M);M(ones(n))

Summary: Using field and cell array notation to access data in a variable with the same name as a built-in function crashes MATLAB.
Description: MATLAB crashes if you use a built-in function name as a variable name and index into it using both cell indexing (curly braces) and structure field indexing (dot notation). The crash occurs regardless of the order of the indexing.  For example, if you create the variable?format? and index into it using format.a{1}  or format{1}.b , MATLAB crashes.
Workaround: Do not use built-in names as variable names in your functions or scripts. This is a best practice in MATLAB programming. To check if a name is a built-in function, call?exist.?

Summary: Assigning function handle or anonymous function to comma-separated list errors
Description: Assigning a function handle or an anonymous function to a comma-separated list causes MATLAB to issue an error. MATLAB returns a comma-separated list when you create a vector to index into a cell array or structure array. For example, both of the following assignments error with the messageidentifier MATLAB:mir_error_call_of_non_function . [x{:}] = @sin;[varargout{1:nargout}] = @(y) y;
Workaround: Split the original assignment into two assignments.?First assign the right-hand side to a new variable.? Then use the new variable as the right-hand side of the original assignment. For example, use the followingassignments instead of [x{:}] = @sin.t = @sin;[x{:}] = t;
